en.title: MPs ask: where is CDF money for our areas?
en.body: Parliament heard angry questions on Tuesday about delayed money for local development. Minister Ben Phiri said the Constituency Development Fund (CDF is money for each area to build schools, health centres and roads) will start soon. But MPs said three months have passed with no work on the ground. The fund has K1.145 trillion to share.
en.body_more:
Lilongwe City Lumbadzi MP Denis Chalera said his constituents are tired of waiting. "The budget started on April 1, 2026. Today is July 7. Almost three months have passed with no money reaching the ground," he said. Chalera warned that people now think MPs are not doing anything.

Nkhata Bay Chintheche MP Noah Chimpeni told the minister about two stalled projects in his area. A health post stopped when America ended its funding. A theatre at Chintheche Rural Hospital is also not finished. He asked if these projects can get CDF money. Phiri said the Finance Ministry controls the money, not him. Mzimba West MP Aeckim Kumwenda also asked about stalled health centre work in his area.

Phiri said his ministry has done much work. New rules for CDF were made with the Finance Ministry and Justice Ministry. Local councils opened bank accounts for the money. The government hired 680 officers to help run CDF work and 36 internal auditors to check the money. Phiri said funds have started to go to councils. But he also said all stalled projects can get CDF money if they are done in the right way. District leaders must give details of each stalled project first.

Dedza Kasina MP Joshua Malango, from the ruling party, asked a hard question. He said the government has no money to pay builders now. "How will you pay for CDF projects if you cannot pay builders their money?" he asked. The reformed CDF gives each area K5 billion, much more than the old K200 million. It is meant to be a big new plan for giving money to areas for their own choices.

ny.title: Aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo akufunsa kuti: ndalama za CDF za madera athu zili kuti?
ny.body: Nyumba Yamalamulo idamva mafunso okwiya Lachiwiri okhudza ndalama zochedwetsa chitukuko cha m'deralo. Nduna Ben Phiri adati Ndalama Zothandizira Chitukuko cha Madera (CDF ndi ndalama za dera lililonse zomangira masukulu, zipatala ndi misewu) ziyamba posachedwa. Koma aphungu adati miyezi itatu yadutsa popanda ntchito. Ndalamayi ili ndi K1.145 trillion yoti igawane.
ny.body_more:
MP wa ku Lumbadzi ku Lilongwe City, Denis Chalera, adati anthu ake atopa ndi kudikira. "Bajeti idayamba pa Epulo 1, 2026. Lero ndi pa Julayi 7. Pafupifupi miyezi itatu yapita popanda ndalama," adatero. Chalera adachenjeza kuti anthu tsopano akuganiza kuti aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo sakuchita chilichonse.

MP wa ku Nkhata Bay Chintheche, Noah Chimpeni, adauza ndunayo za mapulojekiti awiri omwe adayima mdera lake. Malo azaumoyo adayima pomwe America idamaliza ndalama zake. Bwalo la zisudzo ku Chintheche Rural Hospital nalonso silinamalizidwe. Adafunsa ngati mapulojekitiwa angapeze ndalama za CDF. Phiri adati Unduna wa Zachuma ndiwo umayang'anira ndalamazo, osati iyeyo. MP wa ku Mzimba West, Aeckim Kumwenda, adafunsanso za ntchito yoyima ya malo azaumoyo mdera lake.

Phiri adati unduna wake wachita ntchito yambiri. Malamulo atsopano a CDF adapangidwa ndi Unduna wa Zachuma ndi Unduna wa Zachilungamo. Mabungwe am'deralo adatsegula maakaunti akubanki a ndalamazo. Boma lidalemba ntchito akuluakulu 680 kuti athandize kuyendetsa ntchito za CDF ndi owerengera ndalama 36 kuti ayang'ane ndalamazo. Phiri adati ndalama zayamba kupita kumabungwe. Koma adatinso mapulojekiti onse omwe atsekedwa akhoza kupeza ndalama za CDF ngati achitika moyenera. Atsogoleri a madera ayenera kupereka tsatanetsatane wa projekiti iliyonse yomwe yatsekedwa kaye.

Mphungu wa Dedza Kasina, Joshua Malango, wochokera ku chipani cholamulira, adafunsa funso lovuta. Iye adati boma lilibe ndalama zolipirira omanga tsopano. "Mudzalipira bwanji mapulojekiti a CDF ngati simungathe kulipira omanga ndalama zawo?" adafunsa. CDF yosinthidwayo imapatsa dera lililonse K5 biliyoni, yoposa K200 miliyoni yakale. Iyenera kukhala dongosolo latsopano lopereka ndalama kumadera omwe angasankhe okha.

en.title: Doctor's husband tells court of home attack and wife's death
en.body: Yamikani Bobe told the High Court in Blantyre how armed men broke into his home on November 17, 2025. His wife, Victoria Bobe, a 33-year-old doctor, was shot and killed. Seven men are on trial for her murder. Yamikani said he watched the attack happen and tried to protect his wife.
en.body_more:
Victoria was a consultant obstetrician and gynaecologist. She worked at Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital and taught at Kamuzu University of Health Sciences. On the night of the attack, she woke Yamikani after hearing footsteps outside their home in Chigumula, Blantyre. When he checked the living room, he found some windows open.

Yamikani told the court that one of the suspects pointed a gun at him. He and Victoria ran to the bedroom and held the door shut. Victoria sent a message on WhatsApp to ask the neighbourhood watch for help. Then Yamikani heard a loud gunshot. The bullet came through the bedroom door and hit Victoria in the head. The attackers forced their way in. They had pangas and asked for money and dollars. Yamikani gave them his wife's handbag, a laptop bag with a MacBook, and a bank machine. He also offered to move money from a bank account, but the men left.

In court, Yamikani said he later pointed out two of the seven accused men. He knew them by marks on their faces and bodies. The men pleaded not guilty to murder, robbery and burglary. A retired police officer, Jimmy Kanyerere, also gave evidence. He was the neighbourhood watch leader. He said he saw Victoria's WhatsApp message at 11.34 pm. When he went outside, men told him in Chichewa to mind his business or he would die. He later saw stolen mobile phones near the Bobe home. The state says it will call nine witnesses in total.

ny.title: Mwamuna wa dokotala auza khoti za kuukiridwa kunyumba ndi imfa ya mkazi wake
ny.body: Yamikani Bobe adauza Khoti Lalikulu ku Blantyre momwe amuna okhala ndi zida adalowa mnyumba mwake pa Novembala 17, 2025. Mkazi wake, Victoria Bobe, dokotala wazaka 33, adawomberedwa ndi kuphedwa. Amuna asanu ndi awiri akuimbidwa mlandu wopha mkazi wake. Yamikani adati adawonera kuukiraku kukuchitika ndipo adayesetsa kuteteza mkazi wake.
ny.body_more:
Victoria anali katswiri wa za mimba komanso dokotala wa matenda a akazi. Ankagwira ntchito ku Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital ndipo ankaphunzitsa ku Kamuzu University of Health Sciences. Usiku wa kuukirako, anadzutsa Yamikani atamva mapazi kunja kwa nyumba yawo ku Chigumula, Blantyre. Atayang'ana chipinda chochezera, anapeza mawindo otseguka.

Yamikani anauza khoti kuti m'modzi mwa okayikiridwayo anamuloza mfuti. Iye ndi Victoria anathamangira kuchipinda chogona ndipo anatseka chitseko. Victoria anatumiza uthenga pa WhatsApp kuti akapemphe thandizo kwa oyang'anira m'deralo. Kenako Yamikani anamva kulira kwa mfuti kwakukulu. Chipolopolocho chinadutsa pakhomo la chipinda chogona ndipo chinagunda Victoria pamutu. Owukirawo analowa mwachangu. Anali ndi ma panga ndipo anapempha ndalama ndi ndalama. Yamikani anawapatsa chikwama cha mkazi wake, chikwama cha laputopu chokhala ndi MacBook, ndi makina a banki. Anaperekanso kuti achotse ndalama kuchokera ku akaunti ya banki, koma amunawo anachoka.

M'khoti, Yamikani anati pambuyo pake analoza amuna awiri mwa asanu ndi awiri omwe akuimbidwa mlandu. Anawadziwa ndi zizindikiro pankhope ndi matupi awo. Amunawo anakana mlandu wakupha, kuba ndi kuba. Wapolisi wopuma pantchito, Jimmy Kanyerere, nayenso anapereka umboni. Iye anali mtsogoleri wa gulu loyang'anira anthu ammudzi. Anati adawona uthenga wa pa WhatsApp wa Victoria nthawi ya 11:34 pm. Atatuluka panja, amuna adamuuza mu Chichewa kuti asamalire nkhani zake apo ayi angamwalire. Pambuyo pake adawona mafoni obedwa pafupi ndi nyumba ya Bobe. Boma likuti liitana mboni zisanu ndi zinayi zonse.

en.title: Malawi football sets K1 billion fundraising goal for women's team
en.body: The Football Association of Malawi wants to raise K1 billion. They will hold a dinner on Friday. The dinner is for the Malawi Scorchers women's football team. The team will play at the 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations. The dinner will be at Bingu International Convention Centre in Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
The First Lady Gertrude Mutharika will attend the dinner as guest of honour. The Football Association has set aside 500 tables for companies and supporters to buy. Money from the dinner will help the team prepare for the tournament in Morocco.

Mayamiko Kafwamba works for the Football Association. He said the dinner will help raise money for the team. Blantyre City Mayor Isaac Jomo Osman will help sell tickets in the Southern Region. Ivy Chinangwa will help sell tickets in Lilongwe. Ivy Chinangwa works for Malawi National Council of Sports.

The dinner will also show the new Scorchers shirt. The team will wear this shirt at the tournament. Government leaders, companies and football fans will come to the dinner. They will say goodbye to the team before the tournament.

The Scorchers are in Group C at the tournament. They will play Nigeria, Zambia and Egypt. Nigeria won the last tournament. The Football Association asked companies and fans to buy tables and tickets. They said the money will help women's football grow in Malawi.

ny.title: Mpira wa ku Malawi wakhazikitsa cholinga chopezera ndalama zokwana K1 biliyoni ku timu ya azimayi
ny.body: Bungwe la Mpira wa Miguu la Malawi likufuna kusonkhanitsa K1 biliyoni. Adzachita chakudya chamadzulo Lachisanu. Chakudya chamadzulo ndi cha timu ya mpira wa azimayi ya Malawi Scorchers. Gululo lidzasewera pa mpikisano wa Women's Africa Cup of Nations wa 2026. Chakudya chamadzulo chidzachitikira ku Bingu International Convention Centre ku Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Mkazi Woyamba Gertrude Mutharika adzapezeka pa chakudya chamadzulo ngati mlendo wolemekezeka. Bungwe la Mpira laika pambali matebulo 500 kuti makampani ndi othandizira agule. Ndalama zochokera pa chakudya chamadzulo zithandiza gululo kukonzekera mpikisano ku Morocco.

Mayamiko Kafwamba amagwira ntchito ku Bungwe la Mpira. Iye anati chakudya chamadzulo chithandiza kupeza ndalama za gululo. Meya wa mzinda wa Blantyre Isaac Jomo Osman athandiza kugulitsa matikiti ku Southern Region. Ivy Chinangwa athandiza kugulitsa matikiti ku Lilongwe. Ivy Chinangwa amagwira ntchito ku Malawi National Council of Sports.

Chakudya chamadzulo chidzawonetsanso malaya atsopano a Scorchers. Gululo lidzavala malaya awa pa mpikisano. Atsogoleri aboma, makampani ndi okonda mpira adzabwera ku chakudya chamadzulo. Adzatsanzikana ndi gululo mpikisano usanayambe.

Scorchers ali mu Gulu C pa mpikisano. Adzasewera ndi Nigeria, Zambia ndi Egypt. Nigeria idapambana mpikisano womaliza. Bungwe la Mpira lapempha makampani ndi okonda kuti agule matebulo ndi matikiti. Anati ndalamazo zithandiza kukula kwa mpira wa azimayi ku Malawi.

en.title: Egypt loses to Argentina in World Cup match
en.body: Egypt lost 3-2 to Argentina in a World Cup game on Tuesday in Atlanta. Egypt led 2-0 until the 79th minute. Argentina then scored three goals to win. Coach Hossam Hassan said the referee made bad decisions.
en.body_more:
The match was played at Atlanta Stadium. Egypt took the lead in the 15th minute when Yasser Ibrahim scored with his head. Egypt made it 2-0 in the 67th minute when Mostafa Ziko scored. Argentina started their comeback in the 79th minute. Cristian Romero scored first. Lionel Messi then scored to make it 2-2. In the second minute of stoppage time, Enzo Fernandez scored the winning goal for Argentina. The final score was 3-2.

Coach Hossam Hassan was angry after the game. He said the referee made unfair decisions. Hassan said Egypt should have had a penalty. He also said a goal by Mostafa Ziko was wrongly taken away. "What happened to us was not fair," Hassan said. "Even if mistakes happened, the biggest mistake is not getting what you must get from the people who decide." Hassan said he would not watch more games in the tournament. He also praised his players. "With our home players, plus Mohamed Salah and Omar Marmoush, we could play with any team," he said.

Forward Mostafa Ziko also spoke against the referee. "The referee was bad and unfair," Ziko said. "He did not want us to win." Ziko ended with a joke: "Well done to Argentina on another World Cup win." Messi scored eight goals in the tournament. This was his best result so far. Argentina will now play the winner of the match between Switzerland and Colombia. Egypt leaves the tournament after reaching the knockout stage for the first time since 2014.

ny.title: Egypt yagonja ndi Argentina pamasewera a World Cup
ny.body: Egypt idagonjetsedwa ndi Argentina 3-2 pamasewera a World Cup Lachiwiri ku Atlanta. Egypt idatsogolera 2-0 mpaka mphindi 79. Kenako Argentina idapeza zigoli zitatu kuti ipambane. Mphunzitsi Hossam Hassan adati woweruzayo adapanga zisankho zoyipa.
ny.body_more:
Masewerowa adachitikira ku Atlanta Stadium. Egypt idatsogola mu mphindi ya 15 pomwe Yasser Ibrahim adagoletsa ndi mutu wake. Egypt idagoletsa 2-0 mu mphindi ya 67 pomwe Mostafa Ziko adagoletsa. Argentina idayamba kubwerera kwawo mu mphindi ya 79. Cristian Romero adagoletsa koyamba. Lionel Messi kenako adagoletsa kuti 2-2. Mu mphindi yachiwiri yopuma, Enzo Fernandez adagoletsa chigoli chopambana cha Argentina. Chigoli chomaliza chinali 3-2.

Mphunzitsi Hossam Hassan adakwiya pambuyo pa masewerawa. Adati woweruza adapanga zisankho zosalungama. Hassan adati Egypt iyenera kukhala ndi penalty. Adanenanso kuti chigoli cha Mostafa Ziko chidachotsedwa molakwika. "Chomwe chidatichitikira sichinali cholungama," adatero Hassan. "Ngakhale zolakwa zitachitika, cholakwika chachikulu ndi kusalandira zomwe muyenera kupeza kuchokera kwa anthu omwe amasankha." Hassan adati sadzawonera masewera ambiri mu mpikisanowu. Adayamikiranso osewera ake. "Ndi osewera athu akunyumba, kuphatikiza Mohamed Salah ndi Omar Marmoush, titha kusewera ndi timu iliyonse," adatero.


Wosewera kutsogolo Mostafa Ziko nayenso analankhula motsutsana ndi woweruzayo. "Woweruzayo anali woipa komanso wosalungama," anatero Ziko. "Sanafune kuti tipambane." Ziko anamaliza ndi nthabwala: "Tinachita bwino kwambiri ku Argentina pa chigonjetso china cha World Cup." Messi anagoletsa zigoli zisanu ndi zitatu mu mpikisanowu. Ichi chinali chigonjetso chake chabwino kwambiri mpaka pano. Argentina tsopano isewera ndi wopambana pamasewera pakati pa Switzerland ndi Colombia. Egypt yachoka mu mpikisanowu itafika pagawo la knockout koyamba kuyambira 2014.

en.title: Malawi moves economic plan from paper to real action
en.body: A conference on Malawi's new economic recovery plan was held in Lilongwe. The plan names what the country must do: fix money problems, lower debt, make more goods, sell more abroad, and create jobs. But leaders say the hard work is just starting. They must now act on the plan, not just write it down.
en.body_more:
Phillip Madinga is the head of Standard Bank and helps lead the National Planning Commission. He said a plan on paper means nothing without action. "The government does not create wealth. It creates conditions for wealth creation," Madinga said. The private sector makes goods, jobs, and money. So the government and private sector must work together.

To make this work, Malawi must restart the Public-Private Dialogue Forum, or PPDF. This forum brings the government and business leaders together to talk and make decisions. In the past, the PPDF lost power because of slow bureaucracy and weak political support. Old resolutions became just statements. No one was held accountable. No deadlines were set. This time must be different.

The PPDF must help achieve two sets of goals. The immediate goal is economic recovery. Malawi must close the gap between what it imports and what it exports. It must cut spending and focus on making things to sell. The long-term goal is the Malawi 2063 vision. This vision says Malawi will become a wealthy, self-reliant, industrial country. This requires more private business investment in farming, manufacturing, and city growth.

Standard Bank plc has promised to provide money for recovery. The bank will ring-fence substantial funding for 2026 and 2027. Priority sectors include agro-processing, renewable energy, export manufacturing, and mining. Madinga warned that "dialogue without capital is a seminar." Money is required. Business leaders and government must now move fast from planning to delivery.

ny.title: Malawi yasintha dongosolo la zachuma kuchoka pa pepala kupita ku kuchitapo kanthu kwenikweni
ny.body: Msonkhano wokhudza dongosolo latsopano la kubwezeretsa chuma cha Malawi unachitikira ku Lilongwe. Dongosololi limatchula zomwe dzikolo liyenera kuchita: kukonza mavuto azachuma, kuchepetsa ngongole, kupanga katundu wambiri, kugulitsa zambiri kunja, ndikupanga ntchito. Koma atsogoleri akuti ntchito yovutayi ikuyamba kumene. Tsopano ayenera kuchitapo kanthu pa dongosololi, osati kungolemba chabe.
ny.body_more:
Phillip Madinga ndi mtsogoleri wa Standard Bank ndipo amathandiza kutsogolera National Planning Commission. Iye anati dongosolo lolembedwa papepala silitanthauza kanthu popanda kuchitapo kanthu. "Boma silipanga chuma. Limapanga mikhalidwe yopangira chuma," adatero Madinga. Makampani achinsinsi amapanga katundu, ntchito, ndi ndalama. Chifukwa chake boma ndi mabungwe achinsinsi ayenera kugwirira ntchito limodzi.

Kuti izi zitheke, Malawi iyenera kuyambitsanso Msonkhano wa Public-Private Dialogue, kapena PPDF. Msonkhanowu umabweretsa boma ndi atsogoleri amalonda kuti akambirane ndikupanga zisankho. Kale, PPDF idataya mphamvu chifukwa cha mabungwe ocheperako komanso thandizo lofooka la ndale. Malingaliro akale adakhala mawu chabe. Palibe amene adayankhidwa mlandu. Palibe nthawi yomaliza yomwe idakhazikitsidwa. Nthawi ino iyenera kukhala yosiyana.

PPDF iyenera kuthandiza kukwaniritsa zolinga ziwiri. Cholinga chachangu ndikubwezeretsa chuma. Malawi iyenera kutseka kusiyana pakati pa zomwe imatumiza kunja ndi zomwe imatumiza kunja. Iyenera kuchepetsa ndalama ndikuyang'ana kwambiri pakupanga zinthu zogulitsa. Cholinga cha nthawi yayitali ndi masomphenya a Malawi a 2063. Masomphenyawa akuti Malawi idzakhala dziko lolemera, lodzidalira, komanso la mafakitale. Izi zimafuna ndalama zambiri zamabizinesi achinsinsi pa ulimi, kupanga, ndi kukula kwa mzinda.

Standard Bank plc yalonjeza kupereka ndalama zobwezera ndalama. Bankiyi ipereka ndalama zambiri zogulira zinthu mu 2026 ndi 2027. Magawo ofunikira kwambiri ndi monga kukonza zinthu zaulimi, mphamvu zongowonjezwdwanso, kupanga zinthu zotumiza kunja, ndi migodi. Madinga adachenjeza kuti "kukambirana kopanda ndalama ndi semina." Ndalama zikufunika. Atsogoleri amalonda ndi boma ayenera tsopano kusintha mwachangu kuchoka pakukonzekera mpaka kukwaniritsa zomwe akufuna.

en.title: Maranatha Opens Three Health Clinics with Free Care
en.body: Maranatha International is opening three new health clinics this week. The clinics are called Matete Medical Care. Two are in Blantyre: one in Lunzu and one in Machinjiri. One is in Mponela in Dowa District. Patients will get free check-ups in the first week. They only pay for medicine the doctor gives them.
en.body_more:
Ernest Kaonga is the Managing Director of Maranatha Academy. He said the new clinics will help poor people. Many Malawians do not go to hospital because they cannot pay. Their sickness gets worse. Free check-ups will help people go to the doctor early. Kaonga said: "The free services are meant to encourage people to seek medical attention early."

Maranatha International is well known for its schools and education work. Now it is moving into healthcare too. Kaonga said healthcare is as important as education for helping people. He said: "We are opening Matete Medical Care facilities in Lunzu, Machinjiri and Mponela this week. This is one way of giving back to the community."

Malawi's public hospitals are very busy. There are not enough doctors and nurses. There is not enough medicine. Many people go to private clinics instead, but those are expensive. Maranatha hopes the new Matete Medical Care clinics will help. The clinics will serve people in towns and near towns where many people need cheap healthcare.

Maranatha said it will open more clinics in other parts of Malawi. The organisation has not yet said how many new clinics it will build or when. But the plan is to make a network of health centres across the country.

ny.title: Maranatha Yatsegula Zipatala Zitatu Zachipatala Zokhala ndi Chisamaliro Chaulere
ny.body: Maranatha International ikutsegula zipatala zitatu zatsopano sabata ino. Zipatalazi zimatchedwa Matete Medical Care. Ziwiri zili ku Blantyre: chimodzi ku Lunzu ndi chimodzi ku Machinjiri. Chimodzi chili ku Mponela m'boma la Dowa. Odwala adzalandira chithandizo chaulere m'sabata yoyamba. Amalipira mankhwala okhawo omwe dokotala amawapatsa.
ny.body_more:
Ernest Kaonga ndi Managing Director wa Maranatha Academy. Iye anati zipatala zatsopanozi zithandiza anthu osauka. Anthu ambiri aku Malawi sapita kuchipatala chifukwa sangakwanitse kulipira. Matenda awo akuipiraipira. Kuyezetsa kwaulere kudzathandiza anthu kupita kwa dokotala msanga. Kaonga anati: "Ntchito zaulere cholinga chake ndi kulimbikitsa anthu kufunafuna chithandizo chamankhwala msanga."

Maranatha International imadziwika bwino ndi masukulu ake ndi ntchito zake zophunzitsa. Tsopano ikulowanso mu chisamaliro chaumoyo. Kaonga anati chisamaliro chaumoyo n'chofunika kwambiri monga maphunziro othandiza anthu. Iye anati: "Tikutsegula malo a Matete Medical Care ku Lunzu, Machinjiri ndi Mponela sabata ino. Iyi ndi njira imodzi yobwezera anthu ammudzi."

Zipatala za boma ku Malawi zili ndi anthu ambiri. Palibe madokotala ndi anamwino okwanira. Palibe mankhwala okwanira. Anthu ambiri amapita kuzipatala zachinsinsi m'malo mwake, koma zimenezo n'zokwera mtengo. Maranatha akuyembekeza kuti zipatala zatsopano za Matete Medical Care zithandiza. Zipatalazi zidzatumikira anthu m'matauni ndi m'matauni omwe anthu ambiri amafunikira chithandizo chamankhwala chotsika mtengo.

Maranatha adati idzatsegula zipatala zambiri m'madera ena a Malawi. Bungweli silinanenebe kuti lidzamanga zipatala zingati zatsopano kapena liti. Koma dongosololi ndi loti pakhale malo olumikizirana zipatala mdziko lonselo.

en.title: UTM leader calls for university fee cut
en.body: UTM president Dalitso Kabambe wants the government to cut university fees. The government doubled fees from K650,000 to K1.3 million per year. Kabambe spoke to reporters in Lilongwe on Monday. He said students now pay about K3.8 million a year for all costs.
en.body_more:
Kabambe said the fee increase is not fair. He said it stops poor students from going to university. He told the government to spend less money in other places. He said the State House budget is K80 billion. That money could help pay for universities instead, he said.

Kabambe also wants the government to give free food to students. He said food helps students learn better. He said the government should cut money from the Office of the President. He said the government should cut money from State Residences too. Kabambe said a pension fund could build new student houses near universities. He said other countries do this.

Kabambe is a former Reserve Bank of Malawi governor. He said higher fees make poor students leave university. He said this is unfair. UTM legislator Matthews Mtumbuka agreed. He said the government gives free school to young children but makes university very expensive. He said this does not make sense. Opposition leader Simplex Chithyola Banda also said the fees are too high. The government says the new fees are needed. The debate will continue for many months, people say.

ny.title: Mtsogoleri wa UTM apempha kuti ndalama zolipirira mayunivesite zichepe
ny.body: Purezidenti wa UTM, Dalitso Kabambe, akufuna kuti boma lichepetse ndalama zolipirira ku yunivesite. Boma lachulukitsa kawiri ndalama zolipirira kuchokera pa K650,000 kufika pa K1.3 miliyoni pachaka. Kabambe adalankhula ndi atolankhani ku Lilongwe Lolemba. Iye adati ophunzira tsopano amalipira pafupifupi K3.8 miliyoni pachaka pa ndalama zonse zomwe amalipira.
ny.body_more:
Kabambe anati kukweza ndalama sikoyenera. Anati kumaletsa ophunzira osauka kupita ku yunivesite. Anauza boma kuti ligwiritse ntchito ndalama zochepa m'malo ena. Anati bajeti ya State House ndi K80 biliyoni. Ndalama zimenezo zingathandize kulipira mayunivesite m'malo mwake, anatero.

Kabambe akufunanso kuti boma lipereke chakudya chaulere kwa ophunzira. Anati chakudya chimathandiza ophunzira kuphunzira bwino. Anati boma liyenera kuchepetsa ndalama kuchokera ku Ofesi ya Purezidenti. Anati boma liyenera kuchepetsanso ndalama kuchokera ku Nyumba Zogona za Boma. Kabambe anati thumba la penshoni likhoza kumanga nyumba zatsopano za ophunzira pafupi ndi mayunivesite. Anati mayiko ena amachita izi.

Kabambe ndi bwanamkubwa wakale wa Reserve Bank of Malawi. Anati ndalama zambiri zimapangitsa ophunzira osauka kusiya yunivesite. Anati izi sizolungama. Woyimira nyumba yamalamulo wa UTM Matthews Mtumbuka adavomereza. Anati boma limapereka sukulu yaulere kwa ana aang'ono koma limapangitsa yunivesite kukhala yokwera mtengo kwambiri. Anati izi sizikumveka. Mtsogoleri wotsutsa Simplex Chithyola Banda adatinso ndalamazo ndi zokwera kwambiri. Boma likuti ndalama zatsopanozo zikufunika. Mkanganowu upitirira kwa miyezi yambiri, anthu akutero.

en.title: Parliament Meets to Address Citizens' Big Problems
en.body: Malawi's Parliament opened on Monday for a new meeting. Leaders will talk about Malawians stuck in South Africa, university fees, and farm problems. The Speaker and opposition leader both said they will work on things that help ordinary people, not just make speeches.
en.body_more:
Speaker of Parliament Sameer Suleman opened the Third Meeting of the 52nd Session. He said the meeting will last four weeks. It will handle unfinished work and new matters the government wants to discuss.

The most urgent matter is Malawians who are coming home from South Africa. Many people had to leave because of violence against foreigners there. The Speaker said Parliament will also hear about inland water transport. The House will receive reports from committees. Members will ask questions to government ministers. The government will bring new Bills for Parliament to vote on.

Speaker Suleman praised two committees for their hard work. The Public Accounts Committee checks how government spends money. The Ad Hoc Committee is investigating a plane crash in Chikangawa. The Speaker told all committees to do this same hard work. He warned members not to miss meetings. He also said committees must follow proper rules when they meet with people outside Parliament.

Minister of Foreign Affairs George Chaponda gave a statement about the Malawians coming home from South Africa. He said government is working with South African officials. The government is helping people return safely and return to their lives at home. Members of Parliament then debated the government's work. Many said the government must do more. They want the government to help people with their feelings after the violence. They also want the government to create jobs so people do not need to leave Malawi to find work. Parliament will also host a big meeting in Lilongwe. About 300 lawmakers from other African countries will come. The meeting is in August at the Bingu International Convention Centre.

ny.title: Nyumba Yamalamulo Yakumana Kuti Ithetse Mavuto Aakulu a Nzika
ny.body: Nyumba Yamalamulo ku Malawi yatsegulidwa Lolemba kuti pakhale msonkhano watsopano. Atsogoleri akambirana za anthu aku Malawi omwe akukhala ku South Africa, ndalama zolipirira mayunivesite, komanso mavuto a ulimi. Sipikala komanso mtsogoleri wotsutsa onse adati agwira ntchito pazinthu zothandiza anthu wamba, osati kungopereka nkhani zokha.
ny.body_more:
Sipika wa Nyumba Yamalamulo Sameer Suleman anatsegula Msonkhano Wachitatu wa Msonkhano wa 52. Anati msonkhanowu udzakhala wa milungu inayi. Udzagwira ntchito yosatha komanso nkhani zatsopano zomwe boma likufuna kukambirana.

Nkhani yofunika kwambiri ndi ya anthu aku Malawi omwe akubwera kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa. Anthu ambiri adachoka chifukwa cha nkhanza zomwe zimachitika kwa alendo omwe akuchokera kumeneko. Sipika anati Nyumba Yamalamulo idzamvanso za mayendedwe amadzi akumidzi. Nyumba Yamalamulo idzalandira malipoti ochokera kumakomiti. Mamembala adzafunsa mafunso kwa nduna za boma. Boma lidzabweretsa Malamulo atsopano kuti Nyumba Yamalamulo ivotere.

Sipika Suleman adayamika makomiti awiri chifukwa cha ntchito yawo yolimba. Komiti Yoona za Maakaunti a Anthu imayang'ana momwe boma limagwiritsira ntchito ndalama. Komiti Yoyang'anira Zachuma ikufufuza ngozi ya ndege ku Chikangawa. Sipika anauza makomiti onse kuti achite ntchito yolimba yomweyi. Anachenjeza mamembala kuti asaphonye misonkhano. Anatinso makomiti ayenera kutsatira malamulo oyenera akakumana ndi anthu omwe ali kunja kwa Nyumba Yamalamulo.

Nduna Yoona za Zakunja George Chaponda adapereka chiganizo chokhudza anthu aku Malawi omwe akubwera kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa. Anati boma likugwira ntchito ndi akuluakulu aku South Africa. Boma likuthandiza anthu kubwerera bwino ndikubwerera ku miyoyo yawo kunyumba. Kenako aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo adakambirana za ntchito ya boma. Ambiri adati boma liyenera kuchita zambiri. Akufuna kuti boma lithandize anthu ndi malingaliro awo pambuyo pa chiwawa. Akufunanso kuti boma lipange ntchito kuti anthu asachoke ku Malawi kuti akapeze ntchito. Nyumba Yamalamulo ichititsanso msonkhano waukulu ku Lilongwe. Opanga malamulo pafupifupi 300 ochokera kumayiko ena aku Africa adzabwera. Msonkhanowu udzachitika mu Ogasiti ku Bingu International Convention Centre.

en.title: Malawians in USA mark two independence days together
en.body: Malawian communities in the United States held double Independence Day celebrations on Saturday. They marked Malawi's Independence Day (July 6) and the US Independence Day (July 4) on the same day. Groups in Indiana and Washington State organised the events because Saturday was good for many people.
en.body_more:
In Indiana, Malawians started with prayers at the Seventh-Day Adventist Church in South Bend. They also played a football match. The main event was held at The Bend Lounge in the evening. Martin Chiphwanya, the organising committee chairperson, said Saturday was the best day because many people were free on weekends. He added that Malawi's outgoing Ambassador to the US, Esmie Chombo, was expected to attend. Chiphwanya said about 5,000 Malawians live in Indiana.

In Washington State, the Malawi-Washington Association held a red-carpet event in Lynnwood. More than 100 Malawians came. The event had food, music and dances. Mphatso Namwali, the association chairperson, said the timing worked well for members. He said the association has about 1,500 members. Namwali explained that they usually hold events on weekends so more people can come. He added that July 4 gives them a chance to celebrate both nations at the same time.

Across the US, many cities had fireworks to mark the US Independence Day. The United States marked 250 years since it became a free nation. Malawi marked 62 years since it became free from British rule on July 6, 1964.

ny.title: Anthu aku Malawi ku USA akondwerera masiku awiri odziyimira pawokha
ny.body: Anthu a ku Malawi ku United States adachita zikondwerero ziwiri za Tsiku la Ufulu Wachibadwidwe Loweruka. Anakondwerera Tsiku la Ufulu Wachibadwidwe ku Malawi (Julayi 6) ndi Tsiku la Ufulu Wachibadwidwe ku US (Julayi 4) tsiku lomwelo. Magulu ku Indiana ndi Washington State adakonza zochitikazo chifukwa Loweruka linali labwino kwa anthu ambiri.
ny.body_more:
Ku Indiana, anthu aku Malawi anayamba ndi mapemphero ku Tchalitchi cha Seventh-Day Adventist ku South Bend. Anaseweranso masewera a mpira. Chochitika chachikulu chinachitikira ku The Bend Lounge madzulo. Martin Chiphwanya, wapampando wa komiti yokonzekera, anati Loweruka linali tsiku labwino kwambiri chifukwa anthu ambiri anali omasuka kumapeto kwa sabata. Anawonjezera kuti Kazembe wa Malawi ku US, Esmie Chombo, akuyembekezeka kupezekapo. Chiphwanya anati anthu aku Malawi pafupifupi 5,000 amakhala ku Indiana.

Ku Washington State, Malawi-Washington Association inachita chochitika cha kapeti wofiira ku Lynnwood. Anthu aku Malawi oposa 100 anabwera. Chochitikacho chinali ndi chakudya, nyimbo ndi magule. Mphatso Namwali, wapampando wa bungweli, anati nthawiyo inagwira ntchito bwino kwa mamembala. Anati bungweli lili ndi mamembala pafupifupi 1,500. Namwali anafotokoza kuti nthawi zambiri amachita zochitika kumapeto kwa sabata kuti anthu ambiri abwere. Anawonjezeranso kuti pa 4 Julayi amawapatsa mwayi wokondwerera mayiko onse awiri nthawi imodzi.

Ku US konse, mizinda yambiri inali ndi zozimitsa moto kuti ikumbukire Tsiku la Ufulu wa US. Dziko la United States linakondwerera zaka 250 kuyambira pamene linakhala dziko lomasuka. Dziko la Malawi linakondwerera zaka 62 kuyambira pamene linamasuka ku ulamuliro wa Britain pa Julayi 6, 1964.

en.title: Belgium beats USA 4-1 and goes to quarter finals
en.body: Belgium beat the USA 4-1 in a World Cup match. The game was in Seattle. Belgium played very well. The USA is out of the tournament. This ends the hope for the home team.
en.body_more:
Charles De Ketelaere scored two goals for Belgium. Hans Vanaken and Romelu Lukaku scored one goal each. Malik Tillman scored the one goal for the USA. The match was very one-sided. Belgium had the ball many times. Belgium made many chances to score. The USA could not stop Belgium's attacks.

All three countries that held the World Cup are now out. Mexico lost to England. Canada lost to Morocco. The USA lost to Belgium. Home games did not help any of them.

Belgium will play Spain in the quarter finals. The match will be in Los Angeles on Friday. Spain beat Portugal 1-0. Mikel Merino scored the winning goal for Spain. It was a very late goal. The match was between two teams from Europe.

Before the game, the USA had one player back. His name was Folarin Balogun. He was stopped from playing. But FIFA let him play again. Some people said the USA President asked FIFA to help. Even with Balogun, the USA could not beat Belgium. Belgium played too well on the day.

ny.title: Belgium yagonjetsa USA 4-1 ndipo yapita ku kotala fainali
ny.body: Belgium idagonjetsa USA 4-1 pamasewera a World Cup. Masewerawa anali ku Seattle. Belgium idasewera bwino kwambiri. USA yatuluka mu mpikisano. Izi zikuthetsa chiyembekezo cha timu yakunyumba.
ny.body_more:
Charles De Ketelaere adagoletsa zigoli ziwiri ku Belgium. Hans Vanaken ndi Romelu Lukaku adagoletsa chigoli chimodzi aliyense. Malik Tillman adagoletsa chigoli chimodzi ku USA. Masewerowa anali ogwirizana kwambiri. Belgium idagoletsa mpira kangapo. Belgium idapanga mipata yambiri yogoletsa. USA sinathe kuletsa ziwopsezo za Belgium.

Mayiko onse atatu omwe adagwira World Cup tsopano atuluka. Mexico idagoletsa England. Canada idagoletsa Morocco. USA idagoletsa Belgium. Masewero apakhomo sanathandize aliyense wa iwo.

Belgium idzasewera ndi Spain mu kotala fainali. Masewerowa adzachitikira ku Los Angeles Lachisanu. Spain idagonjetsa Portugal 1-0. Mikel Merino adagoletsa chigoli chopambana cha Spain. Unali chigoli chochedwa kwambiri. Masewerowa anali pakati pa magulu awiri ochokera ku Europe.

Masewerowa asanachitike, USA inali ndi wosewera m'modzi wobwerera. Dzina lake linali Folarin Balogun. Analetsedwa kusewera. Koma FIFA idamulola kuseweranso. Anthu ena adati Purezidenti wa USA adapempha FIFA kuti imuthandize. Ngakhale ndi Balogun, USA sinathe kugonjetsa Belgium. Belgium idasewera bwino kwambiri patsikulo.

en.title: Parliament meets to debate delayed fund payments
en.body: Parliament meets today for three weeks. MPs will debate a delayed fund payment. The Constituency Development Fund (CDF) is money for local projects. K5 billion was set aside for this fund. Many areas are waiting for the money. Opposition leaders say the delays hurt communities.
en.body_more:
The meeting runs until July 31. The Constituency Development Fund is a key source. It pays for small projects across all 229 constituencies. The fund builds classroom blocks, health centers, bridges, markets, roads and boreholes. These are projects that communities choose themselves.

Leader of the Opposition, Simplex Chithyola-Banda, said his party will demand answers from the government. "Communities deserve to know why these funds have not been released," he said. Several MPs have already said they are frustrated. Contractors wait for money. Planned projects cannot start. Some communities may miss out on development this year if the funds do not come soon.

Chithyola-Banda also said the opposition will push the government on the economy. Prices of goods are rising. Many people struggle to buy food. Inflation is high. Jobs are hard to find. "Parliament has a responsibility to hold the Executive accountable," he said. MPs will also look at foreign exchange shortages, rising costs of living, farm support programmes and the national budget. The government will make ministerial statements. MPs will ask questions about key national issues. Lawmakers say the delayed CDF disbursement and the economy will be the main topics of debate.

ny.title: Nyumba Yamalamulo ikukumana kuti ikambirane za kuchedwa kwa malipiro a ndalama
ny.body: Nyumba Yamalamulo ikumana lero kwa milungu itatu. Aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo akambirana za kuchedwa kwa malipiro a ndalama. Ndalama Yothandizira Kukonza Madera (CDF) ndi ndalama zogwirira ntchito za m'deralo. K5 biliyoni idayikidwa pambali pa ndalamazi. Madera ambiri akuyembekezera ndalamazo. Atsogoleri otsutsa akuti kuchedwaku kwavulaza madera.
ny.body_more:
Msonkhanowu ukuchitika mpaka pa Julayi 31. Ndalama Zothandizira Kukonza Madera ndi gwero lofunika kwambiri. Zimalipira mapulojekiti ang'onoang'ono m'maboma onse 229. Ndalamazi zimamanga zipinda zamakalasi, malo azaumoyo, milatho, misika, misewu ndi mabowo. Awa ndi mapulojekiti omwe anthu ammudzi amasankha okha.

Mtsogoleri wa Otsutsa, Simplex Chithyola-Banda, adati chipani chake chidzafuna mayankho kuchokera ku boma. "Madera ayenera kudziwa chifukwa chake ndalamazi sizinatulutsidwe," adatero. Aphungu angapo a Nyumba Yamalamulo anena kale kuti akhumudwa. Omanga akudikira ndalama. Mapulojekiti omwe akukonzekera sangayambe. Madera ena angaphonye chitukuko chaka chino ngati ndalamazo sizibwera posachedwa.

Chithyola-Banda adatinso otsutsa adzakakamiza boma pazachuma. Mitengo ya katundu ikukwera. Anthu ambiri amavutika kugula chakudya. Kukwera kwa mitengo ya zinthu kuli kokwera. Ntchito n'zovuta kupeza. "Nyumba Yamalamulo ili ndi udindo wokakamiza Akuluakulu," adatero. Aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo adzayang'ananso kusowa kwa ndalama zakunja, kukwera kwa mitengo ya moyo, mapulogalamu othandizira ulimi ndi bajeti ya dziko. Boma lipereka mawu a unduna. Aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo adzafunsa mafunso okhudza nkhani zazikulu za dziko. Opanga malamulo akuti kuchedwa kwa ndalama za CDF ndi chuma zidzakhala nkhani zazikulu zomwe zidzakambidwa.

en.title: Malawi at 62: Free from rule, but not from poverty
en.body: Malawi marks 62 years of independence this week. But Professor Paul Aaron Gadama asks: Is the country truly free? Political freedom came in 1964. Economic freedom has not. Millions of Malawians still live in poverty. Gadama says this is the real problem now.
en.body_more:
Gadama is a scholar who studies politics and the economy. He says Malawi has the resources to be rich. The soil is good. Rain falls. Minerals are in the ground. Water flows through rivers. But the country stays poor. "The problem is not what Malawi has," Gadama said. "The problem is how Malawi has managed what it has."

Every year, billions of litres of water leave Malawi through rivers. But farmers still cannot water their crops well. Minerals leave the country with little value added. The country exports wealth and imports poverty, Gadama says. In the 1970s, Malawi was seen as a strong economy in Southern Africa. Today it is weaker. Prices rise fast. The kwacha loses value. Jobs are hard to find. University costs have doubled. These are signs of an economy under stress.

Others note Malawi has made progress. The courts have shown more freedom. In 2020, judges cancelled a bad election result. Schools are free now for many children. More people have electricity than before. Roads are better. But problems remain big. About 21,000 Malawians went to South Africa to find work in 2026 alone. The country borrows money from other nations. Hunger comes back each year. Corruption is still common. Young people cannot find jobs.

Gadama says Malawi must change. The country must use its resources better. It must build factories and farms. It must stand on its own feet, not beg from other countries. True freedom, he says, means a nation that feeds its people and gives them work. That is the victory still to come.

ny.title: Malawi ali ndi zaka 62: Ufulu ku ulamuliro, koma osati ku umphawi
ny.body: Malawi ikukondwerera zaka 62 za ufulu wodzilamulira sabata ino. Koma Pulofesa Paul Aaron Gadama akufunsa kuti: Kodi dzikolo lilidi ndi ufulu? Ufulu wandale unabwera mu 1964. Ufulu wa zachuma sunabwere. Anthu mamiliyoni ambiri aku Malawi akukhalabe mu umphawi. Gadama akunena kuti ili ndiye vuto lenileni tsopano.
ny.body_more:
Gadama ndi katswiri wofufuza za ndale ndi zachuma. Akuti Malawi ili ndi zinthu zambiri zoti ikule bwino. Nthaka ndi yabwino. Mvula imagwa. Miyala ili m'nthaka. Madzi amayenda m'mitsinje. Koma dzikolo likupitirirabe kusauka. "Vuto si lomwe Malawi ili nalo," adatero Gadama. "Vuto ndi momwe Malawi yayendetsera zomwe ili nazo."

Chaka chilichonse, malita mabiliyoni ambiri amadzi amachoka ku Malawi kudzera m'mitsinje. Koma alimi samatha kuthirira mbewu zawo bwino. Miyala imachoka mdzikolo ndi phindu lochepa. Dzikoli limatumiza chuma kunja ndi kubweretsa umphawi, akutero Gadama. M'zaka za m'ma 1970, Malawi inkaonedwa ngati chuma champhamvu ku Southern Africa. Masiku ano ndi chofooka. Mitengo imakwera mofulumira. Kwacha imataya mtengo. Ntchito n'zovuta kupeza. Ndalama za ku yunivesite zawirikiza kawiri. Izi ndi zizindikiro za chuma chomwe chili pansi pa mavuto.

Ena akunena kuti Malawi yapita patsogolo. Makhothi awonetsa ufulu wambiri. Mu 2020, oweruza aletsa zotsatira zoyipa za chisankho. Masukulu ndi aulere tsopano kwa ana ambiri. Anthu ambiri ali ndi magetsi kuposa kale. Misewu ndi yabwino. Koma mavuto akadali akulu. Anthu pafupifupi 21,000 aku Malawi adapita ku South Africa kukafuna ntchito mu 2026 yokha. Dzikoli limabwereka ndalama kuchokera kumayiko ena. Njala imabwereranso chaka chilichonse. Ziphuphu zikadali zofala. Achinyamata sangapeze ntchito.

Gadama akuti Malawi iyenera kusintha. Dzikoli liyenera kugwiritsa ntchito bwino chuma chake. Liyenera kumanga mafakitale ndi minda. Liyenera kuyima palokha, osati kupemphapempha kumayiko ena. Ufulu weniweni, akutero, umatanthauza dziko lomwe limadyetsa anthu ake ndikuwapatsa ntchito. Ndicho chipambano chomwe chikubwera.

en.title: FIFA chief defends Trump call over player red card
en.body: FIFA President Gianni Infantino said he spoke with United States President Donald Trump about a red card given to player Folarin Balogun. Trump asked for the red card to be removed. FIFA then lifted the ban. The decision has caused much anger.
en.body_more:
Infantino said he gets calls from many world leaders and business people about World Cup matters. He told Trump that FIFA's court system would decide the case on its own. "During our conversation, I explained that there was an ongoing legal process involving FIFA's independent judicial bodies and that the case would be decided in due course by the competent bodies," Infantino said.

Trump told reporters he asked FIFA to review the decision because he did not think it was a foul. "All I did was ask for a review. That wasn't even an infraction. That was two guys running full speed that happened to crash into each other," Trump said. News reports say Trump called FIFA three times after Balogun got a red card in the United States' match against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Former FIFA President Sepp Blatter said FIFA was wrong to lift the ban. "Red cards are not overturned by political phone calls. They are overturned by rules, evidence and independent bodies," Blatter wrote. "Football must never become a playground for political power." The European football group UEFA also said FIFA made a bad choice. They said the decision hurt the game's credibility. United States coach Mauricio Pochettino said the original red card was too harsh. Balogun had scored three goals in the World Cup before this match.

ny.title: Mtsogoleri wa FIFA akuteteza pempho la Trump chifukwa cha khadi lofiira la wosewerayo
ny.body: Purezidenti wa FIFA, Gianni Infantino, adati adalankhula ndi Purezidenti wa United States, Donald Trump, za khadi lofiira lomwe adapatsa wosewera Folarin Balogun. Trump adapempha kuti khadi lofiira lichotsedwe. Kenako FIFA idachotsa chiletsocho. Chisankhochi chakwiyitsa kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
Infantino anati amalandira mafoni kuchokera kwa atsogoleri ambiri padziko lonse lapansi komanso amalonda okhudza nkhani za World Cup. Anauza Trump kuti khothi la FIFA lidzagamula mlanduwo lokha. "Pakati pa zokambirana zathu, ndinafotokoza kuti pali njira yopitira patsogolo yokhudza mabungwe oweruza odziyimira pawokha a FIFA ndipo mlanduwu udzagamulidwa panthawi yake ndi mabungwe oyenerera," adatero Infantino.

Trump adauza atolankhani kuti adapempha FIFA kuti iwunikenso chisankhocho chifukwa sankaganiza kuti chinali cholakwika. "Ndinangopempha kuti ndiwunikenso. Sichinali cholakwa. Awo anali anyamata awiri omwe ankathamanga mwachangu kwambiri omwe adagundana," adatero Trump. Malipoti a nkhani amati Trump adayimbira FIFA katatu Balogun atalandira khadi lofiira pamasewera a United States motsutsana ndi Bosnia ndi Herzegovina.

Purezidenti wakale wa FIFA Sepp Blatter adati FIFA idalakwitsa kuchotsa chiletsocho. "Makhadi ofiira sasinthidwa ndi mafoni andale. Amasinthidwa ndi malamulo, umboni ndi mabungwe odziyimira pawokha," Blatter adalemba. "Mpira suyenera kukhala malo osewerera mphamvu zandale." Gulu la mpira waku Europe UEFA lidatinso FIFA idasankha molakwika. Iwo anati chisankhochi chinawononga chikhulupiriro cha masewerawa. Mphunzitsi wa United States, Mauricio Pochettino, anati khadi lofiira loyambirira linali loopsa kwambiri. Balogun anali atagoletsa zigoli zitatu mu World Cup masewerawa asanachitike.

en.title: President Mutharika frees 134 prisoners on Independence Day
en.body: President Arthur Peter Mutharika has pardoned 134 prisoners as part of Malawi's 62nd Independence Day celebrations. The Ministry of Homeland Security made the announcement on 6 July 2026. Secretary Linda Pacharo Moyo said the prisoners met all rules for a pardon. The group includes elderly prisoners, sick prisoners, mothers with babies, and those who served half their sentences.
en.body_more:
The pardon uses power given to the President in Section 89(2) of the Constitution. This section lets the President show mercy to prisoners. Moyo said all 134 prisoners had shown good behaviour while in jail. They also met the rules in the Guidelines for the Granting of Pardon to Convicted Prisoners.

The Ministry said the choice was not random. It was guided by law and by how each prisoner acted in jail. Officials said the pardon helps prisons that are very full. When elderly prisoners, sick prisoners, and mothers with babies leave, there is less crowding. This also helps prison health and welfare work better.

The pardon is part of a tradition in Malawi. The President grants mercy on Independence Day and at Christmas. Human rights groups have long asked the government to free prisoners and make prisons better. The groups say prisons are too full and have poor conditions. They want the government to make bigger changes, not just free prisoners on holidays.

For the families of freed prisoners, the news brings relief. The prisoners can now go home. The Ministry did not say which prisons the 134 prisoners came from. But officials said the work to verify and free them had already begun.

ny.title: Pulezidenti Mutharika watulutsa akaidi 134 pa tsiku lodzilamulira
ny.body: Purezidenti Arthur Peter Mutharika wakhululukira akaidi 134 monga gawo la chikondwerero cha 62 cha Tsiku la Ufulu Wodzilamulira ku Malawi. Unduna wa Zachitetezo cha M'dziko udalengeza izi pa 6 Julayi 2026. Nduna Linda Pacharo Moyo adati akaidiwo adakwaniritsa malamulo onse a chikhululukiro. Gululi likuphatikizapo akaidi okalamba, akaidi odwala, amayi omwe ali ndi makanda, ndi omwe adagwira ntchito theka la zilango zawo.
ny.body_more:
Chikhululukirochi chimagwiritsa ntchito mphamvu zomwe Purezidenti adapereka mu Gawo 89(2) la Malamulo Oyendetsera Dziko. Gawoli limalola Purezidenti kuchitira chifundo akaidi. Moyo adati akaidi onse 134 adawonetsa khalidwe labwino ali m'ndende. Adakwaniritsanso malamulo omwe ali mu Malangizo Opereka Chikhululukiro kwa Akaidi Oweruzidwa.

Undunawu udati chisankhocho sichinali chachisawawa. Chinali chotsogozedwa ndi lamulo komanso momwe mkaidi aliyense amachitira m'ndende. Akuluakulu adati chikhululukirochi chimathandiza ndende zomwe zili ndi anthu ambiri. Akaidi okalamba, akaidi odwala, ndi amayi omwe ali ndi makanda akachoka, pamakhala kudzazana kochepa. Izi zimathandizanso thanzi ndi ubwino wa ndende kugwira ntchito bwino.

Chikhululukirochi ndi gawo la mwambo ku Malawi. Purezidenti amapereka chifundo pa Tsiku la Ufulu Wachibadwidwe ndi pa Khirisimasi. Magulu a ufulu wa anthu akhala akupempha boma kwa nthawi yayitali kuti limasulire akaidi ndikupangitsa ndende kukhala zabwino. Maguluwa akuti ndende zili ndi anthu ambiri ndipo zili ndi mavuto. Akufuna kuti boma lisinthe kwambiri, osati kungomasula akaidi pa tchuthi.

Kwa mabanja a akaidi omasulidwa, nkhaniyi imabweretsa mpumulo. Akaidi tsopano akhoza kupita kwawo. Undunawu sunanene kuti ndende ziti zomwe akaidi 134 amachokera. Koma akuluakulu aboma adati ntchito yowatsimikizira ndikuwamasula yayamba kale.

en.title: Airtel Africa Uses Less Diesel, Helps the Earth
en.body: Airtel Africa used 9.1 million litres less diesel in the last year. The big phone company changed 390 power sites to use grid electricity instead. Airtel Africa boss Sunil Taldar said this helps the earth. He spoke in Lusaka, Zambia.
en.body_more:
Taldar said the company wants to grow its business and protect the earth at the same time. The change from diesel to grid electricity makes the company work better and makes less pollution. Airtel Africa also recycled almost all of its waste during the year.

Airtel Africa works in 14 African countries. The company's network now reaches more than eight in ten people across these countries. This helps millions of people use phones, get education, and find work. Airtel Money is the company's money service. It now serves 54.1 million people. The company uses 2.4 million agents to help customers use Airtel Money. Women are four in ten Airtel Money users. This shows the service helps women use money services.

Airtel Africa gave 6.2 million US dollars to help communities. The money went to four main areas: money services, education, protecting nature, and digital services. The company worked with UNICEF to give free internet to 3,296 schools. More than two million young people and almost 39,000 teachers got this free internet. The company also made 64 free learning websites. More than 11 million learners used these websites. Over 30,000 young people got training to use digital tools. The company gave out more than 250 scholarships for science and technology students through the Airtel Africa Tech Fellowship. These programmes help young Africans become technology leaders.

ny.title: Airtel Africa Ikugwiritsa Ntchito Dizilo Yochepa, Yathandiza Dziko Lapansi
ny.body: Kampani ya Airtel Africa idagwiritsa ntchito dizilo yochepera malita 9.1 miliyoni chaka chatha. Kampani yayikulu ya mafoni idasintha malo opangira magetsi 390 kuti agwiritse ntchito magetsi a gridi m'malo mwake. Mkulu wa Airtel Africa, Sunil Taldar, adati izi zithandiza dziko lapansi. Adalankhula ku Lusaka, Zambia.
ny.body_more:
Taldar anati kampaniyo ikufuna kukulitsa bizinesi yake ndikuteteza dziko lapansi nthawi yomweyo. Kusintha kwa magetsi kuchokera ku dizilo kupita ku gridi kumapangitsa kampaniyo kugwira ntchito bwino komanso kuchepetsa kuipitsa. Airtel Africa idagwiritsanso ntchito zinyalala zake zonse chaka chino.

Airtel Africa imagwiranso ntchito m'maiko 14 aku Africa. Netiweki ya kampaniyo tsopano imafika anthu opitilira asanu ndi atatu mwa khumi m'maiko awa. Izi zimathandiza anthu mamiliyoni ambiri kugwiritsa ntchito mafoni, kupeza maphunziro, ndikupeza ntchito. Airtel Money ndi ntchito ya ndalama ya kampaniyo. Tsopano ikutumikira anthu 54.1 miliyoni. Kampaniyo imagwiritsa ntchito othandizira 2.4 miliyoni kuthandiza makasitomala kugwiritsa ntchito Airtel Money. Azimayi ndi anayi mwa ogwiritsa ntchito khumi a Airtel Money. Izi zikusonyeza kuti ntchitoyo imathandiza azimayi kugwiritsa ntchito ntchito za ndalama.

Airtel Africa idapereka madola 6.2 miliyoni aku US kuti athandize madera. Ndalamazo zidapita kumadera anayi akuluakulu: ntchito za ndalama, maphunziro, kuteteza chilengedwe, ndi ntchito zama digito. Kampaniyo idagwira ntchito ndi UNICEF kuti ipereke intaneti yaulere ku masukulu 3,296. Achinyamata opitilira mamiliyoni awiri ndi aphunzitsi pafupifupi 39,000 adapeza intaneti yaulere iyi. Kampaniyo idapanganso mawebusayiti 64 ophunzirira aulere. Ophunzira oposa 11 miliyoni adagwiritsa ntchito mawebusayiti awa. Achinyamata opitilira 30,000 adalandira maphunziro ogwiritsira ntchito zida zamagetsi. Kampaniyo idapereka maphunziro opitilira 250 kwa ophunzira asayansi ndi ukadaulo kudzera mu Airtel Africa Tech Fellowship. Mapulogalamuwa amathandiza achinyamata aku Africa kukhala atsogoleri aukadaulo.

en.title: Mutharika: Malawi needs economic freedom now
en.body: President Arthur Peter Mutharika says Malawi must now fight for economic freedom. On Monday, he told the nation that political freedom alone is not enough. He said poverty has grown worse over the last five years. To show this, he moved money from Independence Day parties to help poor people.
en.body_more:
Mutharika made these words on Malawi's 62nd Independence Day. He said the country's mission is not finished. "The next frontier is economic independence," he told Malawi. He asked all citizens to work together to make Malawi rich and strong.

The President gave a new order. He said there would be no big celebrations this year. Instead of parties and big costs, only prayers would happen. All the money set aside for Independence Day events would now go to farms, schools, shops, and other work that makes jobs and money. Mutharika said this money would help reduce poverty that has grown very deep in five years.

Mutharika said his government has kept food safe and kept money stable. He said the country is now ready to bring in more money from other lands, make more jobs, and grow stronger. He told Malawi to trust that together they can build a rich country where all people share the wealth. The President said he is sure Malawi will reach this dream if citizens stand together and work as one for a better future.

Mutharika came back to power after winning the 2025 elections. Before this, Lazarus Chakwera was the President. Mutharika said hard times grew during that time. He now wants Malawi to turn from celebration to hard work and building real wealth for all.

ny.title: Mutharika : Dziko la Malawi likufunika ufulu pazachuma tsopano
ny.body: Purezidenti Arthur Peter Mutharika akuti Malawi tsopano iyenera kumenyera ufulu wa zachuma. Lolemba, adauza dzikolo kuti ufulu wandale wokha siwokwanira. Iye adati umphawi wakula kwambiri m'zaka zisanu zapitazi. Pofuna kusonyeza izi, adasamutsa ndalama kuchokera ku zipani za Tsiku la Ufulu Wodzilamulira kuti zithandize anthu osauka.
ny.body_more:
Mutharika adanena mawu awa pa Tsiku la Ufulu Wachibadwidwe la Malawi la 62. Anati cholinga cha dzikolo sichinathe. "Malire otsatira ndi ufulu wachuma," adatero kwa Malawi. Anapempha nzika zonse kuti zigwire ntchito limodzi kuti Malawi ikhale yolemera komanso yolimba.

Purezidenti adapereka lamulo latsopano. Anati sipadzakhala zikondwerero zazikulu chaka chino. M'malo mwa maphwando ndi ndalama zambiri, mapemphero okha ndi omwe adzachitike. Ndalama zonse zomwe zasungidwa pazochitika za Tsiku la Ufulu Wachibadwidwe tsopano zipita ku minda, masukulu, masitolo, ndi ntchito zina zomwe zimapanga ntchito ndi ndalama. Mutharika adati ndalamazi zithandiza kuchepetsa umphawi womwe wakula kwambiri m'zaka zisanu.

Mutharika adati boma lake lasunga chakudya chotetezeka ndikusunga ndalama zokhazikika. Anati dzikolo tsopano lakonzeka kubweretsa ndalama zambiri kuchokera kumayiko ena, kupanga ntchito zambiri, ndikukula. Anauza Malawi kuti ikhulupirire kuti pamodzi akhoza kumanga dziko lolemera komwe anthu onse amagawana chuma. Purezidenti adati akutsimikiza kuti Malawi idzakwaniritsa malotowa ngati nzika zigwirizana ndikugwira ntchito limodzi kuti zikhale ndi tsogolo labwino.

Mutharika adabwerera ku mphamvu atapambana chisankho cha 2025. Izi zisanachitike, Lazarus Chakwera anali Purezidenti. Mutharika anati nthawi zovuta zinakula panthawiyo. Tsopano akufuna kuti Malawi isinthe kuchoka pa chikondwerero kupita pa ntchito yolimba ndikumanga chuma chenicheni kwa aliyense.

en.title: Joyce Banda will not run for president in 2030
en.body: Former president Joyce Banda says she will not try to become president again in 2030. She told local television she has already done her part to help Malawi. Banda said she wants younger people to lead the country now. She will help Malawi in other ways, not from the top job.
en.body_more:
Banda leads the People's Party. She asked: "Do I have to be in State House to serve this nation?" She said she is happy with what she did for Malawi. "I have done my part. I have served my nation," she said. She said anyone can see what she did for the country.

Banda became president in April 2012. She left office in May 2014. She was Malawi's fourth president and the first female president. Before that, she was a Member of Parliament for Zomba-Malosa. She joined the United Democratic Front in 1999.

Banda now does work around the world. Since September 2025, she has worked on peace and development. She leads the SADC Panel of Elders. This group works on peace in Madagascar. She also leads the Global Fairness Initiative. This is an international group that helps poor people get fair chances in the economy. She has been to India, Nigeria, Mozambique, South Africa, Sierra Leone and Madagascar.

Banda also said young Malawians must work hard and start their own businesses. She said young people should not wait for jobs from the government. She called for Malawi to help its people who face trouble in South Africa. "We must hold hands and help our people," she said.

ny.title: Joyce Banda sadzapikisana paudindo wa pulezidenti mu 2030
ny.body: Purezidenti wakale Joyce Banda akuti sadzayesanso kukhala purezidenti mu 2030. Iye adauza wailesi yakanema yakomweko kuti wachita kale gawo lake pothandiza Malawi. Banda adati akufuna achinyamata atsogolere dzikolo tsopano. Adzathandiza Malawi m'njira zina, osati kuchokera paudindo wapamwamba.
ny.body_more:
Banda akutsogolera chipani cha People's Party. Anafunsa kuti: "Kodi ndiyenera kukhala ku State House kuti nditumikire dziko lino?" Anati akusangalala ndi zomwe adachitira dziko la Malawi. "Ndachita gawo langa. Ndatumikira dziko langa," adatero. Anati aliyense angaone zomwe adachitira dzikolo.

Banda adakhala purezidenti mu Epulo 2012. Anasiya udindo mu Meyi 2014. Anali purezidenti wachinayi wa Malawi komanso purezidenti woyamba wachikazi. Asanayambe izi, anali membala wa Nyumba Yamalamulo ya Zomba-Malosa. Analowa nawo gulu la United Democratic Front mu 1999.

Banda tsopano akugwira ntchito padziko lonse lapansi. Kuyambira Seputembala 2025, wagwira ntchito yokhudza mtendere ndi chitukuko. Akutsogolera SADC Panel of Elders. Gululi limagwira ntchito yokhudza mtendere ku Madagascar. Amatsogoleranso Global Fairness Initiative. Ili ndi gulu lapadziko lonse lapansi lomwe limathandiza anthu osauka kupeza mwayi wabwino pazachuma. Wapita ku India, Nigeria, Mozambique, South Africa, Sierra Leone ndi Madagascar.

Banda adatinso achinyamata aku Malawi ayenera kugwira ntchito molimbika ndikuyamba mabizinesi awoawo. Iye anati achinyamata sayenera kudikira ntchito kuchokera ku boma. Iye anapempha Malawi kuti ithandize anthu ake omwe akukumana ndi mavuto ku South Africa. "Tiyenera kugwirana manja ndikuthandiza anthu athu," adatero.

en.title: Mighty Wanderers beat Mafco 3-0 in football match
en.body: Mighty Wanderers won 3-0 against Mafco FC on Sunday at Mpira Stadium in Blantyre. Isaac Kaliyati scored two goals. Clement Nyondo scored one goal. Wanderers now have 18 points from nine games and sit in third place in the FDH Bank Premiership.
en.body_more:
Kaliyati scored the first goal from a penalty kick in the 37th minute. A Mafco player put his hand on the ball in the penalty box. Kaliyati then scored again with a free kick early in the second half. The ball went past Mafco goalkeeper William Mbale. Nyondo added the third goal in the 55th minute from a corner kick.

Wanderers coach Bob Mpinganjira said his team played better in the second half. He said the team made some good chances but did not score all of them. "I am happy with the result," Mpinganjira said. Mafco coach Temwa Msuku said his team did not play well. "We did not play well today and that is why we lost," Msuku said. This was Mafco's second straight loss in the league.

Wanderers came to the match after a 1-1 draw against Dedza Dynamos. Mafco had lost 1-0 to Ekhaya FC the week before. In other games, Silver Strikers drew 0-0 with Chitipa United at Chitipa Stadium. Silver Strikers also have 18 points but sit in fourth place because of fewer goals. Masters FC beat Kamuzu Barracks 1-0. Luanar Mitundu and Ekhaya FC drew 0-0. Red Lions beat Civil Service United 1-0 with a goal from Matamando Matchipisa in the 77th minute.

ny.title: Mighty Wanderers idagonjetsa Mafco 3-0 pamasewera a mpira
ny.body: Mighty Wanderers yapambana 3-0 motsutsana ndi Mafco FC Lamlungu pa Mpira Stadium ku Blantyre. Isaac Kaliyati adagoletsa zigoli ziwiri. Clement Nyondo adagoletsa chigoli chimodzi. Wanderers tsopano ali ndi mapointi 18 kuchokera pamasewera asanu ndi anayi ndipo ali pa nambala 3 mu FDH Bank Premiership.
ny.body_more:
Kaliyati ndiye adagoletsa chigoli choyamba kuchokera pa penalty kick mu mphindi ya 37. Wosewera wa Mafco adayika dzanja lake pa mpira mu bokosi la penalty. Kenako Kaliyati adagoletsanso ndi free kick kumayambiriro kwa theka lachiwiri. Mpirawo udadutsa William Mbale, yemwe anali chigoli cha Mafco. Nyondo adawonjezera chigoli chachitatu mu mphindi ya 55 kuchokera pa corner kick.

Mphunzitsi wa Wanderers, Bob Mpinganjira, adati timu yake idasewera bwino mu theka lachiwiri. Iye adati timuyi idapanga mipata yabwino koma sinagoletse yonse. "Ndikusangalala ndi zotsatira zake," adatero Mpinganjira. Mphunzitsi wa Mafco, Temwa Msuku, adati timu yake sinasewere bwino. "Sitinasewere bwino lero ndipo ndichifukwa chake tidataya," adatero Msuku. Uku kunali kutayika kwachiwiri kwa Mafco motsatizana mu ligi.

Wanderers idabwera pamasewerawa itatha 1-1 motsutsana ndi Dedza Dynamos. Mafco idataya 1-0 ndi Ekhaya FC sabata yatha. M'masewera ena, Silver Strikers idagoletsa 0-0 ndi Chitipa United ku Chitipa Stadium. Silver Strikers ilinso ndi mapointi 18 koma ili pa nambala 4 chifukwa cha zigoli zochepa. Masters FC idagonjetsa Kamuzu Barracks 1-0. Luanar Mitundu ndi Ekhaya FC adagwirizana 0-0. Red Lions idagonjetsa Civil Service United 1-0 ndi chigoli cha Matamando Matchipisa mu mphindi ya 77.

en.title: Businessman linked to missing K2.4 billion energy contract
en.body: A businessman named Mahmood Azhar Chaudhry is linked to a government contract worth more than K2.4 billion. The contract is part of a programme called MAREP. It brings electricity to rural areas. A company called Kumakoka Trading Company got money but did not deliver materials. The government is now taking legal action.
en.body_more:
Investigations say Chaudhry fronted Kumakoka Trading Company in the Ministry of Energy contract. Kumakoka got an advance payment of K523,538,243.57. The company did not supply the materials it promised. A source at the Ministry of Energy said that if one person is fronting a company in bidding, that is suspicious. These are matters for the government to look into.

The Attorney General's office is trying to get back more than K1.4 billion. Three companies took this money. Kumakoka Trading Company received K523,538,243.57. Africa Green Economy received K698,050,991.00. Loui Holdings Group (PTV) Limited received K144,674,756.91. All three companies are accused of taking government funds but not doing the work they promised.

Africa Green Economy says it agrees with the government's claims. The company wants to pay the money back in 30 monthly payments. But it does not agree it must pay interest and damages. Loui Holdings Group says it only did part of the work. It says the money lost value made it hard to finish. The money is called currency devaluation. Kumakoka's lawyer says the company got the Attorney General's claim. The company wants to use arbitration instead of court. Arbitration is when two sides ask a neutral person to decide.

Chaudhry did not answer when asked for comment. The Ministry says getting the money back is important. It will protect public resources and restore trust in MAREP contracts. The government also wants damages and compensation. The delays in bringing electricity to rural trading centres cost more money.

ny.title: Wabizinesi akukhudzidwa ndi kulephera kwa mgwirizano wa mphamvu wa K2.4 biliyoni
ny.body: Munthu wamalonda dzina lake Mahmood Azhar Chaudhry akugwirizana ndi mgwirizano wa boma wamtengo woposa K2.4 biliyoni. Mgwirizanowu ndi gawo la pulogalamu yotchedwa MAREP. Umabweretsa magetsi kumadera akumidzi. Kampani yotchedwa Kumakoka Trading Company idalandira ndalama koma sinapereke zipangizo. Boma tsopano likuchitapo kanthu pamilandu.
ny.body_more:
Kafukufuku akuti Chaudhry adatsogolera Kumakoka Trading Company mu mgwirizano wa Unduna wa Zamagetsi. Kumakoka adalandira ndalama zokwana K523,538,243.57. Kampaniyo sinapereke zinthu zomwe idalonjeza. Gwero la Unduna wa Zamagetsi linati ngati munthu m'modzi akutsogolera kampani popereka ndalama, ndizokayikitsa. Izi ndi nkhani zomwe boma liyenera kuziyang'anira.

Ofesi ya Attorney General ikuyesera kubweza ndalama zoposa K1.4 biliyoni. Makampani atatu adatenga ndalamazi. Kumakoka Trading Company idalandira K523,538,243.57. Africa Green Economy idalandira K698,050,991.00. Loui Holdings Group (PTV) Limited idalandira K144,674,756.91. Makampani onse atatu akuimbidwa mlandu wotenga ndalama za boma koma osachita ntchito yomwe adalonjeza.

Africa Green Economy ikunena kuti ikugwirizana ndi zomwe boma likunena. Kampaniyo ikufuna kubweza ndalamazo m'magawo 30 pamwezi. Koma sikugwirizana kuti iyenera kulipira chiwongola dzanja ndi zowonongeka. Loui Holdings Group ikunena kuti idangogwira ntchito imodzi yokha. Ikunena kuti ndalama zomwe zidatayika zidapangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta kuzimaliza. Ndalamazo zimatchedwa kuchepa kwa mtengo wa ndalama. Loya wa Kumakoka akuti kampaniyo idalandira zomwe Attorney General adanena. Kampaniyo ikufuna kugwiritsa ntchito chigamulo m'malo mwa khothi. Kuweruza milandu ndi pamene mbali ziwiri zimapempha munthu wosalowerera ndale kuti asankhe.

Chaudhry sanayankhe atafunsidwa kuti apereke ndemanga. Undunawu ukunena kuti kubweza ndalamazo ndikofunikira. Kudzateteza chuma cha anthu ndikubwezeretsa chidaliro m'mapangano a MAREP. Boma likufunanso kuwonongeka ndi chipukuta misozi. Kuchedwa kubweretsa magetsi ku malo ogulitsira akumidzi kumawononga ndalama zambiri.

en.title: New questions rise in Chilima plane crash inquiry
en.body: A parliamentary committee ended the first phase of its inquiry into a plane crash on Friday. The crash killed Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others on June 10, 2024. A witness said the government may have known about the crash much earlier than it told the public.
en.body_more:
Moses Nthakomwa, a close friend of Chilima, gave testimony to the committee. He said people were searching when those in power already knew where the crash was and how bad it was. "On the evening of 10 June 2024, people were still searching when information about the crash site and the state of fatalities was already known," he told lawmakers. This claim goes against what the government said at the time. Officials said they only found and confirmed the crash site the next day.

The plane went down in Chikangawa Forest while flying from Lilongwe to Mzuzu. Bad weather and hard ground made the search difficult. For more than 24 hours, the nation waited as teams looked for the aircraft. Nthakomwa said key facts were known to some people hours before the public and the families heard about them. Committee members asked who had this information, when they got it, and why they did not tell people sooner.

Colleen Zamba, the old Secretary to the President and Cabinet, also spoke to the committee. She said the government did what it could given the weather and the crash site. "We did our job as it was supposed to be done," she said. She told lawmakers how alerts came in, how the military moved fast, and how teams went into the forest. The committee wants to know if politics got in the way of the rescue work and if more could have been done in the first hours.

The families of the nine dead have asked why it took so long to find the plane. The inquiry now must look at what was known, who knew it, and why some people kept it quiet. Analysts say how the committee handles this question will show if the probe is fair and true.

ny.title: Mafunso atsopano akubuka pa kafukufuku wokhudza ngozi ya ndege ya Chilima
ny.body: Komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo inamaliza gawo loyamba la kafukufuku wake wokhudza ngozi ya ndege Lachisanu. Ngoziyi inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu pa June 10, 2024. Mboni ina inati boma mwina linadziwa za ngoziyi kale kwambiri kuposa momwe linauzira anthu.
ny.body_more:
Moses Nthakomwa, bwenzi lapamtima la Chilima, anapereka umboni ku komitiyi. Iye anati anthu anali kusaka pamene anthu omwe anali ndi mphamvu ankadziwa kale komwe ngoziyo inali komanso momwe inalili yoipa. "Madzulo a pa 10 June 2024, anthu anali kusakabe pamene zambiri zokhudza malo omwe ngoziyo inachitikira komanso momwe anthu omwe anamwalira zinali zitadziwika kale," adatero kwa opanga malamulo. Izi zikusemphana ndi zomwe boma linanena panthawiyo. Akuluakulu aboma adati adapeza ndikutsimikizira malo omwe ngoziyo inachitikira tsiku lotsatira.

Ndegeyo inagwa ku Chikangawa Forest pamene inkauluka kuchokera ku Lilongwe kupita ku Mzuzu. Nyengo yoipa komanso nthaka yolimba zinapangitsa kuti kufufuzako kukhale kovuta. Kwa maola opitilira 24, dzikolo linadikira pamene magulu a anthu anali kufunafuna ndegeyo. Nthakomwa adati mfundo zofunika zinadziwika kwa anthu ena maola ambiri anthu asanamve za izo. Mamembala a komitiyi adafunsa omwe anali ndi izi, nthawi yomwe adazipeza, komanso chifukwa chake sanauze anthu mwachangu.

Colleen Zamba, Mlembi wakale wa Purezidenti ndi Kabungwe, nayenso adalankhula ndi komitiyi. Iye adati boma lidachita zomwe lingathe poganizira za nyengo ndi malo omwe ngoziyo inachitikira. "Tinachita ntchito yathu monga momwe idayenera kuchitikira," adatero. Adauza opanga malamulo momwe machenjezo adafikira, momwe asilikali adayendera mwachangu, komanso momwe magulu adalowera m'nkhalango. Komitiyi ikufuna kudziwa ngati ndale zidasokoneza ntchito yopulumutsa anthu komanso ngati zina zambiri zikanatheka kuchitika m'maola oyamba.

Mabanja a anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe adamwalira afunsa chifukwa chake zidatenga nthawi yayitali kuti ndegeyo ipezeke. Kafukufuku tsopano ayenera kuyang'ana zomwe zinali zodziwika, ndani ankadziwa, komanso chifukwa chake anthu ena adazisunga chete. Akatswiri akuti momwe komitiyi imayang'anira funsoli ziwonetsa ngati kafukufukuyu ndi wolungama komanso wowona.

en.title: Bullets face Creck in Blantyre today
en.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets will play Creck Sporting Club today at Mpira Stadium in Chiwembe Township, Blantyre. Bullets are third on the table with 16 points and have not lost a match. Creck are third from bottom with five points from eight games. Creck coach Ernest Mtawali said they will fight hard to win.
en.body_more:
Mtawali told the media before the game that every match is a cup final for his team because they are in the relegation zone. He said Creck lost 0-1 to Civil Service United last weekend and that lack of confidence has been their biggest problem. "We have been working on that and I am optimistic that we will play better," he said. "But we know this will be a very tough game because Bullets are a big team."

Bullets coach Wedson Nyirenda said his team is working to score more goals. Nyirenda has just helped Bullets to two wins in a row — they beat Kamuzu Barracks 2-1 and Masters 1-0. Bullets are the only team that has not lost a match this season after eight games. Nyirenda said: "The flow of the game has been very good. The confidence of the players is slowly coming back."

This will be the fifth league meeting between the two teams since Creck got promotion to the top league. In those four games, Bullets have won twice, Creck won once, and one ended in a draw. Creck's only win this season came against Masters when they beat them 3-1 at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. They have lost five matches and drawn twice.

ny.title: Bullets ikumana ndi Creck ku Blantyre lero
ny.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets isewera ndi Creck Sporting Club lero pa Mpira Stadium ku Chiwembe Township, Blantyre. Bullets ili pa nambala 3 pa tebulo ndi mapointi 16 ndipo sinataye masewero aliwonse. Creck ili pa nambala 3 kuchokera pansi ndi mapointi asanu kuchokera pamasewera asanu ndi atatu. Mphunzitsi wa Creck Ernest Mtawali adati adzalimbana mwamphamvu kuti apambane.
ny.body_more:
Mtawali adauza atolankhani masewerawa asanayambe kuti masewera aliwonse ndi otsiriza chikho kwa timu yake chifukwa ali m'dera lotsika. Anati Creck adagonja 0-1 ndi Civil Service United kumapeto kwa sabata yatha ndipo kusowa chidaliro kwakhala vuto lawo lalikulu. "Takhala tikugwira ntchito pa izi ndipo ndili ndi chiyembekezo kuti tidzasewera bwino," adatero. "Koma tikudziwa kuti iyi idzakhala masewera ovuta kwambiri chifukwa Bullets ndi timu yayikulu."

Mphunzitsi wa Bullets Wedson Nyirenda adati timu yake ikugwira ntchito kuti ipeze zigoli zambiri. Nyirenda wangothandiza Bullets kupambana kawiri motsatizana - adagonjetsa Kamuzu Barracks 2-1 ndi Masters 1-0. Bullets ndi timu yokhayo yomwe sinataye masewera nyengo ino pambuyo pa masewera asanu ndi atatu. Nyirenda adati: "Kuyenda kwa masewerawa kwakhala kwabwino kwambiri. Chidaliro cha osewera chikubwerera pang'onopang'ono."

Iyi idzakhala masewera achisanu a ligi pakati pa magulu awiriwa kuyambira pomwe Creck adakwezedwa ku ligi yapamwamba. M'masewera anayi amenewo, Bullets yapambana kawiri, Creck yapambana kamodzi, ndipo imodzi idathera motsatizana. Creck apambana nyengo ino pomwe adagonjetsa Masters 3-1 pa Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe. Ataya masewera asanu ndipo alephera kawiri.

en.title: Court to decide: Stop case against wildlife criminal Yunhua Lin?
en.body: The High Court of Malawi now decides if the case against Chinese wildlife trafficker Yunhua Lin will stop. Director of Public Prosecutions Fostino Maere told the Anti-Corruption Bureau to withdraw the case on May 21. This has made many people angry. Maere was Lin's lawyer before he became DPP.
en.body_more:
The trial had already started. Prosecutors had shown three of 18 witnesses they planned to use. Now Maere wants to stop it. He said the case should end so prison officials' bad acts can be handled by the government instead of the court.

The Department of National Parks and Wildlife said it was never asked about this plan. Director Brighton Kumchedwa said stopping the case is bad for Malawi. "This is a heavy blow to us and to the nation," he told the media. Malawi has earned praise from other countries for fighting wildlife crime. Stopping this case puts that work at risk, he said.

Twelve groups that work to save wildlife gave a joint statement. They said stopping the case will undo years of work. They asked if Maere should make choices about his old client. "Although he stopped being Lin's lawyer when he became DPP, this raises questions about if he can be fair," they said. The groups want full answers about why the case should stop.

ACB Chief Legal and Prosecution Officer Peter Sambani said the court will decide. The judge is busy now, so no ruling has come yet. Many people are watching this. The question is simple: will Malawi hold powerful people to the law, or will this case show that some people are above it?

ny.title: Khoti ligamula: Kuletsa mlandu wotsutsana ndi chigawenga cha nyama zakuthengo Yunhua Lin?
ny.body: Khothi Lalikulu la Malawi tsopano lasankha ngati mlandu wokhudza wogulitsa nyama zakuthengo ku China, Yunhua Lin, utha. Mtsogoleri wa Makhothi a Boma, Fostino Maere, adauza Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu kuti lichotse mlanduwu pa Meyi 21. Izi zakwiyitsa anthu ambiri. Maere anali loya wa Lin asanakhale DPP.
ny.body_more:
Mlanduwu unali utayamba kale. Otsutsa milandu adawonetsa mboni zitatu mwa 18 zomwe adakonza zogwiritsa ntchito. Tsopano Maere akufuna kuimitsa mlanduwu. Anati mlanduwu uyenera kutha kuti zochita zoyipa za akuluakulu a ndende zitha kuchitidwa ndi boma m'malo mwa khothi.

Dipatimenti ya National Parks and Wildlife yati sinafunsidwepo za dongosololi. Mtsogoleri Brighton Kumchedwa adati kuletsa mlanduwu ndi koipa kwa Malawi. "Izi ndi tsoka lalikulu kwa ife komanso kwa dziko lonse," adatero kwa atolankhani. Malawi yayamikiridwa ndi mayiko ena chifukwa cholimbana ndi umbanda wa nyama zakuthengo. Kuletsa mlanduwu kumaika ntchito imeneyo pachiwopsezo, adatero.

Magulu khumi ndi awiri omwe amagwira ntchito yopulumutsa nyama zakuthengo adapereka chikalata chogwirizana. Adati kuletsa mlanduwu kudzathetsa ntchito ya zaka zambiri. Adafunsa ngati Maere ayenera kusankha kasitomala wake wakale. "Ngakhale adasiya kukhala loya wa Lin pamene adakhala DPP, izi zikubweretsa mafunso okhudza ngati angakhale wolungama," adatero. Maguluwa akufuna mayankho athunthu okhudza chifukwa chake mlanduwu uyenera kutha.

Mkulu wa Zamalamulo ndi Otsutsa a ACB Peter Sambani adati khothi lidzasankha. Woweruza ali wotanganidwa tsopano, kotero palibe chigamulo chomwe chaperekedwa pakadali pano. Anthu ambiri akuyang'ana izi. Funso ndi losavuta: kodi dziko la Malawi lidzakakamiza anthu amphamvu kuti amvere lamulo, kapena nkhaniyi ikusonyeza kuti anthu ena ali pamwamba pa lamulo?

en.title: African Union calls for new debt rules to help Malawi
en.body: The African Union has made a new plan to change how Africa pays debts. The plan calls for faster debt relief and cheaper loans for African countries. Malawi is one of 25 countries in Africa with very high debt. The African Union says the old debt system does not work for Africa.
en.body_more:
