Mahmoud Ali Youssouf is the chairperson of the African Union. He said without quick action, the debt crisis will stop Africa from reaching its goals. He asked banks, lenders and other countries to work with Africa to find fair answers.

Malawi's debt is very large. In December 2025, Malawi owed K23.9 trillion. This is 90.9 percent of all the money Malawi makes in one year. Of this debt, K16 trillion is money Malawi borrowed at home. In the coming year, Malawi will pay K2.7 trillion just to pay interest on old debts. This is 22.9 percent more than the year before. Joseph Mwanamvekha is Malawi's Minister of Finance. He said the debt is holding back Malawi's money and plans.

The African Union says African countries should borrow less from outside. Instead, they should collect more tax at home, stop illegal money flows and manage debt better. The Union also wants Africa to use new ways to get money and to bring in more private investment. Data shows that between 2012 and 2024, African debt rose from 28.8 percent to 65.1 percent. Debt payments rose 132 percent. Now 32 African countries pay more for debt than for health and school.

Malawi started a plan to fix its debt in mid-2022, but the plan stopped. The World Bank says talks with banks are slow. China and India have helped, but business lenders have not moved fast. The World Bank says Malawi is in debt distress. This means Malawi may not pay back what it owes.

ny.title: Bungwe la African Union likupempha malamulo atsopano okhudza ngongole kuti athandize Malawi
ny.body: Bungwe la African Union lapanga dongosolo latsopano losintha momwe Africa imalipira ngongole. Dongosololi likufuna kuti mayiko aku Africa achepetse ngongole mwachangu komanso kuti mayiko aku Africa alandire ngongole zotsika mtengo. Malawi ndi limodzi mwa mayiko 25 ku Africa omwe ali ndi ngongole zambiri. Bungwe la African Union likuti dongosolo lakale la ngongole silikugwira ntchito ku Africa.
ny.body_more:
Mahmoud Ali Youssouf ndi wapampando wa bungwe la African Union. Iye anati popanda kuchitapo kanthu mwachangu, vuto la ngongole lidzalepheretsa Africa kukwaniritsa zolinga zake. Iye anapempha mabanki, obwereketsa ndalama ndi mayiko ena kuti agwire ntchito ndi Africa kuti apeze mayankho oyenera.

Ngongole ya Malawi ndi yayikulu kwambiri. Mu Disembala 2025, Malawi inali ndi ngongole ya K23.9 trillion. Izi ndi 90.9 peresenti ya ndalama zonse zomwe Malawi imapeza m'chaka chimodzi. Pa ngongole iyi, K16 trillion ndi ndalama zomwe Malawi inabwereka kunyumba. Chaka chikubwerachi, Malawi idzalipira K2.7 trillion kuti ingolipira chiwongola dzanja pa ngongole zakale. Izi ndi 22.9 peresenti kuposa chaka cham'mbuyomo. Joseph Mwanamvekha ndi Nduna ya Zachuma ku Malawi. Iye anati ngongoleyi ikulepheretsa ndalama ndi mapulani a Malawi.

Bungwe la African Union likunena kuti mayiko aku Africa ayenera kubwereka ndalama zochepa kuchokera kunja. M'malo mwake, ayenera kusonkhanitsa misonkho yambiri kunyumba, kuletsa kuyenda kwa ndalama mosaloledwa ndikusamalira ngongole bwino. Bungweli likufunanso kuti Africa igwiritse ntchito njira zatsopano zopezera ndalama ndikubweretsa ndalama zambiri zachinsinsi. Deta ikusonyeza kuti pakati pa 2012 ndi 2024, ngongole za ku Africa zinakwera kuchoka pa 28.8 peresenti kufika pa 65.1 peresenti. Malipiro a ngongole anakwera ndi 132 peresenti. Tsopano mayiko 32 aku Africa amalipira ndalama zambiri pa ngongole kuposa zaumoyo ndi sukulu.

Malawi inayamba dongosolo lokonza ngongole zake pakati pa 2022, koma dongosololi linayima. Banki Yapadziko Lonse ikunena kuti zokambirana ndi mabanki zikuchedwa. China ndi India zathandiza, koma obwereketsa mabizinesi sanachitepo kanthu mwachangu. Banki Yapadziko Lonse ikunena kuti Malawi ili muvuto la ngongole. Izi zikutanthauza kuti Malawi sangabweze zomwe ili nazo.

en.title: Press Corporation posts K261 billion profit, plans expansion
en.body: Press Corporation, Malawi's biggest company, made K261 billion profit in 2025. The group plans to grow into health, transport, education, energy, and agriculture. Chief Executive Ronald Mangani told people in Blantyre about the plans. Shareholders said the profit was very good.
en.body_more:
The profit for 2025 was more than double the K126 billion from last year. Mangani said the company wants to invest in sectors that help all of Malawi grow. He said Press is building a 50-megawatt solar power project. This project will give Malawi more electricity, which many factories and small shops need.

Press also plans to make fertilizer and ethanol in farming. Mangani said this will help farmers get what they need and reduce what Malawi must buy from other countries. The company will also move into transport, tourism, and health work. These will create new jobs and help people move around the country more easily. Press will also trade goods with help from banks in Africa. This could help Malawi use less foreign money for imports.

Frank Harawa, from the Minority Shareholders of Listed Companies group, said the profit shows good work by Press leaders. He said the expansion into these sectors is good for all of Malawi. Harawa said better work in energy, farming, and roads could help the country grow more in years to come. Mangani said foreign money shortage is still a big problem, but Press wants to help by investing in many different areas and making trade work better.

ny.title: Press Corporation yapeza phindu la K261 biliyoni, ikukonzekera kukulitsa
ny.body: Kampani ya Press Corporation, yomwe ndi kampani yayikulu kwambiri ku Malawi, inapeza phindu la K261 biliyoni mu 2025. Gululi likukonzekera kukula kukhala laumoyo, mayendedwe, maphunziro, mphamvu, ndi ulimi. Mtsogoleri wamkulu Ronald Mangani anauza anthu ku Blantyre za mapulaniwo. Ogawana nawo adati phindu lomwe adapeza linali labwino kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
Phindu la 2025 linali loposa kawiri K126 biliyoni poyerekeza ndi chaka chatha. Mangani anati kampaniyo ikufuna kuyika ndalama m'magawo omwe angathandize Malawi yonse kukula. Anati Press ikupanga pulojekiti ya mphamvu ya dzuwa ya 50-megawatt. Ntchitoyi ipatsa Malawi magetsi ambiri, omwe mafakitale ambiri ndi masitolo ang'onoang'ono amafunikira.

Press ikukonzekeranso kupanga feteleza ndi ethanol muulimi. Mangani anati izi zithandiza alimi kupeza zomwe akufunikira ndikuchepetsa zomwe Malawi iyenera kugula kuchokera kumayiko ena. Kampaniyo idzalowanso mu ntchito zoyendera, zokopa alendo, ndi zaumoyo. Izi zipanga ntchito zatsopano ndikuthandiza anthu kuyenda mosavuta mdzikolo. Press idzagulitsanso katundu ndi thandizo kuchokera ku mabanki aku Africa. Izi zingathandize Malawi kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama zochepa zakunja pogula zinthu kunja.

Frank Harawa, wochokera ku gulu la Minority Shareholders of Listed Companies, anati phinduli likuwonetsa ntchito yabwino ya atsogoleri a Press. Anati kukula m'magawo awa ndi kwabwino kwa Malawi yonse. Harawa anati ntchito yabwino mu mphamvu, ulimi, ndi misewu ingathandize dzikolo kukula kwambiri m'zaka zikubwerazi. Mangani anati kusowa kwa ndalama zakunja kudakali vuto lalikulu, koma Press ikufuna kuthandiza poika ndalama m'magawo osiyanasiyana ndikupangitsa kuti malonda azigwira ntchito bwino.

en.title: Civil group warns against intimidating plane crash inquiry
en.body: The Centre for Democracy and Economic Development Initiatives (CDEDI) has warned that people appearing before a parliamentary committee investigating a plane crash are trying to intimidate the inquiry. CDEDI executive director Sylvester Namiwa spoke at a media briefing in Lilongwe on Friday. He said witnesses must answer questions honestly and not threaten those asking them.
en.body_more:
The plane crashed on 10 June 2024 in Chikangawa. The crash killed former Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others. President Peter Mutharika ordered a new parliamentary inquiry after concerns that the first investigation was not thorough enough.

Namiwa said the committee has the power to call any person to give evidence. He said anyone who tries to threaten or stop the committee is working against the Malawian people. "Anyone that tries to threaten the committee is against the will of Malawians who want to know the truth about the plane crash," he said.

Namiwa warned the Malawi Defence Force (MDF) not to use national security as an excuse to avoid the inquiry. He said the MDF is funded by taxpayers and must answer questions about what happened. He stated that national security should never be used to block openness and honesty. "No-one should be allowed to get away with impunity in the pretext of safeguarding State security," he said. Namiwa urged the committee to follow all the facts without fear. He said Malawians want truth and answers about the crash, not excuses. He added that failing to complete the inquiry in an open way would waste public money.

ny.title: Gulu la anthu wamba lachenjeza za kuopseza kafukufuku wa ngozi ya ndege
ny.body: Bungwe la Centre for Democracy and Economic Development Initiatives (CDEDI) lachenjeza kuti anthu omwe akuwonekera pamaso pa komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo yofufuza za ngozi ya ndege akuyesera kuopseza kafukufukuyu. Mtsogoleri wamkulu wa CDEDI Sylvester Namiwa adalankhula pamsonkhano wa atolankhani ku Lilongwe Lachisanu. Iye adati mboni ziyenera kuyankha mafunso moona mtima osati kuopseza omwe akuwafunsa.
ny.body_more:
Ndegeyo inagwa pa 10 June 2024 ku Chikangawa. Ngoziyo inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu. Purezidenti Peter Mutharika analamula kuti pakhale kafukufuku watsopano wa nyumba yamalamulo pambuyo pa nkhawa kuti kafukufuku woyamba sunakwanitse.

Namiwa anati komitiyi ili ndi mphamvu zoitana munthu aliyense kuti apereke umboni. Anati aliyense amene akuyesera kuopseza kapena kuletsa komitiyi akuchita zinthu motsutsana ndi anthu aku Malawi. "Aliyense amene akuyesera kuopseza komitiyi akuchita zinthu zosemphana ndi chifuniro cha anthu aku Malawi omwe akufuna kudziwa zoona zokhudza ngozi ya ndegeyo," adatero.

Namiwa anachenjeza a Malawi Defence Force (MDF) kuti asagwiritse ntchito chitetezo cha dziko ngati chifukwa chopewera kafukufukuyu. Anati MDF imathandizidwa ndi okhometsa msonkho ndipo ayenera kuyankha mafunso okhudza zomwe zinachitika. Anati chitetezo cha dziko sichiyenera kugwiritsidwa ntchito kuletsa kutseguka ndi kuwona mtima. "Palibe amene ayenera kuloledwa kupulumuka popanda chilango podzinenera kuti ateteza chitetezo cha Boma," adatero. Namiwa analimbikitsa komitiyi kuti itsatire mfundo zonse popanda mantha. Anati anthu aku Malawi akufuna chowonadi ndi mayankho okhudza ngoziyi, osati zifukwa. Iye anawonjezera kuti kulephera kumaliza kafukufukuyu poyera kungawononge ndalama za boma.

en.title: NBS Bank stops SME banking, starts new business banking plan
en.body: NBS Bank has stopped its small business banking and started a new plan called Business Banking. The bank launched the plan on June 30, 2026, at the Bingu International Convention Centre in Lilongwe. The new plan is not just a name change. It changes how the bank works with all kinds of businesses in Malawi.
en.body_more:
The bank says businesses should not be called small or medium. Instead, the bank wants to see what each business can become in the future. CEO Temwani Simwaka said the old way of banking was wrong. Banks made businesses fit into their systems. Now NBS Bank will fit itself to the needs of businesses.

Alfred Nhlema, Chief Wholesale Banking Officer, said the bank asked itself one hard question: What if banking changed as fast as business does? He said the old SME name was limiting. It looked at business size, not at what businesses could do. "When we look at you, we do not see a small business," Nhlema said. "We see someone building the future of Malawi."

Under the new plan, NBS Bank will offer different banking for different types of business. The bank will work with agriculture, mining, manufacturing, trade, transport, health care, and education. Each area will have its own banking managers who know about that type of work. A farmer does not work like a builder. A hospital does not work like a shop. So each will get banking made just for them.

The bank is also making banking faster and easier. Business Banking Managers will make decisions in five days. Businesses can open accounts online without going to a bank office. The bank will also look at how much money a business makes, not just what it owns. NBS Bank is also helping women business owners through its Bold & Bankable plan. The bank says success means more jobs and stronger businesses, not just more loans given out. Simwaka said, "Your growth has a partner. Your future has a bank that believes in it."

ny.title: Banki ya NBS yasiya mabanki ang'onoang'ono komanso apakatikati, yayambitsa dongosolo latsopano la mabanki abizinesi
ny.body: Banki ya NBS yasiya ntchito yake yogulitsa mabanki ang'onoang'ono ndipo yayamba dongosolo latsopano lotchedwa Business Banking. Bankiyi idayambitsa dongosololi pa June 30, 2026, ku Bingu International Convention Centre ku Lilongwe. Dongosolo latsopanoli si kungosintha dzina chabe. Limasintha momwe bankiyi imagwirira ntchito ndi mabizinesi amitundu yonse ku Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Bankiyo ikunena kuti mabizinesi sayenera kutchedwa ang'onoang'ono kapena apakatikati. M'malo mwake, bankiyo ikufuna kuona zomwe bizinesi iliyonse ingakhale mtsogolo. CEO Temwani Simwaka adati njira yakale yogwiritsira ntchito mabanki inali yolakwika. Mabanki adapangitsa mabizinesi kuti agwirizane ndi machitidwe awo. Tsopano NBS Bank idzikwaniritsa yokha malinga ndi zosowa za mabizinesi.

Alfred Nhlema, Chief Wholesale Banking Officer, adati bankiyo idadzifunsa funso limodzi lovuta: Nanga bwanji ngati mabanki asintha mwachangu monga momwe mabizinesi amasinthira? Iye adati dzina lakale la SME linali loletsa. Limayang'ana kukula kwa bizinesi, osati zomwe mabizinesi angachite. "Tikamakuyang'anani, sitikuwona bizinesi yaying'ono," adatero Nhlema. "Tikuwona wina akumanga tsogolo la Malawi."

Motsatira dongosolo latsopanoli, NBS Bank ipereka mabanki osiyanasiyana pamitundu yosiyanasiyana ya mabizinesi. Bankiyo idzagwira ntchito ndi ulimi, migodi, kupanga, malonda, mayendedwe, chisamaliro chaumoyo, ndi maphunziro. Dera lililonse lidzakhala ndi oyang'anira mabanki ake omwe amadziwa za mtundu wa ntchitoyo. Mlimi sagwira ntchito ngati womanga. Chipatala sichigwira ntchito ngati shopu. Chifukwa chake banki iliyonse idzapangidwira iwo okha.

Bankiyi ikupanganso kuti banki ikhale yofulumira komanso yosavuta. Oyang'anira Mabanki Amalonda adzapanga zisankho m'masiku asanu. Mabizinesi amatha kutsegula maakaunti pa intaneti popanda kupita ku ofesi ya banki. Bankiyi idzawonanso kuchuluka kwa ndalama zomwe bizinesi imapeza, osati zomwe ili nazo zokha. Banki ya NBS ikuthandizanso azimayi omwe ali ndi mabizinesi kudzera mu dongosolo lake la Bold & Bankable. Bankiyi ikunena kuti kupambana kumatanthauza ntchito zambiri komanso mabizinesi olimba, osati kungopereka ngongole zambiri. Simwaka adati, "Kukula kwanu kuli ndi mnzanu. Tsogolo lanu lili ndi banki yomwe imakhulupirira."

en.title: MDF Officer Denies Murder Claims in Plane Crash
en.body: Brigadier General Dan Kuwali of the Malawi Defence Force told Parliament's investigating committee on Thursday that he did not plan or help arrange Vice President Saulos Chilima's fatal flight. The military plane crashed on June 10 2024 near Chikangawa, killing Chilima and eight others. Kuwali said his only role was to help arrange a charter for the family of dead lawyer Raphael Kasambara.
en.body_more:
Kuwali told the Ad-hoc Committee that when the Vice President's office asked to use the same aircraft, he asked the Air Force Commander about it. The commander said he had not been told about the request. Kuwali said he and the commander both agreed the flight plan was not possible because the plane was already booked for the Kasambara family funeral.

Kuwali also spoke about claims on social media that linked some military officers to the deaths. He said these claims damage people's reputations without any proof. Committee chairperson Walter Nyamilandu said Parliament would protect witnesses and would not listen to claims without evidence.

Two other officers also gave evidence. Major General Mandiza Kalisha, the former chief of staff, said he took office on May 13 2024, just before the crash, and had no role in aircraft operations. Davie Kalilombe, who worked in the Vice President's office, said he only helped with travel plans and airport arrangements. He told the committee he was one of the last people to see Chilima before he boarded the plane at Kamuzu International Airport.

Assistant Superintendent of Police Limbikani Banda, who led the Vice President's security team in Mzuzu, described losing contact with the aircraft. He said airport control lost radio contact with the plane. A German investigation completed in October 2025 found the navigation system stopped recording at 10.16am, just before the crash. In December 2024, a commission led by High Court Judge Jabbar Alide ruled out foul play and said bad weather and human factors caused the accident.

ny.title: Mkulu wa MDF Akana Zonena za Kupha Munthu Pa Ngozi ya Ndege
ny.body: Brigadier General Dan Kuwali wa Malawi Defense Force adauza komiti yofufuza ya Nyumba Yamalamulo Lachinayi kuti sanakonzekere kapena kuthandiza kukonza ulendo wopita kudziko la Vice President Saulos Chilima. Ndege yankhondo idagwa pa June 10 2024 pafupi ndi Chikangawa, zomwe zidapha Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu. Kuwali adati ntchito yake yokha ndi kuthandiza kukonza chilolezo cha banja la loya wakufa Raphael Kasambara.
ny.body_more:
Kuwali anauza Komiti Yoyang'anira Zachitetezo kuti ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti itapempha kuti agwiritse ntchito ndege yomweyo, anafunsa Mtsogoleri wa Air Force za nkhaniyi. Mtsogoleriyo anati sanauzidwe za pempholi. Kuwali anati iye ndi mkuluyo onse anagwirizana kuti dongosolo la ndege silingatheke chifukwa ndegeyo inali italembetsedwa kale pa maliro a banja la Kasambara.

Kuwali analankhulanso za zomwe zanenedwa pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti zomwe zimagwirizanitsa akuluakulu ena ankhondo ndi imfa. Anati izi zikuwononga mbiri ya anthu popanda umboni uliwonse. Wapampando wa komiti Walter Nyamilandu anati Nyumba Yamalamulo idzateteza mboni ndipo sidzamvetsera zomwe zanenedwa popanda umboni.

Akuluakulu ena awiri nawonso anapereka umboni. Major General Mandiza Kalisha, yemwe kale anali mkulu wa asilikali, anati anayamba ntchito pa Meyi 13 2024, ngozi isanachitike, ndipo sanali ndi gawo lililonse pa ntchito za ndege. Davie Kalilombe, yemwe ankagwira ntchito mu ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti, anati anathandiza kokha ndi mapulani oyendera ndi kukonzekera bwalo la ndege. Anauza komitiyo kuti anali m'modzi mwa anthu omaliza kuona Chilima asanakwere ndegeyo pa Kamuzu International Airport.

Wachiwiri kwa Superintendent wa Apolisi Limbikani Banda, yemwe anatsogolera gulu lachitetezo la Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti ku Mzuzu, anafotokoza kuti ndegeyo sinalumikizane ndi ndegeyo. Iye anati oyang'anira bwalo la ndege anataya kulumikizana kwa wailesi ndi ndegeyo. Kafukufuku wa ku Germany womwe unamalizidwa mu Okutobala 2025 unapeza kuti makina oyendetsera ndege anasiya kujambula nthawi ya 10:16am, ngozi isanachitike. Mu Disembala 2024, bungwe lotsogozedwa ndi Woweruza wa Khothi Lalikulu Jabbar Alide linakana kuti panalibe vuto lililonse ndipo linati nyengo yoipa ndi zinthu zomwe anthu ndi omwe adayambitsa ngoziyi.

en.title: DPP, MCP, and others share by-election wins
en.body: Malawi held by-elections on June 30. The Malawi Electoral Commission ran elections in three constituencies and two wards. The Malawi Congress Party won Lilongwe-Bwaila with Precious Kabambe getting 4,707 votes. The Democratic Progressive Party got 2,725 votes. Results show different parties won different seats.
en.body_more:
In Balaka, the DPP won Balaka-Mulunguzi constituency. James Makhumula got 7,311 votes. Independent candidate Ireen Mambala came second with 6,462 votes. The DPP won by 849 votes in this race.

In Machinga-Mikoko constituency, independent candidate Ishmael Louis Japan won with 4,935 votes. Ibrahim Ojess from the United Democratic Progressive Party came close behind with 4,853 votes. Japan won by just 81 votes in one of the closest races.

For the local government elections, Robert Kondowe from the People's Party won Uliwa Ward in Karonga with 2,800 votes. In Machinga, Lali Atwabi from the DPP won Mikoko Ward with 2,351 votes. The DPP won this seat.

The elections took place because of empty seats. Some seats became empty after the deaths of people who held them. Other seats became empty because courts said the September 16, 2025 election results were wrong. The MCP victory in Lilongwe-Bwaila was seen as important for the opposition party in the capital city.

ny.title: DPP, MCP, ndi ena apambana pa zisankho zapang'onopang'ono
ny.body: Malawi idachita zisankho zapang'ono pa June 30. Malawi Electoral Commission idachita zisankho m'maboma atatu ndi ma wadi awiri. Chipani cha Malawi Congress Party chidapambana Lilongwe-Bwaila ndipo Precious Kabambe adapeza mavoti 4,707. Chipani cha Democratic Progressive Party chidapeza mavoti 2,725. Zotsatira zikusonyeza kuti zipani zosiyanasiyana zidapambana mipando yosiyana.
ny.body_more:
Ku Balaka, chipani cha DPP chinapambana Balaka-Mulunguzi. James Makhumula adapeza mavoti 7,311. Woyimira payokha Ireen Mambala adakhala wachiwiri ndi mavoti 6,462. DPP idapambana ndi mavoti 849 pa mpikisano uwu.

Mu chipani cha Machinga-Mikoko, woyimira payokha Ishmael Louis Japan adapambana ndi mavoti 4,935. Ibrahim Ojess wa United Democratic Progressive Party adapambana ndi mavoti 4,853. Japan idapambana ndi mavoti 81 okha pa mpikisano umodzi wapafupi.

Pa zisankho za boma, Robert Kondowe wa People's Party adapambana Uliwa Ward ku Karonga ndi mavoti 2,800. Ku Machinga, Lali Atwabi wa DPP adapambana Mikoko Ward ndi mavoti 2,351. DPP idapambana mpando uwu.

Zisankho zidachitika chifukwa cha mipando yopanda anthu. Mipando ina idasowa anthu atamwalira. Mipando ina idasowa chifukwa makhothi adati zotsatira za chisankho cha pa Seputembala 16, 2025 zinali zolakwika. Kupambana kwa chipani cha MCP ku Lilongwe-Bwaila kunaonedwa ngati kofunika kwambiri kwa chipani chotsutsa mumzinda waukulu.

en.title: Second woman dies as Malawians return from South Africa
en.body: A second Malawian woman has died while returning home from South Africa under the government's evacuation programme. She died on Friday morning after crossing into Malawi at Mwanza Border. The first death happened in Zimbabwe while a woman was travelling back. Wilson Moleni, who leads the disaster office, said a clinic at Mwanza will check why she died.
en.body_more:
Moleni told reporters in Lilongwe that both people who died were women. The second death came after the woman crossed Mwanza Border on Friday morning. The government is now working with the Mwanza District Health Office to find out what caused her death and to learn more about what happened.

So far, the government has brought back more than 21,000 Malawians from South Africa. This is more than the first target of 15,000 people. The evacuation started because of attacks against foreigners in South Africa. Malawi and South Africa both worked together to move the people. Other groups also helped by giving buses and food.

Malawi's government gave K5 billion for the work. Other helpers gave K2 billion. The total cost is about K7 billion. Moleni said the evacuation is still going on and has been successful. But he also said there was one violent incident at Mwanza. Some people who came back became angry because money was late. Police came and stopped the trouble. The government added more police to keep the place safe.

ny.title: Mkazi wachiwiri wamwalira pamene anthu aku Malawi akubwera kuchokera ku South Africa
ny.body: Mayi wina wa ku Malawi wamwalira akubwerera kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa motsatira ndondomeko ya boma yochotsa anthu m'nyumba zawo. Iye wamwalira Lachisanu m'mawa atadutsa ku Malawi pa Mwanza Border. Imfa yoyamba inachitika ku Zimbabwe pamene mayi wina anali kubwerera. Wilson Moleni, yemwe akutsogolera ofesi yoona za ngozi, anati chipatala ku Mwanza chidzafufuza chifukwa chake wamwalira.
ny.body_more:
Moleni anauza atolankhani ku Lilongwe kuti anthu onse awiri omwe anamwalira anali akazi. Imfa yachiwiri idachitika mayiyo atadutsa malire a Mwanza Lachisanu m'mawa. Boma tsopano likugwira ntchito ndi Ofesi ya Zaumoyo ya Chigawo cha Mwanza kuti adziwe chomwe chinachititsa imfa yake ndikuphunzira zambiri za zomwe zinachitika.

Pakadali pano, boma labweretsa anthu aku Malawi oposa 21,000 ochokera ku South Africa. Izi ndi zoposa cholinga choyamba cha anthu 15,000. Kuchotsedwa kwa anthu kunayamba chifukwa cha ziwawa zomwe zinachitikira alendo ku South Africa. Malawi ndi South Africa onse adagwira ntchito limodzi kuti asamutse anthu. Magulu ena adathandizanso popereka mabasi ndi chakudya.

Boma la Malawi lidapereka K5 biliyoni pa ntchitoyi. Othandizira ena adapereka K2 biliyoni. Ndalama zonse ndi pafupifupi K7 biliyoni. Moleni adati kuchotsedwa kwa anthu kukupitirirabe ndipo kwakhala kopambana. Koma adatinso panali chochitika chimodzi chachiwawa ku Mwanza. Anthu ena omwe adabwerera adakwiya chifukwa ndalama zidachedwa. Apolisi adabwera ndikuletsa vutoli. Boma lidawonjezera apolisi ambiri kuti asunge malo otetezeka.

en.title: Army officer told Chilima's friend of death on June 10
en.body: A close friend of late Vice President Saulos Chilima told parliament that a retired Major General called him on June 10, 2024, and told him Chilima had died. Moses Nthakomwa said Major General John Msonthi called at 8:19pm that night. The call came hours before the government made any official news. Nthakomwa said Msonthi gave details of the crash that had not yet been made public.
en.body_more:
Nthakomwa told the Parliamentary Ad-Hoc Committee on Friday, July 3, 2026, that Msonthi described how the aircraft's wing hit a tree and the plane slid about 80 metres uphill. Nthakomwa said he was confused because the wreckage had not been found or confirmed at that time. He said Msonthi also told him how the bodies were found, even though no official government news had been shared.

Nthakomwa did not tell anyone else about the call, not even Chilima's family. He was afraid the news would upset them before the government made an official statement. On June 11, 2024, President Lazarus Chakwera told the nation that Chilima and others on the plane had died.

Nthakomwa said he had sent Chilima a WhatsApp message on June 10 while in South Africa, but Chilima did not reply. When he landed at Chileka Airport, he called a Malawi Defence Force captain at around 2:36pm and was told the Vice President's plane was missing. He then called Chilima's wife, Mary Chilima, and later went to her house in Lilongwe. Former Minister of Health Khumbize Chiponda and the then Inspector General of Police Marlyn Yolamu came to brief the family later that day.

When asked why he never told the public about the early call, Nthakomwa said he did not know where to report it. He confirmed he still has the same phone from June 2024, but the call records from that time are no longer on the phone.

ny.title: Mkulu wa asilikali anauza mnzake wa Chilima za imfa pa June 10
ny.body: Bwenzi lapamtima la wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima adauza nyumba yamalamulo kuti Major General wopuma pantchito adamuyimbira foni pa June 10, 2024, ndipo adamuuza kuti Chilima wamwalira. Moses Nthakomwa adati Major General John Msonthi adayimba foni nthawi ya 8:19pm usiku womwewo. Kuyimbako kudabwera maola angapo boma lisananene chilichonse chovomerezeka. Nthakomwa adati Msonthi adapereka tsatanetsatane wa ngoziyi yomwe sinadziwikebe.
ny.body_more:
Nthakomwa adauza Komiti Yoyang'anira Nyumba Yamalamulo Lachisanu, pa Julayi 3, 2026, kuti Msonthi adafotokoza momwe phiko la ndegeyo linagunda mtengo ndipo ndegeyo idatsika pafupifupi mamita 80 kukwera phiri. Nthakomwa adati adasokonezeka chifukwa zotsalira sizinapezeke kapena kutsimikizika panthawiyo. Adati Msonthi adamuwuzanso momwe matupiwo adapezekera, ngakhale kuti palibe nkhani yaboma yomwe idagawidwa.

Nthakomwa sanauze wina aliyense za kuyitanako, ngakhale banja la Chilima. Ankaopa kuti nkhaniyi iwakwiyitsa boma lisanapereke chiganizo chovomerezeka. Pa June 11, 2024, Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera adauza dziko lonse kuti Chilima ndi ena omwe anali m'ndegemo amwalira.

Nthakomwa adati adatumiza uthenga wa WhatsApp kwa Chilima pa June 10 ali ku South Africa, koma Chilima sanayankhe. Atafika pa Chileka Airport, adayimbira foni kapitawo wa Malawi Defence Force pafupifupi 2:36pm ndipo adauzidwa kuti ndege ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti idasowa. Kenako anaimbira foni mkazi wa Chilima, Mary Chilima, kenako anapita kunyumba kwake ku Lilongwe. Nduna yakale ya zaumoyo Khumbize Chiponda komanso yemwe anali Inspector General wa apolisi panthawiyo Marlyn Yolamu anabwera kudzauza banjali tsiku lomwelo.

Atafunsidwa chifukwa chake sanauze anthu za kuyimba foni koyambirira, Nthakomwa anati sakudziwa komwe anganene. Anatsimikiza kuti akadali ndi foni yomweyi kuyambira mu June 2024, koma zolemba za kuyimba foni kuyambira nthawi imeneyo sizikupezekanso pafoni.

en.title: Babatunde signs for Mozambique club, Bullets miss out
en.body: Striker Babatunde Adepoju has signed a short-term contract with Costa do Sol of Mozambique. The Malawi national team player was free to leave after his contract with South African club Venda Academy ended. Bullets had hoped to bring him back, but Adepoju chose the Mozambican club instead.
en.body_more:
Adepoju had two offers: a six-month deal and a one-year contract. He picked the shorter option. He was in Maputo to complete the move. Bullets chief executive Albert Chigoga said the club was still thinking about re-signing him. "There was no deal between us. He recently came back to Malawi and we hear that he will go to Mozambique," Chigoga said.

Bullets coach Wedson Nyirenda said he was sad about the move. Nyirenda told reporters that Adepoju was one of the players Bullets wanted to sign to make their attack stronger for the 2026/27 season. The coach said Bullets still need a good goal scorer. He believed Adepoju's old success at Bullets and his knowledge of the team would have helped them score more goals.

Adepoju first joined Bullets in 2021 as a free agent from Mighty Wanderers. He left for Venda Academy in 2023. He came back to Bullets in 2024 after he did not play well in South Africa, where he scored three goals in 18 games. In the 2025 season, he scored 19 goals in 35 matches for all Bullets competitions. He was joint top scorer in the TNM Super League with 15 goals. He has scored 97 goals in 122 games for Bullets.

Football analyst Mike Ndala said Bullets must find a strong striker fast. Ndala said Bullets make many chances but do not score enough. Sports analyst Ibrahim Phiri said Bullets should not just focus on Adepoju. He said the club needs new strikers to make the attack better.

ny.title: Babatunde wasayina ku timu ya ku Mozambique, koma Bullets yaphonya mwayi
ny.body: Wosewera Babatunde Adepoju wasayina mgwirizano wa kanthawi kochepa ndi Costa do Sol ya ku Mozambique. Wosewera wa timu ya dziko la Malawi anali ndi ufulu wochoka mgwirizano wake ndi timu ya ku South Africa ya Venda Academy utatha. Bullets inkayembekezera kumubweza, koma Adepoju anasankha timu ya ku Mozambique m'malo mwake.
ny.body_more:
Adepoju anali ndi ma offer awiri: mgwirizano wa miyezi isanu ndi umodzi ndi mgwirizano wa chaka chimodzi. Anasankha njira yachidule. Anali ku Maputo kuti amalize kusamukaku. Mkulu wa Bullets Albert Chigoga anati kalabuyo ikuganizirabe zomusayinanso. "Panalibe mgwirizano pakati pathu. Posachedwapa wabwerera ku Malawi ndipo tamva kuti apita ku Mozambique," adatero Chigoga.

Mphunzitsi wa Bullets Wedson Nyirenda anati anali wachisoni ndi kusamukaku. Nyirenda adauza atolankhani kuti Adepoju anali m'modzi mwa osewera omwe Bullets imafuna kusaina kuti alimbikitse kuukira kwawo kwa nyengo ya 2026/27. Mphunzitsiyo adati Bullets ikufunikabe wogoletsa zigoli wabwino. Akukhulupirira kuti kupambana kwa Adepoju wakale ku Bullets komanso kudziwa kwake timuyi kukanawathandiza kupeza zigoli zambiri.

Adepoju adayamba kujowina Bullets mu 2021 ngati free agent kuchokera ku Mighty Wanderers. Anachoka ku Venda Academy mu 2023. Anabwerera ku Bullets mu 2024 atalephera kusewera bwino ku South Africa, komwe adagoletsa zigoli zitatu m'masewera 18. Mu nyengo ya 2025, adagoletsa zigoli 19 m'masewera 35 pamasewera onse a Bullets. Anali wogoletsa zigoli wamkulu mu TNM Super League ndi zigoli 15. Wagoletsa zigoli 97 m'masewera 122 a Bullets.

Katswiri wa mpira Mike Ndala anati Bullets iyenera kupeza wogoletsa wamphamvu mwachangu. Ndala anati Bullets imapanga mipata yambiri koma sagoletsa mokwanira. Katswiri wa zamasewera Ibrahim Phiri anati Bullets sayenera kungoyang'ana pa Adepoju. Anati kalabuyo ikufunika ogoletsa atsopano kuti akonze bwino kuukira.

en.title: Corruption and Weak Governance Scare Away Investors from Malawi
en.body: Investors in Malawi now put good governance at the top of their list before they give money to businesses. The Malawi Investment and Trade Centre (Mitc) says investors want to see clear rules, honest work, and steady laws. A World Bank report says corruption is the biggest problem for companies in Malawi—worse than power cuts, bad roads, or tax troubles.
en.body_more:
Mitc spokesperson Ndondwa Msaka said governance is now as important as the state of the economy and what each business sector offers. "Investors always ask for transparency, honesty and rules that do not change," she said. "They want to know the government will keep its word."

Willy Kambwandira, who leads the Centre for Social Accountability and Transparency, said corruption has become part of the cost of doing business here. Investors fear not just bribes, but also surprise changes in rules, hidden buying processes, unfair use of laws, and contracts that courts will not enforce. James Chiutsi, from the Chamber for Small and Medium Businesses Association, said small firms hurt most. "For small businesses, corruption is the difference between paying workers and having to close the shop," he said.

The government is trying to fix this. The Mitc One-Stop Service Centre makes approvals faster. Digital systems cut delays. Msaka said investors see these changes as real steps to cut corruption. She said the country must make the Whistleblower Protection law work better—so people can tell the government about corruption without fear. Integrity Committees in each ministry and office must also be stronger. "These changes will cut corruption and make rules more fair," Msaka said. "That will bring more money from investors to Malawi."

ny.title: Ziphuphu ndi Ulamuliro Wofooka Zikuopseza Ogulitsa Ndalama Ochokera ku Malawi
ny.body: Amalonda ku Malawi tsopano akuika ulamuliro wabwino patsogolo pa mndandanda wawo asanapereke ndalama ku mabizinesi. Malo Ogulitsira ndi Kugulitsa ku Malawi (Mitc) akuti amalonda akufuna kuwona malamulo omveka bwino, ntchito yowona mtima, ndi malamulo okhazikika. Lipoti la World Bank likuti ziphuphu ndiye vuto lalikulu la makampani ku Malawi—loipa kuposa kudula magetsi, misewu yoipa, kapena mavuto amisonkho.
ny.body_more:
Mneneri wa Mitc, Ndondwa Msaka, anati ulamuliro tsopano ndi wofunika monga momwe chuma chilili komanso zomwe bizinesi iliyonse imapereka. "Ogulitsa nthawi zonse amapempha kuti pakhale kuwonekera bwino, kuona mtima ndi malamulo osasintha," adatero. "Akufuna kudziwa kuti boma lidzasunga mawu ake."

Willy Kambwandira, yemwe akutsogolera Center for Social Accountability and Transparency, adati ziphuphu zakhala gawo la mtengo wochita bizinesi pano. Ogulitsa ndalama saopa ziphuphu zokha, komanso kusintha kwa malamulo, njira zobisika zogulira, kugwiritsa ntchito malamulo molakwika, ndi mapangano omwe makhothi sadzawagwiritsa ntchito. James Chiutsi, wochokera ku Chamber for Small and Medium Businesses Association, adati makampani ang'onoang'ono amavutika kwambiri. "Kwa mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono, ziphuphu ndiye kusiyana pakati pa kulipira antchito ndi kutseka shopu," adatero.

Boma likuyesera kukonza izi. Mitc One-Stop Service Centre imapanga zilolezo mwachangu. Makina a digito amachepetsa kuchedwa. Msaka adati osunga ndalama amawona kusinthaku ngati njira zenizeni zochepetsera ziphuphu. Anati dzikolo liyenera kupangitsa kuti lamulo la Whistleblower Protection ligwire ntchito bwino - kuti anthu athe kuuza boma za ziphuphu popanda mantha. Makomiti Oona za Umphumphu mu unduna uliwonse ndi ofesi iliyonse ayeneranso kukhala olimba. "Kusintha kumeneku kudzachepetsa ziphuphu ndikupangitsa malamulo kukhala achilungamo," adatero Msaka. "Zimenezo zidzabweretsa ndalama zambiri kuchokera kwa osunga ndalama ku Malawi."

en.title: Mozambique First Lady Helps 700 Malawians Flee South Africa Violence
en.body: Mozambique's First Lady Gueta Chapo visited the Maputo Bus Terminal on Thursday night and gave help to about 700 Malawians. The families had run away from xenophobic attacks in South Africa. She gave them blankets, food and water. She also arranged buses to take them to Tete Province and then to Mwanza District in Malawi.
en.body_more:
The Malawians had travelled for many days with little food and few things. They left South Africa because of violence and had to leave their jobs and homes. At the bus terminal, they waited for a way to go home, tired and worried. Gueta Chapo gave blankets to keep families warm at night. She gave food to eat and clean water to drink. She spoke to mothers and children and told them they were not forgotten.

The First Lady said every person is her child, no matter what country they come from. She said all people need care and respect, especially when they are in trouble. The families said they had lost almost everything and did not think a neighbouring country would help them. Many said they came to Mozambique scared and tired, but left with new hope.

The buses left Maputo for Tete with the Malawians inside. Children wrapped themselves in the new blankets. Parents packed the food and water for the next part of the journey. The trip through Mozambique — from Maputo to Tete to Mwanza — is now a main road home for Malawians coming from South Africa. The Malawi and Mozambique governments are working together to help people go home safely. The First Lady's help shows that kindness can bring hope in hard times.

ny.title: Mkazi wa Purezidenti wa ku Mozambique Athandiza Anthu 700 a ku Malawi Kuthawa Nkhanza ku South Africa
ny.body: Mkazi wa First Lady wa ku Mozambique, Gueta Chapo, adapita ku Maputo Bus Terminal Lachinayi usiku ndipo adathandiza anthu pafupifupi 700 aku Malawi. Mabanjawo adathawa ziwawa zankhanza ku South Africa. Adawapatsa mabulangete, chakudya ndi madzi. Adakonzanso mabasi kuti awatengere ku Tete Province kenako ku Mwanza District ku Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Anthu aku Malawi anayenda masiku ambiri ndi chakudya chochepa komanso zinthu zochepa. Anachoka ku South Africa chifukwa cha chiwawa ndipo anayenera kusiya ntchito zawo ndi nyumba zawo. Pa siteshoni ya basi, anadikira njira yobwerera kwawo, atatopa komanso akuda nkhawa. Gueta Chapo anapereka mabulangete kuti mabanja azifunda usiku. Anapereka chakudya kuti adye ndi madzi oyera kuti amwe. Analankhula ndi amayi ndi ana ndipo anawauza kuti sanaiwalike.

Dona Wachiwiri anati munthu aliyense ndi mwana wake, mosasamala kanthu kuti akuchokera kudziko liti. Anati anthu onse amafunika chisamaliro ndi ulemu, makamaka akakhala pamavuto. Mabanjawo anati ataya chilichonse ndipo sankaganiza kuti dziko loyandikana nalo lingawathandize. Ambiri anati anafika ku Mozambique ali ndi mantha komanso kutopa, koma anachoka ndi chiyembekezo chatsopano.

Mabasi anachoka ku Maputo kupita ku Tete ndi anthu aku Malawi mkati. Ana anadziphimba ndi mabulangete atsopano. Makolo ananyamula chakudya ndi madzi kuti apite ku gawo lotsatira la ulendo. Ulendo wodutsa ku Mozambique — kuchokera ku Maputo kupita ku Tete kupita ku Mwanza — tsopano ndi msewu waukulu wopita kwa anthu aku Malawi ochokera ku South Africa. Maboma a Malawi ndi Mozambique akugwira ntchito limodzi kuti athandize anthu kubwerera kwawo mosamala. Thandizo la Mayi Woyamba likusonyeza kuti kukoma mtima kumabweretsa chiyembekezo m'nthawi zovuta.

en.title: Pay delay hurts Malawi civil servants, government blames technical fault
en.body: Civil servants in Malawi did not get their June salaries on time. They are normally paid by the 27th of each month, but by July 1 the money had not come. The Department of Accountant General said a technical fault stopped the salary system. The department fixed the problem, and workers started to get paid on July 2.
en.body_more:
Trade unions said the delay caused real hardship. Solomon Chomba, who leads the Federation of Civil Service Unions, said workers could not pay bills or buy food on time. "When salaries delay, employees are unable to plan their finances effectively, pay bills on time or purchase household necessities," he said. Workers rely on their monthly pay to meet basic needs.

Charles Kumchenga, general secretary of Teachers Union of Malawi, said the union spoke to the Accountant General's Department on Wednesday. The department told them the technical problems were fixed. But Kumchenga warned that this is not the first time pay has been late. He said unless the government finds a real fix, the delays will keep happening and harm education and other public services.

Lameck Magawa, who leads the Civil Servants Trade Union, called on all government offices to pay workers on time. He said this would help protect workers if technical problems happen again. Loness Gwazanga Chowawa, a spokeswoman for the Department of Accountant General, said the problem was technical, not a money shortage. She said salaries were ready on June 30, but the system failed to send them to workers' bank accounts. A few government offices were affected by the fault, she added.

ny.title: Kuchedwa kwa malipiro kwavulaza antchito aboma aku Malawi, boma lati ndi vuto laukadaulo
ny.body: Antchito aboma ku Malawi sanalandire malipiro awo a mwezi wa June pa nthawi yake. Nthawi zambiri amalipidwa pofika pa 27 mwezi uliwonse, koma pofika pa 1 Julayi ndalamazo sizinabwere. Dipatimenti Yoona za Akaunti inati vuto laukadaulo linayimitsa dongosolo la malipiro. Dipatimentiyo inakonza vutoli, ndipo antchito anayamba kulipidwa pa 2 Julayi.
ny.body_more:
Mabungwe ogwira ntchito anati kuchedwa kumeneku kwabweretsa mavuto aakulu. Solomon Chomba, yemwe akutsogolera Federation of Civil Service Unions, anati antchito sangalipire ma bilu kapena kugula chakudya pa nthawi yake. "Malipiro akachedwa, antchito sangathe kukonzekera bwino ndalama zawo, kulipira ma bilu pa nthawi yake kapena kugula zinthu zofunika panyumba," adatero. Ogwira ntchito amadalira malipiro awo pamwezi kuti akwaniritse zosowa zawo zofunika.

Charles Kumchenga, mlembi wamkulu wa Teachers Union of Malawi, adati bungweli lalankhula ndi Dipatimenti ya Accountant General Lachitatu. Dipatimentiyi inawauza kuti mavuto aukadaulo akonzedwa. Koma Kumchenga anachenjeza kuti iyi si nthawi yoyamba kuti malipiro achedwe. Anati pokhapokha ngati boma lipeza njira yeniyeni yothetsera vutoli, kuchedwaku kudzapitirira kuchitika ndikuwononga maphunziro ndi ntchito zina za boma.

Lameck Magawa, yemwe akutsogolera Civil Servants Trade Union, adapempha maofesi onse aboma kuti azilipira antchito pa nthawi yake. Anati izi zithandiza kuteteza antchito ngati mavuto aukadaulo achitikanso. Loness Gwazanga Chowawa, wolankhulira Dipatimenti ya Accountant General, adati vutoli ndi laukadaulo, osati kusowa kwa ndalama. Iye anati malipiro anali okonzeka pa June 30, koma dongosololi linalephera kuwatumiza ku maakaunti akubanki a ogwira ntchito. Maofesi angapo aboma adakhudzidwa ndi vutoli, adatero.

en.title: FDH Bank funds 58 kilometre road fix in Mangochi
en.body: FDH Bank plc gave K100 billion to fix the Golomoti-Monkey Bay Road in Mangochi. The road is 58 kilometres long. Minister of Transport Jappie Mhango said this is a big step for Malawi's roads. The bank will pay for one part of the road. Another bank will pay for the second part.
en.body_more:
The road splits into two parts. FDH Bank will pay for the 30 kilometre section from Chantulo to Monkey Bay Turn-off. This part is called Lot 1. Unik Construction will do the work. The project will take 24 months to finish. Lot 2, from Chantulo to Golomoti Trading Centre, will be paid for by another partner that the government has not named yet. Mota-Engil will build Lot 2.

Mhango said the road is important for Lilongwe and will help tourism, farming and fishing grow. The road gets damaged by floods often. Travel on it is slow, about 50 kilometres per hour. The new road will be strong against floods and climate change. The road was built about 20 years ago with help from the European Union.

William Mpinganjira is the chief executive officer of FDH Financial Holdings Limited, which owns FDH Bank plc. He said the bank grew strong and can now pay for big projects. He said FDH Bank wants to help Malawi grow, not just rely on money from other countries. The Roads Authority chairperson Engineer Witness Kuwotcha said the authority will watch the work to make sure it is good. The road helps people go to schools, health places, and markets in Mangochi.

Mhango also warned people not to build houses inside the road space. The government will not pay money to people who build there. This is against the Public Roads Act, 2023.

ny.title: Banki ya FDH yapereka ndalama zokonzanso misewu ya makilomita 58 ku Mangochi
ny.body: Banki ya FDH plc yapereka K100 biliyoni kuti ikonze msewu wa Golomoti-Monkey Bay ku Mangochi. Msewuwu ndi wautali makilomita 58. Nduna ya Zamayendedwe Jappie Mhango adati iyi ndi sitepe yayikulu pamisewu ya Malawi. Banki idzalipira gawo limodzi la msewu. Banki ina idzalipira gawo lachiwiri.
ny.body_more:
Msewuwu wagawika m'magawo awiri. FDH Bank idzalipira gawo la makilomita 30 kuchokera ku Chantulo kupita ku Monkey Bay Turn-off. Gawoli limatchedwa Lot 1. Unik Construction idzachita ntchitoyi. Ntchitoyi idzatenga miyezi 24 kuti ithe. Lot 2, kuchokera ku Chantulo kupita ku Golomoti Trading Centre, idzalipidwa ndi mnzake wina yemwe boma silinatchule dzina lake. Mota-Engil adzamanga Lot 2.

Mhango adati msewuwu ndi wofunika kwambiri ku Lilongwe ndipo uthandiza zokopa alendo, ulimi ndi usodzi kukula. Msewuwu umawonongeka nthawi zambiri chifukwa cha kusefukira kwa madzi. Kuyendamo kumakhala pang'onopang'ono, pafupifupi makilomita 50 pa ola limodzi. Msewu watsopanowu udzakhala wolimba motsutsana ndi kusefukira kwa madzi ndi kusintha kwa nyengo. Msewuwu unamangidwa zaka pafupifupi 20 zapitazo ndi thandizo la European Union.

William Mpinganjira ndi mkulu wa FDH Financial Holdings Limited, yomwe ili ndi FDH Bank plc. Anati bankiyi yakula ndipo tsopano ikhoza kulipira mapulojekiti akuluakulu. Anati FDH Bank ikufuna kuthandiza Malawi kukula, osati kungodalira ndalama zochokera kumayiko ena. Wapampando wa Roads Authority, Injiniya Witness Kuwotcha, anati bungweli lidzayang'anira ntchitoyo kuti litsimikizire kuti ikuyenda bwino. Msewuwu umathandiza anthu kupita kusukulu, ku malo azaumoyo, komanso m'misika ku Mangochi.

Mhango adachenjezanso anthu kuti asamange nyumba mkati mwa msewu. Boma silidzalipira ndalama kwa anthu omwe amamanga pamenepo. Izi zikusemphana ndi lamulo la Public Roads Act, 2023.

en.title: Bank gives clean water to Thyolo health post
en.body: National Bank of Malawi gave a new solar water system to Mikate Health Post in Thyolo on Thursday. The bank spent money from its K200 million charity programme. The water system will help patients and health workers. It is part of the bank's work to help health, education and the environment.
en.body_more:
Rachel Chirambo is the bank's Relationship Manager. She said the bank wanted to give people clean and safe places for health care. She said healthy people can work and help the country grow. "When Thyolo District Council told us about the problems at Mikate Health Post, we saw it matched our health work. We wanted to invest in people's health and dignity," she said.

George Jailosi is the Thyolo District Council Chairperson. He said the water gift came at the right time. He asked other groups to help other projects in the district the same way. "People will now have clean water, which is good for health," he said.

Dr. Milliam Jere Nyasulu is the District Health Director. She said the health post had no water before. This stopped them from keeping the place clean and safe. "Before this gift, we could not fully stop germs spreading because we had no water. Now we can give safer health care," she said. She also asked other helpers to bring clean water to other health posts in the district.

Traditional Authority Kapichi said thank you to the bank. He asked people to look after the water system and not break it. The new water system will help over 16,000 people from Mikate and other villages near it. It will make health care better for all of them.

ny.title: Banki yapereka madzi oyera ku malo azaumoyo ku Thyolo
ny.body: Banki Yadziko Lonse ya Malawi yapereka njira yatsopano yogwiritsira ntchito madzi a dzuwa ku Mikate Health Post ku Thyolo Lachinayi. Bankiyi idagwiritsa ntchito ndalama kuchokera ku pulogalamu yake yothandiza anthu yokwana K200 miliyoni. Njira yamadzi iyi ithandiza odwala ndi ogwira ntchito zachipatala. Ndi gawo la ntchito ya bankiyi kuthandiza zaumoyo, maphunziro ndi chilengedwe.
ny.body_more:
Rachel Chirambo ndi Woyang'anira Ubale wa banki. Iye anati banki ikufuna kupatsa anthu malo oyera komanso otetezeka kuti azisamalira thanzi lawo. Iye anati anthu athanzi amatha kugwira ntchito ndikuthandizira dzikolo kukula. "Pamene Khonsolo ya Chigawo cha Thyolo inatiuza za mavuto omwe ali ku Mikate Health Post, tinaona kuti zikugwirizana ndi ntchito yathu yazaumoyo. Tinkafuna kuyika ndalama pa thanzi la anthu ndi ulemu wawo," iye anatero.

George Jailosi ndi Wapampando wa Khonsolo ya Chigawo cha Thyolo. Iye anati mphatso ya madzi inabwera panthawi yoyenera. Anapempha magulu ena kuti athandize mapulojekiti ena m'boma momwemonso. "Anthu tsopano adzakhala ndi madzi oyera, zomwe ndi zabwino pa thanzi lawo," iye anatero.

Dr. Milliam Jere Nyasulu ndi Mtsogoleri wa Zaumoyo wa Chigawo. Iye anati malo azaumoyo analibe madzi kale. Izi zinawaletsa kusunga malowo ali aukhondo komanso otetezeka. "Tisanapereke mphatsoyi, sitingathe kuletsa kwathunthu kufalikira kwa majeremusi chifukwa tinalibe madzi. Tsopano tikhoza kupereka chithandizo chaumoyo chotetezeka," iye anatero. Anapemphanso othandizira ena kuti abweretse madzi oyera ku malo ena azaumoyo m'boma.

Akuluakulu a Zachikhalidwe Kapichi anati zikomo ku banki. Iye anapempha anthu kuti asamalire makina amadzi osawawononga. Makina atsopano amadzi athandiza anthu oposa 16,000 ochokera ku Mikate ndi midzi ina yapafupi. Izi zipangitsa kuti chisamaliro chaumoyo chikhale chabwino kwa onse.

en.title: Plane crash inquiry hears of weak command and poor communication
en.body: Malawi's parliament is looking into the plane crash that killed Vice President Saulos Chilima and nine others on 10 June 2024. Brigadier General Dan Kuwali told the inquiry committee that he only helped pass on a request for a military aircraft. He said he made no operational decisions about the plane.
en.body_more:
Kuwali said his role was limited and strictly administrative. He relayed a request from the Kasambara family, who wanted a military aircraft to carry relatives and a casket from Blantyre to Mzuzu. Kuwali told then deputy commander Lieutenant General George Alexander Jaffu Jr about this request. He also contacted then Air Force commander Brigadier General Robert Ishmael, who said an aircraft was available but formal approval was needed.

When Chilima's aide Joshua Valera asked on 9 June about using the same aircraft for the Vice President's travel, Kuwali refused to help. He told Valera and Counsel Chuka Mwale he would not arrange any travel for Chilima. He said the proposed schedule was not operationally achievable. The Air Force commander agreed the aircraft was already committed to the Kasambara family and could not meet Chilima's schedule.

The inquiry is examining how decisions were made before the crash. Testimonies have come from many witnesses, including Mary Chilima, the Vice President's spouse, and Mabvuto Ngwilinji, spouse of the late pilot Flora Selemani. These accounts show gaps in how information moved through government and the military during emergencies. Officials are being questioned on standard procedures, chains of command, and how departments talk to each other.

The inquiry has expanded beyond just examining what happened to looking at weaknesses in systems and institutions. The committee wants to understand why communication failed at the highest levels. The investigation is now focusing on how to improve coordination between military and civilian authorities and strengthen disaster response procedures for the future.

ny.title: Kafukufuku wa ngozi ya ndege wamva za kufooka kwa ulamuliro ndi kusalankhulana bwino
ny.body: Nyumba yamalamulo ya Malawi ikufufuza za ngozi ya ndege yomwe inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi anayi pa 10 June 2024. Brigadier General Dan Kuwali adauza komiti yofufuza kuti adangothandiza kuvomereza pempho la ndege yankhondo. Iye adati sanapange zisankho zokhudzana ndi ndegeyo.
ny.body_more:
Kuwali anati udindo wake unali wochepa komanso wolamulira kwambiri. Anapereka pempho kuchokera kwa banja la a Kasambara, omwe ankafuna ndege yankhondo yonyamulira achibale ndi bokosi kuchokera ku Blantyre kupita ku Mzuzu. Kuwali anauza wachiwiri kwa mkulu wa asilikali Lieutenant General George Alexander Jaffu Jr za pempholi. Analankhulanso ndi mkulu wa Air Force panthawiyo Brigadier General Robert Ishmael, yemwe anati ndegeyo inalipo koma chilolezo chinali chofunikira.

Wothandizira wa Chilima Joshua Valera atafunsa pa 9 June za kugwiritsa ntchito ndege yomweyi paulendo wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti, Kuwali anakana kuthandiza. Anauza Valera ndi Loya Chuka Mwale kuti sangakonze ulendo uliwonse wa Chilima. Anati ndondomeko yomwe idaperekedwayo sinali yotheka. Mtsogoleri wa Air Force adavomereza kuti ndegeyo inali kale yoperekedwa kwa banja la a Kasambara ndipo silingathe kukwaniritsa ndondomeko ya Chilima.

Kafukufukuyu akuwunika momwe zisankho zinapangidwira ngozi isanachitike. Umboni wachokera kwa mboni zambiri, kuphatikizapo Mary Chilima, mkazi wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti, ndi Mabvuto Ngwilinji, mkazi wa woyendetsa ndege womwalirayo Flora Selemani. Nkhanizi zikusonyeza mipata yomwe chidziwitso chinadutsa m'boma ndi asilikali panthawi yamavuto. Akuluakulu akufunsidwa mafunso okhudza njira zodziwika bwino, unyolo wa akuluakulu, ndi momwe madipatimenti amalankhulirana.

Kafukufukuyu wakula kuposa kungoyang'ana zomwe zinachitika poyang'ana zofooka m'machitidwe ndi mabungwe. Komitiyi ikufuna kumvetsetsa chifukwa chake kulumikizana kunalephera pamlingo wapamwamba. Kafukufukuyu tsopano akuyang'ana kwambiri momwe angakonzere mgwirizano pakati pa akuluakulu ankhondo ndi anthu wamba ndikulimbitsa njira zothetsera masoka mtsogolo.

en.title: Public universities raise tuition fees by 100 percent
en.body: Public universities in Malawi have raised tuition fees by 100 percent from K650,000 to K1.3 million per year from the 2027/2028 academic year. Kamuzu University of Health Sciences (KUHeS) will charge K2 million instead of K1 million. All students will also pay K60,000 for medical cover. Parents, students and civil society groups are worried about the increase.
en.body_more:
The five universities making the change are Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources (Luanar), Mzuzu University (Mzuni), Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences (Mubas), University of Malawi (Unima) and Malawi University of Science and Technology (Must). The universities said the increase is necessary because the cost of teaching and learning has gone up. Mzuni said inflation and other money problems forced the change. Going forward, Mzuni will raise fees by 10 percent each year instead of making big jumps.

Student leaders say the 100 percent increase is too much. Vitumbiko Mzumara, president of Mzuni Students' Representative Council, said: "We are not against the hike, but 100 percent is too much." He asked why the government gives free school fees in secondary school but makes university so expensive. Wesley Malekano, president of Malawi Public Universities Students' Union, said they would meet to discuss what to do next. Benedicto Kondowe from the Civil Society Education Coalition said universities asked for K536 billion but the government only gave K133.7 billion. The real cost of training one student is K4.8 million at Mzuni, K8.6 million at Must, K8.8 million at Unima, K10.9 million at Mubas, K11.2 million at Luanar and K20.7 million at KUHeS.

The government said it will give more money to help poor students. The Higher Education Students' Loans and Grants Board plans to help 38,000 students with K42 billion in the 2026/27 budget. Opposition leader Simplex Chithyola Banda said the government should reverse the fee increase. He said it is unfair to make university free in secondary school and then charge so much at university. Chithyola asked the government to find other ways to pay for universities, such as through research and business activities, instead of raising fees so much.

ny.title: Mayunivesite aboma akweza ndalama zolipirira maphunziro ndi 100 peresenti
ny.body: Mayunivesite aboma ku Malawi akweza ndalama zolipirira maphunziro ndi 100 peresenti kuchoka pa K650,000 kufika pa K1.3 miliyoni pachaka kuyambira chaka chamaphunziro cha 2027/2028. Yunivesite ya Kamuzu University of Health Sciences (KUHeS) idzalipiritsa K2 miliyoni m'malo mwa K1 miliyoni. Ophunzira onse adzalipiranso K60,000 kuti alandire chithandizo chamankhwala. Makolo, ophunzira ndi magulu a anthu wamba akuda nkhawa ndi kuwonjezeka kwa ndalamazi.
ny.body_more:
Mayunivesite asanu omwe akusintha ndi Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources (Luanar), Mzuzu University (Mzuni), Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences (Mubas), University of Malawi (Unima) ndi Malawi University of Science and Technology (Must). Mayunivesitewa adati kukweraku n'kofunika chifukwa mtengo wophunzitsira ndi kuphunzira wakwera. Mzuni adati kukwera kwa mitengo ndi mavuto ena azachuma ndizomwe zidapangitsa kuti kusinthaku kuchitike. Kupitilira apo, Mzuni idzakweza ndalama zolipirira ndi 10 peresenti chaka chilichonse m'malo mopanga zinthu zambiri.

Atsogoleri a ophunzira akuti kukwera kwa 100 peresenti ndi kwakukulu kwambiri. Vitumbiko Mzumara, purezidenti wa Mzuni Students' Representative Council, adati: "Sitikutsutsa kukweraku, koma 100 peresenti ndi kwakukulu kwambiri." Adafunsa chifukwa chake boma limapereka ndalama zaulere kusukulu yasekondale koma zimapangitsa kuti yunivesite ikhale yokwera mtengo kwambiri. Wesley Malekano, purezidenti wa Malawi Public Universities Students' Union, adati akumana kuti akambirane zomwe achite. Benedicto Kondowe wochokera ku Civil Society Education Coalition adati mayunivesite adapempha K536 biliyoni koma boma lidapereka K133.7 biliyoni yokha. Ndalama zenizeni zophunzitsira wophunzira m'modzi ndi K4.8 miliyoni ku Mzuni, K8.6 miliyoni ku Must, K8.8 miliyoni ku Unima, K10.9 miliyoni ku Mubas, K11.2 miliyoni ku Luanar ndi K20.7 miliyoni ku KUHeS.

Boma lati lipereka ndalama zambiri kuti lithandize ophunzira osauka. Bungwe la Maphunziro Apamwamba la Ophunzira a Loans and Grants Board likukonzekera kuthandiza ophunzira 38,000 ndi K42 biliyoni mu bajeti ya 2026/27. Mtsogoleri wotsutsa Simplex Chithyola Banda adati boma liyenera kusintha kukweza kwa ndalama zolipirira. Iye anati n'kosayenera kupangitsa mayunivesite kukhala aulere kusukulu ya sekondale kenako n’kulipiritsa ndalama zambiri ku yunivesite. Chithyola adapempha boma kuti lipeze njira zina zolipirira mayunivesite, monga kudzera mu kafukufuku ndi ntchito zamabizinesi, m'malo mokweza ndalama zambiri.

en.title: Malawi fights rising deaths from pesticide poisoning
en.body: Malawi has started a new plan to stop deaths and injuries from pesticide poisoning. The Pesticide Control Board, led by James Chiku Kaphale, held a meeting in Lilongwe on 30 June. Government workers, health experts, police and researchers came together to find answers. They said the country has a big health problem that most people do not see.
en.body_more:
The meeting was called after recent deaths. A 54-year-old driver named Mwakaira died in Jenda, Mzimba District after taking pesticides. Officials said many more people have died this way in other areas too. Health experts said pesticides cause about 79% of all poisoning cases in Malawi. Organophosphates and fumigants are used most often and cause the most harm.

Agriculture is very important to Malawi. It makes about 30% of the money the country earns and gives jobs to more than 60% of people. But more pesticide use has brought big dangers. Children at hospitals are now being poisoned more often. This happens because families store pesticides in unsafe places at home. People do not know how dangerous these chemicals are. One expert said the country is only seeing a small part of a much bigger problem.

Kaphale said Malawi has no good system to count poisoning cases. There is no central place to keep this information. Police, hospitals and farm workers do not share data with each other. Some districts like Dedza, Mulanje, Balaka and Lilongwe have seen many suspected suicide cases where people took pesticides. Kaphale said: "We cannot protect lives when we cannot see the problem clearly." He said a product made to help the economy should never kill people. The new working group will make better ways to track cases and help different government parts work together.

ny.title: Malawi ikulimbana ndi kuwonjezeka kwa imfa chifukwa cha poizoni wa mankhwala ophera tizilombo
ny.body: Malawi yayamba dongosolo latsopano loletsa imfa ndi kuvulala chifukwa cha poizoni wa mankhwala ophera tizilombo. Bungwe Loyang'anira Mankhwala Ophera Tizilombo, lotsogozedwa ndi James Chiku Kaphale, linachita msonkhano ku Lilongwe pa 30 June. Ogwira ntchito m'boma, akatswiri azaumoyo, apolisi ndi ofufuza adasonkhana kuti apeze mayankho. Iwo anati dzikolo lili ndi vuto lalikulu la thanzi lomwe anthu ambiri saliona.
ny.body_more:
Msonkhanowu unayitanidwa pambuyo pa imfa zaposachedwa. Dalaivala wazaka 54 dzina lake Mwakaira anamwalira ku Jenda, Chigawo cha Mzimba atamwa mankhwala ophera tizilombo. Akuluakulu adati anthu ambiri afa motere m'madera ena. Akatswiri azaumoyo adati mankhwala ophera tizilombo amayambitsa pafupifupi 79% ya milandu yonse ya poizoni ku Malawi. Organophosphates ndi fumigants zimagwiritsidwa ntchito nthawi zambiri ndipo zimayambitsa mavuto ambiri.

Ulimi ndi wofunika kwambiri ku Malawi. Umapanga pafupifupi 30% ya ndalama zomwe dzikolo limapeza ndipo umapatsa anthu oposa 60% ntchito. Koma kugwiritsa ntchito mankhwala ophera tizilombo kwambiri kwabweretsa zoopsa zazikulu. Ana m'zipatala tsopano akupatsidwa poizoni pafupipafupi. Izi zimachitika chifukwa mabanja amasunga mankhwala ophera tizilombo m'malo osatetezeka kunyumba. Anthu sakudziwa kuti mankhwala awa ndi oopsa bwanji. Katswiri wina adati dzikolo likuwona gawo laling'ono chabe la vuto lalikulu kwambiri.

Kaphale adati Malawi ilibe njira yabwino yowerengera milandu ya poizoni. Palibe malo osungira izi. Apolisi, zipatala ndi ogwira ntchito m'mafamu sagawana deta. Maboma ena monga Dedza, Mulanje, Balaka ndi Lilongwe awona milandu yambiri yokayikiridwa yodzipha komwe anthu adamwa mankhwala ophera tizilombo. Kaphale anati: "Sitingathe kuteteza miyoyo pamene sitingathe kuwona bwino vutoli." Iye anati chinthu chopangidwa kuti chithandize chuma sichiyenera kupha anthu. Gulu latsopanoli lidzapanga njira zabwino zotsatirira milandu ndikuthandizira magawo osiyanasiyana aboma kugwira ntchito limodzi.

en.title: Two South African experts join Malawi women's football team
en.body: The Football Association of Malawi has hired two South African football experts to help the Scorchers at the 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations. Sudesh Singh and Tyrese Steyn arrived in the country on Monday. Singh will be technical adviser and Steyn will be data analyst. They have two-month contracts for the tournament only.
en.body_more:
FAM General Secretary Abdul Chiwalo said the two experts are now in the country. Singh and Steyn will work with head coach Lovemore Fazili and assistant coach Maggie Chombo Sadik. The Confederation of African Football asked that the coach at the tournament hold a CAF A Licence. Fazili has a Uefa Pro Coaching Licence and was given a grace period of two years to upgrade his qualifications.

Singh has worked with big football clubs in Africa. He was with Mamelodi Sundowns and Amazulu in South Africa. He also worked as technical director for Ho Chi Minh City FC in Vietnam and helped them move to the First Division. Singh has been a technical expert for Super Sport media. Steyn now works as a data analyst for UJ Soweto Women's Football Club. FAM President Fleetwood Haiya said Singh's role does not mean the association lacks faith in Fazili. Haiya said Singh will only give technical help. Fazili said the new experts will make the team stronger. Singh said he has watched the Scorchers for a long time and wants to help Malawi do well.

The team is now in camp at Mpira Village in Blantyre. Fazili named a 32-member squad. Captain Tabitha Chawinga plays in France. Striker Temwa Chawinga plays in the United States. Midfielder Faith Chimzimu plays in Sweden. The Scorchers are in Group C with Nigeria, Zambia and Egypt. They play Nigeria on July 28, Egypt on August 1, and Zambia on August 5. All matches are in Morocco.

ny.title: Akatswiri awiri aku South Africa alowa nawo timu ya mpira wa azimayi ku Malawi
ny.body: Bungwe la Mpira wa Miguu la Malawi lalemba ntchito akatswiri awiri a mpira wa ku South Africa kuti athandize Scorchers pa mpikisano wa Women's Africa Cup of Nations wa 2026. Sudesh Singh ndi Tyrese Steyn afika mdziko muno Lolemba. Singh adzakhala mlangizi waukadaulo ndipo Steyn adzakhala katswiri wofufuza deta. Ali ndi mapangano a miyezi iwiri okha pa mpikisanowu.
ny.body_more:
Mlembi Wamkulu wa FAM, Abdul Chiwalo, adati akatswiri awiriwa ali mdziko muno tsopano. Singh ndi Steyn adzagwira ntchito ndi mphunzitsi wamkulu, Lovemore Fazili, ndi mphunzitsi wothandizira, Maggie Chombo Sadik. Bungwe la Confederation of African Football linapempha mphunzitsi pa mpikisanowu kuti akhale ndi CAF A Licence. Fazili ali ndi Uefa Pro Coaching Licence ndipo adapatsidwa nthawi yachisomo ya zaka ziwiri kuti akweze ziyeneretso zake.

Singh wagwira ntchito ndi magulu akuluakulu a mpira ku Africa. Anali ndi Mamelodi Sundowns ndi Amazulu ku South Africa. Anagwiranso ntchito ngati director waukadaulo ku Ho Chi Minh City FC ku Vietnam ndipo adawathandiza kusamukira ku First Division. Singh wakhala katswiri waukadaulo ku Super Sport media. Steyn tsopano amagwira ntchito ngati katswiri wofufuza deta ku UJ Soweto Women's Football Club. Purezidenti wa FAM, Fleetwood Haiya, adati udindo wa Singh sukutanthauza kuti bungweli silikhulupirira Fazili. Haiya adati Singh adzangopereka thandizo laukadaulo. Fazili adati akatswiri atsopanowa apangitsa timuyi kukhala yolimba. Singh adati wakhala akuyang'anira Scorchers kwa nthawi yayitali ndipo akufuna kuthandiza Malawi kuchita bwino.

Timuyi tsopano ili mu msasa ku Mpira Village ku Blantyre. Fazili wasankha gulu la anthu 32. Captain Tabitha Chawinga amasewera ku France. Wosewera Temwa Chawinga amasewera ku United States. Wosewera pakati Faith Chimzimu amasewera ku Sweden. A Scorchers ali mu Gulu C ndi Nigeria, Zambia ndi Egypt. Asewera ndi Nigeria pa Julayi 28, Egypt pa Ogasiti 1, ndi Zambia pa Ogasiti 5. Masewera onse ali ku Morocco.

en.title: Zamba tells of plane crash search and protocol confusion
en.body: Former Secretary to the President Colleen Zamba told a parliamentary committee on Wednesday how government searched for Vice President Saulos Chilima's plane after it went missing on June 10 2024. The plane crashed and killed Chilima and eight others. Zamba testified from South Africa about the events of that day.
en.body_more:
Zamba explained that protocol arrangements changed on the morning of June 10. The government had first asked Minister of Local Government Richard Chimwendo Banda to represent President Lazarus Chakwera at the funeral of former Attorney General Ralph Kasambara in Nkhata Bay. But when officials learnt that Chilima also planned to go, Zamba said Chilima became the senior government representative because he was the highest-ranking official expected at the funeral.

Zamba told the committee that she advised the military commander that Chilima should represent the president instead. She said there was confusion when she wrote to Chakwera that the vice-president might attend. Chakwera misunderstood and thought she meant Harry Mkandawire, the minister of defence. This led Chakwera to ask Mkandawire to go to the funeral. Samson Ngutwa, the former Clerk to the Cabinet, supported Zamba's account in separate testimony.

When the aircraft disappeared, Zamba said she immediately contacted the military commander, the police chief and the airport commandant to start a search. She obtained the passenger list and phone numbers. She then called the Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority to help trace the phones. One phone was last found at 12:30pm on a Raiply tower, giving a ten-kilometre search area. Ngutwa told the committee that President Chakwera held a short Cabinet meeting lasting about fifteen minutes after the plane went missing, before he spoke to the nation.

Zamba also testified about events before the crash. Lawyer Kelvin Sentala, a friend of Chilima, said he met the vice-president on June 9 at his home in Area 43. They discussed church matters and Kasambara's funeral. Sentala said Chilima sent him a text message at about 4:30am on June 10 asking if he had left for Nkhata Bay. That was their last communication.

ny.title: Zamba akufotokoza za kusaka ndege zomwe zagwa komanso kusokonezeka kwa njira zoyendetsera ndege
ny.body: Mlembi wakale wa Purezidenti Colleen Zamba adauza komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo Lachitatu momwe boma lidafufuzira ndege ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima itasowa pa June 10 2024. Ndegeyo idagwa ndikupha Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu. Zamba adapereka umboni wochokera ku South Africa za zomwe zinachitika tsiku limenelo.
ny.body_more:
Zamba anafotokoza kuti makonzedwe a protocol adasintha m'mawa wa pa 10 June. Boma lidayamba kupempha Nduna ya Zamalamulo a m'deralo Richard Chimwendo Banda kuti ayimire Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera pa maliro a wakale Attorney General Ralph Kasambara ku Nkhata Bay. Koma akuluakulu atamva kuti Chilima nayenso akukonzekera kupita, Zamba adati Chilima adakhala woyimira boma wamkulu chifukwa anali mkulu waudindo wapamwamba kwambiri yemwe amayembekezeredwa pa malirowo.

Zamba adauza komitiyo kuti adalangiza mkulu wa asilikali kuti Chilima ayenera kuyimirira purezidenti m'malo mwake. Adati panali chisokonezo pamene adalembera Chakwera kuti wachiwiri kwa purezidenti abwere. Chakwera sanamvetse bwino ndipo adaganiza kuti akutanthauza Harry Mkandawire, nduna ya chitetezo. Izi zidapangitsa Chakwera kupempha Mkandawire kuti apite ku malirowo. Samson Ngutwa, yemwe kale anali Kalaliki wa Cabinet, adathandizira nkhani ya Zamba m'maumboni osiyana.

Ndegeyo itasowa, Zamba adati nthawi yomweyo adalumikizana ndi mkulu wa asilikali, mkulu wa apolisi ndi mkulu wa eyapoti kuti ayambe kufufuza. Adapeza mndandanda wa okwera ndi manambala a foni. Kenako adayimbira Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority kuti amuthandize kupeza mafoniwo. Foni imodzi inapezeka komaliza nthawi ya 12:30pm pa nsanja ya Raiply, yomwe inali pamalo ofufuzira a makilomita khumi. Ngutwa anauza komitiyo kuti Purezidenti Chakwera adachita msonkhano waufupi wa nduna womwe unatenga mphindi pafupifupi khumi ndi zisanu ndegeyo itasowa, asanalankhule ndi dzikolo.

Zamba adaperekanso umboni wa zomwe zinachitika ngozi isanachitike. Loya Kelvin Sentala, bwenzi la Chilima, adati adakumana ndi wachiwiri kwa purezidenti pa June 9 kunyumba kwake ku Area 43. Anakambirana nkhani za tchalitchi ndi maliro a Kasambara. Sentala adati Chilima adamutumizira uthenga pafupifupi 4:30am pa June 10 akufunsa ngati adapita ku Nkhata Bay. Umenewo unali uthenga wawo womaliza.

en.title: Malawians leave South Africa after big marches against migrants
en.body: South Africa saw large marches on June 30 against foreign nationals without proper papers. Thousands marched in many cities. Police sent 6,000 officers to Cape Town and more to other areas. Some shops were looted. Many Malawians left the country by bus. The marches came after groups set June 30 as the date for migrants to leave.
en.body_more:
The marches took place in Johannesburg, Pretoria, Durban, Soweto, Cape Town and other cities. In Soweto, south of Johannesburg, the day started quiet but later became violent. Some buildings where foreign nationals lived were looted. Police clashed with marchers. In Alexandra, north of Johannesburg, looting went on through the night of June 30 and ended on July 1 morning.

In Durban, hundreds of people gathered at King Dinizulu Park. The city also showed the cost of the unrest. Hundreds of refugees and asylum seekers camped outside the Home Affairs office on Che Guevara Road. They said anti-migrant groups had pushed them out and they needed a safer place. In Pietermaritzburg, hundreds of Malawian nationals waited in an old building for buses home. More than 150 buses carried Malawian, Zimbabwean and Zambian migrants to the border at Beitbridge in Musina.

Police arrested people in Hammarsdale, Germiston, Hillbrow and Mapetla. In Hillbrow, two people were shot during a protest, one aged 17. Four people without papers from Mozambique were taken by police in Mapetla. President Cyril Ramaphosa met protest leaders on June 29. He told them protests must be lawful and that the government alone controls migration.

Migration expert Julius Chapunga said the attacks were sad and wrong. He said Africa should work as one and that people fighting over nationality go the wrong way. Robert Mkwezalamba, head of the Human Rights group, said the marches were unacceptable. Reports show about 25,000 people have gone home, most from other African countries including Malawi.

ny.title: Anthu aku Malawi achoka ku South Africa pambuyo pa ziwonetsero zazikulu zotsutsana ndi anthu othawa kwawo
ny.body: South Africa idawona ziwonetsero zazikulu pa June 30 motsutsana ndi nzika zakunja popanda zikalata zoyenera. Anthu zikwizikwi adayenda m'mizinda yambiri. Apolisi adatumiza apolisi 6,000 ku Cape Town ndi ena kumadera ena. Masitolo ena adabedwa. Anthu ambiri aku Malawi adachoka mdzikolo ndi basi. Ziwonetserozi zidachitika magulu atakhazikitsa June 30 ngati tsiku loti anthu osamukira achoke.
ny.body_more:
Ziwonetserozi zinachitika ku Johannesburg, Pretoria, Durban, Soweto, Cape Town ndi mizinda ina. Ku Soweto, kum'mwera kwa Johannesburg, tsikulo linayamba chete koma pambuyo pake linakhala lachiwawa. Nyumba zina zomwe anthu akunja ankakhala zinabedwa. Apolisi anakangana ndi anthu omwe ankachita ziwonetserozi. Ku Alexandra, kumpoto kwa Johannesburg, kuba kunapitirira usiku wonse wa pa 30 June ndipo kunatha pa 1 July m'mawa.

Ku Durban, anthu mazana ambiri anasonkhana ku King Dinizulu Park. Mzindawu unawonetsanso mtengo wa zipolowezo. Anthu mazana ambiri othawa kwawo ndi ofuna chitetezo anamanga msasa kunja kwa ofesi ya Home Affairs pa Che Guevara Road. Anati magulu otsutsa anthu othawa kwawo anawathamangitsa ndipo amafunikira malo otetezeka. Ku Pietermaritzburg, anthu mazana ambiri a ku Malawi anali kuyembekezera m'nyumba yakale kuti mabasi abwerere kwawo. Mabasi oposa 150 ananyamula anthu ochokera ku Malawi, Zimbabwe ndi Zambia kupita kumalire ku Beitbridge ku Musina.

Apolisi anagwira anthu ku Hammarsdale, Germiston, Hillbrow ndi Mapetla. Ku Hillbrow, anthu awiri anawomberedwa panthawi ya ziwonetsero, m'modzi wazaka 17. Anthu anayi opanda mapepala ochokera ku Mozambique anatengedwa ndi apolisi ku Mapetla. Purezidenti Cyril Ramaphosa anakumana ndi atsogoleri a ziwonetsero pa June 29. Anawauza kuti ziwonetsero ziyenera kukhala zovomerezeka ndipo boma lokha ndilo limayang'anira kusamuka.

Katswiri wodziwa za kusamuka Julius Chapunga anati ziwopsezozo zinali zomvetsa chisoni komanso zolakwika. Anati Africa iyenera kugwira ntchito limodzi ndipo anthu okangana chifukwa cha unzika amapita m'njira yolakwika. Robert Mkwezalamba, mtsogoleri wa gulu la Human Rights, anati ziwonetserozo sizinali zovomerezeka. Malipoti akusonyeza kuti anthu pafupifupi 25,000 abwerera kwawo, ambiri ochokera kumayiko ena aku Africa kuphatikizapo Malawi.

en.title: Malawi water target at risk as budget cuts grow
en.body: Minister of Agriculture, Irrigation and Water Development Roza Mbilizi said on Tuesday that budget cuts are making it hard for Malawi to give all people clean water by 2030. She told the Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (Wash) Joint Sector Review in Lilongwe that the sector's budget fell from 3.35 percent to 1.97 percent of the national budget.
en.body_more:
Mbilizi said access to safe water is a basic human right. She shared data from the 2024 Demographic and Health Survey showing that 89 percent of Malawians can reach improved water, but only 73 percent have basic drinking water services. Just 18.7 percent have water that is clean and always available. Only 40 percent of people have basic sanitation.

In health facilities, the situation is worse. About 96 percent lack basic sanitation, 73 percent have no hygiene services, and 24 percent have no water supply. Mbilizi said government is now working to bring piped water systems into rural areas so communities can reach water more easily.

Experts at the review said Malawi can still reach its 2030 targets, but money must come faster. Dr Greenwell Matchaya of the International Water Management Institute said the country needs both government money and help from outside. Malawi needs about $238 million (K416 billion) each year, but gets less than $100 million. Dr Matchaya said coordination between all groups helps use money well and avoids waste.

Unicef country representative Penelope Campbell said the government's Presidential Commitment on Wash and Executive Order No. 2 on sanitation in schools and workplaces are important steps. She said safe water and sanitation are the base for health, learning, work and dignity. The annual Wash Joint Sector Review brings together government, development partners, civil society and district councils to check progress and set priorities.

ny.title: Dziko la Malawi likufuna kuchepetsa ndalama zogulira madzi chifukwa cha kuchepa kwa madzi
ny.body: Nduna ya zaulimi, ulimi wothirira ndi chitukuko cha madzi Roza Mbilizi adati Lachiwiri kuti kuchepetsa bajeti kukupangitsa kuti Malawi ikhale yovuta kupatsa anthu onse madzi oyera pofika chaka cha 2030. Iye adauza Water, Sanitation and Hygiene (Wash) Joint Sector Review ku Lilongwe kuti bajeti ya gawoli yatsika kuchoka pa 3.35 peresenti kufika pa 1.97 peresenti ya bajeti ya dziko lonse.
ny.body_more:
Mbilizi anati kupeza madzi abwino ndi ufulu wa munthu.
