Iye adagawana deta kuchokera ku Kafukufuku wa Anthu ndi Zaumoyo wa 2024 yomwe ikuwonetsa kuti 89 peresenti ya anthu aku Malawi amatha kupeza madzi abwino, koma 73 peresenti yokha ndi omwe ali ndi madzi oyambira akumwa. 18.7 peresenti yokha ndi omwe ali ndi madzi oyera komanso omwe amapezeka nthawi zonse. 40 peresenti yokha ndi omwe ali ndi ukhondo woyambira.

M'zipatala, vutoli ndi loipa kwambiri. Pafupifupi 96 peresenti alibe ukhondo woyambira, 73 peresenti alibe ukhondo, ndipo 24 peresenti alibe madzi. Mbilizi adati boma likugwira ntchito yobweretsa njira zamadzi zapaipi kumidzi kuti madera athe kufikira madzi mosavuta.

Akatswiri pa ndemangayi adati Malawi ikhozabe kukwaniritsa zolinga zake za 2030, koma ndalama ziyenera kubwera mwachangu. Dr Greenwell Matchaya wa International Water Management Institute adati dzikolo likufunika ndalama za boma komanso thandizo kuchokera kunja. Malawi ikufunika pafupifupi $238 miliyoni (K416 biliyoni) chaka chilichonse, koma imapeza ndalama zosakwana $100 miliyoni. Dr Matchaya adati mgwirizano pakati pa magulu onse umathandiza kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama bwino komanso kupewa kuwononga.

Woyimira dziko la Unicef, Penelope Campbell, adati kudzipereka kwa boma kwa Purezidenti pa Wash ndi Executive Order No. 2 pa ukhondo m'masukulu ndi malo ogwirira ntchito ndi njira zofunika kwambiri. Iye anati madzi abwino ndi ukhondo ndiye maziko a thanzi, kuphunzira, ntchito ndi ulemu. Kuwunika kwa pachaka kwa Wash Joint Sector Review kumabweretsa pamodzi boma, ogwira nawo ntchito zachitukuko, mabungwe a anthu wamba ndi makhonsolo a madera kuti aone momwe zinthu zikuyendera ndikukhazikitsa zofunika patsogolo.

en.title: Plane crash widows tell of missing phones and damage
en.body: Wives of nine people who died in a plane crash on June 10, 2024 told Parliament on Monday and Tuesday that some mobile phones are still missing. Mary Chilima, widow of Vice-President Saulos Chilima, said she got her husband's phones one month after the crash. Other widows said the phones were badly broken.
en.body_more:
Mary Chilima told Parliament's Ad-hoc Committee at the Parliament Building in Lilongwe that she waited one month to get two of her husband's three phones back. The phones were broken. She said keeping them so long made her think someone may have damaged them on purpose. "Why were they holding on to them? We were only given two phones after a month. We are still missing one," she said.

Sarah Lapukeni, wife of Abdul Lapukeni who worked in the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, said her husband's phone looked like it had been crushed in a mortar. The wires were pulled out and the SIM card was twisted so much it could not be straightened. But his passport came back in good shape. Effiness Katunga, wife of Dr Dan Kanyemba who was Chilima's doctor, said her husband's phone could not be fixed at all. She tried to call him at about 4pm on the day of the crash and his phone rang, but he did not answer. On the second try, the phone was already off.

Taona Aidin, wife of aircraft engineer Major Wales Aidin, said only one of her husband's two phones was found. He sent her a WhatsApp message at 9.42 am saying "enroute to Mzuzu". She wrote back one minute later, but got no reply. His phone still showed he was online until 10.16am, the time the crash happened.

Other families also gave evidence. Martha Kapheni, wife of Lucas Kapheni the Guard Commander, said she first heard about the crash from her brother-in-law while she was at the market. Charity Chimaneni, wife of Aide-de-Camp Chisomo Chimaneni, and Mabvuto Ngwilinji, the widower of co-pilot Flora Selemani, both asked the committee to do another autopsy. They said families never got copies of the first autopsy reports and want to know more about how their loved ones died. The committee is the third to look into the crash.

ny.title: Akazi amasiye a ngozi ya ndege akufotokoza za mafoni omwe akusowa ndi kuwonongeka
ny.body: Akazi a anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndege pa June 10, 2024 adauza Nyumba Yamalamulo Lolemba ndi Lachiwiri kuti mafoni ena akusowabe. Mary Chilima, mkazi wamasiye wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima, adati adalandira mafoni a mwamuna wake mwezi umodzi pambuyo pa ngoziyo. Amasiye ena adati mafoniwo anali osweka kwambiri.
ny.body_more:
Mary Chilima adauza Komiti Yoyang'anira Nyumba Yamalamulo ku Nyumba Yamalamulo ku Lilongwe kuti adadikira mwezi umodzi kuti apeze mafoni awiri mwa atatu a mwamuna wake. Mafoniwo anali atasweka. Iye adati kuwasunga kwa nthawi yayitali kunamupangitsa kuganiza kuti winawake adawawononga dala. "N'chifukwa chiyani ankawagwira? Tinapatsidwa mafoni awiri okha patatha mwezi umodzi. Tikusowabe imodzi," adatero.

Sarah Lapukeni, mkazi wa Abdul Lapukeni yemwe ankagwira ntchito ku Unduna wa Zachilendo, adati foni ya mwamuna wake imawoneka ngati yaphwanyidwa mu mortar. Mawaya adatulutsidwa ndipo SIM khadi idapindika kwambiri kotero kuti singathe kuwongoledwa. Koma pasipoti yake idabwerera bwino. Effiness Katunga, mkazi wa Dr. Dan Kanyemba yemwe anali dokotala wa Chilima, adati foni ya mwamuna wake singathe kukonzedwa konse. Anayesa kumuyimbira foni pafupifupi 4pm tsiku lomwe ngoziyo idachitika ndipo foni yake idalira, koma sanayankhe. Pa kuyesa kwachiwiri, foniyo inali itazimitsidwa kale.

Taona Aidin, mkazi wa injiniya wa ndege Major Wales Aidin, anati foni imodzi yokha mwa ziwiri za mwamuna wake ndi yomwe yapezeka. Anamutumizira uthenga wa WhatsApp nthawi ya 9:42 koloko m'mawa akunena kuti "ali paulendo wopita ku Mzuzu". Anamulemberanso uthenga patatha mphindi imodzi, koma sanayankhe. Foni yake inasonyezabe kuti anali pa intaneti mpaka 10:16 koloko m'mawa, nthawi yomwe ngoziyo inachitikira.

Mabanja ena nawonso anapereka umboni. Martha Kapheni, mkazi wa Lucas Kapheni, Mtsogoleri wa Alonda, anati anayamba kumva za ngoziyi kuchokera kwa mlamu wake pamene anali pamsika. Charity Chimaneni, mkazi wa Aide-de-Camp Chisomo Chimaneni, ndi Mabvuto Ngwilinji, mkazi wamasiye wa woyendetsa ndege mnzake Flora Selemani, onse anapempha komitiyi kuti ipange kafukufuku wina wa imfa. Anati mabanja sanalandire makope a malipoti oyamba a kafukufuku wa imfa ndipo akufuna kudziwa zambiri za momwe okondedwa awo anamwalira. Komitiyi ndi yachitatu kufufuza za ngoziyi.

en.title: Committee hears how funeral plan led to plane trip
en.body: Malawi's Ad Hoc Committee is probing the June 2024 military plane crash that killed Vice President Saulos Chilima. Former Clerk to the Cabinet Samson Ngutwa told MPs that Chilima's attendance at Ralph Kasambara's funeral required a Malawi Defence Force aircraft. The funeral was planned for 10 June in Nkhata Bay.
en.body_more:
President Lazarus Chakwera first asked Local Government Minister Richard Chimwendo Banda to represent him at Kasambara's funeral. But on the evening of 9 June, Secretary to the President Colleen Zamba told Ngutwa that Chilima might attend instead. Zamba said that if the Vice President went, the Cabinet minister would not need to go.

Ngutwa said he then asked officials in the Vice President's office if Chilima would attend. They told him that Chilima would only travel if a Malawi Defence Force aircraft could be found. The aircraft was confirmed available. Money that had been prepared to support Chimwendo Banda's delegation was then redirected to the Vice President's office. By the morning of 10 June, the aircraft and the money were ready.

The committee also heard that the government had earlier approved condolence funds and a casket for Kasambara's widow. Officials had debated whether Kasambara, who had a criminal conviction, should receive military honours. They decided against it. Ngutwa's testimony shows that the funeral arrangements changed quickly. What began as a simple representation by a Cabinet minister became a trip by the Vice President. Hours after the funeral, the aircraft carrying Chilima and eight others disappeared while flying home.

ny.title: Komiti ikumva momwe dongosolo la maliro linathandizira paulendo wa pandege
ny.body: Komiti Yoyang'anira Zachilendo ku Malawi ikufufuza za ngozi ya ndege yankhondo ya mu June 2024 yomwe idapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima. Samson Ngutwa, yemwe kale anali Kalaliki wa Cabinet, adauza aphungu a Nyumba Yamalamulo kuti kupezeka kwa Chilima pa maliro a Ralph Kasambara kumafuna ndege ya Malawi Defence Force. Malirowo adakonzedwa pa 10 June ku Nkhata Bay.
ny.body_more:
Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera adapempha koyamba Nduna ya Boma la M'deralo Richard Chimwendo Banda kuti amuyimire pa maliro a Kasambara. Koma madzulo a pa 9 June, Mlembi wa Purezidenti Colleen Zamba adauza Ngutwa kuti Chilima akhoza kupezekapo m'malo mwake. Zamba adati ngati Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti apita, nduna ya Cabinet sidzafunika kupita.

Ngutwa adati kenako adafunsa akuluakulu a ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti ngati Chilima apitapo. Adamuuza kuti Chilima ayenda pokhapokha ngati ndege ya Malawi Defense Force ipezeka. Ndegeyo idatsimikizika kuti ilipo. Ndalama zomwe zidakonzedwa kuti zithandizire gulu la Chimwendo Banda zidatumizidwa ku ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti. Pofika m'mawa wa pa 10 June, ndegeyo ndi ndalamazo zinali zitakonzeka.

Komitiyi idamvanso kuti boma lidavomereza kale ndalama zopepesera komanso bokosi la maliro la mkazi wamasiye wa Kasambara. Akuluakulu adakambirana ngati Kasambara, yemwe adapezeka ndi mlandu wolakwa, ayenera kulandira ulemu wankhondo. Adasankha kukana. Umboni wa Ngutwa ukuwonetsa kuti mapulani a maliro adasintha mwachangu. Chimene chinayamba ngati chiyimiriro chosavuta cha nduna chinakhala ulendo wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti. Patatha maola angapo maliro atachitika, ndege yomwe inanyamula Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu inasowa pamene inali kubwerera kwawo.

en.title: Inquiry hears about Chilima's last words and plane search
en.body: A lawyer told the parliament committee that Vice President Saulos Chilima's famous last words were misunderstood. Kelvin Sentala said Chilima did not mean he would die soon. The words were from a normal talk, not a warning. The committee is still looking at the plane crash from June 10, 2024, which killed Chilima and eight other people.
en.body_more:
Sentala told MPs that Chilima's statement — 'If I die, I will die' — came from a talk about death in general. The two men were speaking after former Attorney General Ralph Kasambara died. Sentala asked Chilima if he thought about death often. Chilima then gave the answer that people now know about. Sentala said the real words in the local language were 'Alipo amene sadzafa?' which means 'Is there anyone who will not die?' He told the committee this was a thought about life, not fear or bad dreams about what would come.

Sentala also spoke about the search for the plane. He said the search was not well organised. Men used phone lights to look for the plane. They asked people in villages if they saw it fall. There were fewer than 20 police officers and some army men. Sentala said those who searched did not lead — they just followed orders. At 9:30 PM that night, a police officer told them to stop. Sentala said the search was in the hands of the government, and when they were told to stop, they had to obey.

On the day before the crash, Sentala saw Chilima at his home. They spoke about church, a trip to South Korea and South Africa, and the funeral for Kasambara the next day. Sentala said Chilima seemed normal and calm. He never heard Chilima speak much about death before, and he saw no sign that something bad would happen.

ny.title: Kafukufuku wamva za mawu omaliza a Chilima ndi kusaka ndege
ny.body: Loya wina anauza komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo kuti mawu omaliza otchuka a Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima sanamveke bwino. Kelvin Sentala anati Chilima sakutanthauza kuti amwalira posachedwa. Mawuwo anali ochokera ku nkhani wamba, osati chenjezo. Komitiyi ikuyang'anabe ngozi ya ndege kuyambira pa 10 June, 2024, yomwe inapha Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu.
ny.body_more:
Sentala anauza aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo kuti mawu a Chilima — 'Ngati ndifa, ndidzafa' — adachokera ku nkhani yokhudza imfa nthawi zambiri. Amuna awiriwa ankalankhula pambuyo poti Ralph Kasambara yemwe kale anali Loya Wamkulu wa boma anamwalira. Sentala anafunsa Chilima ngati amaganizira za imfa nthawi zambiri. Kenako Chilima anapereka yankho lomwe anthu tsopano akulidziwa. Sentala anati mawu enieni m'chinenero cha komweko anali 'Alipo amene sadzafa?' kutanthauza kuti 'Kodi pali wina amene sadzafa?' Anauza komitiyo kuti izi zinali lingaliro lonena za moyo, osati mantha kapena maloto oipa okhudza zomwe zingachitike.

Sentala analankhulanso za kusaka ndegeyo. Anati kusaka ndegeyo sikunakonzedwe bwino. Amuna anagwiritsa ntchito magetsi a foni kufunafuna ndegeyo. Anafunsa anthu m'midzi ngati anaiona ikugwa. Panali apolisi osakwana 20 ndi asilikali ena. Sentala anati omwe anasaka sanatsogolere — anangotsatira malamulo. Nthawi ya 9:30 PM usiku womwewo, apolisi anawauza kuti asiye. Sentala anati kusaka kunali m'manja mwa boma, ndipo atauzidwa kuti asiye, anayenera kumvera.

Tsiku lisanafike ngoziyi, Sentala adawona Chilima kunyumba kwake. Anakambirana za tchalitchi, ulendo wopita ku South Korea ndi South Africa, komanso maliro a Kasambara tsiku lotsatira. Sentala adati Chilima ankaoneka wabwinobwino komanso wodekha. Sanamvepo Chilima akulankhula zambiri za imfa kale, ndipo sanaone chizindikiro chilichonse choti chinachake choipa chingachitike.

en.title: Bus crash kills driver, injures 11 Malawians in South Africa
en.body: A bus carrying Malawian nationals crashed near Musina in South Africa's Limpopo Province. The driver died at the scene. Eleven passengers were injured and taken to hospital. The bus was travelling from KwaZulu-Natal to a repatriation centre in Musina.
en.body_more:
The crash happened as the bus made its way to the Musina Reception Centre, where undocumented foreign nationals are being processed before being sent back to their home countries. According to the South African Broadcasting Corporation (SABC), the driver lost control of the vehicle. Early reports say fatigue caused the crash, making the bus overturn.

Emergency services arrived quickly at the scene and gave first aid to the injured passengers. The wounded people were then taken to nearby hospitals for medical care. South African authorities have not yet given the names and nationalities of all those who were hurt, but passengers are believed to have been Malawian nationals. Police have started an investigation into what caused the crash.

Hundreds of Malawians have gathered at the Musina facility in recent weeks while waiting to go home. Many have left South Africa because of violent attacks and threats against foreign nationals in parts of the country. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs in Lilongwe is expected to work with South African officials to check who was injured and to give help to Malawians.

The crash brings attention to the dangers that undocumented migrants face. Many travel in crowded and poorly looked-after vehicles on long and hard journeys across South Africa. The incident has raised questions about migration rules, border control, and the safety of migrants in both countries.

ny.title: Ngozi ya basi yapha dalaivala, kuvulaza anthu 11 aku Malawi ku South Africa
ny.body: Basi yomwe inali ndi nzika za ku Malawi inagwa pafupi ndi Musina m'chigawo cha Limpopo ku South Africa. Dalaivala anamwalira pamalopo. Anthu khumi ndi mmodzi anavulala ndipo anatengedwera kuchipatala. Basiyo inali paulendo wochokera ku KwaZulu-Natal kupita ku malo osungira anthu obwerera kwawo ku Musina.
ny.body_more:
Ngoziyi idachitika pamene basiyo inkapita ku Musina Reception Centre, komwe anthu ochokera kumayiko ena osavomerezeka akukonzedwa asanabwezeretsedwe kumayiko awo. Malinga ndi bungwe la South African Broadcasting Corporation (SABC), dalaivala adataya mphamvu yoyendetsa galimotoyo. Malipoti oyambirira akuti kutopa ndi komwe kudayambitsa ngoziyi, zomwe zidapangitsa kuti basiyo igwe.

Anthu ovulala adafika mwachangu pamalopo ndipo adapereka chithandizo choyamba kwa okwera omwe adavulala. Kenako anthu ovulalawo adatengedwa kupita kuzipatala zapafupi kuti akalandire chithandizo chamankhwala. Akuluakulu aku South Africa sanapereke mayina ndi mayiko a onse omwe adavulala, koma okwerawo akukhulupirira kuti anali nzika za ku Malawi. Apolisi ayamba kufufuza chomwe chidayambitsa ngoziyi.

Anthu ambiri aku Malawi asonkhana ku malo olandirira alendo ku Musina m'masabata apitawa akudikira kubwerera kwawo. Ambiri achoka ku South Africa chifukwa cha ziwawa zankhanza komanso ziwopsezo kwa anthu ochokera kumayiko ena m'madera ena a dzikolo. Unduna wa Zachilendo ku Lilongwe ukuyembekezeka kugwira ntchito ndi akuluakulu aku South Africa kuti awone omwe adavulala ndikuthandiza anthu aku Malawi.

Ngoziyi ikudziwitsa zoopsa zomwe anthu osaloledwa omwe akukumana nazo. Ambiri amayenda m'magalimoto odzaza ndi anthu komanso osasamalidwa bwino paulendo wautali komanso wovuta kudutsa ku South Africa. Nkhaniyi yabweretsa mafunso okhudza malamulo osamukira kumayiko ena, kuwongolera malire, komanso chitetezo cha anthu osamukira kumayiko ena m'maiko onse awiri.

en.title: Malawi universities double fees; private schools may follow
en.body: Malawi's public universities have doubled tuition fees for most students from K650,000 to K1.3 million. Luanar and Mzuzu University have confirmed the new fees. Other universities are expected to announce similar increases. The move comes as students face rising living costs and universities struggle to pay for basic operations.
en.body_more:
Universities say they need more money to run. They face old buildings, high electricity and water bills, and government money that does not keep up with inflation. For many families, K1.3 million is too much to pay. Malawi already has few students in higher education, and higher fees may push even fewer people to apply and enrol.

Student unions have warned that fee increases will make inequality worse. Poorer students may drop out or never start university at all. The Ministry of Education has not yet given a full explanation of the policy. The problem is well-known: universities need more money, students cannot pay more, and the government does not have extra cash to give.

The fee changes are now affecting private schools too. The Private Schools Association of Malawi (PRISAM) says it is reviewing fees at its member schools. PRISAM President Ernest Karonga said private schools face the same cost problems as public universities — electricity, water, teaching materials, and maintenance all cost more. He said private schools must keep good teachers, and up to 70% of any fee increase goes to staff pay. When public universities raise fees, private schools feel pressure to do the same, or they lose teachers to better jobs. Karonga said no private school fee increase has been made yet, but it is now being discussed. He said the university fee increase was the trigger for private schools to look at their own charges.

ny.title: Mayunivesite aku Malawi amalipiritsa kawiri; masukulu achinsinsi akhoza kutsatira
ny.body: Mayunivesite aboma ku Malawi achulukitsa kawiri ndalama zolipirira maphunziro a ophunzira ambiri kuchoka pa K650,000 kufika pa K1.3 miliyoni. Luanar ndi Mzuzu University atsimikiza kuti ndalama zatsopanozi zakwera. Mayunivesite ena akuyembekezeka kulengeza kukwera kofananako. Izi zikuchitika pamene ophunzira akukumana ndi kukwera kwa mitengo ya zinthu zofunika pa moyo komanso mayunivesite akuvutika kulipira zinthu zofunika pa moyo.
ny.body_more:
Mayunivesite akuti akufunika ndalama zambiri kuti ayendetse. Akukumana ndi nyumba zakale, ma bilu okwera magetsi ndi madzi, komanso ndalama za boma zomwe sizikugwirizana ndi kukwera kwa mitengo. Kwa mabanja ambiri, K1.3 miliyoni ndi ndalama zambiri zoti apereke. Malawi ili kale ndi ophunzira ochepa m'maphunziro apamwamba, ndipo ndalama zokwera zingakakamize anthu ochepa kuti alembetse ndikulembetsa.

Mabungwe a ophunzira achenjeza kuti kukwera kwa ndalama kudzapangitsa kuti kusalingana kukhale koipa kwambiri. Ophunzira osauka angasiye sukulu kapena asayambe konse yunivesite. Unduna wa Maphunziro sunapereke kufotokozera kwathunthu za mfundozi. Vutoli ndi lodziwika bwino: mayunivesite amafunika ndalama zambiri, ophunzira sangathe kulipira zambiri, ndipo boma lilibe ndalama zowonjezera zoti lipereke.

Kusintha kwa ndalama tsopano kukukhudzanso masukulu achinsinsi. Bungwe la Private Schools Association of Malawi (PRISAM) likunena kuti likuwunikanso ndalama m'masukulu omwe ali mamembala ake. Purezidenti wa PRISAM Ernest Karonga adati masukulu achinsinsi akukumana ndi mavuto ofanana ndi mayunivesite aboma - magetsi, madzi, zida zophunzitsira, ndi kukonza zonse zimawononga ndalama zambiri. Anati masukulu achinsinsi ayenera kukhala ndi aphunzitsi abwino, ndipo mpaka 70% ya ndalama zomwe zikukwera zimapita ku malipiro a antchito. Mayunivesite aboma akakweza ndalama zolipirira sukulu, masukulu achinsinsi amakakamizidwa kuchita chimodzimodzi, kapena amataya aphunzitsi chifukwa cha ntchito zabwino. Karonga adati palibe kukweza ndalama zolipirira sukulu zachinsinsi komwe kwachitika, koma tsopano zikukambidwa. Iye anati kukweza ndalama zolipirira sukulu zachinsinsi ndiko kunayambitsa masukulu achinsinsi kuti aziyang'ana ndalama zawo.

en.title: Ombudsman: Immigration Department Sold to Highest Bidder
en.body: The Office of the Ombudsman says Malawi's Department of Immigration and Citizenship Services is deeply corrupt. Ombudsman Grace Malera gave a report in Lilongwe on Tuesday. She said bribes, poor leaders and political interference have broken the department. People who pay money get fast service. Ordinary people wait long.
en.body_more:
The Ombudsman found that immigration officers and middlemen called "dobadobas" take extra money from people who need passports and travel papers. These agents demand bribes to speed up work that should be free and fair. The report says the department has become a business where money and connections decide who gets help first.

The investigation also found weak leadership, old laws, not enough workers, and broken digital systems in the department. Border control is poor. These problems have put the country's safety at risk, the Ombudsman warned. The report also looked at problems with two companies: Techno Brain FZE and Hitek Systems. Bad contracts with these firms stopped passport printing work for months. Thousands of Malawians faced long delays and extra costs because passports could not be made.

The Ombudsman said the department has failed to do its job and protect citizens from abuse and unfair treatment. Director General Dennis Chapawo said the department agrees with the findings. He said the department will make the changes that the Ombudsman asked for. The report now puts heavy pressure on the government to stop corruption in immigration services and bring back honest work.

ny.title: Ombudsman: Dipatimenti Yoona za Anthu Osamukira Kumayiko Ena Yagulitsidwa kwa Wogulitsa Kwambiri
ny.body: Ofesi ya Ombudsman yati Dipatimenti Yoona za Anthu Osamukira ku Malawi ndi yachinyengo kwambiri. Ombudsman Grace Malera adapereka lipoti ku Lilongwe Lachiwiri. Iye adati ziphuphu, atsogoleri osauka komanso kulowererapo pandale kwasokoneza dipatimentiyi. Anthu omwe amalipira ndalama amapeza chithandizo mwachangu. Anthu wamba amadikira nthawi yayitali.
ny.body_more:
Ombudsman adapeza kuti akuluakulu oyendayenda ndi apakati otchedwa "dobadobas" amatenga ndalama zowonjezera kwa anthu omwe amafunikira mapasipoti ndi mapepala oyendera. Othandizira awa amafuna ziphuphu kuti afulumizitse ntchito yomwe iyenera kukhala yaulere komanso yolungama. Lipotilo likuti dipatimentiyi yakhala bizinesi pomwe ndalama ndi kulumikizana zimasankha omwe amalandira thandizo choyamba.

Kafukufukuyu adapezanso utsogoleri wofooka, malamulo akale, antchito osakwanira, komanso makina a digito osweka mu dipatimentiyi. Kulamulira malire ndi koipa. Mavutowa aika chitetezo cha dziko pachiwopsezo, Ombudsman adachenjeza. Lipotilo linayang'ananso mavuto omwe ali ndi makampani awiri: Techno Brain FZE ndi Hitek Systems. Mapangano oipa ndi makampaniwa adayimitsa ntchito yosindikiza mapasipoti kwa miyezi ingapo. Anthu masauzande ambiri aku Malawi adakumana ndi kuchedwa kwa nthawi yayitali komanso ndalama zowonjezera chifukwa mapasipoti sangapangidwe.

Ombudsman adati dipatimentiyi yalephera kuchita ntchito yake ndikuteteza nzika ku nkhanza ndi nkhanza zosalungama. Mtsogoleri Wamkulu Dennis Chapawo adati dipatimentiyi ikugwirizana ndi zomwe zapezeka. Anati dipatimentiyi ipanga kusintha komwe Ombudsman adapempha. Lipotilo tsopano likukakamiza boma kuti lisiye ziphuphu mu ntchito zosamukira ndikubwezeretsa ntchito yowona mtima.

en.title: Two big companies in Malawi make more money in 2025
en.body: Sunbird Tourism and MPICO plc both made large profits in 2025. Sunbird made K12.5 billion after tax, up 17% from 2024. MPICO made K19.32 billion after tax, up 59% from 2024. Both companies told their shareholders at meetings that business was strong.
en.body_more:
Sunbird Tourism, which runs hotels across Malawi, saw revenue jump 31% to K71.7 billion. The company is listed on the Malawi Stock Exchange. At the Annual General Meeting in Blantyre, chairperson Luciano Mickeus said the strong results came from cost control and new strategies. The catering division did very well, with more demand for conferences and events. The board gave shareholders a dividend of K3.75 billion, or K14.34 per share.

Sunbird's share price rose sharply during the year. The price climbed 307%, from K240.08 at the end of 2024 to K977.50 by December 2025. The company spent K21 billion on building new rooms and upgrading facilities. Work was finished at Sunbird Mzuzu, Sunbird Livingstonia, Sunbird Nkopola, and Sunbird Capital. More work at Livingstonia, Nkopola, and Sunbird Lilongwe will finish this year.

MPICO plc, a property company, recorded a 59% rise in profit to K19.32 billion. Revenue grew 44%, and rental income rose 16% to K9.31 billion. At the Annual General Meeting in Lilongwe, Board Chairperson Edmund Hami said the results were strong despite hard economic times. Fair value gains on properties rose 63% to K19.63 billion. However, operating costs climbed 20% to K8.07 billion because of inflation.

A big problem for MPICO is government rental arrears. The amount owed more than doubled to K7.24 billion from K3.4 billion in 2024. Hami said delays affect cash flow and project work. However, he said talks with government were improving. MPICO declared a dividend of 50 tambala per share, the first dividend in two years. Shareholder Charles Banda said he was happy to receive money again.

ny.title: Makampani awiri akuluakulu ku Malawi amapeza ndalama zambiri mu 2025
ny.body: Sunbird Tourism ndi MPICO plc zonse zinapanga phindu lalikulu mu 2025. Sunbird inapanga K12.5 biliyoni pambuyo pa msonkho, kukwera ndi 17% kuchokera mu 2024. MPICO inapanga K19.32 biliyoni pambuyo pa msonkho, kukwera ndi 59% kuchokera mu 2024. Makampani onsewa adauza omwe ali ndi magawo awo pamisonkhano kuti bizinesi inali yolimba.
ny.body_more:
Sunbird Tourism, yomwe imayendetsa mahotela ku Malawi konse, yawona ndalama zomwe zapezeka zikukwera ndi 31% kufika pa K71.7 biliyoni. Kampaniyo yalembedwa pa Malawi Stock Exchange. Pa Msonkhano Wapachaka ku Blantyre, wapampando Luciano Mickeus adati zotsatira zake zabwino zachokera ku kuwongolera ndalama ndi njira zatsopano. Gawo loperekera zakudya lidachita bwino kwambiri, ndipo kufunikira kwakukulu kwa misonkhano ndi zochitika. Bungweli lidapatsa eni ake gawo gawo la K3.75 biliyoni, kapena K14.34 pa gawo lililonse.

Mtengo wa magawo a Sunbird udakwera kwambiri chaka chino. Mtengo udakwera ndi 307%, kuchokera pa K240.08 kumapeto kwa 2024 kufika pa K977.50 pofika Disembala 2025. Kampaniyo idagwiritsa ntchito K21 biliyoni kumanga zipinda zatsopano ndi malo okonzanso. Ntchito idamalizidwa ku Sunbird Mzuzu, Sunbird Livingstonia, Sunbird Nkopola, ndi Sunbird Capital. Ntchito yowonjezereka ku Livingstonia, Nkopola, ndi Sunbird Lilongwe idzatha chaka chino.

Kampani ya MPICO plc, yomwe imapanga nyumba, yapeza phindu lokwera ndi 59% kufika pa K19.32 biliyoni. Ndalama zomwe zapezeka zakwera ndi 44%, ndipo ndalama zomwe zapezeka pa lendi zakwera ndi 16% kufika pa K9.31 biliyoni. Pa Msonkhano Wapachaka ku Lilongwe, Wapampando wa Bungwe Edmund Hami adati zotsatira zake zinali zabwino ngakhale kuti chuma chinali chovuta. Kukwera kwa mtengo wa nyumba kwakwera ndi 63% kufika pa K19.63 biliyoni. Komabe, ndalama zogwirira ntchito zakwera ndi 20% kufika pa K8.07 biliyoni chifukwa cha kukwera kwa mitengo.

Vuto lalikulu la MPICO ndi ngongole za lendi za boma. Ndalama zomwe zili ndi ngongolezo zakwera kuwirikiza kawiri kufika pa K7.24 biliyoni kuchokera pa K3.4 biliyoni mu 2024. Hami adati kuchedwa kumakhudza kayendedwe ka ndalama ndi ntchito za polojekiti. Komabe, adati zokambirana ndi boma zikuyenda bwino. MPICO yalengeza kuti yapeza gawo la 50 tambala pa gawo lililonse, gawo loyamba m'zaka ziwiri. Wogawana nawo masheya Charles Banda adati akusangalala kulandiranso ndalama.

en.title: Escom Spends K11 Billion to Fix Power Lines
en.body: Escom, the electricity company of Malawi, is spending US$6.3 million (about K11 billion) to replace old wooden poles with new concrete poles. The work covers two main power lines: the 66-kilovolt Mapanga–Fundis Cross line and the 132-kilovolt Nkula B–Phombeya No. 1 line. Escom Chief Public Relations Officer Pilirani Phiri said the new poles will make the electricity supply more reliable and cut costs.
en.body_more:
The concrete poles are much stronger than wooden poles and can last more than 80 years without needing replacement. Wooden poles break down quickly—some need to be replaced after just five years. The new poles are also harder to damage or steal, which helps fight vandalism. Escom says vandalism costs the company about K3 billion every year.

The Mapanga–Fundis Cross line serves the tea-growing areas of Thyolo, Mulanje and Phalombe. The Nkula B–Phombeya No. 1 line carries power from the Nkula hydroelectric station to the national grid. Engineer Isaac Kunkeyani from the project's consultant, Infrastructure Project Planners, said wooden poles can break due to wildfires, termites, people cutting them, and age. The concrete poles are much stronger.

Work started last month. The contractor Sawa Group has already put up 38 of the 304 poles needed on the Mapanga–Fundis line. James Munyui from Sawa Group said they plan to finish the work by December. The Mapanga–Fundis Cross project costs about US$3.3 million, while the Nkula B–Phombeya No. 1 project costs about US$3 million.

The project will also create a new concrete pole factory in Malawi—the first of its kind. This factory will make about 200 jobs and cut the need to buy poles from other countries. Escom plans to use concrete poles on other power lines later, starting with the Nkhotakota–Nkhata Bay line.

ny.title: Escom Yagwiritsa Ntchito K11 Biliyoni Kukonza Mawaya a Magetsi
ny.body: Kampani ya Escom, yomwe ndi kampani yamagetsi ku Malawi, ikugwiritsa ntchito ndalama zokwana US$6.3 miliyoni (pafupifupi K11 biliyoni) kusintha mizati yakale yamatabwa ndi mizati yatsopano ya konkire. Ntchitoyi ikukhudza mizere iwiri ikuluikulu yamagetsi: mzere wa Mapanga–Fundis Cross wa makilovolti 66 ndi mzere wa Nkula B–Phombeya wa makilovolti 132. Mkulu wa Escom wolumikizana ndi anthu, Pilirani Phiri, adati mizati yatsopanoyi ipangitsa kuti magetsi azigwiritsidwa ntchito bwino komanso kuchepetsa ndalama.
ny.body_more:
Mizati ya konkire ndi yolimba kwambiri kuposa mizati yamatabwa ndipo imatha kukhala zaka zoposa 80 popanda kufunikira kusinthidwa. Mizati yamatabwa imasweka mwachangu—zina zimafunika kusinthidwa patatha zaka zisanu zokha. Mizati yatsopanoyi ndi yovutanso kuiwononga kapena kuba, zomwe zimathandiza kulimbana ndi kuwononga. Escom imati kuwononga kumawononga kampaniyo pafupifupi K3 biliyoni chaka chilichonse.

Mzere wa Mapanga–Fundis Cross umatumikira madera omwe amalima tiyi ku Thyolo, Mulanje ndi Phalombe. Mzere wa Nkula B–Phombeya No. 1 umanyamula magetsi kuchokera ku siteshoni yamagetsi ya Nkula kupita ku gridi ya dziko lonse. Mainjiniya Isaac Kunkeyani wochokera kwa mlangizi wa polojekitiyi, Infrastructure Project Planners, adati mizati yamatabwa imatha kusweka chifukwa cha moto wamtchire, chiswe, anthu oidula, komanso kukalamba. Mizati ya konkire ndi yolimba kwambiri.

Ntchito inayamba mwezi watha. Kontrakitala Sawa Group wayika kale mizati 38 mwa 304 yomwe ikufunika pa mzere wa Mapanga–Fundis. James Munyui wochokera ku Sawa Group adati akukonzekera kumaliza ntchitoyi pofika Disembala. Ntchito ya Mapanga–Fundis Cross imawononga ndalama zokwana US$3.3 miliyoni, pomwe ntchito ya Nkula B–Phombeya No. 1 imawononga ndalama zokwana US$3 miliyoni.

Ntchitoyi ipanganso fakitale yatsopano ya ndodo za konkire ku Malawi—yoyamba ya mtundu wake. Fakitale iyi ipanga ntchito pafupifupi 200 ndikuchepetsa kufunikira kogula ndodo kuchokera kumayiko ena. Escom ikukonzekera kugwiritsa ntchito ndodo za konkire pazingwe zina zamagetsi pambuyo pake, kuyambira ndi mzere wa Nkhotakota–Nkhata Bay.

en.title: Court says Mulli can do business with government again
en.body: A court ruled two weeks ago that a government ban on Leston Mulli's companies was wrong. The ban stopped all government offices from doing business with Mulli-linked firms. Judge Muhome said the ban was done with bad intent and threw it out. Now Mulli can trade with the government again.
en.body_more:
The ban came from a choice by the late Ralph Kasamba, who was minister for constitutional affairs and attorney general. He told all government agencies to stop working with Mulli companies. The court found this was unlawful and driven by malice.

The ban hurt Mulli's business badly. Afreximbank took back a 30 million US dollar loan that was meant to help CELOCOM, a mobile phone company owned by Mulli. With the court win, Mulli can now work with the government and banks again.

Former finance minister Ken Lipenga wrote on Facebook to praise the court ruling. He said Mulli started small, selling farm goods along trading routes in Nyezerera, Chiringa and Limbuli. Lipenga said the case shows Malawi needs its own business people to grow the economy. He wrote: "Our long-term prosperity cannot depend mainly on aid from other countries or chances beyond our borders. It must be built by those who invest, make new things, and make jobs here at home." Lipenga said the loss of foreign aid and hard times for Malawian workers in South Africa prove this lesson.

Former trade minister Eunice Kazembe also spoke well of Mulli. She said he has helped the country's economy for a long time. Today, Mulli's MBL Holdings runs more than 23 companies. The court ruling gives Mulli and his businesses a major win after years of worry.

ny.title: Khoti Lati Mulli Akhozanso Kuchita Bizinesi ndi Boma
ny.body: Khoti linagamula milungu iwiri yapitayo kuti kuletsa kwa boma makampani a Leston Mulli kunali kolakwika. Kuletsako kunaletsa maofesi onse aboma kuchita bizinesi ndi makampani ogwirizana ndi Mulli. Woweruza Muhome anati kuletsako kunachitika ndi cholinga choipa ndipo anakuchotsa. Tsopano Mulli akhoza kuchita malonda ndi boma kachiwiri.
ny.body_more:
Kuletsa kumeneku kunachokera pa chisankho cha malemu Ralph Kasamba, yemwe anali nduna ya zamalamulo komanso loya wamkulu. Anauza mabungwe onse aboma kuti asiye kugwira ntchito ndi makampani a Mulli. Khotilo linapeza kuti izi sizinali zololedwa ndipo zinkachitika chifukwa cha nkhanza.

Kuletsa kumeneku kunavulaza kwambiri bizinesi ya Mulli. Afreximbank idabweza ngongole ya madola 30 miliyoni aku US yomwe cholinga chake chinali kuthandiza CELOCOM, kampani ya mafoni ya Mulli. Khotilo litapambana, Mulli tsopano akhoza kugwiranso ntchito ndi boma ndi mabanki.

Nduna yakale ya zachuma Ken Lipenga adalemba pa Facebook kuti ayamikire chigamulo cha khotilo. Anati Mulli adayamba kugulitsa zinthu zazing'ono m'misewu yogulitsira ku Nyezerera, Chiringa ndi Limbuli. Lipenga adati mlanduwu ukuwonetsa kuti Malawi ikufunika amalonda ake kuti ikule chuma. Analemba kuti: "Kutukuka kwathu kwa nthawi yayitali sikungadalire kwambiri thandizo lochokera kumayiko ena kapena mwayi wopitilira malire athu. Kuyenera kumangidwa ndi omwe amaika ndalama, kupanga zinthu zatsopano, ndikupeza ntchito kuno." Lipenga adati kutayika kwa thandizo lakunja ndi nthawi zovuta kwa ogwira ntchito aku Malawi ku South Africa kumatsimikizira phunziroli.

Nduna yakale ya zamalonda Eunice Kazembe nayenso analankhula bwino za Mulli. Iye anati wakhala akuthandiza chuma cha dzikolo kwa nthawi yayitali. Masiku ano, kampani ya Mulli ya MBL Holdings ikuyendetsa makampani oposa 23. Chigamulo cha khoti chapatsa Mulli ndi mabizinesi ake chipambano chachikulu pambuyo pa zaka zambiri akuda nkhawa.

en.title: MSCE exams start well, but fewer students sit test
en.body: Malawi School Certificate of Education (MSCE) exams started on Monday with no major problems at most centres. A total of 197,502 candidates are sitting the exams this year, down from 202,940 last year. The tests run until 19 July and cover all regions of Malawi.
en.body_more:
Minister of Education, Science and Technology Bright Msaka said the exams had started well across all education divisions. After visiting centres in Mzuzu, he said there were no cases of cheating or disruptions on the first day. He urged all supervisors, examiners and security staff to work with care and honesty.

The drop in candidate numbers has to do with fewer students doing open and distance learning courses, Msaka said. He explained that regular secondary schools have seen more students join, but numbers in distance learning programmes go up and down each year. The free secondary education plan brought more internal candidates to sit the tests, he added.

At centres visited in Lilongwe and Blantyre, candidates were in place on time and papers began without trouble. At Likuni Boys Secondary School, all 786 candidates were in their exam rooms before 8:00 a.m. Deputy head teacher Joshua Kholowa said the school expected good results. At Mary Mount Catholic Girls Secondary School, manager Island Mtambo said there were no incidents of cheating and called on students to trust their preparation.

Benedicto Kondowe, head of the Civil Society Education Coalition, called on government to improve how it tracks students from Form One to Form Four. Better data systems would help predict which students might drop out and stop the big changes in candidate numbers year to year, he said. The Malawi National Examinations Board is running the tests.

ny.title: Mayeso a MSCE ayamba bwino, koma ophunzira ochepa okha ndi omwe amalemba mayeso
ny.body: Mayeso a Malawi School Certificate of Education (MSCE) adayamba Lolemba popanda mavuto akulu m'malo ambiri ophunzirira. Ophunzira 197,502 alemba mayeso chaka chino, kuchokera pa 202,940 chaka chatha. Mayesowa akuchitika mpaka pa 19 Julayi ndipo akukhudza madera onse a Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Nduna ya Maphunziro, Sayansi ndi Ukadaulo Bright Msaka adati mayeso ayamba bwino m'magawo onse a maphunziro. Atapita ku malo ophunzirira ku Mzuzu, adati palibe milandu ya chinyengo kapena kusokoneza pa tsiku loyamba. Analimbikitsa oyang'anira onse, oyesa mayeso ndi ogwira ntchito zachitetezo kuti agwire ntchito mosamala komanso moona mtima.

Kutsika kwa chiwerengero cha ophunzira omwe akufuna mayeso kukugwirizana ndi kuchepa kwa ophunzira omwe akuchita maphunziro otseguka komanso akutali, Msaka adatero. Anafotokoza kuti masukulu wamba a sekondale awona ophunzira ambiri akulowa, koma ziwerengero za mapulogalamu ophunzirira kutali zimakwera ndi kutsika chaka chilichonse. Ndondomeko ya maphunziro aulere a sekondale idabweretsa ophunzira ambiri amkati kuti alembe mayeso, adatero.

Kumalo ophunzirira omwe adayendera ku Lilongwe ndi Blantyre, ophunzira anali pamalopo pa nthawi yake ndipo mapepala adayamba popanda vuto. Ku Likuni Boys Secondary School, ophunzira onse 786 anali m'zipinda zawo zophunzirira isanafike 8:00 koloko m'mawa. Wachiwiri kwa mphunzitsi wamkulu Joshua Kholowa adati sukuluyo ikuyembekezera zotsatira zabwino. Ku Mary Mount Catholic Girls Secondary School, manejala Island Mtambo adati palibe zochitika za chinyengo ndipo adapempha ophunzira kuti akhulupirire kukonzekera kwawo.

Benedicto Kondowe, mtsogoleri wa bungwe la Civil Society Education Coalition, adapempha boma kuti liwongolere momwe limatsatirira ophunzira kuyambira Fomu Yoyamba mpaka Fomu Yachinayi. Makina abwino a deta angathandize kuneneratu ophunzira omwe angasiye sukulu ndikuletsa kusintha kwakukulu kwa ziwerengero za ophunzira chaka ndi chaka, adatero. Bungwe Loona za Mayeso la Malawi National Examinations Board likuchita mayesowa.

en.title: By-elections held in three constituencies and two wards
en.body: Malawi held by-elections today in three constituencies and two local government wards. The Malawi Electoral Commission said all materials and staff were ready. Polling stations opened at 6am. The by-elections took place in Balaka Mulunguzi, Lilongwe City Bwaila, and Machinga Mikoko constituencies, plus Uliwa Ward in Karonga South Constituency and Kanjuni Ward in Machinga Central East Constituency.
en.body_more:
MEC chairperson Annabel Mtalimanja, a judge of the High Court of Malawi, inspected voting materials in Lilongwe and said polling stations had received the correct number of ballot papers and all materials in the right quantities. She said the commission had finished training for polling staff, political party representatives, and representatives of independent candidates. MEC also trained election monitors to help prevent disputes from misunderstandings.

To prevent technical problems, MEC placed technicians at polling centres and kept standby election management devices ready. Polling stations closed at 4pm. The vacancies were caused by deaths of elected representatives and by court rulings that nullified results from the 16 September 2025 General Election. In Lilongwe City Bwaila and Balaka Mulunguzi, the court found irregularities in the September election and ordered fresh elections. No new voter registration was done in Balaka Mulunguzi due to the court ruling.

At Bambino Polling Centre in Lilongwe City Bwaila, voting started at 6am but turnout was slow at first, with only one voter present at opening time. A second voter came 14 minutes later. In Balaka Mulunguzi, at least 35,211 voters were expected to vote. At Utale 1 Polling Centre in that constituency, 1,623 voters were registered.

Election watchers said they had confidence in the process. Ben Chakhame, chairperson of Centre for Multiparty Democracy, said his group had not received formal complaints from political parties. Benedicto Kondowe, chairperson of Civil Society Elections Integrity Forum, said MEC's work had been good and preparations had gone very well.

ny.title: Zisankho zapakati zinachitika m'maboma atatu ndi mawadi awiri
ny.body: Malawi yachita zisankho zapang'onopang'ono lero m'maboma atatu ndi m'maboma awiri. Bungwe Loona za Chisankho la Malawi lati zipangizo zonse ndi antchito onse akonzeka. Malo ovotera atsegulidwa nthawi ya 6 koloko m'mawa. Zisankho zapang'onopang'ono zachitika ku Balaka Mulunguzi, Lilongwe City Bwaila, ndi m'maboma a Machinga Mikoko, kuphatikiza Uliwa Ward ku Karonga South Constituency ndi Kanjuni Ward ku Machinga Central East Constituency.
ny.body_more:
Wapampando wa MEC, Annabel Mtalimanja, woweruza milandu wa Khothi Lalikulu la Malawi, anayang'ana zida zovotera ku Lilongwe ndipo anati malo ovotera alandira mapepala ovotera oyenera komanso zida zonse moyenerera. Iye anati bungweli lamaliza maphunziro a ogwira ntchito yovotera, oimira zipani zandale, ndi oimira oimira oimira odziyimira pawokha. MEC inaphunzitsanso oyang'anira zisankho kuti athandize kupewa mikangano yosamveka bwino.

Pofuna kupewa mavuto aukadaulo, MEC inaika akatswiri m'malo ovotera ndipo inasunga zida zoyendetsera zisankho zokhazikika. Malo ovotera anatsekedwa nthawi ya 4 koloko masana. Malo obisalamo anthu adachitika chifukwa cha imfa ya oimira osankhidwa komanso zigamulo za khothi zomwe zinathetsa zotsatira za Chisankho Chachikulu cha pa 16 Seputembala 2025. Ku Bwaila City ndi Balaka Mulunguzi ku Lilongwe, khotilo linapeza zolakwika pa chisankho cha Seputembala ndipo linalamula zisankho zatsopano. Palibe kulembetsa kwatsopano kwa ovota komwe kunachitika ku Balaka Mulunguzi chifukwa cha chigamulo cha khotilo.

Ku Bambino Polling Centre ku Lilongwe City Bwaila, kuvota kunayamba nthawi ya 6 koloko m'mawa koma anthu ambiri anali ochedwa poyamba, ndipo wovota m'modzi yekha analipo nthawi yotsegulira. Wovota wachiwiri anabwera patatha mphindi 14. Ku Balaka Mulunguzi, ovota osachepera 35,211 akuyembekezeka kuvota. Ku Utale 1 Polling Centre m'boma limenelo, ovota 1,623 adalembetsa.

Owonera zisankho adati ali ndi chidaliro pa ndondomekoyi. Ben Chakhame, wapampando wa Centre for Multiparty Democracy, adati gulu lake silinalandire madandaulo ochokera ku zipani zandale. Benedicto Kondowe, wapampando wa Civil Society Elections Integrity Forum, adati ntchito ya MEC yayenda bwino ndipo kukonzekera kwayenda bwino kwambiri.

en.title: Singer Namadingo raises K1 billion for stranded Malawians
en.body: Singer Patience Namadingo has started a fundraising campaign to raise K1 billion. The money will help bring Malawians home from a camp in Durban, South Africa. Namadingo went to the camp himself to see the situation. He gave K2 million of his own money. People can donate through the website fundmalawi.uk.nf.
en.body_more:
When Namadingo visited the camp, he saw bad conditions. There was no space for people to sleep properly. People had not washed for many days. He made a video to show what he saw. "I came here to see the situation so we can do something good," he said.

The money raised will go to Stanley Onjezani Kenani. Kenani will lead the work to bring the Malawians home. Gold Mountain Media, owned by producer Tapps Bandawe, is helping to collect the donations. Bandawe said the website was made to be simple and clear. People choose how much to give, pick how to pay, and get a receipt.

Other musicians are also helping. Kell Kay gave K5 million. He also spent his own money on food at the camp. "We will keep finding money to help them," he said. Musician Loiso gave K1 million from his show money. Malawi's High Commissioner to South Africa, Stella Ndau, said she cannot count exactly how many Malawians are at the camp. New people arrive every day, so the number keeps growing. Namadingo said he will stay on the ground to watch what happens and help where he can.

ny.title: Woyimba Namadingo asonkhanitsa K1 biliyoni kuti athandize anthu aku Malawi omwe ali pamavuto
ny.body: Woyimba Patience Namadingo wayamba kampeni yopezera ndalama zokwana K1 biliyoni. Ndalamazi zithandiza kubweretsa anthu aku Malawi kunyumba kwawo kuchokera ku msasa ku Durban, South Africa. Namadingo anapita yekha ku msasawo kuti akaone momwe zinthu zilili. Iye anapereka K2 miliyoni ya ndalama zake. Anthu akhoza kupereka ndalama kudzera pa webusaiti ya fundmalawi.uk.nf.
ny.body_more:
Pamene Namadingo adapita ku msasa, adawona zinthu zoipa. Panalibe malo oti anthu agone bwino. Anthu anali asanasambe kwa masiku ambiri. Adapanga kanema wowonetsa zomwe adawona. "Ndabwera kuno kudzawona momwe zinthu zilili kuti tichite zabwino," adatero.

Ndalama zomwe zasonkhanitsidwa zipita kwa Stanley Onjezani Kenani. Kenani adzatsogolera ntchito yobweretsa anthu aku Malawi kunyumba. Gold Mountain Media, yomwe ndi ya wopanga Tapps Bandawe, ikuthandiza kusonkhanitsa zoperekazo. Bandawe adati tsamba lawebusayiti lidapangidwa kuti likhale losavuta komanso lomveka bwino. Anthu amasankha ndalama zomwe angapereke, amasankha momwe angalipire, ndikupeza risiti.

Oimba ena akuthandizanso. Kell Kay adapereka K5 miliyoni. Adagwiritsanso ntchito ndalama zake pa chakudya ku msasa. "Tipitiliza kupeza ndalama zowathandiza," adatero. Woimba Loiso adapereka K1 miliyoni kuchokera ku ndalama zake zowonetsera. Kazembe Wamkulu wa Malawi ku South Africa, Stella Ndau, adati sangawerengere kuchuluka kwa anthu aku Malawi omwe ali ku msasa. Anthu atsopano amafika tsiku lililonse, kotero chiwerengerocho chikupitirira kukula. Namadingo adati adzakhala pansi kuti aone zomwe zikuchitika ndikuthandizira momwe angathere.

en.title: Pan African Mining Says It Sent 67kg, Not 2.5 Tonnes, to India
en.body: Pan African Mining Limited says it did not export 2.5 tonnes of granite from Malawi to India. The company says only 67 kilograms of exploration samples were legally sent to India for scientific testing. Managing Director Suresh Kumar Reddy made the statement to clear up false reports on media and social media.
en.body_more:
Reddy said the 67 kilograms were not raw granite or commercial minerals. They were composite samples made from rock and soil specimens collected during exploration work under Exploration Licence No. EL 714/24. The samples were prepared and sent to accredited laboratories in India for specialised analysis.

The company sent the samples to India because Malawi does not have the specialised laboratories needed to test for mineral composition, grade, and economic value. Reddy said this is normal practice in the global mining industry. Mining companies in many countries send exploration samples abroad when local testing facilities do not exist. He stressed that sending samples for scientific testing is different from commercial mineral export.

Reddy said every stage of sampling, preparation, transport, and export followed Malawi's Mining and Minerals Act and all regulations. The samples were for laboratory analysis only, not for sale. He said the false reports have damaged the company's reputation and created unnecessary public worry.

Pan African Mining has called on media houses, online publishers, and social media platforms to issue corrections and retractions. Reddy said responsible journalism requires correction of false facts once true information is given. The company says it will pursue legal action if false reports continue. Reddy reaffirmed the company's commitment to responsible mineral exploration and full compliance with Malawi's mining laws.

ny.title: Pan African Mining Yati Yatumiza Makilogalamu 67, Osati Matani 2.5, ku India
ny.body: Pan African Mining Limited yati sinatumize matani 2.5 a granite kuchokera ku Malawi kupita ku India. Kampaniyo yati makilogalamu 67 okha a zitsanzo zofufuzira ndi omwe adatumizidwa ku India kuti akayesedwe mwasayansi. Mtsogoleri wamkulu Suresh Kumar Reddy adapereka mawuwa pofuna kutsimikizira kuti malipoti abodza adalembedwa pazama TV ndi malo ochezera a pa Intaneti.
ny.body_more:
Reddy anati makilogalamu 67 sanali miyala ya granite kapena miyala yogulitsa. Anali zitsanzo zopangidwa kuchokera ku miyala ndi nthaka zomwe zinasonkhanitsidwa panthawi yofufuza pansi pa Exploration Licence No. EL 714/24. Zitsanzozo zinakonzedwa ndikutumizidwa ku ma laboratories ovomerezeka ku India kuti akafufuzidwe mwapadera.

Kampaniyo inatumiza zitsanzozo ku India chifukwa Malawi ilibe ma laboratories apadera omwe amafunikira kuti ayesere kapangidwe ka mchere, mtundu wake, komanso phindu lake lachuma. Reddy anati izi ndi zachizolowezi m'makampani opanga migodi padziko lonse lapansi. Makampani opanga migodi m'maiko ambiri amatumiza zitsanzo zofufuzira kunja kwa dziko pamene palibe malo oyesera am'deralo. Anagogomezera kuti kutumiza zitsanzo kuti akayesedwe mwasayansi ndikosiyana ndi kutumiza kunja kwa miyala yamalonda.

Reddy anati gawo lililonse la zitsanzo, kukonzekera, kunyamula, ndi kutumiza kunja limatsatira lamulo la Migodi ndi Migodi la Malawi ndi malamulo onse. Zitsanzozo zinali zowunikira labotale zokha, osati zogulitsa. Anati malipoti abodza awononga mbiri ya kampaniyo ndikupangitsa anthu kuda nkhawa kwambiri.

Pan African Mining yapempha nyumba zofalitsa nkhani, ofalitsa pa intaneti, ndi malo ochezera a pa Intaneti kuti apereke zosintha ndi kubweza. Reddy anati utolankhani wodalirika umafuna kukonza mfundo zabodza akangopereka chidziwitso chowona. Kampaniyo ikunena kuti ipita kukhothi ngati malipoti abodza apitiliza. Reddy adatsimikizanso kuti kampaniyo yadzipereka kufufuza miyala mwanzeru komanso kutsatira malamulo a migodi aku Malawi.

en.title: Plane Crash Widows Tell Parliament: We Got No Official Word
en.body: Three widows of men who died in the June 10, 2024 military plane crash told Parliament yesterday that they learned about the deaths from friends and social media, not from the government. Mary Chilima, widow of Vice-President Saulos Chilima, said: "I am still waiting" for an official message. The women gave testimony to a parliamentary committee investigating the crash in Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
Mary Chilima said her husband texted her at 9am on June 10 to say he was boarding a military aircraft to fly to Mzuzu. He was going to the funeral of former Attorney General Ralph Kasambara. Mary and Saulos stayed in contact until 10.01am. At 11.37am, Mary sent him a WhatsApp message but got no answer. At 3pm, a family friend told Mary the plane was missing. "When I saw her face, I knew something was wrong," Mary said. "I just didn't understand why no one had informed me for over five hours."

Mary was only allowed to see her husband's body at the morgue on June 13, three days later. She said officials told her to be strong because his body was badly hurt. But Taona Aidin, whose husband Major Wales Aidin worked as an aircraft engineer, was not allowed to see his body at all. She said the family was told it was too badly damaged. Taona buried her husband in Monkey Bay without seeing him. Sarah Lapukeni, widow of Ministry of Foreign Affairs deputy chief Abdul Lapukeni, also could not view her husband's body. Only her father-in-law was allowed to see it.

Taona challenged the German Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation report into the crash. The German report said the accident happened because the pilots made mistakes. But Taona said the report used only guesses, not facts. "The German report keeps saying, 'we are assuming this happened.' Those are assumptions. In reality, there is nothing," she told the committee. Taona said only one of her husband's two mobile phones was found. She has never received his post-mortem report.

ny.title: Akazi Amasiye Omwe Anagwa Ndege Auza Nyumba Yamalamulo Kuti Sitinalandire Mawu Ovomerezeka
ny.body: Amasiye atatu a amuna omwe anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndege ya asilikali ya pa June 10, 2024 adauza Nyumba Yamalamulo dzulo kuti adamva za imfayi kuchokera kwa anzawo komanso malo ochezera a pa Intaneti, osati kuchokera ku boma. Mary Chilima, mkazi wamasiye wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima, adati: "Ndikudikirabe" uthenga wovomerezeka. Azimayiwa adapereka umboni kwa komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo yomwe ikufufuza za ngoziyi ku Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Mary Chilima anati mwamuna wake anamutumizira uthenga nthawi ya 9 koloko m'mawa pa June 10 kuti amuuze kuti akukwera ndege yankhondo kuti apite ku Mzuzu. Akupita ku maliro a wakale Loya Wamkulu Ralph Kasambara. Mary ndi Saulos anakhalabe olumikizana mpaka 10:01 koloko m'mawa. Nthawi ya 11:37 koloko m'mawa, Mary anamutumizira uthenga wa WhatsApp koma sanayankhe. Nthawi ya 3 koloko masana, mnzake wa banja lake anauza Mary kuti ndegeyo yasowa. "Nditaona nkhope yake, ndinadziwa kuti pali vuto," adatero Mary. "Sindinamvetse chifukwa chake palibe amene anandidziwitsa kwa maola oposa asanu."

Mary analoledwa kuona thupi la mwamuna wake ku morgue pa June 13, patatha masiku atatu. Anati akuluakulu a boma adamuuza kuti akhale wolimba mtima chifukwa thupi lake linali litavulala kwambiri. Koma Taona Aidin, yemwe mwamuna wake Major Wales Aidin ankagwira ntchito ngati injiniya wa ndege, sanaloledwe kuona thupi lake konse. Anati banjali linauzidwa kuti linawonongeka kwambiri. Taona anaika mwamuna wake m'manda ku Monkey Bay osamuwona. Sarah Lapukeni, mkazi wamasiye wa wachiwiri kwa mkulu wa Unduna wa Zachilendo Abdul Lapukeni, nayenso sanathe kuwona mtembo wa mwamuna wake. Apongozi ake okha ndi omwe analoledwa kuuwona.

Taona adatsutsa lipoti la Germany Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation pa ngoziyi. Lipotilo la Germany linati ngoziyi idachitika chifukwa oyendetsa ndege adalakwitsa. Koma Taona adati lipotilo lidagwiritsa ntchito malingaliro okha, osati mfundo zenizeni. "Lipotilo la Germany likupitiliza kunena kuti, 'tikuganiza kuti izi zidachitika.' Zimenezo ndi malingaliro. Kunena zoona, palibe chomwe chikuchitika," adatero kwa komitiyo. Taona adati foni imodzi yokha mwa ziwiri za mwamuna wake ndi yomwe idapezeka. Sanalandirepo lipoti lake la pambuyo pa imfa.

en.title: President Mutharika returns to Malawi from South Africa
en.body: President Peter Mutharika came back to Malawi on Monday after two weeks in South Africa. He flew home on a chartered aircraft with his wife, First Lady Gertrude Mutharika. The trip was private, but the president spoke about his work in South Africa when he landed.
en.body_more:
At the airport, Mutharika said his government will help Malawians who came back from South Africa. Many people left South Africa because of attacks on foreigners there. The president told people he and South African President Cyril Ramaphosa agreed to work together to stop these attacks.

Mutharika's return comes at a hard time for Malawi. The country has money problems. People cannot get enough foreign money, and the cost of living is very high. Many people worry about when the economy will get better. Every time the president moves or speaks, people watch and think about what it means for the country.

Mutharika won the last election just months ago. He got 56.8% of the vote. This was a big win for him. His old victory in 2019 was cancelled by the courts. He had to leave office. Then in 2020, there was a new election and he lost. But five years later, he came back and won again. Even people in his own party were surprised by how big his win was.

ny.title: President Mutharika wabwelera ku Malawi kuchokera ku South Africa
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adabwerera ku Malawi Lolemba patatha milungu iwiri ali ku South Africa. Anakwera ndege yobwereka ndi mkazi wake, Mkazi wa Pulezidenti Gertrude Mutharika. Ulendowu unali wachinsinsi, koma pulezidenti adalankhula za ntchito yake ku South Africa atafika.
ny.body_more:
Pa bwalo la ndege, Mutharika anati boma lake lithandiza anthu aku Malawi omwe adabwerera kuchokera ku South Africa. Anthu ambiri adachoka ku South Africa chifukwa cha ziwawa zomwe zidachitikira alendo kumeneko. Purezidenti adauza anthu omwe iye ndi Purezidenti waku South Africa Cyril Ramaphosa adagwirizana kuti agwire ntchito limodzi kuti aletse ziwawazi.

Kubwerera kwa Mutharika kukubwera panthawi yovuta ku Malawi. Dzikoli lili ndi mavuto azachuma. Anthu sangapeze ndalama zokwanira zakunja, ndipo mtengo wa moyo ndi wokwera kwambiri. Anthu ambiri amadandaula kuti chuma chidzayenda bwanji. Nthawi iliyonse purezidenti akasuntha kapena kulankhula, anthu amaonera ndi kuganizira zomwe zikutanthauza kudzikolo.

Mutharika adapambana chisankho chatha miyezi ingapo yapitayo. Adapeza 56.8% ya mavoti. Uku kunali kupambana kwakukulu kwa iye. Kupambana kwake kwakale mu 2019 kudathetsedwa ndi makhothi. Anayenera kusiya udindo. Kenako mu 2020, panali chisankho chatsopano ndipo adatayika. Koma patatha zaka zisanu, adabweranso ndikupambana kachiwiri. Ngakhale anthu a chipani chake adadabwa ndi kukula kwa kupambana kwake.

en.title: Widow of Vice President Chilima tells MPs her questions about the crash
en.body: Mary Chilima came before a Parliamentary Ad Hoc Committee on Monday to tell MPs what she wants to know about the plane crash that killed her husband, former Vice President Saulos Chilima, and eight others on June 10, 2024. Her testimony was calm and pointed. She asked hard questions about the aircraft, the search, and how the government treated her family after the crash.
en.body_more:
Chilima raised questions about the plane's Emergency Locator Transmitter (ELT) — a device that helps find crashed aircraft. The German Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation said the ELT battery died in 2004, twenty years before the crash. The Malawi Defence Force told investigators they had no spare parts and no money to fix it. Yet search teams still asked South Africa for help to find the ELT signal. "The ELT died in 2004," Chilima told the MPs. "If they knew it was not working, why asking for help to locate it?"

She also questioned why the plane spent 11 minutes flying over dangerous mountain terrain. Chilima asked if the crew truly knew the land below them. She said she scolded the Inspector General of Police, Merlyn Yolamu, because the search did not start quickly enough on the night the plane went missing. She wanted to join the search at once but was told no one knew where the crash was.

Chilima also spoke about phones found at the crash site. She said her husband had three mobile phones. But authorities only gave back two of them, one month after the crash, and both were broken. She received no money to pay for the damage. She learned her husband died from social media before the government told her officially. The family also had no state help until late in 2025, and only got government security staff after she asked Deputy Minister Norman Chisale for it. She asked the government to help all the other families who lost people in the crash.

ny.title: Mkazi wamasiye wa wachiwiri kwa purezidenti Chilima auza aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo mafunso ake okhudza ngoziyi
ny.body: Mary Chilima adafika pamaso pa Komiti Yoyang'anira Nyumba Yamalamulo Lolemba kuti auze aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo zomwe akufuna kudziwa zokhudza ngozi ya ndege yomwe idapha mwamuna wake, Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima, ndi ena asanu ndi atatu pa June 10, 2024. Umboni wake unali wodekha komanso wolunjika. Anafunsa mafunso ovuta okhudza ndegeyo, kusaka, ndi momwe boma linachitira ndi banja lake pambuyo pa ngoziyo.
ny.body_more:
Chilima adafunsa mafunso okhudza Emergency Locator Transmitter (ELT) ya ndegeyo — chipangizo chomwe chimathandiza kupeza ndege zomwe zagwa. Bungwe la Germany Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation linati batire ya ELT idafa mu 2004, zaka makumi awiri ngozi isanachitike. Asilikali a Malawi adauza ofufuza kuti analibe zida zina zosinthira ndipo analibe ndalama zokonzera. Komabe magulu ofufuza adapemphabe South Africa kuti iwathandize kupeza chizindikiro cha ELT. "ELT idafa mu 2004," Chilima adauza aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo. "Ngati ankadziwa kuti sikugwira ntchito, bwanji kupempha thandizo kuti apeze?"

Adafunsanso chifukwa chake ndegeyo idathera mphindi 11 ikuuluka m'mapiri oopsa. Chilima adafunsa ngati ogwira ntchitowo ankadziwadi malo omwe ali pansi pawo. Adati adadzudzula Inspector General wa apolisi, Merlyn Yolamu, chifukwa kufufuzako sikunayambe mwachangu mokwanira usiku womwe ndegeyo idasowa. Ankafuna kulowa nawo kusaka nthawi yomweyo koma adauzidwa kuti palibe amene ankadziwa komwe ngoziyo inali.

Chilima adalankhulanso za mafoni omwe adapezeka pamalo omwe ngoziyo idagwa. Adati mwamuna wake anali ndi mafoni atatu. Koma akuluakulu aboma adabweza ziwiri zokha, mwezi umodzi pambuyo pa ngoziyi, ndipo zonse ziwiri zidasweka. Sanalandire ndalama zolipirira kuwonongekako. Anadziwa kuti mwamuna wake wamwalira kuchokera pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti boma lisanamuuze mwalamulo. Banjali silinalandire thandizo la boma mpaka kumapeto kwa chaka cha 2025, ndipo linangolandira antchito achitetezo aboma atapempha Wachiwiri kwa Nduna Norman Chisale kuti amupatse. Anapempha boma kuti lithandize mabanja ena onse omwe adataya anthu pangoziyi.

en.title: FDH Bank gives more money to netball league
en.body: FDH Bank plc has given more money to the FDH Premier Netball League. The bank raised the sponsorship from K250 million to K350 million. This is a 40 percent increase. The second season of the league will start on July 4 in Lilongwe. The bank's marketing manager Tiyese Kaimila told the news this yesterday.
en.body_more:
Kaimila said the bank wants to use the league to help netball grow in Malawi. "We are excited about the upcoming second season," he said. "That is why we gave more money."

The league gets K1.2 billion over four years from FDH Bank. In the first season, the league had 12 teams and played over 200 matches. It helps young players learn netball and move from local teams to the national team. Kaimila said the first season was very good. "We are happy to be part of netball change," he said. "We expect the league to help find and train players for the national team."

The Netball Association of Malawi also welcomed the extra money. Vice President Tadala Billie said the league is very important for Malawian netball. "We thank FDH Bank for this gift," she said. "The league helps us find talent and make the game better." The first season started in August 2025 and ended in March this year. Blue Eagles Sisters won the 12-team league with 44 points and 22 straight wins. They beat Tigresses 58-33 in the final match. Tigresses came second with 37 points. Kukoma Diamonds came third with 36 points. The champions got K13 million. Tigresses got K10 million and Kukoma Diamonds got K8 million.

FDH Bank also helps netball in other ways. The bank gives money to the Mayor's Trophy and school sports. This money helps young players play netball from primary school to the national team level.

ny.title: Banki ya FDH yapereka ndalama zambiri ku ligi ya netiboli
ny.body: Banki ya FDH plc yapereka ndalama zambiri ku FDH Premier Netball League. Bankiyo yakweza ndalama zothandizira kuchokera pa K250 miliyoni kufika pa K350 miliyoni. Izi ndi kuwonjezeka kwa 40 peresenti. Nyengo yachiwiri ya ligi iyamba pa Julayi 4 ku Lilongwe. Woyang'anira malonda wa bankiyo, Tiyese Kaimila, adauza nkhaniyi dzulo.
ny.body_more:
Kaimila anati banki ikufuna kugwiritsa ntchito ligi kuthandiza netiball kukula ku Malawi. "Tikusangalala ndi nyengo yachiwiri ikubwerayi," adatero. "Ndicho chifukwa chake tapereka ndalama zambiri."

Ligiyi yalandira K1.2 biliyoni m'zaka zinayi kuchokera ku FDH Bank. Mu nyengo yoyamba, ligiyi inali ndi magulu 12 ndipo idasewera masewera opitilira 200. Imathandiza osewera achichepere kuphunzira netiball ndikusamuka kuchoka ku magulu am'deralo kupita ku timu ya dziko. Kaimila anati nyengo yoyamba inali yabwino kwambiri. "Tikusangalala kukhala mbali ya kusintha kwa netiball," adatero. "Tikuyembekeza kuti ligiyi ithandiza kupeza ndikuphunzitsa osewera a timu ya dziko."

Bungwe la Netball Association of Malawi lalandiranso ndalama zowonjezera. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Tadala Billie adati ligiyi ndi yofunika kwambiri pa netiball ya ku Malawi. "Tikuthokoza FDH Bank chifukwa cha mphatso iyi," adatero. "Ligiyi imatithandiza kupeza luso ndikupanga masewerawa kukhala abwino." Nyengo yoyamba idayamba mu Ogasiti 2025 ndipo idatha mu Marichi chaka chino. Blue Eagles Sisters idapambana ligi ya magulu 12 ndi mapointi 44 ndi zipambano 22 motsatizana. Iwo adagonjetsa Tigresses 58-33 pamasewera omaliza. Tigresses adabwera pa nambala 2 ndi mapointi 37. Kukoma Diamonds adabwera pa nambala 2 ndi mapointi 36. Opambana adapeza K13 miliyoni. Tigresses adapeza K10 miliyoni ndipo Kukoma Diamonds adapeza K8 miliyoni.

FDH Bank imathandizanso netball m'njira zina. Banki imapereka ndalama ku Meya's Trophy ndi masewera a kusukulu. Ndalamazi zimathandiza osewera achichepere kusewera netball kuyambira kusukulu ya pulayimale mpaka pamlingo wa timu ya dziko.

en.title: Wife says husband may have stopped plane fuel tank
en.body: The wife of an aircraft engineer told MPs she thinks her husband emptied the plane's fuel tank to stop an explosion. Taona Aidin said Major Wales Aidin did this in the last moments before the crash on June 10. The plane fell near Viphya Plantations. Vice-President Saulos Chilima and eight other people died.
en.body_more:
Taona Aidin told the MPs her husband watched many aircraft accident programmes. She said he often talked about how an engineer's job was to "damp fuel." She believes he used this knowledge in the final moments of the flight.

Aidin asked the committee to look at who her husband spoke to last. She said these final talks may show what happened that morning. The question of why the plane did not burn has been part of the inquiry from the start. The aircraft had enough fuel for the full journey.

The German Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation said the plane was filled with 483 litres of Jet A1 fuel at Kamuzu International Airport. Captain Henry Nthani, who worked at Zomba Air Base, gave a different answer. He said pilots learn to turn off the master electrical switch if a crash will happen. This turns off power and stops fire. He said the crew may have done this. This could explain why there were no flames and why the bodies could be found.

The crash killed Vice-President Saulos Chilima, former first lady Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, pilot Colonel Owen Sambalopa, and co-pilot Major Flora Selemani, plus four other people. The inquiry continues to search for answers about what took place at Nthungwa in Viphya Plantations that day.

ny.title: Mkazi wake akuti mwamuna wake mwina wayimitsa thanki ya mafuta ya ndege
ny.body: Mkazi wa mainjiniya wa ndege anauza aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo kuti akuganiza kuti mwamuna wake anatulutsa mafuta m'galimoto kuti aletse kuphulika. Taona Aidin adati Major Wales Aidin adachita izi mphindi zomaliza ngozi isanachitike pa June 10. Ndegeyo inagwa pafupi ndi Viphya Plantations. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu anamwalira.
ny.body_more:
Taona Aidin anauza aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo kuti mwamuna wake ankaonera mapulogalamu ambiri a ngozi za ndege. Iye anati nthawi zambiri ankalankhula za momwe ntchito ya mainjiniya inali "kunyowetsa mafuta." Iye akukhulupirira kuti anagwiritsa ntchito chidziwitsochi m'mphindi zomaliza za ulendowo.

Aidin anapempha komiti kuti ione yemwe mwamuna wake analankhula naye komaliza. Iye anati zokambirana zomalizazi zingasonyeze zomwe zinachitika m'mawa umenewo. Funso loti chifukwa chiyani ndegeyo sinatenthe lakhala mbali ya kafukufuku kuyambira pachiyambi. Ndegeyo inali ndi mafuta okwanira ulendo wonse.

Bungwe la Germany Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation linati ndegeyo inali yodzaza ndi malita 483 a mafuta a Jet A1 ku Kamuzu International Airport. Captain Henry Nthani, yemwe ankagwira ntchito ku Zomba Air Base, anapereka yankho losiyana. Iye anati oyendetsa ndege amaphunzira kuzimitsa switch yamagetsi ngati ngozi itachitika. Izi zimazimitsa magetsi ndikuyimitsa moto. Anati ogwira ntchito mwina adachita izi. Izi zitha kufotokoza chifukwa chake panalibe malawi ndi chifukwa chake matupiwo amapezeka.

Ngoziyi inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima, mayi woyamba wakale Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, woyendetsa ndege Colonel Owen Sambalopa, ndi woyendetsa ndege mnzake Major Flora Selemani, pamodzi ndi anthu ena anayi. Kafukufukuyu akupitilizabe kufunafuna mayankho a zomwe zinachitika ku Nthungwa ku Viphya Plantations tsiku lomwelo.

en.title: District stops HRCC march against South Africa attacks
en.body: The Lilongwe District Commissioner has stopped a planned march today by the Human Rights Consultative Committee (HRCC) against attacks on foreign nationals in South Africa. HRCC wanted to march to the South African High Commission in Lilongwe to give a petition asking for better protection for Malawians. The commissioner said HRCC sent notice too late on Friday to plan the march properly.
en.body_more:
HRCC says it speaks for 97 civil society groups in Malawi. The march was meant to show support for Malawians living in South Africa who face danger from attacks. Many have left their homes and come back to Malawi.

Lilongwe District Commissioner Stallichi Mwambiwa said the notification reached his office on Friday afternoon. By law, organisers must tell the commissioner at least 48 hours before any march. The law also says the commissioner, police, and organisers must meet to plan things like the march route, traffic, and safety. Mwambiwa said there was not enough time to hold this meeting before today. He asked HRCC to come to a meeting today instead to talk about what happens next.

HRCC also told the South African High Commission about the march plan. The march comes after reports that groups in South Africa set a June 30 deadline for foreign nationals to leave. South Africa's government said this deadline is not an official government order. A South African government official named William Baloyi said the government will not allow any problems or violence.

It is not yet clear if HRCC will plan a new march date after today's meeting with the district council.

ny.title: Chigawo chaletsa chiwonetsero cha HRCC chotsutsa ziwawa ku South Africa
ny.body: Mtsogoleri wa Chigawo cha Lilongwe wayimitsa ulendo womwe Komiti Yoona za Ufulu Wachibadwidwe (HRCC) idakonza lero wotsutsana ndi ziwawa zomwe zidachitikira nzika zakunja ku South Africa. HRCC ikufuna kupita ku South African High Commission ku Lilongwe kukapereka pempho lopempha chitetezo chabwino kwa anthu aku Malawi. Mtsogoleriyo adati HRCC idatumiza chidziwitso mochedwa Lachisanu kuti ikonzekere bwino ulendowu.
ny.body_more:
HRCC ikunena kuti ikuyimira magulu 97 a anthu wamba ku Malawi. Ulendowu unali woti uwonetse chithandizo kwa anthu aku Malawi okhala ku South Africa omwe akukumana ndi zoopsa chifukwa cha ziwopsezo. Ambiri achoka m'nyumba zawo ndikubwerera ku Malawi.

Mkulu wa Chigawo cha Lilongwe, Stallichi Mwambiwa, adati chidziwitsochi chidafika ku ofesi yake Lachisanu masana. Malinga ndi lamulo, okonza ayenera kuuza komishonala maola osachepera 48 ulendo uliwonse usanachitike. Lamuloli limanenanso kuti komishonala, apolisi, ndi okonza ayenera kukumana kuti akonze zinthu monga njira yoyendera, magalimoto, ndi chitetezo. Mwambiwa adati panalibe nthawi yokwanira yochitira msonkhanowu lero. Adapempha HRCC kuti ibwere kumsonkhano lero m'malo mwake kuti akambirane zomwe zidzachitike pambuyo pake.

HRCC idauzanso South African High Commission za dongosolo la ulendowu. Ulendowu ukubwera pambuyo pa malipoti akuti magulu aku South Africa adakhazikitsa tsiku lomaliza la June 30 kuti nzika zakunja zichoke. Boma la South Africa lati tsiku lomalizali si lamulo lovomerezeka la boma. Mkulu wa boma la South Africa dzina lake William Baloyi adati boma sililola mavuto kapena chiwawa chilichonse.

Sizikudziwika bwino ngati HRCC idzakonza tsiku latsopano la machi pambuyo pa msonkhano wa lero ndi khonsolo ya chigawo.

en.title: Plane crash inquiry hears from victims' families
en.body: Parliament's committee is asking families of the nine people who died in a plane crash on June 10, 2024 to come and tell their stories. The crash killed Vice-President Saulos Chilima and eight others. The committee began hearing from the families this week. Former spy chief Dokani Ngwira was called but stopped his testimony because of secret information rules.
en.body_more:
The plane came down at Nthungwa in Viphya Plantation while flying to Mzuzu. The nine people on the plane were going to a funeral in Nkhata Bay. Besides Chilima, the crash killed former first lady Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, aide Chisomo Chimaneni, doctor Dan Kanyemba, Deputy Chief of Protocol Abdul Lapukeni, pilot Colonel Owen Sambalopa, co-pilot Major Flora Selemani, engineer Major Wales Aidin, and guard commander Lucas Kapheni.

On Monday, the committee heard from Taona Aidin, widow of engineer Major Wales Aidin. Mary Chilima, the Vice-President's widow, spoke in the afternoon. Sarah Lapukeni, widow of Deputy Chief of Protocol Abdul Lapukeni, also gave her story. This week the committee is hearing only from family members. On Tuesday and Wednesday, more family members and other people close to the dead will speak. These include medical officer Dan Kanyemba's wife Effiness Katunga Kanyemba, and co-pilot Major Flora Selemani's widow Mabvuto Ngwilinji.

Dokani Ngwira, the former National Intelligence Service director general, appeared on Monday but stopped his testimony. He said he cannot share secret information without approval from the intelligence service. He told the committee to first ask the National Intelligence Service for permission before he can fully speak. This created a problem for the inquiry because the committee did not get this approval before calling him.

President Peter Mutharika ordered this new inquiry in February. The Minister of Justice and Constitutional Affairs Charles Mhango said two earlier inquiries had gaps and did not answer all questions. Mary Chilima has said the families still have many questions that have not been answered about what happened.

ny.title: Kafukufuku wa ngozi ya ndege wamva kuchokera kwa mabanja a omwe akhudzidwa
ny.body: Komiti ya Nyumba Yamalamulo ikupempha mabanja a anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndege pa June 10, 2024 kuti abwere kudzafotokoza nkhani zawo. Ngoziyi inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu. Komitiyi inayamba kumva kuchokera kwa mabanja sabata ino. Mtsogoleri wakale wa akazitape Dokani Ngwira anaitanidwa koma anasiya kupereka umboni wake chifukwa cha malamulo achinsinsi.
ny.body_more:
Ndegeyo inagwa ku Nthungwa ku Viphya Plantation pamene inali paulendo wopita ku Mzuzu. Anthu asanu ndi anayi omwe anali m'ndegeyo anali kupita ku maliro ku Nkhata Bay. Kupatula Chilima, ngoziyi inapha mayi woyamba wakale Patricia Shanil Dzimbiri, wothandizira Chisomo Chimaneni, dokotala Dan Kanyemba, Wachiwiri kwa Chief of Protocol Abdul Lapukeni, woyendetsa ndege Colonel Owen Sambalopa, woyendetsa ndege mnzake Major Flora Selemani, injiniya Major Wales Aidin, ndi mkulu wa asilikali Lucas Kapheni.

Lolemba, komitiyi idamva kuchokera kwa Taona Aidin, mkazi wamasiye wa injiniya Major Wales Aidin. Mary Chilima, mkazi wamasiye wa Vice-President, adalankhula masana. Sarah Lapukeni, mkazi wamasiye wa Wachiwiri kwa Chief of Protocol Abdul Lapukeni, nayenso adapereka nkhani yake. Sabata ino komitiyi ikumva kuchokera kwa achibale okha. Lachiwiri ndi Lachitatu, achibale ambiri ndi anthu ena omwe ali pafupi ndi akufa adzalankhula. Izi zikuphatikizapo mkazi wa dokotala Dan Kanyemba, Effiness Katunga Kanyemba, ndi mkazi wamasiye wa Major Flora Selemani, Major Flora Selemani.

Dokani Ngwira, yemwe kale anali mkulu wa bungwe la National Intelligence Service, adawonekera Lolemba koma adasiya kupereka umboni wake. Anati sangagawane zambiri zachinsinsi popanda chilolezo kuchokera ku bungwe la intelligence. Anauza komitiyi kuti iyambe yapempha chilolezo ku bungwe la National Intelligence Service asanayambe kulankhula mokwanira. Izi zinayambitsa vuto pa kafukufukuyu chifukwa komitiyi sinalandire chilolezochi isanamuyimbire foni.

Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adalamula kafukufuku watsopanoyu mu February. Nduna ya Zachilungamo ndi Zamalamulo Charles Mhango adati mafunso awiri oyambirira anali ndi mipata ndipo sanayankhe mafunso onse. Mary Chilima wanena kuti mabanjawa akadali ndi mafunso ambiri omwe sanayankhidwe pa zomwe zinachitika.

en.title: Vice-President Chihana Gets Second Regional Job in One Week
en.body: President Peter Mutharika has sent Second Vice-President Enoch Chihana to represent Malawi at a Sadc summit today. This is the second time in seven days that Chihana has been given a regional task. The Office of the Second Vice-President said yes to the assignment. Chihana will attend the meeting on behalf of the President, who is on a private visit to South Africa.
en.body_more:
