The virtual Extraordinary Summit of Sadc Heads of State and Government is chaired by South African President Cyril Ramaphosa. The meeting will bring together leaders from the 16 Sadc member states. They will talk about the political and security situation in Madagascar. The meeting will start at 5:00 p.m. Malawi time on Monday, June 29, 2026.

Chihana chaired a different Sadc meeting in Lilongwe on June 22. That was the Extraordinary Sadc Troika Summit. He led it while Mutharika was away. The earlier meeting sent a goodwill mission to the Democratic Republic of Congo and showed support for Madagascar's move towards elections.

Chihana leads the Alliance for Democracy party. He is a long-time political ally of Mutharika. The repeated assignments show the President's trust in Chihana. First Vice-President Jane Ansah has not been given these regional jobs. The Office of the President and Cabinet said the President will choose who to send. A spokesman called Focus Maganga said this is normal. He said: "The Vice-Presidency is, by its nature, a delegated office. From time to time, the President may, at his discretion, reassign or adjust responsibilities." Maganga said such changes should not be seen as signs of trouble between leaders.

Mutharika has also sent Cabinet ministers to major events instead of attending himself. He sent Education Minister Bright Msaka to a memorial service for late Vice-President Saulos Klaus Chilima in Ntcheu, even though both Vice-Presidents were there. Some people have asked why a minister was chosen instead of a Vice-President.

ny.title: Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Chihana Apeza Ntchito Yachiwiri Yachigawo M'sabata Imodzi
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika watumiza Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enoch Chihana kuti akaimire Malawi pamsonkhano wa Sadc lero. Iyi ndi nthawi yachiwiri m'masiku asanu ndi awiri kuti Chihana apatsidwe ntchito yachigawo. Ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti yavomereza ntchitoyi. Chihana adzapezeka pamsonkhanowu m'malo mwa Purezidenti, yemwe ali paulendo wapadera ku South Africa.
ny.body_more:
Msonkhano Wapadera wa Atsogoleri a Maboma ndi Maboma a Sadc womwe umachitika pa intaneti, udzatsogozedwa ndi Purezidenti wa South Africa Cyril Ramaphosa. Msonkhanowu udzasonkhanitsa atsogoleri ochokera m'maiko 16 a Sadc. Adzakambirana za momwe zinthu zilili pa ndale ndi chitetezo ku Madagascar. Msonkhanowu udzayamba nthawi ya 5:00 p.m. nthawi ya Malawi Lolemba, June 29, 2026.

Chihana adatsogolera msonkhano wina wa Sadc ku Lilongwe pa June 22. Umenewo unali Msonkhano Wapadera wa Sadc Troika. Iye adatsogolera pamene Mutharika anali kutali. Msonkhano woyamba unatumiza uthenga wabwino ku Democratic Republic of Congo ndipo unasonyeza kuti Madagascar ikufuna kuchita zisankho.

Chihana akutsogolera chipani cha Alliance for Democracy. Iye ndi mnzake wandale wa Mutharika kwa nthawi yayitali. Ntchito zobwerezabwerezazi zikusonyeza kuti Purezidenti akukhulupirira Chihana. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Jane Ansah sanapatsidwe ntchito zachigawozi. Ofesi ya Purezidenti ndi Cabinet inati Purezidenti adzasankha woti amutumize. Wolankhulira wotchedwa Focus Maganga adati izi ndi zachilendo. Iye anati: "Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti, mwachibadwa chake, ndi udindo wopatsidwa. Nthawi ndi nthawi, Purezidenti, mwakufuna kwake, akhoza kusintha maudindo kapena kusintha." Maganga anati kusintha koteroko sikuyenera kuonedwa ngati zizindikiro za mavuto pakati pa atsogoleri.

Mutharika watumizanso nduna za boma ku zochitika zazikulu m'malo mopita yekha. Watumiza Nduna ya Maphunziro Bright Msaka ku mwambo wokumbukira wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Klaus Chilima ku Ntcheu, ngakhale kuti Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti onse analipo. Anthu ena afunsa chifukwa chake nduna inasankhidwa m'malo mwa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti.

en.title: Silver Strikers go top of the league after big win
en.body: Silver Strikers moved to the top of the FDH Bank Premiership on Sunday after they beat Red Lions 3-0 at Silver Stadium in Lilongwe. Innocent Shema scored two goals in the first half. Festus Duwe added a third goal in the second half. Silver now have 17 points from eight games.
en.body_more:
Silver took the lead from Blue Eagles, who lost 3-2 to Luanar Mitundu on Saturday at Dedza Stadium. Blue Eagles now have 16 points. Silver are one point ahead of them.

Mighty Wanderers, the defending champions, had a difficult day. They drew 1-1 with Goshen City Dedza Dynamos at Dedza Stadium on Sunday. Dynamos scored first in the 20th minute through Chifuniro Mpinganjira. Wanderers fought back and Blessings Mwalilino scored in the 71st minute to make it equal. Wanderers now have 15 points from eight games. Coach Bob Mpinganjira was unhappy with the result. "This was a very difficult game for us. We were second on the ball in the first half and we were punished for that," he said. Wanderers have now drawn twice in a row and dropped four points in two games.

In other matches, Ekhaya FC beat Mafco FC 1-0 at Mpira Stadium in Chiwembe Township, Blantyre to move to fourth place with 14 points. Civil Service United also won 1-0 against Creck Sporting at Aubrey Dimba Stadium in Mchinji. Karonga United and Moyale Barracks drew goalless at Karonga Stadium.

Silver assistant coach Mapopa Msukwa said his team will not relax. "We are excited to be on top of the table but we will not relax because the league is tough," he said.

ny.title: Silver Strikers yakwera pamwamba pa ligi pambuyo popambana kwambiri
ny.body: Silver Strikers yakwera pamwamba pa FDH Bank Premiership Lamlungu atagonjetsa Red Lions 3-0 ku Silver Stadium ku Lilongwe. Innocent Shema adagoletsa zigoli ziwiri mu theka loyamba. Festus Duwe adawonjezera chigoli chachitatu mu theka lachiwiri. Silver tsopano ili ndi mapointi 17 kuchokera pamasewera asanu ndi atatu.
ny.body_more:
Silver idatsogolera Blue Eagles, yomwe idataya 3-2 ndi Luanar Mitundu Loweruka ku Dedza Stadium. Blue Eagles tsopano ili ndi mapointi 16. Silver ili patsogolo pa mfundo imodzi.

Mighty Wanderers, omwe ndi akatswiri oteteza, anali ndi tsiku lovuta. Adasewera 1-1 ndi Goshen City Dedza Dynamos ku Dedza Stadium Lamlungu. Dynamos idasewera 1-1 mu mphindi ya 20 kudzera mwa Chifuniro Mpinganjira. Wanderers idamenyana ndipo Blessings Mwalilino adasewera 71 mu mphindi kuti agwirizane. Wanderers tsopano ili ndi mapointi 15 mu masewera asanu ndi atatu. Mphunzitsi Bob Mpinganjira sanasangalale ndi zotsatira zake. "Uwu unali masewera ovuta kwambiri kwa ife. Tidali achiwiri pa mpira mu theka loyamba ndipo tidalangidwa chifukwa cha zimenezo," adatero. Wanderers tsopano yasewera 1-0 motsatizana ndipo yataya mapointi anayi mu masewera awiri.

M'masewera ena, Ekhaya FC idagonjetsa Mafco FC 1-0 ku Mpira Stadium ku Chiwembe Township, Blantyre kuti ipite pamalo achinayi ndi mapointi 14. Civil Service United idapambananso 1-0 motsutsana ndi Creck Sporting pa Aubrey Dimba Stadium ku Mchinji. Karonga United ndi Moyale Barracks adasewera 1-0 pa Karonga Stadium.

Mphunzitsi wothandizira wa siliva, Mapopa Msukwa, adati timu yake sidzapumula. "Tikusangalala kukhala pamwamba pa tebulo koma sitidzapumula chifukwa ligi ndi yovuta," adatero.

en.title: Police plan to buy new aircraft for Air Wing
en.body: The Malawi Police Service wants to buy modern aircraft. The police helicopter has been broken for years at Mtakataka Air Wing in Dedza. Police Spokesperson Lael Chimtembo said the service is checking if it can fix the helicopter or if it must buy a new one.
en.body_more:
The helicopter stopped working after it got mechanical faults. It has been sitting at Mtakataka Air Wing in Dedza for several years without use. This means the police have no aircraft for search and rescue work, watching areas, or fast response to security problems.

Chimtembo said the police do not want to spend more money on fixing the helicopter than what it is worth to use. If the cost of fixing it is too high, the police may decide to write it off and not use it again. The police must make a choice: fix the old helicopter or buy new aircraft instead.

To get ready, the police started to train officers. On 12 January, several police officers began local training in aircraft work and care. These officers are learning how to run and fix aircraft. The police are building up the skills they need before the Air Wing works again.

If the plans work, the new Air Wing will help police work better across Malawi. The aircraft will be used for watching areas, search and rescue, and quick response to keep people safe.

ny.title: Apolisi akukonzekera kugula ndege zatsopano za Air Wing
ny.body: Apolisi aku Malawi akufuna kugula ndege zamakono. Helikopita ya apolisi yakhala ikusweka kwa zaka zambiri ku Mtakataka Air Wing ku Dedza. Mneneri wa apolisi Lael Chimtembo adati bungweli likufufuza ngati lingakonze helikopitayo kapena ngati liyenera kugula yatsopano.
ny.body_more:
Helikopita inasiya kugwira ntchito itakumana ndi mavuto a makina. Yakhala ikugwira ntchito ku Mtakataka Air Wing ku Dedza kwa zaka zingapo popanda kugwiritsa ntchito. Izi zikutanthauza kuti apolisi alibe ndege zofufuzira ndi kupulumutsa, malo owonera, kapena kuyankha mwachangu mavuto achitetezo.

Chimtembo adati apolisi sakufuna kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama zambiri kukonza helikopita kuposa zomwe zingagwiritsidwe ntchito. Ngati mtengo woikonza ndi wokwera kwambiri, apolisi angasankhe kuichotsa ndikusagwiritsanso ntchito. Apolisi ayenera kusankha: kukonza helikopita yakale kapena kugula ndege yatsopano m'malo mwake.

Kuti akonzekere, apolisi anayamba kuphunzitsa apolisi. Pa 12 Januwale, apolisi angapo adayamba maphunziro am'deralo pantchito yoyendetsa ndege ndi kusamalira. Apolisi awa akuphunzira momwe angayendetsere ndikukonza ndege. Apolisi akumanga luso lomwe amafunikira Air Wing isanagwirenso ntchito.

Ngati mapulaniwo agwira ntchito, Air Wing yatsopano ithandiza apolisi kugwira ntchito bwino ku Malawi konse. Ndegeyo idzagwiritsidwa ntchito poyang'anira madera, kufufuza ndi kupulumutsa, komanso kuyankha mwachangu kuti anthu akhale otetezeka.

en.title: 50 SMEs showcase goods at NBM Expo in Mzuzu
en.body: About 50 small and medium businesses showed their products at an expo in Mzuzu on Saturday. The National Bank of Malawi (NBM) plc organised the event to mark World Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises Day on 27 June. Businesses from farming, making clothes, and creative work took part. The expo gave SMEs a chance to meet new customers and grow their sales.
en.body_more:
The event took place at Springs Park in Mzuzu. SMEs from across the Northern Region came to showcase what they do and connect with people. NBM Chief Executive Officer Harold Jiya said SMEs are very important to Malawi's economy. He said they create jobs and help the country grow. Jiya told the crowd that small businesses make up nearly 70 percent of jobs in developing countries.

NBM continues to help small businesses grow. The bank lends money to new business owners and gives them coaching and support. Jiya said NBM has lent about K175 billion to SMEs. This shows the bank wants small businesses to do well. Managing Director of Mizu Events Gardens, Rehema Simbeye, said the expo was good for her business. She said it helps her reach more people in Mzuzu and makes people know her company better. Simbeye added that such events help SMEs learn from each other and become stronger.

CapeStars General Manager Darlington Ndasauka said times are hard but small businesses keep going. He said Malawians support local companies, which helps them stay alive. Even though growth is slow, he said they remain hopeful about the future.

The event was also fun for families. Many people came to enjoy live music from artists Eli Njuchi and Saint Realest. The expo showed how SMEs drive Malawi forward through work and new ideas.

ny.title: Mabizinesi 50 ang'onoang'ono akuwonetsa katundu wawo pa NBM Expo ku Mzuzu
ny.body: Mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono ndi apakatikati pafupifupi 50 adawonetsa zinthu zawo pa chiwonetsero ku Mzuzu Loweruka. National Bank of Malawi (NBM) plc idakonza chochitikachi pokumbukira Tsiku la Mabizinesi Ang'onoang'ono, Ang'onoang'ono ndi Apakatikati Padziko Lonse pa 27 June. Mabizinesi ochokera ku ulimi, kupanga zovala, ndi ntchito zolenga adatenga nawo gawo. Chiwonetserochi chidapatsa makampani ang'onoang'ono ndi apakatikati mwayi wokumana ndi makasitomala atsopano ndikukulitsa malonda awo.
ny.body_more:
Chochitikachi chinachitikira ku Springs Park ku Mzuzu. Mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono ochokera kudera lonse la kumpoto anabwera kudzawonetsa zomwe amachita komanso kulumikizana ndi anthu. Mkulu wa NBM, Harold Jiya, anati mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono ndi ofunikira kwambiri pa chuma cha Malawi. Anati amapanga ntchito ndikuthandiza dzikolo kukula. Jiya anauza khamulo kuti mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono amapanga pafupifupi 70 peresenti ya ntchito m'maiko osatukuka.

NBM ikupitiliza kuthandiza mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono kukula. Bankiyo imabwereketsa ndalama kwa eni mabizinesi atsopano ndikuwapatsa upangiri ndi chithandizo. Jiya anati NBM yabwereketsa ndalama zokwana K175 biliyoni kwa mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono. Izi zikusonyeza kuti bankiyo ikufuna kuti mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono azichita bwino. Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa Mizu Events Gardens, Rehema Simbeye, adati chiwonetserochi chinali chabwino pa bizinesi yake. Anati chimamuthandiza kufikira anthu ambiri ku Mzuzu ndikupangitsa anthu kudziwa bwino kampani yake. Simbeye anawonjezera kuti zochitika zotere zimathandiza mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono kuphunzirana ndikukhala olimba.

Woyang'anira Wamkulu wa CapeStars, Darlington Ndasauka, anati nthawi ndi yovuta koma mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono amapitilizabe. Anati anthu aku Malawi amathandizira makampani am'deralo, zomwe zimawathandiza kukhalabe ndi moyo. Ngakhale kukula kukuchepa, iye anati akuyembekezerabe za tsogolo.

Chochitikachi chinalinso chosangalatsa kwa mabanja. Anthu ambiri anabwera kudzasangalala ndi nyimbo zamoyo kuchokera kwa oimba Eli Njuchi ndi Saint Realest. Chiwonetserochi chinawonetsa momwe makampani ang'onoang'ono ndi apakatikati amayendetsera dziko la Malawi patsogolo kudzera mu ntchito ndi malingaliro atsopano.

en.title: Malawi Football Leaders End Dispute and Plan Unity
en.body: The Football Association of Malawi (FAM) and Super League of Malawi (Sulom) have agreed to end weeks of fighting. FAM president Fleetwood Haiya said the two groups met on Friday evening and decided to work together. The meeting happened during FAM's annual general meeting in Salima on Saturday.
en.body_more:
Haiya told people after the meeting that he was happy with what they agreed. He said FAM members made good progress in 2025 across four key areas of their plan. These areas include youth football, women's football, coach training, and fair rules.

Sulom president Brigadier General Gilbert Mittawa said the meeting was good. He said FAM and Sulom are like a father and child — they need to talk and solve problems together. Mittawa said both groups will now work on big problems, such as how clubs can help players better. He said they will not fight in public anymore. "All issues that people thought were problems between FAM and Sulom have been fixed," Mittawa said.

The two groups had been in dispute since April. Sulom used Kamuzu Stadium for a cup match without FAM's approval. FAM then brought four charges against Sulom. A hearing started on 20 June but did not finish. The case is now closed.

Haiya asked all football people in Malawi to stay together and help each other. He thanked member clubs, coaches, referees, and players for their work. Haiya said FAM exists to help all football groups in the country. He said the strength of Malawi football comes from teamwork and a shared dream for change.

ny.title: Atsogoleri a Mpira wa Mpira ku Malawi Athetsa Mikangano ndi Kukonzekera Umodzi
ny.body: Bungwe la Football Association of Malawi (FAM) ndi Super League of Malawi (Sulom) agwirizana kuthetsa mikangano ya milungu ingapo. Purezidenti wa FAM Fleetwood Haiya adati magulu awiriwa adakumana Lachisanu madzulo ndipo adaganiza zogwirira ntchito limodzi. Msonkhanowu udachitika pamsonkhano wapachaka wa FAM ku Salima Loweruka.
ny.body_more:
Haiya anauza anthu pambuyo pa msonkhano kuti akusangalala ndi zomwe adagwirizana. Anati mamembala a FAM adapita patsogolo bwino mu 2025 m'magawo anayi ofunikira a dongosolo lawo. Magawo awa akuphatikizapo mpira wa achinyamata, mpira wa azimayi, maphunziro a makocha, ndi malamulo olungama.

Purezidenti wa Sulom Brigadier General Gilbert Mittawa adati msonkhanowo unali wabwino. Anati FAM ndi Sulom ali ngati bambo ndi mwana - ayenera kukambirana ndikuthetsa mavuto pamodzi. Mittawa adati magulu onse awiri tsopano agwira ntchito pamavuto akuluakulu, monga momwe makalabu angathandizire osewera bwino. Anati sadzamenyananso pagulu. "Nkhani zonse zomwe anthu ankaganiza kuti ndi mavuto pakati pa FAM ndi Sulom zakonzedwa," adatero Mittawa.

Magulu awiriwa akhala akukangana kuyambira Epulo. Sulom idagwiritsa ntchito Kamuzu Stadium pamasewera a chikho popanda chilolezo cha FAM. Kenako FAM idapereka milandu inayi motsutsana ndi Sulom. Kumva kunayamba pa 20 June koma sikunathe. Mlanduwu watsekedwa tsopano.

Haiya adapempha anthu onse a mpira ku Malawi kuti akhale limodzi ndikuthandizana. Iye anayamikira makalabu a mamembala, aphunzitsi, oweruza milandu, ndi osewera chifukwa cha ntchito yawo. Haiya anati FAM ilipo kuti ithandize magulu onse a mpira mdziko muno. Anati mphamvu ya mpira wa ku Malawi imachokera ku mgwirizano komanso maloto ofanana a kusintha.

en.title: Police officer dies in accidental shooting at training school
en.body: A police sergeant died on Saturday after a fellow officer's rifle went off by accident at the Police Training School in Limbe, Blantyre. Sergeant Chiyembekezo Misinde, 41, was shot multiple times. Three other people were hurt and taken to Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital.
en.body_more:
The officers had come from Zomba and stopped at Limbe before going to Nkula firing range for tactical training. As they were getting ready to leave, Sergeant Kintalo Kadambo was checking an R4 rifle. He did not remove the magazine first. The magazine held 20 bullets and the rifle was set to automatic mode. The weapon fired 15 bullets in a burst.

Sergeant Misinde was hit by the bullets and died from blood loss. The other people hurt were Sergeant Laston Chabwera, Sergeant Kintalo Kadambo, and Davis Denis, a civilian police worker. They were all taken to Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital. Misinde's body was taken to Chiradzulu District Hospital for examination.

National Police spokesperson Lael Chimtembo said the trainer was checking if the gun was safe when it fired. "One of the trainers was checking his firearm to ensure it was safe. Unfortunately, he accidentally discharged the weapon, shooting colleagues who were standing nearby," Chimtembo said. Police are now investigating what caused the shot to go off. The incident happened during work to get ready for a tactical training exercise.

ny.title: Wapolisi wafa chifukwa chowombera mwangozi kusukulu yophunzitsira
ny.body: Sajenti wa apolisi wamwalira Loweruka mfuti ya wapolisi mnzake itaphulika mwangozi ku Sukulu Yophunzitsa Apolisi ku Limbe, Blantyre. Sajenti Chiyembekezo Misinde, wazaka 41, adawomberedwa kangapo. Anthu ena atatu adavulala ndipo adatengedwera kuchipatala cha Queen Elizabeth Central.
ny.body_more:
Apolisiwo anachokera ku Zomba ndipo anaima ku Limbe asanapite ku Nkula kukaphunzira zankhondo. Pamene anali kukonzekera kuchoka, Sergeant Kintalo Kadambo anali kuyang'ana mfuti ya R4. Sanachotse magaziniyo kaye. Magaziniyo inali ndi zipolopolo 20 ndipo mfutiyo inali itayikidwa yokha. Chidacho chinawombera zipolopolo 15 mwadzidzidzi.

Sergeant Misinde anagundidwa ndi zipolopolozo ndipo anamwalira chifukwa cha kutaya magazi. Anthu ena omwe anavulala anali Sergeant Laston Chabwera, Sergeant Kintalo Kadambo, ndi Davis Denis, wantchito wa apolisi wamba. Onsewa anatengedwa kupita ku Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital. Thupi la Misinde linatengedwa kupita ku Chiradzulu District Hospital kuti akawunikidwe.

Mneneri wa apolisi a dziko lino Lael Chimtembo anati mphunzitsiyo anali kuyang'ana ngati mfutiyo inali yotetezeka pamene inawomba. "Mmodzi mwa aphunzitsi anali kuyang'ana mfuti yake kuti atsimikizire kuti inali yotetezeka. Mwatsoka, mwangozi anatulutsa mfutiyo, nawombera anzake omwe anali pafupi," adatero Chimtembo. Apolisi tsopano akufufuza chomwe chinapangitsa kuti mfutiyo iphulike. Chochitikachi chinachitika panthawi ya ntchito kuti akonzekere maphunziro ankhondo.

en.title: Young man builds shoe business from small start
en.body: Blessings Phiri is 21 years old. He started by selling charcoal and tomatoes. Now he sells designer shoes online across Malawi. He says every big business starts small. He used money he saved and put all profits back into the work.
en.body_more:
Phiri chose charcoal and tomatoes because people needed them every day and they were easy to sell. He took pictures during slow days because he believed one day he would look back and feel proud. When sales were low, he did not give up. He kept working and saving money.

After a few years, the business grew strong. Phiri saw that many people wanted stylish shoes they could buy online. He used all the money he made from charcoal and tomatoes to start his new shoe business. He did not spend money on things he did not need. Every kwacha went back into the work. Today his shoe business serves customers from many parts of Malawi. He works with CTS Courier to send shoes to customers safely and fast.

But the business faces problems. Costs are high. More shops sell shoes now. Customers want expensive shoes that cost more money to buy. Phiri needs more money to grow. He focuses on good customer service and quality shoes to stay strong in the market.

Phiri says his success is not just about money. He helps more than 30 children with school books, pens and food. He wants young people to see that small starts can lead to big things. In the future, he wants to open real shops, farm land, and give jobs to other people. He tells others: start with what you have, work hard, trust God, and never give up.

ny.title: Mnyamata wapanga bizinesi ya nsapato kuyambira ali wamng'ono
ny.body: Blessings Phiri ali ndi zaka 21. Anayamba ndi kugulitsa makala ndi tomato. Tsopano amagulitsa nsapato zapamwamba pa intaneti ku Malawi konse. Akuti bizinesi iliyonse yayikulu imayamba ndi yaying'ono. Amagwiritsa ntchito ndalama zomwe adasunga ndikubwezeretsa phindu lonse pantchitoyo.
ny.body_more:
Phiri ankasankha makala ndi tomato chifukwa anthu ankawafuna tsiku lililonse ndipo anali osavuta kuwagulitsa. Ankajambula zithunzi m'masiku ochedwa chifukwa ankakhulupirira kuti tsiku lina adzayang'ana m'mbuyo ndikudzikuza. Pamene malonda anali otsika, sanasiye. Anapitiriza kugwira ntchito ndikusunga ndalama.

Patatha zaka zingapo, bizinesiyo inakula kwambiri. Phiri anaona kuti anthu ambiri ankafuna nsapato zokongola zomwe angagule pa intaneti. Anagwiritsa ntchito ndalama zonse zomwe adapeza kuchokera ku makala ndi tomato kuyambitsa bizinesi yake yatsopano ya nsapato. Sanagwiritse ntchito ndalama pazinthu zomwe sankafunikira. Kwacha iliyonse inabwerera kuntchito. Masiku ano bizinesi yake ya nsapato imatumikira makasitomala ochokera m'madera ambiri a Malawi. Amagwira ntchito ndi CTS Courier kutumiza nsapato kwa makasitomala mosamala komanso mwachangu.

Koma bizinesiyo ikukumana ndi mavuto. Mitengo ndi yokwera. Masitolo ambiri amagulitsa nsapato tsopano. Makasitomala akufuna nsapato zodula zomwe zimadula ndalama zambiri kuti agule. Phiri akufunika ndalama zambiri kuti akule. Amayang'ana kwambiri ntchito yabwino kwa makasitomala ndi nsapato zabwino kuti akhale olimba pamsika.

Phiri akunena kuti kupambana kwake sikungokhudza ndalama zokha. Amathandiza ana opitilira 30 ndi mabuku a kusukulu, mapensulo ndi chakudya. Akufuna kuti achinyamata aone kuti kuyamba pang'ono kungayambitse zinthu zazikulu. M'tsogolomu, akufuna kutsegula masitolo enieni, malo olima, ndikupereka ntchito kwa anthu ena. Amauza ena kuti: yambani ndi zomwe muli nazo, gwirani ntchito molimbika, khulupirirani Mulungu, ndipo musataye mtima.

en.title: ESCOM and Press Energy sign power deal for Malawi
en.body: Malawi's power supply will grow after two companies signed a big agreement on 26 June 2026 in Blantyre. The Electricity Supply Corporation of Malawi (ESCOM) and Press Energy Limited agreed to add 50 megawatts of solar power to the national grid. This will help reduce blackouts and power cuts that affect homes and businesses across the country.
en.body_more:
Press Energy Limited, which is owned by Press Corporation Limited, will build a transmission line. This line will carry electricity from a 50 megawatt solar power plant at Nkhoma in Lilongwe District to ESCOM's network. The agreement was signed at Umoyo House in Blantyre on Friday. This deal follows an earlier agreement signed in December 2024 that allowed Press Energy to build the solar plant.

ESCOM Chief Executive Officer Eng. William Kaipa said the deal shows progress. "In April, we made a promise to Malawians that within eight months we would improve power supply and stop load shedding. Today's agreement is another important step," Kaipa said. He added that ESCOM will keep working with other power companies to add more electricity. "We are connecting power to the grid, but also connecting Malawians to chances and businesses to growth," he said.

Press Corporation Limited Chief Executive Officer Professor Ronald Mangani said the company has a duty to help Malawi. "Press Corporation is a Malawian company, and we believe it is our duty to make life better for Malawians and help the country grow," Prof. Mangani said. He said the project will give Malawi more power and make the country safer for business. Once finished, the 50 megawatt plant should greatly reduce blackouts in homes and factories across Malawi.

ny.title: ESCOM ndi Press Energy asayina mgwirizano wamagetsi ku Malawi
ny.body: Mphamvu yamagetsi ku Malawi ikula makampani awiri atasaina mgwirizano waukulu pa 26 June 2026 ku Blantyre. Kampani Yopereka Mphamvu ya Magetsi ku Malawi (ESCOM) ndi Press Energy Limited adagwirizana kuwonjezera ma megawatts 50 a mphamvu ya dzuwa ku gridi ya dziko. Izi zithandiza kuchepetsa kuzimitsidwa kwa magetsi ndi kuchepetsedwa kwa magetsi komwe kumakhudza nyumba ndi mabizinesi mdziko lonselo.
ny.body_more:
Press Energy Limited, yomwe ndi ya Press Corporation Limited, ipanga chingwe chotumizira magetsi. Mzerewu udzanyamula magetsi kuchokera ku fakitale yamagetsi yamphamvu yamphamvu ya 50 megawatts ku Nkhoma m'boma la Lilongwe kupita ku netiweki ya ESCOM. Mgwirizanowu udasainidwa ku Umoyo House ku Blantyre Lachisanu. Mgwirizanowu ukutsatira mgwirizano wakale womwe udasainidwa mu Disembala 2024 womwe udalola Press Energy kumanga fakitale yamagetsi yamphamvu ya dzuŵa.

Mkulu wa ESCOM Eng. William Kaipa adati mgwirizanowu ukuwonetsa kupita patsogolo. "Mu Epulo, tidalonjeza anthu aku Malawi kuti mkati mwa miyezi isanu ndi itatu tidzakonza magetsi ndikuletsa kutsika kwa magetsi. Mgwirizano wa lero ndi gawo lina lofunika," adatero Kaipa. Anawonjezera kuti ESCOM ipitiliza kugwira ntchito ndi makampani ena amagetsi kuti awonjezere magetsi ambiri. "Tikulumikiza magetsi ku gridi, komanso kulumikiza anthu aku Malawi ku mwayi ndi mabizinesi kuti akule," adatero.

Mkulu wa Press Corporation Limited Pulofesa Ronald Mangani adati kampaniyo ili ndi udindo wothandiza Malawi. "Press Corporation ndi kampani ya ku Malawi, ndipo tikukhulupirira kuti ndi udindo wathu kupangitsa moyo wa anthu aku Malawi kukhala wabwino komanso kuthandiza dzikolo kukula," adatero Pulofesa Mangani. Iye anati ntchitoyi ipatsa Malawi mphamvu zambiri ndikupangitsa dzikolo kukhala lotetezeka pa bizinesi. Ikamalizidwa, fakitale ya 50 megawatt iyenera kuchepetsa kwambiri kuzima kwa magetsi m'nyumba ndi mafakitale ku Malawi konse.

en.title: Airtel and TNM raise data bundle prices without telling customers
en.body: Airtel Malawi and TNM raised the prices of their data bundles on Friday without telling their customers first. TNM changed its Mixit Weekly bundle from K2,100 to K2,500 but gave less data — from 3GB down to 2GB. Airtel made the same change to its Chezafaya Weekly bundle. Many people were angry and complained on social media.
en.body_more:
TNM's Mixit Weekly bundle now costs K2,500 instead of K2,100, but the data fell from 3GB to 2GB. Airtel's Chezafaya Weekly bundle had the same price change and data cut. Users on social media said the two companies should have told them before making these changes.

TNM and Airtel also offer 30-day bundles at different prices. TNM's Smart packages start at 1GB for K4,000 and go up to a 66GB bundle for K40,000. Airtel's MoFaya bundles match TNM's low price of K4,000 for 1GB. At the high end, Airtel gives 65GB for K40,000 plus a 20 percent bonus of 13GB, making 78GB total. Both companies charge the same price — K4,000 for 1GB and K22,000 for bundles around 25GB to 28GB.

Some people on social media said the price rises show how the market works in Malawi. They said that when one company raises prices, the other one follows because there are only a few big mobile networks. One person wrote that Airtel Malawi, TNM and MTL are in an oligopoly — when just a few companies control the market. Another person said that while bundle prices go up, agents who sell the bundles get the same low pay. They said one agent received K1,750 from selling K12,000 worth of bundles. By Thursday, neither Airtel nor TNM had given any reason for the price changes or the smaller data amounts.

ny.title: Airtel ndi TNM akweza mitengo ya ma data bundle popanda kuuza makasitomala awo
ny.body: Airtel Malawi ndi TNM adakweza mitengo ya ma data bundle awo Lachisanu osauza makasitomala awo kaye. TNM idasintha ma data bundle ake a Mixit Weekly kuchoka pa K2,100 kufika pa K2,500 koma idapereka data yochepa — kuchoka pa 3GB kufika pa 2GB. Airtel idasinthanso ma data bundle ake a Chezafaya Weekly. Anthu ambiri adakwiya ndipo adadandaula pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti.
ny.body_more:
Phukusi la Mixit Weekly la TNM tsopano limadula K2,500 m'malo mwa K2,100, koma detayo yatsika kuchoka pa 3GB kufika pa 2GB. Phukusi la Chezafaya Weekly la Airtel linasintha mtengo mofanana komanso kuchepetsedwa kwa deta. Ogwiritsa ntchito pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti anati makampani awiriwa ayenera kuwauza asanasinthe izi.

TNM ndi Airtel amaperekanso maphukusi a masiku 30 pamitengo yosiyana. Maphukusi a Smart a TNM amayambira pa 1GB pa K4,000 ndipo amafika pa 66GB pa phukusi la K40,000. Maphukusi a MoFaya a Airtel akufanana ndi mtengo wotsika wa TNM wa K4,000 pa 1GB. Pamtengo wapamwamba, Airtel imapereka 65GB pa K40,000 kuphatikiza bonasi ya 20 peresenti ya 13GB, zomwe zimapangitsa kuti 78GB yonse ikhale 78GB. Makampani onsewa amalipiritsa mtengo womwewo — K4,000 pa 1GB ndi K22,000 pa maphukusi pafupifupi 25GB mpaka 28GB.

Anthu ena pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti anati kukwera kwa mitengo kukusonyeza momwe msika umagwirira ntchito ku Malawi. Anati kampani imodzi ikakweza mitengo, inayo imatsatira chifukwa pali ma network akuluakulu ochepa chabe a mafoni. Munthu mmodzi analemba kuti Airtel Malawi, TNM ndi MTL ali mu oligopoly - pomwe makampani ochepa okha ndi omwe amalamulira msika. Munthu wina anati ngakhale mitengo ya ma bundle ikukwera, othandizira omwe amagulitsa ma bundle amalandira malipiro otsika omwewo. Anati wothandizira mmodzi adalandira K1,750 chifukwa chogulitsa ma bundle a K12,000. Pofika Lachinayi, Airtel kapena TNM sanapereke chifukwa chilichonse chosinthira mitengo kapena kuchuluka kwa deta kochepa.

en.title: Mary Chilima to give evidence in plane crash inquiry
en.body: Mary Chilima, widow of former Vice President Saulos Klaus Chilima, will speak before Parliament on Monday, June 29, 2026. She will answer questions about the plane crash in Chikangawa Forest in June 2024. All nine people died in the crash, including former First Lady Patricia Shanil Muluzi.
en.body_more:
Parliament opened this new inquiry because the first report left some questions unanswered. A Commission of Inquiry was set up by former President Lazarus Chakwera on October 25, 2024. Justice Jabbar Alide led this commission. It finished work and gave its final report in December 2024. Now Parliament has made a fresh Ad-Hoc Committee to look at the crash again in 2026.

The plane crashed on Monday, June 10, 2024. It was a Malawi Defence Force Dornier 228. Vice President Chilima and his group were flying from Kamuzu International Airport in Lilongwe to Mzuzu Airport. They were going to the funeral of former Attorney General Ralph Kasambara. The plane went down in Chikangawa Forest Reserve in Nkhata Bay District. Everyone on the plane died.

Mrs. Chilima has been formally asked to come and give evidence. The committee wants to hear her account of what happened. Public interest is high. Many people in Malawi want to know the truth about the crash. The Ad-Hoc Committee hopes her words will help build a full record of the tragedy.

Meanwhile, current and former Malawi Air Force engineers have told Parliament to be careful. They said the inquiry should not make technical conclusions without help from trained aviation accident investigators. They warned that mixing up military and civil aviation systems could give wrong findings.

ny.title: Mary Chilima apereka umboni wake pa kafukufuku wokhudza ngozi ya ndege
ny.body: Mary Chilima, mkazi wamasiye wa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Klaus Chilima, adzalankhula pamaso pa Nyumba Yamalamulo Lolemba, pa 29 Juni, 2026. Adzayankha mafunso okhudza ngozi ya ndege ku Nkhalango ya Chikangawa mu June 2024. Anthu onse asanu ndi anayi adamwalira pangoziyi, kuphatikizapo mkazi woyamba wakale Patricia Shanil Muluzi.
ny.body_more:
Nyumba Yamalamulo inatsegula kafukufuku watsopanowu chifukwa lipoti loyamba linasiya mafunso ena osayankhidwa. Komiti Yofufuza idakhazikitsidwa ndi Purezidenti wakale Lazarus Chakwera pa Okutobala 25, 2024. Woweruza Jabbar Alide adatsogolera komishoniyi. Inamaliza ntchito ndipo idapereka lipoti lake lomaliza mu Disembala 2024. Tsopano Nyumba Yamalamulo yapanga Komiti Yatsopano Yoyang'anira kuti iwunikenso ngoziyi mu 2026.

Ndegeyo idagwa Lolemba, Juni 10, 2024. Unali gulu la Malawi Defense Force Dornier 228. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Chilima ndi gulu lake anali kuuluka kuchokera ku Kamuzu International Airport ku Lilongwe kupita ku Mzuzu Airport. Ankapita ku maliro a wakale Attorney General Ralph Kasambara. Ndegeyo idagwa ku Chikangawa Forest Reserve ku Nkhata Bay District. Aliyense amene anali mu ndegeyo adamwalira.

Ayi Chilima apemphedwa mwalamulo kuti abwere kudzapereka umboni. Komitiyi ikufuna kumva zomwe zidachitika. Chidwi cha anthu onse chilipo. Anthu ambiri ku Malawi akufuna kudziwa zoona za ngoziyi. Komiti Yoyang'anira Zachitetezo ikuyembekeza kuti mawu ake athandiza kupanga mbiri yonse ya tsokali.

Pakadali pano, mainjiniya a Air Force omwe alipo pano komanso akale a Malawi auza Nyumba Yamalamulo kuti ikhale yosamala. Iwo anati kafukufukuyu sayenera kupanga ziganizo zaukadaulo popanda thandizo kuchokera kwa ofufuza ngozi za ndege ophunzitsidwa bwino. Iwo adachenjeza kuti kusakaniza machitidwe ankhondo ndi ndege za anthu wamba kungapereke zotsatira zolakwika.

en.title: Standard Bank says Malawi's economy is getting better
en.body: Standard Bank plc says falling prices, lower interest rates, more fuel, and more food are helping Malawi's economy grow. The bank's chief, Phillip Madinga, spoke at its yearly meeting in Blantyre on Friday. He said prices have fallen to 23.4 percent. The bank made K122 billion profit in 2025, up 41 percent from the year before.
en.body_more:
Board chairperson Alex Mkandawire told shareholders that the bank made K122 billion profit after tax in the year ending 31 December 2025. This was 41 percent more than K118 billion the year before. Mkandawire said the results show the bank is strong even though times are hard.

Madinga told the meeting that prices falling will help the government cut interest rates soon. When the main interest rate falls, banks charge less money to lend. This means families and businesses can borrow money more cheaply. He said this will help more people and shops to borrow and grow their work. He also said fuel and food are easier to find now. These things are helping people and shops feel less worried about money.

The bank's net interest income grew 47 percent in the year. Loans to customers grew 31 percent, and money in investments grew 63 percent. Frank Harawa, from the Minority Shareholders Association of Listed Companies, said Standard Bank did well. He said the bank was smart to save money early for problems with debt. Standard Bank is one of five banks on the Malawi Stock Exchange. The others are FDH Bank plc, National Bank of Malawi plc, NBS Bank plc, and FMB Capital Holdings plc, which owns First Capital Bank.

ny.title: Standard Bank yati chuma cha Malawi chikuyenda bwino
ny.body: Standard Bank plc yati kutsika kwa mitengo, kutsika kwa chiwongola dzanja, mafuta ambiri, ndi chakudya chochuluka zikuthandizira kukula kwa chuma cha Malawi. Mkulu wa bankiyo, Phillip Madinga, adalankhula pamsonkhano wake wapachaka ku Blantyre Lachisanu. Iye adati mitengo yatsika kufika pa 23.4 peresenti. Bankiyo idapeza phindu la K122 biliyoni mu 2025, lomwe ndi lokwera ndi 41 peresenti poyerekeza ndi chaka cham'mbuyomo.
ny.body_more:
Wapampando wa bungweli, Alex Mkandawire, adauza eni masheya kuti bankiyi idapeza phindu la K122 biliyoni pambuyo pa msonkho chaka chomwe chimatha pa 31 Disembala 2025. Izi zinali zochulukirapo ndi 41 peresenti kuposa K118 biliyoni chaka chatha. Mkandawire adati zotsatira zake zikuwonetsa kuti bankiyi ndi yolimba ngakhale kuti nthawi ndi zovuta.

Madinga adauza msonkhanowo kuti mitengo ikutsika ithandiza boma kuchepetsa chiwongola dzanja posachedwa. Chiwongola dzanja chachikulu chikatsika, mabanki amalipiritsa ndalama zochepa kuti abwereke. Izi zikutanthauza kuti mabanja ndi mabizinesi akhoza kubwereka ndalama motchipa. Anati izi zithandiza anthu ambiri ndi masitolo kubwereka ndikukulitsa ntchito zawo. Anatinso mafuta ndi chakudya ndizosavuta kupeza tsopano. Zinthu izi zikuthandiza anthu ndi masitolo kuti asadandaule kwambiri ndi ndalama.

Ndalama zonse zomwe bankiyi imapeza mu chiwongola dzanja zidakwera ndi 47 peresenti pachaka. Ngongole kwa makasitomala zidakwera ndi 31 peresenti, ndipo ndalama zomwe zimayikidwa mu ndalama zidakwera ndi 63 peresenti. Frank Harawa, wochokera ku Minority Shareholders Association of Listed Companies, adati Standard Bank idachita bwino. Anati bankiyi inali yanzeru kusunga ndalama msanga pamavuto a ngongole. Standard Bank ndi imodzi mwa mabanki asanu omwe ali pa Malawi Stock Exchange. Zina ndi FDH Bank plc, National Bank of Malawi plc, NBS Bank plc, ndi FMB Capital Holdings plc, yomwe ndi mwini wa First Capital Bank.

en.title: Farmer groups get 15 tractors to grow food better
en.body: Fourteen farmer groups across Malawi have got 15 tractors. The Agriculture Ministry gave them to the groups on Thursday at Capital Hill in Lilongwe. Minister Roza Mbilizi said the tractors will help farmers move away from old ways and use machines instead. This is part of a big plan to make farming modern and strong.
en.body_more:
The tractors came with tools like ploughs, harrows, ridgers and trailers. The World Bank gave money for this through the Malawi Food Systems Resilience Programme. Mbilizi said this work helps Malawi reach its big goals. The country wants to grow more food, earn money from farming, and help poor families in villages.

Mbilizi was happy that many women farmers will get the machines. She said women do most of the farming work in Malawi. She told women to use tractors and other tools to work better and have less hard work. The government wants all farmers, especially women, to stop using only their hands and use machines instead.

Ted Nankhumwa runs the farm programme. He said the tractors help farmers sell more food. He said if farmers use machines, it costs less to grow food. This helps them sell to other countries. Farmers can also farm more land now because machines are faster than hand work.

Jenini Chikwondwa got a tractor for her group, Anzanga Group. She said the tractor will help them grow more sunflower in Malingunde. She said they can now buy more land and make the group bigger. Mbilizi told all the groups to take good care of the machines and fix them when they break. She said if the groups do this, the tractors will work well for many years.

ny.title: Magulu a alimi apeza mathirakitala 15 kuti alime bwino chakudya
ny.body: Magulu khumi ndi anayi a alimi ku Malawi konse apeza mathirakitala 15. Unduna wa Zaulimi unapereka mathirakitalawa kwa maguluwo Lachinayi ku Capital Hill ku Lilongwe. Nduna Roza Mbilizi anati mathirakitalawa athandiza alimi kusiya njira zakale ndikugwiritsa ntchito makina m'malo mwake. Ichi ndi gawo la dongosolo lalikulu lopangitsa ulimi kukhala wamakono komanso wolimba.
ny.body_more:
Matrakitala anabwera ndi zida monga mapulawu, ma harrow, ma ridger ndi ma trailer. Banki Yapadziko Lonse inapereka ndalama zochitira izi kudzera mu Malawi Food Systems Resilience Programme. Mbilizi anati ntchitoyi ikuthandiza Malawi kukwaniritsa zolinga zake zazikulu. Dzikoli likufuna kulima chakudya chochuluka, kupeza ndalama kuchokera ku ulimi, ndi kuthandiza mabanja osauka m'midzi.

Mbilizi anali wokondwa kuti akazi ambiri alimi adzapeza makinawo. Anati akazi ndi omwe amagwira ntchito zambiri zaulimi ku Malawi. Anauza akazi kuti agwiritse ntchito matrakitala ndi zida zina kuti agwire ntchito bwino komanso kuti asamagwire ntchito molimbika kwambiri. Boma likufuna kuti alimi onse, makamaka akazi, asiye kugwiritsa ntchito manja awo okha ndikugwiritsa ntchito makina m'malo mwake.

Ted Nankhumwa ndiye amayendetsa pulogalamu yaulimi. Anati matrakitala amathandiza alimi kugulitsa chakudya chochuluka. Anati ngati alimi agwiritsa ntchito makina, zimawononga ndalama zochepa kulima chakudya. Izi zimawathandiza kugulitsa kumayiko ena. Alimi amathanso kulima malo ambiri tsopano chifukwa makina ndi othamanga kuposa ntchito yamanja.

Jenini Chikwondwa adapeza thirakitala ya gulu lake, Anzanga Group. Anati thirakitala idzawathandiza kulima mpendadzuwa wambiri ku Malingunde. Iye anati tsopano akhoza kugula malo ambiri ndikukulitsa gululo. Mbilizi adauza magulu onse kuti asamalire bwino makinawo ndikukonza akawonongeka. Iye adati ngati maguluwo achita izi, mathirakitala adzagwira ntchito bwino kwa zaka zambiri.

en.title: Premiership clubs will not go to FAM meeting in Salima
en.body: All 16 teams in the FDH Bank Premiership have said no to a Football Association of Malawi (FAM) meeting in Salima. The teams made this choice at a meeting on Thursday. Sulom, which runs the top league, says FAM tried to push clubs to attend by offering money.
en.body_more:
Gilbert Mittawa, the Sulom president, said FAM officials called clubs late at night and asked them to come to the meeting. He asked: "Is that football?" But FAM president said about six or seven teams had called him to say they were coming.

Mittawa held a virtual meeting with the clubs on Thursday morning. He said the clubs asked for this meeting because they were not happy. FAM had invited all 16 clubs to the AGM without telling Sulom first. The clubs had a problem because FAM's own rules say who should attend. The letter said it was about a workshop, but it was really about the AGM, so the clubs said no.

FAM president said FAM can talk to the clubs because they play in FAM tournaments and get their players through FAM. He said the clubs are licensed by FAM, not by Sulom. He said the meeting was to hear what clubs think about a new reserve league. But Mittawa said this was never in the letter. FAM president also said he would like to see the clubs run the league without Sulom. Mittawa said FAM should have tried this idea when FAM president was the Sulom leader.

Sulom wrote to FAM and said all talk between FAM and Sulom must go through Sulom. The letter also said FAM and Sulom must fix their problems for the good of football. Mittawa said he will not try to be Sulom president again next year, but he will work until his time ends.

ny.title: Makalabu a Premiership sapita ku msonkhano wa FAM ku Salima
ny.body: Magulu onse 16 omwe ali mu FDH Bank Premiership anena kuti ayi pamsonkhano wa Football Association of Malawi (FAM) womwe unachitika ku Salima. Maguluwa adasankha izi pamsonkhano womwe unachitika Lachinayi. Sulom, yomwe imayendetsa ligi yayikulu, ikunena kuti FAM idayesa kukakamiza makalabu kuti abwere popereka ndalama.
ny.body_more:
Gilbert Mittawa, purezidenti wa Sulom, anati akuluakulu a FAM anaimbira makalabu usiku kwambiri ndikuwapempha kuti abwere ku msonkhanowo. Anafunsa kuti: "Kodi ndi mpira?" Koma purezidenti wa FAM anati magulu pafupifupi asanu ndi limodzi kapena asanu ndi awiri anamuimbira foni kuti amuuze kuti akubwera.

Mittawa adachita msonkhano wa pa intaneti ndi makalabu Lachinayi m'mawa. Anati makalabu adapempha msonkhanowu chifukwa sanasangalale. FAM idayitana makalabu onse 16 ku AGM popanda kuuza Sulom kaye. Makalabu anali ndi vuto chifukwa malamulo a FAM amanena kuti ndani ayenera kupezekapo. Kalatayo inati inali yokhudza msonkhano, koma kwenikweni inali yokhudza AGM, kotero makalabu adati ayi.

Purezidenti wa FAM adati FAM ikhoza kulankhula ndi makalabu chifukwa amasewera m'mipikisano ya FAM ndikupangitsa osewera awo kudzera mu FAM. Anati makalabu ali ndi chilolezo cha FAM, osati cha Sulom. Anati msonkhanowo unali woti amve zomwe makalabu amaganiza za ligi yatsopano yosungira. Koma Mittawa adati izi sizinali m'kalatayo. Purezidenti wa FAM adatinso akufuna kuwona makalabu akuyendetsa ligi popanda Sulom. Mittawa anati FAM iyenera kuyesa lingaliro ili pamene pulezidenti wa FAM anali mtsogoleri wa Sulom.

Sulom adalembera FAM ndipo adati zokambirana zonse pakati pa FAM ndi Sulom ziyenera kupitilira ku Sulom. Kalatayo idatinso FAM ndi Sulom ayenera kukonza mavuto awo kuti mpira ukhale wabwino. Mittawa adati sadzayesanso kukhala pulezidenti wa Sulom chaka chamawa, koma adzagwira ntchito mpaka nthawi yake itatha.

en.title: Miss Malawi tells youth: learn skills, not just degrees
en.body: Thandi Chisi, Miss Malawi 2025, told hundreds of young people on Thursday that skills matter more than degrees. She spoke at the closing of the National Skills Competition at Gateway Mall in Lilongwe. Chisi said artificial intelligence cannot replace human creativity and problem-solving. She called on young Malawians to learn trades instead of following the degree path alone.
en.body_more:
Chisi founded the Sustainable Fashion and Women Empowerment Initiative, which has trained more than 600 women in tailoring, fashion, and design across Malawi. She used her work to show why skills change lives. "A skill is not just something you do with your hands. It is an asset that nobody can take away from you. It is dignity, independence and a pathway to opportunity," she said.

When one woman learns a skill, she feeds her family, educates her children, and helps her community, Chisi explained. She said the strongest economies are built by skilled people, not resources alone. "Every skill learned today becomes a building block for the Malawi we want tomorrow," she told the crowd.

The three-day competition was held under the theme "Showcasing Talent and Skills, Fostering National Growth." Minister of Labour, Skills and Innovation Joel Chigona attended as guest of honour. Young people from across Malawi came to show what they could do in trades like mechanics, construction, and fashion. The event brought together government, training schools, and businesses to support vocational education.

Chisi's message comes at a time when many young Malawians cannot find jobs. The government wants to focus on building more local industry and skills. Foreign workers from Malawi have faced problems abroad in recent months, so good jobs at home are now more important than ever. Many people at the event said it was important that the most well-known young woman in Malawi chose to speak for tailors, mechanics, and technicians instead of easier topics.

ny.title: Abiti Malawi akuuza achinyamata kuti: phunzirani maluso, osati madigiri okha
ny.body: Thandi Chisi, Miss Malawi 2025, adauza achinyamata mazana ambiri Lachinayi kuti luso ndi lofunika kwambiri kuposa madigiri. Adalankhula potseka Mpikisano wa Maluso Padziko Lonse ku Gateway Mall ku Lilongwe. Chisi adati luntha lochita kupanga silingalowe m'malo mwa luso la anthu komanso kuthetsa mavuto. Adapempha achinyamata aku Malawi kuti aphunzire ntchito zamanja m'malo mongotsatira njira ya digiri yokha.
ny.body_more:
Chisi anayambitsa bungwe la Sustainable Fashion and Women Empowerment Initiative, lomwe laphunzitsa akazi oposa 600 kusoka, mafashoni, ndi kupanga mapulani ku Malawi konse. Anagwiritsa ntchito ntchito yake kusonyeza chifukwa chake luso limasintha miyoyo. "Luso si chinthu chomwe munthu amachita ndi manja ake okha. Ndi chuma chomwe palibe amene angakuchotsere. Ndi ulemu, kudziyimira pawokha komanso njira yopezera mwayi," adatero.

Mayi wina akaphunzira luso, amadyetsa banja lake, amaphunzitsa ana ake, komanso amathandiza anthu ammudzi mwake, Chisi anafotokoza. Iye anati chuma champhamvu kwambiri chimamangidwa ndi anthu aluso, osati zinthu zokha. "Luso lililonse lomwe laphunziridwa lero limakhala maziko a Malawi yomwe tikufuna mawa," adatero kwa khamulo.

Mpikisano wa masiku atatu unachitika pansi pa mutu wakuti "Kuwonetsa Luso ndi Maluso, Kulimbikitsa Kukula kwa Dziko." Nduna ya Labor, Luso ndi Zatsopano Joel Chigona adakhalapo ngati mlendo wolemekezeka. Achinyamata ochokera ku Malawi konse anabwera kudzawonetsa zomwe angachite pantchito monga makanika, zomangamanga, ndi mafashoni. Chochitikachi chinasonkhanitsa boma, masukulu ophunzitsa, ndi mabizinesi kuti athandizire maphunziro aukadaulo.

Uthenga wa Chisi ukubwera panthawi yomwe achinyamata ambiri aku Malawi sangapeze ntchito. Boma likufuna kuyang'ana kwambiri pakupanga mafakitale ndi luso la anthu am'deralo. Ogwira ntchito ochokera kumayiko ena ochokera ku Malawi akumana ndi mavuto kunja kwa dziko m'miyezi yaposachedwa, kotero ntchito zabwino kunyumba tsopano ndizofunikira kwambiri kuposa kale lonse. Anthu ambiri omwe anali pamsonkhanowu adati ndikofunikira kuti mtsikana wodziwika bwino kwambiri ku Malawi asankhe kulankhula m'malo mwa osoka, makaniko, ndi akatswiri m'malo mwa mitu yosavuta.

en.title: Malawi Races to Bring Home People from South Africa
en.body: The Department of Disaster Management Affairs (Dodma) is working to bring Malawians back home from South Africa. Fighting against immigrants has grown worse in recent weeks. Anti-immigrant groups gave foreign workers until June 30, 2026 to leave. Dodma says it has brought back more than 3,000 Malawians so far, but cannot promise all will return by the deadline.
en.body_more:
Dodma director Charles Matabwa spoke in Blantyre yesterday. He said the chances of finishing by June 30 are 50:50. "Our plan is to bring almost all Malawians home," he said. "But I don't want to promise we will manage it."

About 15,000 Malawians first asked for help to return. But reports from South Africa now say the number is closer to 30,000. By June 22, Dodma records showed 2,845 people had arrived home. The Malawi Government planned to hire 55 buses. Almost half of these buses have already brought people back. The South African Government, Prophet Shepherd Bushiri, and Gift of the Givers Foundation also gave buses to help.

Dodma moved its arrival camp from Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre to Joyce Chitsulo Stadium in Mwanza. This change came after South Africa decided to drop returnees at Mwanza Border and gave 40 buses to help.

One returnee, Adija Bwanali, said she was happy to be home safe. But she said she would think about going back to South Africa if things get better. The anti-immigrant groups March and March and Operation Dudula say immigrants, especially those without papers, are taking jobs from South African people. These groups say all undocumented foreigners must leave by June 30.

ny.title: Malawi Yayamba Kupikisana Kuti Ibweretse Anthu Ochokera ku South Africa
ny.body: Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka (Dodma) ikugwira ntchito yobwezeretsa anthu aku Malawi kuchokera ku South Africa. Kulimbana ndi anthu othawa kwawo kwakula kwambiri m'masabata aposachedwa. Magulu otsutsa anthu othawa kwawo apatsa antchito akunja ntchito mpaka pa June 30, 2026 kuti achoke. Dodma akuti yabweretsa anthu aku Malawi oposa 3,000 mpaka pano, koma sangalonjeze kuti onse adzabwerera pofika nthawi yomaliza.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri wa Dodma, Charles Matabwa, adalankhula ku Blantyre dzulo. Iye adati mwayi woti anthu azitha pofika pa June 30 ndi 50:50. "Dongosolo lathu ndi kubweretsa anthu pafupifupi onse aku Malawi kunyumba," adatero. "Koma sindikufuna kulonjeza kuti tidzayendetsa bwino."

Anthu pafupifupi 15,000 aku Malawi adapempha thandizo kuti abwerere. Koma malipoti ochokera ku South Africa tsopano akuti chiwerengerochi chili pafupi ndi 30,000. Pofika pa June 22, zolemba za Dodma zidawonetsa kuti anthu 2,845 afika kunyumba. Boma la Malawi likukonzekera kubwereka mabasi 55. Pafupifupi theka la mabasi awa abweretsa kale anthu. Boma la South Africa, Prophet Shepherd Bushiri, ndi Gift of the Givers Foundation nawonso adapereka mabasi kuti athandize.

Dodma idasamutsa msasa wake wofikira anthu kuchokera ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre kupita ku Joyce Chitsulo Stadium ku Mwanza. Kusinthaku kudachitika South Africa itaganiza zosiya anthu obwerera ku Mwanza Border ndikupereka mabasi 40 kuti athandize.

Mmodzi wobwerera, Adija Bwanali, adati anali wokondwa kukhala kunyumba otetezeka. Koma anati angaganize zobwerera ku South Africa ngati zinthu zikuyenda bwino. Magulu otsutsa anthu othawa kwawo a March ndi March ndi Operation Dudula akuti anthu othawa kwawo, makamaka omwe alibe mapepala, akutenga ntchito kuchokera kwa anthu aku South Africa. Maguluwa akuti alendo onse osavomerezeka ayenera kuchoka pofika pa 30 June.

en.title: Old mining rules slow down Malawi's sector growth
en.body: Malawi still uses mining rules from 1985, even though the country made new laws in 2019 and 2023. The old rules slow down growth in the mining sector. The government drafted new rules to go with the 2023 law, but has not yet published them. This leaves the mining sector unclear and hard to work with.
en.body_more:
The official form for a Reserved Minerals Licence still names the Mines and Minerals (Reserved Minerals Licence) Regulations of 1985 as the law it follows. This is even though Malawi passed a new Mines and Minerals Act in 2019 and then Law No. 25 of 2023. The 2023 law covers all prospecting, exploration and mining of minerals in the country.

The Ministry of Mining asked people to give feedback on ten sets of draft Mineral Title Regulations. These rules would cover exploration licences, mining licences, community development agreements and mine rehabilitation. The deadline for feedback was June 30, 2024. But the new rules have not been published in the Gazette since then. The Mining and Minerals Regulatory Authority (MMRA) was made under the 2023 law to run the sector and check that licence holders follow the rules. Without new published rules, the MMRA cannot fully enforce the new law.

Kennedy Rashid, the National Coordinator of the Natural Resources Justice Network (NRJN), said that using old rules creates a gap in how the sector is run. He said this risks making things unclear for investors and reducing protection for communities. Mining expert Grain Malunga said the delay is likely because the Ministry of Justice must check the rules before they are published.

Mining Minister Thoko Tembo said the new rules were drafted after the 2019 Act was passed. The 2023 changes moved power from the ministry to the MMRA, so all draft rules had to be checked again. The ministry and MMRA then made ten sets of Mineral Title Regulations and one more set on mineral processing and value addition. This follows a 2025 Presidential Order that bans the export of raw minerals.

ny.title: Malamulo akale a migodi achepetsa kukula kwa gawo la Malawi
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi likugwiritsabe ntchito malamulo a migodi kuyambira mu 1985, ngakhale kuti dzikolo linapanga malamulo atsopano mu 2019 ndi 2023. Malamulo akalewa amachepetsa kukula kwa gawo la migodi. Boma linalemba malamulo atsopano kuti agwirizane ndi lamulo la 2023, koma silinawasindikizebe. Izi zikusiya gawo la migodi losamveka bwino komanso lovuta kugwira ntchito nalo.
ny.body_more:
Fomu yovomerezeka ya Reserved Minerals Licence imatchulabe Mines and Minerals (Reserved Minerals Licence) Regulations ya 1985 ngati lamulo lomwe ikutsatira. Izi zili choncho ngakhale kuti Malawi idapereka Mines and Minerals Act yatsopano mu 2019 kenako Law No. 25 ya 2023. Lamulo la 2023 limakhudza kufufuza, kufufuza ndi migodi yonse ya mchere mdziko muno.

Unduna wa Migodi udapempha anthu kuti apereke ndemanga pa magawo khumi a Mineral Title Regulations. Malamulowa adzakhudza zilolezo zofufuzira, zilolezo za migodi, mapangano opititsa patsogolo anthu ammudzi komanso kukonzanso migodi. Tsiku lomaliza lopereka ndemanga linali pa June 30, 2024. Koma malamulo atsopanowa sanasindikizidwe mu Gazette kuyambira pamenepo. Mining and Minerals Regulatory Authority (MMRA) idapangidwa motsatira lamulo la 2023 kuti liyendetse gawoli ndikuwonetsetsa kuti eni zilolezo akutsatira malamulowo. Popanda malamulo atsopano ofalitsidwa, MMRA singathe kukakamiza lamulo latsopanoli mokwanira.

Kennedy Rashid, Wogwirizanitsa Dziko Lonse wa Natural Resources Justice Network (NRJN), anati kugwiritsa ntchito malamulo akale kumapangitsa kuti gawoli lisamayendetsedwe bwino. Iye anati izi zingachititse kuti zinthu zisamveke bwino kwa osunga ndalama komanso kuchepetsa chitetezo cha anthu ammudzi. Katswiri wa migodi Grain Malunga anati kuchedwaku kungachitike chifukwa Unduna wa Zachilungamo uyenera kuyang'ana malamulowo asanafalitsidwe.

Nduna ya Zamigodi Thoko Tembo adati malamulo atsopanowa adalembedwa lamulo la 2019 litaperekedwa. Kusintha kwa 2023 kunasuntha mphamvu kuchokera ku unduna kupita ku MMRA, kotero malamulo onse okonzedwa adayenera kuyang'aniridwanso. Unduna ndi MMRA kenako adapanga magulu khumi a Mineral Title Regulations ndi lina lowonjezera pa kukonza mchere ndi kuwonjezera phindu. Izi zikutsatira Lamulo la Purezidenti la 2025 lomwe limaletsa kutumiza kunja kwa mchere wosaphika.

en.title: Chiefs reject road shutdown plan in Karonga
en.body: Traditional leaders in Karonga say no to planned demonstrations by the Human Rights Defenders Coalition (HRDC) on the M1 Road. On Wednesday, Paramount Chief Kyungu said the HRDC should talk to government instead of blocking the road on July 1. The road between Karonga and Chiweta is damaged and needs repair. Kyungu warns that shutting the road will hurt trade and the economy.
en.body_more:
Kyungu said Karonga people still remember damage from 2019 demonstrations. Shops and offices were destroyed then, and some buildings are still not fixed. He said blocking the road would be bad for his district. "We do not want history to repeat itself," he told journalists at a meeting on Wednesday. "Our focus should be building Karonga, not destroying it."

The Karonga-Chiweta stretch of the M1 Road is important. It links Malawi to Tanzania and helps traders move goods across the region. Kyungu asked government to start fixing the road from Karonga towards Chiweta soon. He said good companies should do the work.

Edgar Phiri, chairperson of the Karonga Civil Society Organisations Network, said local groups will not join the planned shutdown. "We will not take part in demonstrations or the shutdown of the road because we believe dialogue and engagement are the best approaches," Phiri said. He said groups will meet government on July 8 to discuss road repair plans. The government, the transport minister, and the Roads Authority will come to the meeting. Phiri said he hopes the government will listen to the people of Karonga and start work to fix the damaged road.

ny.title: Mafumu atsutsa dongosolo lotseka misewu ku Karonga
ny.body: Atsogoleri achikhalidwe ku Karonga ati ayi ku ziwonetsero zomwe bungwe la Human Rights Defenders Coalition (HRDC) likukonzekera kuchita pa M1 Road. Lachitatu, Chief Kyungu adati HRDC iyenera kulankhula ndi boma m'malo motseka msewu pa Julayi 1. Msewu wapakati pa Karonga ndi Chiweta wawonongeka ndipo ukufunika kukonzedwa. Kyungu akuchenjeza kuti kutseka msewuwo kudzawononga malonda ndi chuma.
ny.body_more:
Kyungu anati anthu aku Karonga akukumbukirabe kuwonongeka kwa ziwonetsero za 2019. Masitolo ndi maofesi anawonongedwa panthawiyo, ndipo nyumba zina sizinakonzedwebe. Anati kutseka msewu kungakhale koipa kwa chigawo chake. "Sitikufuna kuti mbiri ibwerezenso," adatero kwa atolankhani pamsonkhano Lachitatu. "Cholinga chathu chiyenera kukhala kumanga Karonga, osati kuiwononga."

Msewu wa M1 Road wa Karonga-Chiweta ndi wofunika kwambiri. Umalumikiza Malawi ndi Tanzania ndipo umathandiza amalonda kusuntha katundu m'dera lonselo. Kyungu adapempha boma kuti liyambe kukonza msewu kuchokera ku Karonga kupita ku Chiweta posachedwa. Anati makampani abwino ayenera kugwira ntchitoyo.

Edgar Phiri, wapampando wa Karonga Civil Society Organisations Network, adati magulu am'deralo sadzalowa nawo mu kutsekedwa komwe kukukonzekera. "Sitidzatenga nawo mbali mu ziwonetsero kapena kutsekedwa kwa msewu chifukwa tikukhulupirira kuti kukambirana ndi kutenga nawo mbali ndi njira zabwino kwambiri," adatero Phiri. Anati magulu adzakumana ndi boma pa Julayi 8 kuti akambirane za mapulani okonza misewu. Boma, nduna ya zamayendedwe, ndi Roads Authority adzabwera pamsonkhanowo. Phiri anati akuyembekeza kuti boma lidzamvera anthu aku Karonga ndikuyamba ntchito yokonza msewu wowonongeka.

en.title: Court Frozen Accounts Frozen Again by FIA
en.body: The Financial Intelligence Authority (FIA) has frozen bank accounts of Yusuf Investments Limited and Amaryllis Hotel. This comes just days after the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) failed to extend court restrictions on the same accounts. Legal experts say this raises questions about whether the government is following court orders.
en.body_more:
On 4 May 2026, High Court Judge Justice Kapindu made a decision in Miscellaneous Application No. 03 of 2026. The judge recorded that the FIA had withdrawn all freezing directives on these accounts after finishing its investigations. The FIA had also told National Bank of Malawi to lift the restrictions. Many people thought the matter was finished.

But weeks later, the accounts were frozen again. Legal experts are confused. The accounts have been frozen since early March 2026. They have remained blocked for almost the entire time. If no money could move in or out, what new activity could justify a fresh freeze? The FIA has not publicly explained what new evidence triggered this action.

Lawyers for Yusuf Investments confirmed the freeze. Critics say this shows a pattern. They worry that government agencies are using their power to pressure businesses rather than to pursue real investigations. This troubles the rule of law—the idea that even government must follow the law and court orders. When the same accounts are frozen, then unfrozen by court order, then frozen again, people ask: is the law being used fairly?

The case also costs money. Every time government agencies act unlawfully or without clear legal basis, they risk being sued. The State then pays damages and legal costs. These payments come from taxpayer money. So ordinary Malawians pay twice: once through taxes to fund the agencies, and again when those agencies are ordered to pay compensation. Lawyers say every questionable decision carries a cost to the public purse. Until the FIA explains its new grounds for freezing these accounts, questions about abuse of power will not go away.

ny.title: Maakaunti Otsekedwa a Khothi Atsekedwanso ndi FIA
ny.body: Bungwe la Financial Intelligence Authority (FIA) latseka maakaunti akubanki a Yusuf Investments Limited ndi Amaryllis Hotel. Izi zachitika patangopita masiku ochepa kuchokera pamene bungwe la Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) lalephera kuwonjezera ziletso za khoti pa maakaunti omwewo. Akatswiri azamalamulo akuti izi zikubweretsa mafunso okhudza ngati boma likutsatira malamulo a khothi.
ny.body_more:
Pa 4 Meyi 2026, Woweruza wa Khothi Lalikulu, Justice Kapindu, adapanga chisankho mu Fomu Yosiyanasiyana Nambala 03 ya 2026. Woweruzayo adalemba kuti FIA idachotsa malangizo onse oletsa maakaunti awa atamaliza kufufuza kwawo. FIA idauzanso National Bank of Malawi kuti ichotse ziletsozo. Anthu ambiri amaganiza kuti nkhaniyi yatha.

Koma milungu ingapo pambuyo pake, maakauntiwo adatsekedwanso. Akatswiri azamalamulo asokonezeka. Maakauntiwo adatsekedwa kuyambira kumayambiriro kwa Marichi 2026. Akhalabe otsekedwa kwa nthawi yonse. Ngati palibe ndalama zomwe zingalowe kapena kutuluka, ndi ntchito iti yatsopano yomwe ingalungamitse kuyimitsidwa kwatsopano? FIA sinafotokoze poyera umboni watsopano womwe unayambitsa izi.

Maloya a Yusuf Investments adatsimikiza kuyimitsidwa. Otsutsa akuti izi zikuwonetsa momwe zinthu zilili. Akuda nkhawa kuti mabungwe aboma akugwiritsa ntchito mphamvu zawo kukakamiza mabizinesi m'malo mofufuza zenizeni. Izi zikuvutitsa ulamuliro wa lamulo—lingaliro lakuti ngakhale boma liyenera kutsatira lamulo ndi malamulo a khothi. Maakaunti omwewo akatsekedwa, kenako nkutsegulidwa ndi lamulo la khothi, kenako nkutsekedwanso, anthu amafunsa kuti: kodi lamuloli likugwiritsidwa ntchito mwachilungamo?

Mlanduwu umawononganso ndalama. Nthawi iliyonse mabungwe aboma akachita zinthu mosaloledwa kapena popanda maziko omveka bwino alamulo, amakhala pachiwopsezo chomangidwa. Boma limalipira ndalama zowonongera ndi ndalama zamilandu. Malipiro awa amachokera ku ndalama za okhometsa msonkho. Chifukwa chake anthu wamba aku Malawi amalipira kawiri: kamodzi kudzera mumisonkho kuti athandizire mabungwewo, komanso pamene mabungwewo alamulidwa kuti alipire chipukuta misozi. Maloya amati chisankho chilichonse chokayikitsa chimakhala ndi ndalama ku boma. Mpaka FIA ifotokoze zifukwa zake zatsopano zoyimitsira maakaunti awa, mafunso okhudza kugwiritsa ntchito mphamvu molakwika sadzatha.

en.title: Military officers say crash plane was safe to fly
en.body: Two senior officers from Malawi Defence Force Air Base have told a parliamentary committee that the Dornier 228 aircraft was airworthy before it crashed on June 10 2024 in Chikangawa. The plane killed Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others. The officers said the aircraft had been checked and the crew was very experienced.
en.body_more:
Colonel Alex Mwachande, the Zomba Air Base Commander, testified to the ad hoc committee at Parliament in Lilongwe. He said the aircraft was checked on June 9 2024 at Zomba Air Base before flying to Blantyre. The plane then went to Mzuzu, where the crew reported severe weather. On the morning of June 10, the aircraft flew from Mzuzu to Kamuzu International Airport in Lilongwe to pick up Vice President Chilima and five other passengers.

Mwachande said the aircraft left Kamuzu at 9.17am but did not arrive in Mzuzu at the expected time of 10.02am. He told the committee that the decision to fly or stop a flight rested with the flight crew, not with him. He also said the aircraft had experienced mechanical problems in the past, but these were fixed before the June 9 mission. Captain Henry Nthani, a pilot, praised the Dornier 228 as one of the safest aircraft he had flown. He said he had flown it more than 400 times with no mechanical problems. He last flew it on April 16 2024.

Nthani said the experienced pilots, Colonel Owen Sambalopa and Major Flora Selemani, likely tried their best during the crash. He suggested they may have switched off the aircraft's electrical systems before impact to prevent fire. Without a cockpit voice recorder or flight data recorder, he said, what happened in the final moments may never be known. Nthani said the crew deserved recognition for their actions, which may have helped preserve the bodies of the victims.

ny.title: Asilikali ati ndege yomwe yagwa inali yotetezeka kuuluka
ny.body: Apolisi awiri akuluakulu ochokera ku Malawi Defence Force Air Base auza komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo kuti ndege ya Dornier 228 inali yoyenera kuuluka isanagwe pa June 10, 2024 ku Chikangawa. Ndegeyo inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu. Apolisiwo anati ndegeyo inayang'aniridwa ndipo ogwira ntchito anali odziwa bwino ntchito.
ny.body_more:
Colonel Alex Mwachande, Mtsogoleri wa Zomba Air Base, adapereka umboni kwa komiti yapadera ku Nyumba Yamalamulo ku Lilongwe. Anati ndegeyo idayang'aniridwa pa June 9 2024 ku Zomba Air Base isananyamuke kupita ku Blantyre. Kenako ndegeyo idapita ku Mzuzu, komwe ogwira ntchito adalengeza za nyengo yoipa. M'mawa wa June 10, ndegeyo idanyamuka kuchokera ku Mzuzu kupita ku Kamuzu International Airport ku Lilongwe kukatenga Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Chilima ndi okwera ena asanu.

Mwachande adati ndegeyo idachoka ku Kamuzu nthawi ya 9:17am koma sinafike ku Mzuzu nthawi yomwe amayembekezera ya 10:02am. Adauza komitiyo kuti chisankho chouluka kapena kuyimitsa ndege chinali cha ogwira ntchito, osati iye. Ananenanso kuti ndegeyo idakumana ndi mavuto amakina m'mbuyomu, koma izi zidakonzedwa asanafike June 9. Captain Henry Nthani, woyendetsa ndege, adayamika Dornier 228 ngati imodzi mwa ndege zotetezeka kwambiri zomwe adanyamuka nazo. Anati adayendetsa ndegeyo nthawi zoposa 400 popanda mavuto amakina. Iye adaiulutsa komaliza pa Epulo 16 2024.

Nthani adati oyendetsa ndege odziwa bwino ntchito yawo, Colonel Owen Sambalopa ndi Major Flora Selemani, mwina adayesetsa momwe angathere panthawi ya ngoziyi. Iye adati mwina adazimitsa magetsi a ndegeyo asanagwe kuti apewe moto. Popanda chojambulira mawu cha cockpit kapena chojambulira deta ya ndege, adatero, zomwe zidachitika mphindi zomaliza sizingadziwike. Nthani adati ogwira ntchitoyo akuyenera kuyamikiridwa chifukwa cha zomwe adachita, zomwe mwina zidathandiza kusunga matupi a anthu omwe adamwalira.

en.title: Court Orders FAM to Let Expelled Groups Attend AGM
en.body: The High Court in Blantyre has ordered the Football Association of Malawi (FAM) to allow three expelled groups to attend the annual meeting on Saturday. The groups—the National Football Coaches Association (NFCA), National Football Referees Association (NFRA), and National Youth Football Association (NYFA)—will attend as affiliates, not just as watchers.
en.body_more:
Judge Allan Muhome gave the ruling after hearing from both sides. FAM's lawyer, Luciano Mickeus, said the association did not have enough time to study the court documents because they came late—only last Monday. He asked the court to delay the case so FAM could prepare properly.

The three groups' lawyer, David Kanyenda, was happy with the ruling. He said all the papers were sent on time and had official FAM stamps and signatures. "We are happy that the court has allowed the expelled associations to attend the AGM as affiliates and not merely as watchers," Kanyenda said. He added that his side wanted to wait for a full hearing in court later.

Mickeus said FAM did receive the papers but thought some may have been lost by mistake. He said FAM still believes it will win when the court hears the full case. "We are confident that everything will be fine when we get the chance to be heard," he said.

FAM threw out the three groups in June 2025 during its annual meeting. The next AGM will be held this Saturday at Sunbird Livingstonia in Salima.

ny.title: Khothi Lalamula FAM Kuti Ilole Magulu Ochotsedwa Kupita ku Msonkhano Wachigawo
ny.body: Khoti Lalikulu ku Blantyre lalamula Bungwe la Mpira wa Manja la Malawi (FAM) kuti lilole magulu atatu omwe achotsedwa kuti akakhale nawo pamsonkhano wapachaka Loweruka. Maguluwa—National Football Coaches Association (NFCA), National Football Referees Association (NFRA), ndi National Youth Football Association (NYFA)—adzakhalapo ngati ogwirizana nawo, osati ngati owonera okha.
ny.body_more:
Woweruza Allan Muhome wapereka chigamulocho atamva kuchokera ku mbali zonse ziwiri. Loya wa FAM, Luciano Mickeus, adati bungweli silinali ndi nthawi yokwanira yophunzirira zikalata za khothi chifukwa zidabwera mochedwa—koma Lolemba lapitali. Adapempha khothi kuti lichedwetse mlanduwo kuti FAM ikonzekere bwino.

Loya wa magulu atatuwa, David Kanyenda, adakondwera ndi chigamulocho. Adati mapepala onse adatumizidwa pa nthawi yake ndipo anali ndi masitampu ndi ma siginecha ovomerezeka a FAM. "Tikusangalala kuti khothi lalola mabungwe omwe adachotsedwa kuti apite ku AGM ngati ogwirizana osati ngati owonera okha," adatero Kanyenda. Adawonjezera kuti gulu lake likufuna kudikira kuti mlandu wonse uchitike kukhothi pambuyo pake.

Mickeus adati FAM idalandira mapepalawo koma idaganiza kuti ena adatayika molakwika. Adati FAM ikukhulupirirabe kuti ipambana khothi likamvera mlandu wonse. "Tili ndi chidaliro kuti chilichonse chidzakhala bwino tikapeza mwayi womvedwa," adatero.

FAM idachotsa magulu atatuwa mu June 2025 pamsonkhano wake wapachaka. Msonkhano wotsatira wa AGM udzachitika Loweruka lino ku Sunbird Livingstonia ku Salima.

en.title: Fuel prices fall, minibus fares start to drop
en.body: The Malawi Energy Regulatory Authority cut fuel prices on 19 June. Minibus operators in Blantyre and Lilongwe have now reduced passenger fares. In Blantyre, the Blantyre-Limbe fare fell from K2 000 to K1 500. Some Lilongwe fares also dropped. The Minibus Owners Association told members to cut fares by at least 20 percent.
en.body_more:
Petrol dropped from K6 209 per litre to K5 619, diesel fell from K6 687 to K6 306, and paraffin went down from K5 709 to K4 771. The authority said fuel prices had fallen on world markets.

In Blantyre, the Blantyre-Nancholi fare fell by 25 percent. The Limbe to Mangochi route dropped from K30 000 to K25 000. In Lilongwe, Area 24 to Lilongwe Minibus Depot fares fell from K2 000 to K1 500. Fares from Lilongwe Bus Depot to Nsungwi also fell from K4 000 to K3 000. Coaxley Kamange, general secretary of the Minibus Owners Association of Malawi, said the Passenger Welfare Association and other groups asked operators to cut fares. He said some operators had raised fares when fuel prices went up, but had not cut them when fuel came down. Kamange asked the Competition and Fair Trading Commission and the Consumers Association of Malawi to watch transport prices closely.

Moses Mwalabu, chairperson of the Malawi Coalition of Kabaza Associations, said some motorcycle taxi owners would not cut fares because they had high daily targets of K30 000. Don Napuwa, executive director of the Passenger Welfare Association, said the fare cuts were good news. John Kapito of the Consumers Association said he hoped other prices would fall too. Linda Mkandawire, who lives in Lilongwe, said the cuts saved her about K2 000 per day.

ny.title: Mitengo ya mafuta yatsika, mitengo ya minibus yayamba kutsika
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Energy Regulatory Authority lachepetsa mitengo ya mafuta pa 19 June. Ogwira ntchito zamabasi ang'onoang'ono ku Blantyre ndi Lilongwe tsopano achepetsa mitengo ya okwera. Ku Blantyre, mtengo wa Blantyre-Limbe unatsika kuchoka pa K2 000 kufika pa K1 500. Mitengo ina ku Lilongwe inatsikanso. Bungwe la Owners of the Minibus Association linauza mamembala kuti achepetse mitengo ya mabasi ndi osachepera 20 peresenti.
ny.body_more:
Petroli yatsika kuchoka pa K6 209 pa lita kufika pa K5 619, dizilo yatsika kuchoka pa K6 687 kufika pa K6 306, ndipo parafini yatsika kuchoka pa K5 709 kufika pa K4 771. Akuluakulu adati mitengo yamafuta yatsika pamsika wapadziko lonse.

Ku Blantyre, mtengo wa galimoto kuchokera ku Blantyre kupita ku Nancholi watsika ndi 25 peresenti. Njira yopita ku Limbe kupita ku Mangochi yatsika kuchoka pa K30 000 kufika pa K25 000. Ku Lilongwe, mitengo ya galimoto kuchokera ku Area 24 kupita ku Lilongwe Minibus Depot yatsika kuchoka pa K2 000 kufika pa K1 500. Mitengo yochokera ku Lilongwe Bus Depot kupita ku Nsungwi yatsikanso kuchoka pa K4 000 kufika pa K3 000. Coaxley Kamange, mlembi wamkulu wa Minibus Owners Association of Malawi, adati bungwe la Passenger Welfare Association ndi magulu ena adapempha ogwira ntchito kuti achepetse mitengo. Iye adati ena ogwira ntchito adakweza mitengo ya mafuta pamene mitengo yamafuta idakwera, koma sanachepetse pamene mafuta adatsika. Kamange adapempha Komiti Yopikisana ndi Kugulitsa Zinthu Mwachilungamo ndi Bungwe la Ogula la Malawi kuti aziyang'anira mitengo ya mayendedwe mosamala.

Moses Mwalabu, wapampando wa Malawi Coalition of Kabaza Associations, adati eni njinga zamoto ena sakanachepetsa mitengo chifukwa anali ndi zolinga zapamwamba za tsiku ndi tsiku za K30 000. Don Napuwa, mkulu wa bungwe la Passenger Welfare Association, adati kuchepetsa mitengo ndi nkhani yabwino. John Kapito wa bungwe la Consumers Association adati akuyembekeza kuti mitengo inanso itsika. Linda Mkandawire, yemwe amakhala ku Lilongwe, adati kuchepetsa kumeneku kwamupulumutsa pafupifupi K2 000 patsiku.

en.title: Mpinganjira family gives K100 million for Malawians in South Africa
en.body: Thomson Mpinganjira and his wife Triephornia gave K100 million to help bring Malawians home from South Africa. The gift went to the Department of Disaster Management Affairs (Dodma). It was given in Blantyre on Tuesday. Anti-immigrant attacks in South Africa have made many Malawians want to return home.
en.body_more:
Mpinganjira said his family decided to help after seeing pictures of what is happening. "We watched on television and the pictures are very sad," he said. He called on businesses and people to give money too. "If we all give small bits, we can do big things," he said.

Dodma Commissioner Wilson Moleni received the money. He said it came at the right time. The government needs K24.6 billion to bring home about 15,000 Malawians who left South Africa. About 3,000 have already come back. Moleni said the money will pay for buses and help people travel home safely. He asked more businesses and people to help like the Mpinganjira family did.

Mpinganjira said companies have a duty to help. "The people who are suffering are the ones we do business with," he said. He said giving money is not a loss but the right thing to do. Other people, like social media influencer Onjezani Kenani, are also sending help to South Africa. The attacks in South Africa have grown worse, and more Malawians now want to come home.

ny.title: Banja la Mpinganjira likupereka K100 miliyoni kwa Amalawi ku South Africa
ny.body: Thomson Mpinganjira ndi mkazi wake Triephornia adapereka K100 miliyoni kuti athandize kubweretsa anthu aku Malawi kuchokera ku South Africa. Mphatsoyi idapita ku Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka (Dodma). Idaperekedwa ku Blantyre Lachiwiri. Ziwopsezo zotsutsana ndi anthu othawa kwawo ku South Africa zapangitsa anthu ambiri aku Malawi kufuna kubwerera kwawo.
ny.body_more:
Mpinganjira anati banja lake linaganiza zothandiza ataona zithunzi za zomwe zikuchitika. "Tinaonera pa wailesi yakanema ndipo zithunzizo n'zomvetsa chisoni kwambiri," adatero. Anapempha mabizinesi ndi anthu kuti nawonso apereke ndalama. "Ngati tonse tipereka ndalama zochepa, titha kuchita zinthu zazikulu," adatero.

Dodma Commissioner Wilson Moleni adalandira ndalamazo. Anati zabwera panthawi yoyenera. Boma likufunika K24.6 biliyoni kuti libweretse anthu aku Malawi pafupifupi 15,000 omwe adachoka ku South Africa. Anthu pafupifupi 3,000 abwerera kale.
