Moleni anati ndalamazo zilipira mabasi ndi kuthandiza anthu kuyenda bwino kunyumba. Anapempha mabizinesi ndi anthu ambiri kuti athandize monga momwe banja la Mpinganjira linachitira.

Mpinganjira anati makampani ali ndi udindo wothandiza. "Anthu omwe akuvutika ndi omwe timachita nawo bizinesi," adatero. Anati kupereka ndalama si kutayika koma chinthu choyenera kuchita. Anthu ena, monga Onjezani Kenani, yemwe ndi katswiri pa malo ochezera a pa Intaneti, akutumizanso thandizo ku South Africa. Kuukira ku South Africa kwakula kwambiri, ndipo anthu ambiri aku Malawi tsopano akufuna kubwerera kwawo.

en.title: Groups take ESCOM case to court over political pressure
en.body: Civil society groups in Malawi say they will ask the Constitutional Court to stop the Office of the President and Cabinet from controlling how state companies buy goods. The groups say leaked letters show the President's office tried to block some bidders in Electricity Supply Corporation deals. Robert Mkwezalamba, leader of the Human Rights Consultative Committee, said this could damage Malawi's long-term goals.
en.body_more:
The groups made the announcement in Lilongwe on Wednesday. They say the President's office should not decide who wins contracts at ESCOM or other state companies. The law says procurement committees must make these choices fairly and openly, the groups said.

Mkwezalamba told journalists that electricity is vital for factories and industry. "If this continues, we cannot achieve Malawi Agenda 2063," he said. The groups want the courts to explain the legal limits of what the President can do in procurement matters.

The groups also raised concerns about the Malawi Rural Electrification Programme Phase 9. The government is taking legal action to recover about K1.4 billion in advance payments given to contractors who did not finish their work. The programme delays have hurt rural communities, schools, health facilities and small businesses that need electricity. The groups warned that courts may see an out-of-court settlement and called for full transparency in any agreement.

The organisations — which include the Human Rights Consultative Committee, Human Rights Defenders Coalition, Citizens Alliance and Malawi Black Economic Empowerment Movement — said their case is not about helping any one company. They want to protect the integrity of Malawi's public procurement system and preserve institutions created by law. They have called on the government to explain the leaked memo, apply the law fairly in procurement, guarantee transparency in the MAREP case, and allow courts to give clear guidance on what the President can do in statutory procurement.

ny.title: Magulu atengera mlandu wa ESCOM kukhoti chifukwa cha kukakamizidwa ndi ndale
ny.body: Mabungwe a anthu wamba ku Malawi akuti apempha Khothi Loona za Malamulo kuti liletse Ofesi ya Purezidenti ndi Kabineti kuti isalamulire momwe makampani aboma amagulira katundu. Mabungwewa akuti makalata otuluka akuwonetsa kuti ofesi ya Purezidenti idayesa kuletsa ena omwe akufuna kupereka mapangano a Electricity Supply Corporation. Robert Mkwezalamba, mtsogoleri wa Komiti Yoona za Ufulu Wachibadwidwe, adati izi zitha kuwononga zolinga za nthawi yayitali za Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Maguluwa adalengeza izi ku Lilongwe Lachitatu. Akuti ofesi ya Purezidenti siyenera kusankha amene apambana mapangano ku ESCOM kapena makampani ena aboma. Lamulo limati makomiti ogula zinthu ayenera kupanga zisankhozi mwachilungamo komanso momasuka, maguluwa adatero.

Mkwezalamba adauza atolankhani kuti magetsi ndi ofunikira kwambiri m'mafakitale ndi mafakitale. "Ngati izi zipitirira, sitingathe kukwaniritsa Malawi Agenda 2063," adatero. Maguluwa akufuna kuti makhothi afotokoze malire alamulo a zomwe Purezidenti angachite pankhani zogula zinthu.

Maguluwa adawonetsanso nkhawa za Malawi Rural Electricification Programme Phase 9. Boma likuchitapo kanthu mwalamulo kuti libwezeretse ndalama zokwana K1.4 biliyoni zomwe zimaperekedwa pasadakhale kwa makontrakitala omwe sanamalize ntchito yawo. Kuchedwa kwa pulogalamuyi kwavulaza madera akumidzi, masukulu, zipatala ndi mabizinesi ang'onoang'ono omwe amafunikira magetsi. Maguluwa adachenjeza kuti makhothi angakumane ndi mgwirizano kunja kwa khothi ndipo adapempha kuti pakhale kuwonekera poyera mu mgwirizano uliwonse.

Mabungwewa - kuphatikizapo Human Rights Consultative Committee, Human Rights Defenders Coalition, Citizens Alliance ndi Malawi Black Economic Empowerment Movement - adati mlandu wawo si wokhudza kuthandiza kampani iliyonse. Akufuna kuteteza umphumphu wa njira yogulira zinthu m'dziko la Malawi komanso kusunga mabungwe omwe adakhazikitsidwa ndi lamulo. Apempha boma kuti lifotokoze zomwe zalembedwa m'kalata yowuluka, kugwiritsa ntchito lamulo mwachilungamo pogula zinthu, kutsimikizira kuwonekera poyera pa mlandu wa MAREP, ndikulola makhothi kupereka malangizo omveka bwino pa zomwe Purezidenti angachite pogula zinthu motsatira malamulo.

en.title: Army leaders say military needs more money and equipment
en.body: Two leaders of the Malawi Defence Force told parliament this week that the military does not have enough money, equipment, and resources. Former commander Paul Valentino Phiri and current commander George Jafu spoke at an inquiry into a plane crash on June 10, 2024. The crash killed former Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others.
en.body_more:
The inquiry is looking at what happened when a military aircraft crashed in Chikangawa Forest. The plane was flying to Mzuzu to go to a funeral in Nkhata Bay. Jafu told parliament members: "Please help us. This is your army, this is our army." He said that German investigators and two other inquiries made recommendations after the crash, but the military has not yet done what they asked. He said this must change to stop more crashes.

Jafu told the inquiry that the aircraft had an Emergency Locator Transmitter, but its battery died in 2004. He also said that except for one plane, military aircraft do not carry black boxes. The military made an exception for Vice President Chilima's flight because it was urgent and for a very important person. There was no written request for the flight. Instead, controlling officers talked about it by phone and agreed verbally.

Phiri explained how the flight was arranged. On June 9, 2024, Lucky Sikwese asked for an aircraft for Chilima. The plane had already been given to take people to a funeral for the late Ralph Kasambara, but the Air Force Commander said it could still help. Phiri asked President Lazarus Chakwera, who said yes if the Kasambara family agreed. On June 10, at about 10:45 a.m., Phiri heard the aircraft had gone missing. Bad weather near Mzuzu may have caused it to turn back to Lilongwe.

Phiri ended by urging parliament to give the military more money. He said without enough funding, the country's defence will get weaker. Brigadier General Gilbert Mittawa also testified at the inquiry, which is still working to answer questions that earlier investigations did not fully address.

ny.title: Atsogoleri a asilikali akuti asilikali akufunika ndalama ndi zida zambiri
ny.body: Atsogoleri awiri a Malawi Defense Force adauza nyumba yamalamulo sabata ino kuti asilikali alibe ndalama zokwanira, zida, ndi zinthu zokwanira. Mtsogoleri wakale wa asilikali Paul Valentino Phiri ndi mkulu wakale wa asilikali George Jafu adalankhula pa kafukufuku wokhudza ngozi ya ndege pa June 10, 2024. Ngoziyi idapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu.
ny.body_more:
Kafukufukuyu akuyang'ana zomwe zinachitika pamene ndege yankhondo inagwa mu Nkhalango ya Chikangawa. Ndegeyo inali kuuluka kupita ku Mzuzu kukachita maliro ku Nkhata Bay. Jafu anauza mamembala a nyumba yamalamulo kuti: "Chonde tithandizeni. Awa ndi asilikali anu, awa ndi asilikali athu." Anati ofufuza aku Germany ndi ena awiri adapereka malingaliro pambuyo pa ngoziyi, koma asilikali sanachitebe zomwe adapempha. Anati izi ziyenera kusintha kuti aletse ngozi zina.

Jafu adauza ofufuzawo kuti ndegeyo inali ndi Emergency Locator Transmitter, koma batire yake inatha mu 2004. Ananenanso kuti kupatula ndege imodzi, ndege zankhondo sizinyamula mabokosi akuda. Asilikali adasankha ndege ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Chilima chifukwa inali yofulumira komanso ya munthu wofunika kwambiri. Panalibe pempho lolembedwa la ndegeyo. M'malo mwake, akuluakulu oyang'anira adalankhula za izi pafoni ndipo adavomereza pakamwa.

Phiri adafotokoza momwe ndegeyo idakonzedwera. Pa June 9, 2024, Lucky Sikwese adapempha ndege ya Chilima. Ndegeyo inali itaperekedwa kale kuti inyamule anthu ku maliro a Ralph Kasambara, koma Mtsogoleri wa Air Force anati ingathandizebe. Phiri anafunsa Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera, yemwe anayankha kuti inde ngati banja la a Kasambara livomereza. Pa June 10, pafupifupi 10:45 a.m., Phiri anamva kuti ndegeyo yasowa. Nyengo yoipa pafupi ndi Mzuzu mwina inachititsa kuti ibwerere ku Lilongwe.

Phiri anamaliza ndikupempha nyumba yamalamulo kuti ipatse asilikali ndalama zambiri. Anati popanda ndalama zokwanira, chitetezo cha dzikolo chidzafooka. Brigadier General Gilbert Mittawa nayenso anapereka umboni pa kafukufukuyu, yemwe akadali kugwira ntchito yoyankha mafunso omwe kafukufuku wakale sanawayankhe mokwanira.

en.title: Plane Crash Inquiry Hears Search and Flight Details
en.body: A special committee is investigating a military plane crash that killed Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others. Former Defence Force Commander General Valentino Phiri told the committee that he got a flight request on 8 June 2024 from the vice president's office. The request was to fly Chilima to Mzuzu on 10 June 2024.
en.body_more:
Phiri said Lucky Sikwese, secretary in the office of the vice president, asked for an aircraft. He told Sikwese that the only plane had already flown to Mzuzu carrying lawyer Ralph Kasambara's body. When asked if the aircraft could return to Lilongwe to pick up Chilima, Phiri said he needed to check and speak with Minister of Defence Harry Mkandawire and President Lazarus Chakwera.

The requesters wanted the plane to come to Lilongwe and return the same day because they planned to see the president leave at the airport. Phiri spoke with President Chakwera, who said he did not know if Chilima would return from South Korea. The president gave permission to use the aircraft. Mkandawire was chosen to stand in for the president at funeral arrangements.

When asked about the search operation, Phiri said 172 soldiers took part. He said commanders on the ground chose groups based on where soldiers came from, so exact team numbers were hard to say. When asked why the plane was not found on the first day, Phiri explained that without a signal, they had to use ground teams. Committee member Walter Nyamilandu asked if 172 soldiers were enough to search the dense forest. Phiri said the number was large, and 30 more soldiers from Zomba also joined, making the operation possible.

Meanwhile, current Defence Force Commander General George Jafu Jnr told Parliament that the military has old equipment because money and foreign exchange are short. He said weather reports showed poor visibility in the north a day before the crash. Jafu said flight crews decide whether it is safe to fly based on weather and other risks.

ny.title: Kufufuza za Ngozi ya Ndege Kumamva Kusaka ndi Tsatanetsatane wa Ndege
ny.body: Komiti yapadera ikufufuza ngozi ya ndege ya asilikali yomwe inapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi anthu ena asanu ndi atatu. Mtsogoleri wakale wa asilikali ankhondo, General Valentino Phiri, adauza komitiyo kuti adalandira pempho la ndege pa 8 June 2024 kuchokera ku ofesi ya wachiwiri kwa pulezidenti. Pempho linali loti anyamule ndege ya Chilima kupita ku Mzuzu pa 10 June 2024.
ny.body_more:
Phiri anati Lucky Sikwese, mlembi mu ofesi ya wachiwiri kwa purezidenti, anapempha ndege. Anauza Sikwese kuti ndege yokhayo inali itapita kale ku Mzuzu itanyamula mtembo wa loya Ralph Kasambara. Phiri atafunsidwa ngati ndegeyo ingabwerere ku Lilongwe kukatenga Chilima, anati akufunika kufunsa ndikulankhula ndi Nduna ya Zachitetezo Harry Mkandawire ndi Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera.

Ofunsirawo ankafuna kuti ndegeyo ibwere ku Lilongwe ndi kubwerera tsiku lomwelo chifukwa anali kukonzekera kuona purezidenti akuchoka pa eyapoti. Phiri analankhula ndi Purezidenti Chakwera, yemwe anati sakudziwa ngati Chilima angabwerere kuchokera ku South Korea. Purezidenti anapereka chilolezo chogwiritsa ntchito ndegeyo. Mkandawire anasankhidwa kuti aimire pulezidenti pamaliro.

Atafunsidwa za ntchito yofufuza, Phiri anati asilikali 172 anatenga nawo mbali. Anati akuluakulu a asilikali omwe anali pansi anasankha magulu kutengera komwe asilikali anachokera, kotero ziwerengero zenizeni za magulu zinali zovuta kunena. Atafunsidwa chifukwa chake ndegeyo sinapezeke tsiku loyamba, Phiri anafotokoza kuti popanda chizindikiro, ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito magulu apansi. Membala wa komitiyi, Walter Nyamilandu, adafunsa ngati asilikali 172 anali okwanira kusaka nkhalango yowirira. Phiri adati chiwerengerocho chinali chachikulu, ndipo asilikali ena 30 ochokera ku Zomba nawonso adalowa nawo, zomwe zidapangitsa kuti ntchitoyi itheke.

Pakadali pano, Mtsogoleri wa Gulu Lankhondo, General George Jafu Jnr, adauza Nyumba Yamalamulo kuti asilikali ali ndi zida zakale chifukwa ndalama ndi ndalama zakunja ndizochepa. Iye adati malipoti a nyengo akuwonetsa kuti kumpoto sikunawoneke bwino tsiku limodzi ngozi isanachitike. Jafu adati ogwira ntchito m'ndege amasankha ngati kuli kotetezeka kuuluka kutengera nyengo ndi zoopsa zina.

en.title: Minister Warns Politicians: Do Not Touch CDF Money
en.body: Minister of Local Government Ben Phiri told politicians to stop interfering with Constituency Development Fund money. He said this causes corruption and stops development. Treasury says the money will start going to district councils next week. Full payments will come in July.
en.body_more:
Phiri said leaders must respect their roles and not control how public money is used. He gave an example: "You will find that money for one project goes to another project. It is not only politicians who steal. Even the secretariat can do the same. But when leaders show discipline and are open, it becomes hard for others to steal."

Phiri wants the CDF to be a model for good spending. He said the government wants to use taxpayers' money well and stop corruption. "One reason Malawi is where it is today is that we have not yet chosen to change," Phiri said. "We must set an example. Change begins with one person. As Minister of Local Government, change will begin with me."

Treasury spokesperson Williams Banda said the CDF rollout stays on time. However, Emmanuel Chambulanyina Jere, Member of Parliament for Mzimba South, said district councils did not receive first-quarter money for the 2026/27 financial year as of June 23, 2026. "In past years, we got money every quarter," Jere said.

The Ministry said councils have received five percent for administration and money for project work. Some councils are already advertising projects. Others are going through buying processes. The government will launch a CDF dashboard by July 10 to show how money is spent. Training for Constituency Development Committees is complete in all districts. The ministry has also filled posts for Directors of Planning and Development, Directors of Finance, and Chief Procurement Officers.

ny.title: Nduna Yachenjeza Andale: Musakhudze Ndalama za CDF
ny.body: Nduna ya Boma la Madera Ben Phiri adauza andale kuti asiye kusokoneza ndalama za Constituency Development Fund. Iye adati izi zimayambitsa ziphuphu ndikuletsa chitukuko. Treasury ikunena kuti ndalamazo ziyamba kupita ku makhonsolo a madera sabata yamawa. Malipiro onse adzaperekedwa mu Julayi.
ny.body_more:
Phiri anati atsogoleri ayenera kulemekeza maudindo awo osati kulamulira momwe ndalama za boma zimagwiritsidwira ntchito. Anapereka chitsanzo: "Mudzapeza kuti ndalama za polojekiti imodzi zimapita ku polojekiti ina. Si andale okha omwe amaba. Ngakhale secretariat ingachite chimodzimodzi. Koma atsogoleri akamasonyeza kudziletsa komanso kutseguka, zimakhala zovuta kuti ena aba."

Phiri akufuna kuti CDF ikhale chitsanzo chabwino chogwiritsa ntchito ndalama bwino. Anati boma likufuna kugwiritsa ntchito bwino ndalama za okhometsa msonkho ndikuletsa ziphuphu. "Chifukwa chimodzi chomwe Malawi ili pano ndichakuti sitinasankhebe kusintha," adatero Phiri. "Tiyenera kupereka chitsanzo. Kusintha kumayamba ndi munthu m'modzi. Monga Nduna ya Maboma Apafupi, kusintha kudzayamba ndi ine."

Wolankhulira zachuma Williams Banda adati kukhazikitsidwa kwa CDF kukuchitika panthawi yake. Komabe, Emmanuel Chambulanyina Jere, Membala wa Nyumba Yamalamulo ku Mzimba South, adati makhonsolo a madera sanalandire ndalama za kotala yoyamba ya chaka chachuma cha 2026/27 kuyambira pa June 23, 2026. "M'zaka zapitazi, tinkalandira ndalama kotala iliyonse," adatero Jere.

Undunawu wati makhansala alandira 5 peresenti ya kayendetsedwe ka boma ndi ndalama za ntchito za mapulojekiti. Makhansala ena ayamba kale kulengeza mapulojekiti. Ena akupita patsogolo pogula zinthu. Boma lidzayambitsa dashboard ya CDF pofika pa Julayi 10 kuti liwonetse momwe ndalama zimagwiritsidwira ntchito. Maphunziro a Makomiti Oyendetsera Zigawo atha m'maboma onse. Undunawu wadzazanso maudindo a Oyang'anira Mapulani ndi Chitukuko, Oyang'anira Zachuma, ndi Akuluakulu Oyang'anira Zogula.

en.title: Football Coaches Take FAM to Court Over Removal
en.body: The National Football Coaches Association went to the High Court in Blantyre today. They want the court to bring them back as members of the Football Association of Malawi (FAM). Two other groups also lost their membership: the National Youth Football Association and the National Football Referees Association.
en.body_more:
The coaches body wants the court to stop FAM's annual general meeting. This meeting is set for Saturday at Sunbird Livingstonia Beach in Salima. The coaches want all three groups to take part as full members until the court decides the case.

The lawyer for the coaches, David Kanyenda, told the court what they want. "We ask the court to order FAM to bring us back. We also ask the court to stop the meeting from going ahead without us as members," he said. The three groups want all their old rights back. This includes the right to get letters from FAM, join in FAM work, and vote at meetings.

The coaches say FAM broke its own rules when they threw them out. The removal happened at a meeting on 28 June 2025. The coaches say they got no warning. There was no paper sent to them about it. They say they did not get a chance to speak before the vote. The vote was done by show of hands, not in secret as the FAM rules say. The coaches also say that two-thirds of members did not vote for the removal.

FAM told the three groups they could come to the next meeting. But FAM called them "key stakeholders", not members. The coaches say only members can vote and speak at meetings. Under FAM rules, members must get letters first and time to read about new business. The court will be led by Judge Allan Hans Muhome. FAM's leader Abdul Furhan Chiwalo did not answer questions about the case by the time of print.

ny.title: Aphunzitsi a mpira atengera FAM kukhoti chifukwa chochotsa osewera
ny.body: Bungwe la National Football Coaches Association lapita ku Khoti Lalikulu ku Blantyre lero. Akufuna kuti khotilo liwabwezeretsenso kukhala mamembala a Football Association of Malawi (FAM). Magulu ena awiri nawonso adataya umembala wawo: National Youth Football Association ndi National Football Referees Association.
ny.body_more:
Bungwe la aphunzitsi likufuna kuti khothi liyimitse msonkhano wapachaka wa FAM. Msonkhanowu ukukonzekera Loweruka ku Sunbird Livingstonia Beach ku Salima. Aphunzitsi akufuna kuti magulu onse atatuwa atenge nawo mbali ngati mamembala athunthu mpaka khothi litagamula mlanduwo.

Loya wa aphunzitsi, David Kanyenda, adauza khothi zomwe akufuna. "Tikupempha khothi kuti lilamulire FAM kuti ibwezeretsedwe. Tikupemphanso khothi kuti liyimitse msonkhanowo kuti usapitirire popanda ife ngati mamembala," adatero. Magulu atatuwa akufuna kuti ufulu wawo wakale ubwererenso. Izi zikuphatikizapo ufulu wolandira makalata ochokera ku FAM, kulowa nawo ntchito za FAM, ndikuvota pamisonkhano.

Aphunzitsi akuti FAM inaswa malamulo ake pomwe adawataya. Kuchotsedwako kunachitika pamsonkhano pa 28 June 2025. Aphunzitsi akuti sanalandire chenjezo. Palibe pepala lomwe linatumizidwa kwa iwo lokhudza izi. Akuti sanapeze mwayi wolankhula asanavote. Kuvota kunachitika pokweza manja, osati mwachinsinsi monga momwe malamulo a FAM amanenera. Aphunzitsiwo amanenanso kuti magawo awiri mwa atatu a mamembala sanavote kuti achotsedwe.

FAM idauza magulu atatuwo kuti akhoza kubwera ku msonkhano wotsatira. Koma FAM idawatcha "okhudzidwa kwambiri", osati mamembala. Aphunzitsiwo akuti mamembala okha ndi omwe angavote ndikulankhula pamisonkhano. Malinga ndi malamulo a FAM, mamembala ayenera kupeza makalata kaye ndi nthawi yowerenga za bizinesi yatsopano. Khothi lidzatsogozedwa ndi Woweruza Allan Hans Muhome. Mtsogoleri wa FAM Abdul Furhan Chiwalo sanayankhe mafunso okhudza nkhaniyi pofika nthawi yosindikiza.

en.title: Maranatha Academy Gives Money to Top Students Before MSCE Exams
en.body: Maranatha Academy gave cash prizes to its best Form 4 students at a ceremony in Lilongwe on Sunday. Mary Njewa got K200,000 for doing well in Agriculture. Kingsley Dombola got K200,000 after his classmates voted him as outstanding. Ruth Kanduku got K1.5 million as the top student across all Maranatha Academy schools in the Central Region. The school gave the money before the Malawi School Certificate of Education (MSCE) examinations to help the students feel ready and confident.
en.body_more:
Maranatha Academy Managing Director Ernest Kaonga said the school gave the awards before the exams on purpose. He told the students that the money and certificates were a message: "We believe in you. Go into the MSCE examinations with your heads held high." Kaonga said the awards show that hard work has already been seen and valued. He wanted the Form 4 students to know they were believed in before they even went into the exam room.

The ceremony also honoured teachers who helped the students learn well. Kaonga said no student does well alone. He told the teachers: "We honour you because you give your all." The school wanted to thank the teachers for their hard work, care and teaching throughout the year. Many parents, teachers and visitors came to watch and celebrate with the students.

Kaonga told all the Form 4 students to carry what they learned "into your MSCE examinations." The school believes that recognising good work now will help these students and future classes want to work hard and do well. The ceremony brought the whole school community together to mark one of the most important times in the students' school lives. The MSCE exams were to start the week after the ceremony.

ny.title: Maranatha Academy Yapereka Ndalama kwa Ophunzira Opambana Asanamalize Mayeso a MSCE
ny.body: Maranatha Academy yapereka mphoto zandalama kwa ophunzira ake abwino kwambiri a Fomu 4 pamwambo ku Lilongwe Lamlungu. Mary Njewa adapeza K200,000 chifukwa chochita bwino mu ulimi. Kingsley Dombola adapeza K200,000 ophunzira anzake atamuvotera kuti ndi wabwino kwambiri. Ruth Kanduku adapeza K1.5 miliyoni ngati wophunzira wopambana m'masukulu onse a Maranatha Academy ku Central Region. Sukuluyi idapereka ndalamazo mayeso a Malawi School Certificate of Education (MSCE) asanachite mayeso kuti athandize ophunzirawo kukhala okonzeka komanso odzidalira.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa Maranatha Academy, Ernest Kaonga, anati sukuluyi inapereka mphotozo isanayambe mayeso mwadala. Anauza ophunzirawo kuti ndalama ndi ziphasozo zinali uthenga wakuti: "Timakukhulupirirani. Lowani mayeso a MSCE muli ndi mitu yanu." Kaonga anati mphotozo zikusonyeza kuti kugwira ntchito mwakhama kwawonedwa kale ndipo kwayamikiridwa. Anafuna kuti ophunzira a Fomu 4 adziwe kuti ankakhulupiriridwa asanalowe m'chipinda choyeserera.

Mwambowu unalemekezanso aphunzitsi omwe anathandiza ophunzirawo kuphunzira bwino. Kaonga anati palibe wophunzira amene amachita bwino yekha. Anauza aphunzitsiwo kuti: "Timakulemekezani chifukwa mumapereka zonse zanu." Sukuluyo inkafuna kuyamikira aphunzitsiwo chifukwa cha kugwira ntchito mwakhama, chisamaliro ndi kuphunzitsa kwawo chaka chonse. Makolo ambiri, aphunzitsi ndi alendo anabwera kudzaonera ndikukondwerera ndi ophunzirawo.

Kaonga anauza ophunzira onse a Fomu 4 kuti anyamule zomwe aphunzira "mu mayeso anu a MSCE." Sukuluyi ikukhulupirira kuti kuzindikira ntchito yabwino tsopano kudzathandiza ophunzirawa ndipo makalasi amtsogolo akufuna kugwira ntchito molimbika ndikuchita bwino. Mwambowu unasonkhanitsa gulu lonse la sukulu pamodzi kuti likumbukire nthawi yofunika kwambiri m'miyoyo ya ophunzira kusukulu. Mayeso a MSCE anali kuyamba sabata imodzi pambuyo pa mwambowu.

en.title: South Africa Court Says Lungu Will Stay Buried There
en.body: South Africa's top court has ruled that former Zambian President Edgar Lungu will be buried in South Africa, not Zambia. The Supreme Court of Appeal made this decision on Tuesday, 23 June 2026. The court sided with Lungu's family over the Zambian government.
en.body_more:
The Lungu family, led by former First Lady Esther Lungu and family spokesperson Makebi Zulu, wanted to bury the late president in South Africa. The Zambian government wanted to bring his body back to Lusaka for a state funeral and burial. South Africa's top court ruled in favour of the family.

This ruling overturned an earlier decision from the Gauteng High Court made in 2025. That court had said the Zambian government could take Lungu's body back to Zambia. Now the Supreme Court of Appeal has changed that decision. The court also ordered the Zambian government to pay the legal costs of the case.

Justice R.M. Keightley delivered the main judgment. The judge said South African law protects family dignity, privacy and the right to choose burial arrangements. The court found that the Zambian government had no legal power to override the family's wishes. The judges said the government did not prove that Zambian law or custom required a state burial. The government also did not show that there was any agreement with the family to bring the body back to Zambia.

The ruling ends months of legal dispute between the Lungu family and the Zambian state. Edgar Lungu's body will now remain in South Africa for burial. This court case is important because it shows how family rights can balance against what governments want for their former leaders.

ny.title: Khothi la ku South Africa Lati Lungu Adzakhalabe M'manda Kumeneko
ny.body: Khoti lalikulu la ku South Africa lagamula kuti Purezidenti wakale wa Zambia Edgar Lungu adzaikidwa m'manda ku South Africa, osati ku Zambia. Khoti Lalikulu la Apilo linapanga chisankhochi Lachiwiri, 23 June 2026. Khotilo linagwirizana ndi banja la Lungu pankhani ya boma la Zambia.
ny.body_more:
Banja la Lungu, lotsogozedwa ndi Mkazi woyamba wakale Esther Lungu komanso wolankhulira banja Makebi Zulu, linkafuna kuika m'manda purezidenti womwalirayo ku South Africa. Boma la Zambia linkafuna kubweretsa thupi lake ku Lusaka kuti likaikidwe m'manda ndi kuikidwa m'manda. Khoti lalikulu la South Africa linagamula mokomera banjali.

Chigamulochi chinasintha chigamulo choyambirira cha Khoti Lalikulu la Gauteng chomwe chinaperekedwa mu 2025. Khotilo linati boma la Zambia likhoza kubweza thupi la Lungu ku Zambia. Tsopano Khoti Lalikulu la Apilo lasintha chigamulocho. Khotilo linalamulanso boma la Zambia kuti lilipire ndalama za mlanduwo.

Woweruza milandu R.M. Keightley anapereka chigamulo chachikulu. Woweruza milanduyo anati lamulo la South Africa limateteza ulemu wa banja, chinsinsi komanso ufulu wosankha makonzedwe a maliro. Khotilo linapeza kuti boma la Zambia lilibe mphamvu zovomerezeka zoletsa zofuna za banjali. Oweruza milanduyo anati boma silinatsimikizire kuti lamulo la Zambia kapena mwambo umafuna maliro a boma. Boma silinasonyezenso kuti panali mgwirizano uliwonse ndi banjali kuti libwezeretse thupilo ku Zambia.

Chigamulochi chimathetsa mkangano wa miyezi ingapo pakati pa banja la Lungu ndi boma la Zambia. Mtembo wa Edgar Lungu tsopano udzakhalabe ku South Africa kuti uikidwe m'manda. Mlanduwu kukhothi ndi wofunika chifukwa ukuwonetsa momwe ufulu wa mabanja ungasiyanire ndi zomwe maboma amafuna kwa atsogoleri awo akale.

en.title: Court unfreezes Amaryllis Hotel bank accounts
en.body: A court in Lilongwe has ordered the unfreezing of bank accounts owned by Yusuf Investments Limited, which runs Amaryllis Hotel. Senior Resident Magistrate Shukran Kumbani ruled that the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) failed to show enough reason to keep the accounts frozen. The accounts held more than K30 billion and have been locked since March 2026.
en.body_more:
The ACB wanted to keep the accounts frozen for another three months while it investigated suspected corruption. The bureau said it was looking into the withdrawal of K5.4 billion from the accounts between January 26 and March 6, 2026. The ACB claimed some of this money was paid to three members of the Public Sector Pension Fund Trust (PSPTF) board to speed up a hotel sale. The court disagreed. Magistrate Kumbani said that simply saying investigations are still going on is not enough under the law. The court found that the ACB did not meet the legal test under Section 23(3) of the Corrupt Practices Act. The magistrate said the ACB had time to investigate but failed to show that any corrupt payment actually happened.

Yusuf Investments Limited's lawyer, Kambale, argued that withdrawing money from a company account is not proof of corruption by itself. The court agreed. The ruling means the restriction notice from March 19, 2026 will not continue, and the hotel can use its bank accounts again after more than three months of being locked.

However, the ACB says it will challenge this ruling. Acting ACB Director George Chembezi said investigators still believe the money was used to bribe PSPTF board members and officials in the fund's office. The bureau fears that unfreezing the accounts now will make it harder to trace the money and damage the investigation. The court also said the ACB can issue new restriction notices if new evidence comes to light, but any new restriction order would need to meet the same legal test.

ny.title: Khoti Latsegula Maakaunti Akubanki ku Amaryllis Hotel
ny.body: Khothi ku Lilongwe lalamula kuti maakaunti akubanki a Yusuf Investments Limited, omwe amayendetsa Amaryllis Hotel, atsegulidwe. Woweruza Wamkulu Shukran Kumbani adagamula kuti bungwe lolimbana ndi ziphuphu (ACB) silinapereke zifukwa zokwanira zoletsera maakauntiwo. Maakauntiwo anali ndi ndalama zoposa K30 biliyoni ndipo akhala akutsekedwa kuyambira mu Marichi 2026.
ny.body_more:
ACB ikufuna kusunga maakauntiwo kwa miyezi ina itatu pamene ikufufuza za ziphuphu zomwe zikuganiziridwa. Ofesiyo inati ikuyang'ana kuchotsa K5.4 biliyoni kuchokera ku maakaunti pakati pa Januwale 26 ndi Marichi 6, 2026. ACB idati ndalama zina mwa izi zidaperekedwa kwa mamembala atatu a bungwe la Public Sector Pension Fund Trust (PSPTF) kuti afulumizitse kugulitsa hotelo. Khotilo silinagwirizane nazo. Magistrate Kumbani adati kungonena kuti kafukufuku akuchitikabe sikokwanira malinga ndi lamulo. Khotilo linapeza kuti ACB sinakwaniritse mayeso azamalamulo motsatira Gawo 23(3) la Corrupt Practices Act. Magistrate adati ACB ili ndi nthawi yofufuza koma sinawonetse kuti kulipira kulikonse kwachinyengo kunachitikadi.

Loya wa Yusuf Investments Limited, Kambale, adati kuchotsa ndalama mu akaunti ya kampani si umboni wa ziphuphu zokha. Khotilo linavomereza. Chigamulochi chikutanthauza kuti chidziwitso choletsa kuyambira pa Marichi 19, 2026 sichidzapitirira, ndipo hoteloyo ikhoza kugwiritsanso ntchito maakaunti ake akubanki pambuyo pa miyezi yoposa itatu yotsekedwa.

Komabe, ACB ikunena kuti itsutsa chigamulochi. Woyang'anira wa ACB George Chembezi adati ofufuza akukhulupirirabe kuti ndalamazo zinagwiritsidwa ntchito kupereka ziphuphu kwa mamembala a bungwe la PSPTF ndi akuluakulu a ofesi ya thumba. Ofesiyi ikuopa kuti kutsegula maakaunti tsopano kupangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta kutsatira ndalamazo ndikuwononga kafukufukuyo. Khotilo linanenanso kuti ACB ikhoza kupereka zidziwitso zatsopano zoletsa ngati umboni watsopano utapezeka, koma lamulo lililonse latsopano loletsa liyenera kukwaniritsa mayeso omwewo a zamalamulo.

en.title: MPICO Makes Record K19.3 Billion Profit in 2025
en.body: Malawi Property Investment Company (MPICO) Plc has made a record profit after tax of K19.3 billion for the year 2025, up from K12 billion in 2024. MPICO General Manager Stella Sokosa said this marks a 58 percent growth. She made the announcement at a stakeholder meeting at Amaryllis Hotel in Blantyre.
en.body_more:
The growth was driven largely by fair value gains on the company's investment portfolio. Sokosa said rent collection improved and property values rose as well. Commercial and retail spaces had better occupancy rates during the year.

However, the company faces a challenge: government tenants owe K7.24 billion in unpaid rent as of 31 December 2025. Sokosa said this arrears strain the company's cash flow. MPICO is engaging government departments to recover the money.

Sokosa said the company plans to keep growing by investing in new sectors. These include hospitality, student accommodation, university housing, and tourism developments. She added that MPICO will renovate existing properties and build new infrastructure such as hotels and residential developments. Student housing is an emerging opportunity because demand is high near universities, though Sokosa noted that small and medium investors are driving most new developments.

At the meeting, journalist Jessie Banda questioned whether MPICO's community programs match its financial gains. She said the company should do more for vulnerable groups beyond student hostels. Banda pointed to other organizations that invest directly in projects for poor communities. By press time, MPICO had not given a full response on plans to expand its corporate social responsibility work. Shareholders welcomed the strong profit growth and called for faster action to recover government debt and wider community engagement from the listed company.

ny.title: MPICO Yapanga Phindu Lalikulu Kwambiri la K19.3 Biliyoni mu 2025
ny.body: Kampani ya Malawi Property Investment Company (MPICO) Plc yapeza phindu lalikulu kwambiri pambuyo pa msonkho wa K19.3 biliyoni chaka cha 2025, kuchokera pa K12 biliyoni mu 2024. Woyang'anira wamkulu wa MPICO Stella Sokosa adati izi zikuwonetsa kukula kwa 58 peresenti. Iye adalengeza izi pamsonkhano wa omwe akukhudzidwa ku Amaryllis Hotel ku Blantyre.
ny.body_more:
Kukula kumeneku kudachitika makamaka chifukwa cha phindu loyenera pa ndalama zomwe kampaniyo yayika. Sokosa adati kusonkhanitsa lendi kwakwera ndipo mitengo ya malo yakweranso. Malo ogulitsa ndi ogulitsa anali ndi chiŵerengero chabwino cha anthu okhalamo chaka chino.

Komabe, kampaniyo ikukumana ndi vuto: anthu obwereka nyumba m'boma ali ndi ngongole ya K7.24 biliyoni ya lendi yosalipidwa kuyambira pa 31 Disembala 2025. Sokosa adati ngongole izi zikuchepetsa ndalama zomwe kampaniyo imagwiritsa ntchito. MPICO ikugwiritsa ntchito madipatimenti aboma kuti abweze ndalamazo.

Sokosa adati kampaniyo ikukonzekera kupitiliza kukula poika ndalama m'magawo atsopano. Izi zikuphatikizapo kuchereza alendo, malo ogona ophunzira, nyumba za mayunivesite, ndi chitukuko cha zokopa alendo. Anawonjezera kuti MPICO idzakonzanso malo omwe alipo ndikumanga zomangamanga zatsopano monga mahotela ndi chitukuko cha nyumba. Nyumba za ophunzira ndi mwayi watsopano chifukwa kufunikira kuli kwakukulu pafupi ndi mayunivesite, ngakhale Sokosa adazindikira kuti amalonda ang'onoang'ono ndi apakatikati akuyendetsa chitukuko chatsopano kwambiri.

Pamsonkhanowo, mtolankhani Jessie Banda adafunsa ngati mapulogalamu ammudzi a MPICO akugwirizana ndi phindu lake lazachuma. Adati kampaniyo iyenera kuchita zambiri kwa magulu ovutika kupitirira ma hostel a ophunzira. Banda adatchula mabungwe ena omwe amayika ndalama mwachindunji m'mapulojekiti a madera osauka. Pofika nthawi yofalitsa nkhani, MPICO inali isanapereke yankho lokwanira pa mapulani okulitsa ntchito zake zokhudzana ndi udindo wa anthu. Ogawana nawo adalandila kukula kwa phindu lalikulu ndipo adapempha kuti pakhale kuchitapo kanthu mwachangu kuti abweze ngongole za boma komanso kuti kampaniyo igwirizane ndi anthu ammudzi.

en.title: Former minister says president approved Chilima's fatal flight
en.body: Former Defence Minister Harry Mkandawire told Parliament yesterday that the then-president Lazarus Chakwera gave permission for Vice-President Saulos Chilima to use a military plane. The Dornier aircraft crashed on 10 June 2024 near Chikangawa Forest and killed nine people, including Chilima. Mkandawire said the then-MDF Commander General Paul Valentino Phiri told him the President had cleared the flight.
en.body_more:
Mkandawire gave his testimony to a parliamentary committee investigating the crash. When asked by Mwanza Central MP Felix Njawala if he had checked the approval with Chakwera himself, Mkandawire said no. He explained that Chakwera had only told him to hold the plane ready for Chilima, who might use it to go to the funeral of former Attorney General Ralph Kasambara after returning from South Korea.

The current Defence Minister Feston Kaupa told the same inquiry that he had found no written documents that gave Chilima permission to use the military aircraft. Kaupa said all flights need formal approval, planning, and records. He added that requests normally come as written letters from government departments. But he said the Vice-President's office is different because it is linked to the President's office. Kaupa also told lawmakers that the Dornier had no black box recording device. He said military aircraft do not need one by law, but the government should think about adding black boxes to state aircraft in the future.

Kaupa said the Defence Force needs more money and equipment. He would not say how many military planes exist, saying this information is secret for national safety reasons. He defended the Dornier, which was bought in 1986 and had been flying for nearly 40 years before it crashed. He said the plane worked well until investigators found that the Emergency Locator Transmitter battery was broken.

The inquiry also heard from Luckie Sikwese, the former head of the Vice-President's office. Sikwese said that Cabinet minister Richard Chimwendo Banda was never on the passenger list, even though someone asked about getting him a seat on the day of the flight.

ny.title: Nduna yakale yati pulezidenti wavomereza ulendo wa Chilima womwe unapha anthu ambiri
ny.body: Nduna yakale ya zachitetezo Harry Mkandawire adauza Nyumba Yamalamulo dzulo kuti purezidenti wa nthawiyo Lazarus Chakwera adapereka chilolezo kwa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima kuti agwiritse ntchito ndege yankhondo. Ndege ya Dornier idagwa pa 10 June 2024 pafupi ndi nkhalango ya Chikangawa ndipo idapha anthu asanu ndi anayi, kuphatikiza Chilima. Mkandawire adati mkulu wa asilikali a MDF panthawiyo Paul Valentino Phiri adamuuza kuti Purezidenti walola ndegeyo kuti ikwere.
ny.body_more:
Mkandawire anapereka umboni wake ku komiti ya nyumba yamalamulo yomwe ikufufuza za ngoziyi. Pamene Mphungu wa ku Mwanza Central, Felix Njawala, adamufunsa ngati adawona chilolezocho ndi Chakwera mwiniwake, Mkandawire adati ayi. Anafotokoza kuti Chakwera adangomuuza kuti akonzekeretse ndegeyo kwa Chilima, yemwe angagwiritse ntchito popita ku maliro a Loya Wamkulu wakale Ralph Kasambara atabwerera kuchokera ku South Korea.

Nduna ya Zachitetezo yomwe ilipo pano, Feston Kaupa, adauza kafukufuku yemweyo kuti sanapeze zikalata zolembedwa zomwe zidapatsa Chilima chilolezo chogwiritsa ntchito ndege zankhondo. Kaupa adati maulendo onse amafunika kuvomerezedwa, kukonzekera, ndi zolemba. Anawonjezera kuti zopempha nthawi zambiri zimabwera ngati makalata olembedwa kuchokera ku madipatimenti aboma. Koma adati ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti ndi yosiyana chifukwa imalumikizidwa ndi ofesi ya Purezidenti. Kaupa adauzanso opanga malamulo kuti Dornier ilibe chipangizo chojambulira bokosi lakuda. Anati ndege zankhondo sizifunikira chimodzi mwalamulo, koma boma liyenera kuganizira zowonjezera mabokosi akuda ku ndege za boma mtsogolo.

Kaupa adati Gulu Lankhondo likufunika ndalama ndi zida zambiri. Iye sananene kuti pali ndege zingati zankhondo, ponena kuti izi ndi zachinsinsi pazifukwa zachitetezo cha dziko. Anateteza ndege ya Dornier, yomwe idagulidwa mu 1986 ndipo inali ikuuluka kwa zaka pafupifupi 40 isanagwe. Anati ndegeyo idagwira ntchito bwino mpaka ofufuza atapeza kuti batire ya Emergency Locator Transmitter idasweka.

Kafukufukuyo adamvanso kuchokera kwa Luckie Sikwese, yemwe kale anali mkulu wa ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti. Sikwese adati nduna ya boma Richard Chimwendo Banda sanakhalepo pamndandanda wa okwera, ngakhale wina adamufunsa za kumupezera mpando tsiku lomwe ndegeyo idawulukira.

en.title: Sadc leaders meet on DRC and Madagascar crises
en.body: Second Vice-President Enoch Chihana led a Sadc summit in Lilongwe. He spoke for President Peter Mutharika, who was away in South Africa. Regional leaders agreed to send a team to the Democratic Republic of Congo. They also backed Madagascar's plans for elections.
en.body_more:
The Extraordinary Sadc Organ Troika Summit met to discuss major security problems in the region. Chihana said Sadc must help the DRC and not leave it alone in difficult times. The summit decided to send a high-level mission to the DRC to look at security after regional troops left. The Sadc Mission in the DRC ended in March last year.

Sadc Executive Secretary Elias Magosi warned that the DRC conflict puts the whole region at risk. He said the violence is now affecting neighbouring Rwanda and could spread further. Magosi also asked for a coordinated response to a new Ebola outbreak in the DRC. On Madagascar, leaders reaffirmed support for the country's National Refoundation Programme and called for free and fair elections within 24 months.

Former President Joyce Banda presented the final report of the Sadc Panel of Elders on Madagascar. She has led three missions to Madagascar since January. The summit also included a meeting of ministers from the Sadc Organ on Politics, Defence and Security Cooperation. The ministers prepared recommendations for the heads of state. The Sadc Organ Troika includes Malawi as chairperson, the Kingdom of Eswatini as incoming chairperson, and Tanzania as outgoing chair.

Foreign Affairs Minister George Chaponda mentioned xenophobic attacks on foreign African nationals in South Africa as a pressing regional problem. However, this issue did not appear in the final summit decisions. South Africa was expected to brief the summit on the attacks and what the government is doing to stop them. The reasons for leaving this matter out of the final statements were not clear.

ny.title: Atsogoleri a Sadc akumana pa nkhani ya mavuto ku DRC ndi Madagascar
ny.body: Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enoch Chihana anatsogolera msonkhano wa Sadc ku Lilongwe. Iye analankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti Peter Mutharika, yemwe anali ku South Africa. Atsogoleri a madera anagwirizana kutumiza gulu ku Democratic Republic of Congo. Anathandizanso mapulani a zisankho ku Madagascar.
ny.body_more:
Msonkhano Wapadera wa Sadc Organ Troika unakumana kuti ukambirane za mavuto akuluakulu achitetezo m'chigawochi. Chihana anati Sadc iyenera kuthandiza DRC osati kuisiya yokha nthawi zovuta. Msonkhanowo unaganiza zotumiza gulu lankhondo lapamwamba ku DRC kuti liyang'ane chitetezo asilikali a m'chigawochi atachoka. Msonkhano wa Sadc ku DRC unatha mu Marichi chaka chatha.

Mlembi Wamkulu wa Sadc, Elias Magosi, anachenjeza kuti nkhondo ya DRC ikuika chigawo chonse pachiwopsezo. Anati chiwawachi chikukhudza dziko loyandikana nalo la Rwanda ndipo chikhoza kufalikira kwambiri. Magosi anapemphanso kuti pakhale njira yogwirizana yothanirana ndi mliri watsopano wa Ebola ku DRC. Ponena za Madagascar, atsogoleri adatsimikiziranso kuti akuthandizira Pulogalamu Yokonzanso Dziko Lonse ya dzikolo ndipo adapempha kuti pakhale chisankho chaulere komanso cholungama mkati mwa miyezi 24.

Purezidenti wakale Joyce Banda adapereka lipoti lomaliza la Sadc Panel of Elders pa Madagascar. Watsogolera maulendo atatu ku Madagascar kuyambira Januwale. Msonkhanowo unaphatikizaponso msonkhano wa nduna za Sadc Organ on Politics, Defense and Security Cooperation. Ndunazo zinakonza malingaliro a atsogoleri a mayiko. Bungwe la Sadc Organ Troika likuphatikizapo Malawi ngati wapampando, Ufumu wa Eswatini ngati wapampando watsopano, ndi Tanzania ngati wapampando watsopano.

Nduna ya Zachilendo George Chaponda yatchula kuti kuukira kwa alendo kwa nzika zakunja za ku Africa ku South Africa ndi vuto lalikulu m'chigawochi. Komabe, nkhaniyi sinawonekere m'zigamulo zomaliza za msonkhano. South Africa ikuyembekezeka kufotokozera msonkhano za kuukira kumeneku ndi zomwe boma likuchita kuti liwaletse. Zifukwa zosiya nkhaniyi m'mawu omaliza sizinali zomveka bwino.

en.title: FDH Bank gives K100 million for Malawians to come home from South Africa
en.body: FDH Bank has given K100 million to the government to help bring Malawians home from South Africa. The money goes to the Department of Disaster Management Affairs (DoDMA). About 15,000 Malawians asked to come home because they fear for their safety. South Africa set a deadline of June 30 for undocumented people to leave.
en.body_more:
Ronald Chimchere is the Head of Marketing and Communication at FDH Bank. He said the bank wants to help Malawians return home safely. "We recognize the challenges and uncertainty the affected Malawians are facing, and as FDH, we are stepping in to ensure they return home safety," he said. He added that the money helps the government do its work to bring people back.

Charles Matabwa is the Director of Preparedness and Response at DoDMA. He said thank you to the bank. "We are very appreciative; this will go a long way in assisting the Malawians. It is not the first time they have assisted government after an appeal and we appreciate," Matabwa said. He said the donation came at the right time.

About 3,000 Malawians have already come home through help from the government and other groups. More people are expected to come home soon. The repatriation is happening because of rising safety concerns in South Africa. FDH Bank is listed on the Malawi Stock Exchange.

ny.title: Banki ya FDH yapereka K100 miliyoni kuti a Malawi abwerere kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa
ny.body: Banki ya FDH yapereka ndalama zokwana K100 miliyoni ku boma kuti zithandize kubweretsa anthu aku Malawi kuchokera ku South Africa. Ndalamazo zimapita ku Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka (DoDMA). Anthu aku Malawi pafupifupi 15,000 adapempha kuti abwerere kwawo chifukwa akuopa chitetezo chawo. South Africa idakhazikitsa tsiku lomaliza la June 30 kuti anthu opanda ziphaso achoke.
ny.body_more:
Ronald Chimchere ndi Mtsogoleri wa Zamalonda ndi Kulankhulana ku FDH Bank. Iye anati banki ikufuna kuthandiza anthu aku Malawi kubwerera kwawo mosatekeseka. "Tikuzindikira mavuto ndi kusatsimikizika komwe anthu aku Malawi omwe akhudzidwa akukumana nako, ndipo monga FDH, tikulowererapo kuti atsimikizire kuti abwerera kwawo otetezeka," adatero. Anawonjezera kuti ndalamazo zimathandiza boma kuchita ntchito yake yobwezeretsa anthu.

Charles Matabwa ndi Mtsogoleri wa Kukonzekera ndi Kuyankha ku DoDMA. Anati zikomo kwambiri ku banki. "Tikuyamikira kwambiri; izi zithandiza kwambiri anthu aku Malawi. Si nthawi yoyamba kuti athandize boma pambuyo pa pempho ndipo tikuyamikira," adatero Matabwa. Anati zoperekazo zabwera panthawi yoyenera.

Anthu aku Malawi pafupifupi 3,000 abwerera kale kunyumba kudzera mu thandizo la boma ndi magulu ena. Anthu ambiri akuyembekezeka kubwerera kwawo posachedwa. Kubwerera kwawo kukuchitika chifukwa cha nkhawa zomwe zikukwera ku South Africa. FDH Bank yalembedwa pa Malawi Stock Exchange.

en.title: SADC removes South Africa violence report from summit
en.body: A report about violence against foreign people in South Africa was taken off the agenda at a SADC meeting. Leaders were supposed to hear about attacks on migrants. No one said why the report was removed. The meeting was held online and led by Enoch Chihana.
en.body_more:
The report was meant to tell the leaders about anti-migrant attacks, threats and fights against foreign people in South Africa. It was also supposed to show what South Africa's government is doing to stop the violence. But when the summit started, the item was removed from the agenda with no public reason given.

Enoch Chihana opened the meeting on behalf of Peter Mutharika, who is the Chairperson of the SADC Organ on Politics, Defence and Security. Chihana said the situation in South Africa was serious and needed a briefing. He said the region needed to work together to help South Africa manage the problem. But despite these words, no formal talk about the attacks took place when the meeting began.

The removal of the report has raised questions about whether political reasons or diplomatic issues caused the decision. Many people across Southern Africa worry about the safety of migrants in South Africa. Previous violence has hurt citizens from neighbouring countries, and people have called for stronger regional help. Some analysts say that avoiding this discussion sends the wrong message at a time when the region needs to stay united.

Instead of talking about South Africa, the leaders discussed other matters. They looked at a report from a SADC Panel of Elders led by former Malawian President Joyce Banda about the political situation in Madagascar. They also talked about security problems in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Observers say the absence of a formal debate about the South Africa violence is significant and will draw attention.

ny.title: SADC yachotsa lipoti la zachiwawa ku South Africa pamsonkhano
ny.body: Lipoti lonena za nkhanza kwa anthu akunja ku South Africa linachotsedwa pa mndandanda wa zokambirana pamsonkhano wa SADC. Atsogoleri amayenera kumva za kuukira anthu othawa kwawo. Palibe amene ananena chifukwa chake lipotilo linachotsedwa. Msonkhanowo unachitikira pa intaneti ndipo unatsogozedwa ndi Enoch Chihana.
ny.body_more:
Lipotilo linapangidwa kuti liuze atsogoleri za ziwawa zotsutsana ndi anthu othawa kwawo, ziwopsezo ndi nkhondo yolimbana ndi anthu akunja ku South Africa. Linayeneranso kuwonetsa zomwe boma la South Africa likuchita kuti lithetse ziwawa. Koma msonkhano utangoyamba, nkhaniyi inachotsedwa pa ndondomeko ya zokambirana popanda chifukwa chodziwika.

Enoch Chihana anatsegula msonkhanowo m'malo mwa Peter Mutharika, yemwe ndi Wapampando wa SADC Organ on Politics, Defense and Security. Chihana anati vuto ku South Africa ndi lalikulu ndipo likufunika chidziwitso. Anati derali liyenera kugwirira ntchito limodzi kuti lithandize South Africa kuthana ndi vutoli. Koma ngakhale mawu awa, palibe nkhani yovomerezeka yokhudza ziwawa zomwe zinachitika msonkhano utangoyamba.

Kuchotsedwa kwa lipotilo kwabweretsa mafunso okhudza ngati zifukwa zandale kapena nkhani zandale ndizo zinayambitsa chisankhocho. Anthu ambiri ku South Africa konse akuda nkhawa ndi chitetezo cha anthu othawa kwawo ku South Africa. Ziwawa zakale zavulaza nzika zochokera kumayiko oyandikana nawo, ndipo anthu apempha thandizo lamphamvu kuderali. Akatswiri ena amati kupewa kukambiranaku kumapereka uthenga wolakwika panthawi yomwe derali liyenera kukhala logwirizana.

M'malo molankhula za South Africa, atsogoleriwo adakambirana nkhani zina. Iwo anayang'ana lipoti lochokera ku SADC Panel of Elders lotsogozedwa ndi Purezidenti wakale wa Malawi Joyce Banda lokhudza momwe ndale zilili ku Madagascar. Analankhulanso za mavuto achitetezo ku Democratic Republic of the Congo. Owonera akuti kusakhalapo kwa mkangano wokhudza chiwawa ku South Africa ndikofunikira ndipo kudzakopa chidwi cha anthu.

en.title: High Court says government blacklist of Mulli was unlawful
en.body: Malawi's High Court has ruled that a 2012 government ban on businessman Leston Mulli and five of his companies was unlawful. Justice Allan Hans Muhome said the ban was issued without legal authority by then Attorney General Ralph Kasambara SC. The companies were never heard before the ban was made. The court has ordered the government to pay damages for losses caused by the ban.
en.body_more:
The ban came into force on 5 September 2012. Ralph Kasambara SC was Attorney General and Minister of Justice at the time. He told all government ministries and departments to stop doing business with Mulli and his companies: Mulli Brothers Limited, Sunrise Pharmaceuticals, Celcom Limited, National Bus Company of Malawi, and Chombe Foods Limited. The court found that Kasambara acted with bad intention to harm Mulli. He gave no lawful reason for singling out only these companies when many other firms did business with the government.

The ban caused severe damage. Mulli had planned to start a new telecommunications company called Celcom Limited with US$30 million from Afreximbank. The government ban stopped this plan. Mulli and his companies lost many business deals and say they lost about K270 billion. Although the government ended the ban in 2014, no one was ever charged with the crimes that were said to justify the blacklist. Justice Muhome said this proved the ban was made in bad faith.

The court ruled that Mulli and his companies were treated unfairly. They were never given a chance to speak in their defence before the ban was put in place. The judge said this broke the basic rules of fair treatment. Mulli fought the case for 14 years to clear his name. Now the court has told the government to pay him for all losses from the ban. If Mulli and the government cannot agree on the amount within 14 days, the Chief Registrar will decide how much money Mulli should receive.

ny.title: Khoti Lalikulu Lati Mndandanda Wakuda wa Boma wa Mulli Unali Wosaloledwa
ny.body: Khothi Lalikulu la Malawi lagamula kuti lamulo la boma la 2012 loletsa bizinesi Leston Mulli ndi makampani ake asanu linali losaloledwa. Woweruza Allan Hans Muhome adati lamuloli linaperekedwa popanda chilolezo chalamulo ndi Loya Wamkulu Ralph Kasambara SC panthawiyo. Makampaniwa sanamvepo lamuloli lisanaperekedwe. Khothi lalamula boma kuti lilipire ndalama zomwe zinatayika chifukwa cha lamuloli.
ny.body_more:
Chiletsochi chinayamba kugwira ntchito pa 5 Seputembala 2012. Ralph Kasambara SC anali Loya Wamkulu komanso Nduna ya Zachilungamo panthawiyo. Anauza maunduna ndi madipatimenti onse aboma kuti asiye kuchita bizinesi ndi Mulli ndi makampani ake: Mulli Brothers Limited, Sunrise Pharmaceuticals, Celcom Limited, National Bus Company of Malawi, ndi Chombe Foods Limited. Khotilo linapeza kuti Kasambara anachita zinthu mopanda cholinga chovulaza Mulli. Sanapereke chifukwa chovomerezeka chosiya makampani amenewa okha pamene makampani ena ambiri ankachita bizinesi ndi boma.

Chiletsochi chinawononga kwambiri. Mulli anali atakonza zoyambitsa kampani yatsopano yolumikizirana yotchedwa Celcom Limited ndi US$30 miliyoni kuchokera ku Afreximbank. Chiletsochi chinaletsa dongosololi. Mulli ndi makampani ake anataya mapangano ambiri amalonda ndipo anati anataya pafupifupi K270 biliyoni. Ngakhale kuti boma linathetsa chiletsochi mu 2014, palibe amene anaimbidwa mlandu pa milandu yomwe inanenedwa kuti imapangitsa kuti anthu asamapezeke pamndandanda wakuda. Woweruza Muhome anati izi zatsimikizira kuti chiletsocho chinapangidwa mwachinyengo.

Khotilo linagamula kuti Mulli ndi makampani ake anachitiridwa zinthu mopanda chilungamo. Iwo sanapatsidwe mwayi wolankhula podziteteza asanakhazikitse lamulo loletsa. Woweruza adati izi zinaswa malamulo oyambira okhudza chilungamo. Mulli adalimbana ndi mlanduwu kwa zaka 14 kuti achotse dzina lake. Tsopano khothi lauza boma kuti limulipire ndalama zonse zomwe zatayika chifukwa cha lamuloli. Ngati Mulli ndi boma sangagwirizane pa ndalamazo mkati mwa masiku 14, Wolembetsa Wamkulu adzasankha ndalama zomwe Mulli ayenera kulandira.

en.title: UK and Malawi focus on trade and investment ties
en.body: The United Kingdom wants to work with Malawi on trade and investment, not just give aid. British High Commissioner Leigh Stubblefield said this at an event in Lilongwe last week. She said UK companies invest in Malawi's farms and mines. The two countries have been friends for many years.
en.body_more:
Stubblefield said the UK's way of working with Malawi is changing. "From donor to investor, from aid to expertise," she said. British companies are big investors in Malawi's farms and mines. The UK gives about £40 million to £50 million each year to Malawi through the World Bank and Global Fund. This money helps health, farms, and climate work.

But trade between the two countries fell in 2025. Total trade was £57 million for the year. This was a drop of 31.3 percent from 2024. UK goods sent to Malawi fell by 44.1 percent. Goods from Malawi to the UK stayed almost the same. Malawi is the UK's 173rd biggest trading partner. The trade is less than 0.1 percent of all UK trade.

Foreign Affairs Minister George Chaponda said Malawi is happy with the UK's help. "We value our warm partnership with the United Kingdom," he said. He said the two countries will work together on growth and development. For Malawi's businesses, the message is clear: they need to find new places to sell their goods and make their products stronger.

The drop in trade shows that Malawi must look to other countries and regions. Regional groups like SADC and AfCFTA may help Malawi sell more goods. Malawi's farms, tea, and mines can find buyers in many places, not just the UK.

ny.title: UK ndi Malawi akuyang'ana kwambiri pa mgwirizano wamalonda ndi ndalama
ny.body: Dziko la United Kingdom likufuna kugwira ntchito ndi Malawi pa zamalonda ndi ndalama, osati kungopereka thandizo lokha. Kazembe Wamkulu wa Britain, Leigh Stubblefield, adanena izi pamwambo womwe unachitikira ku Lilongwe sabata yatha. Iye anati makampani aku UK amaika ndalama m'mafamu ndi migodi ya Malawi. Mayiko awiriwa akhala mabwenzi kwa zaka zambiri.
ny.body_more:
Stubblefield anati njira ya UK yogwirira ntchito ndi Malawi ikusintha. "Kuyambira kwa opereka ndalama kupita kwa osunga ndalama, kuyambira thandizo mpaka ukatswiri," adatero. Makampani aku Britain ndi omwe amaika ndalama zambiri m'mafamu ndi migodi ku Malawi. UK imapereka pafupifupi £40 miliyoni mpaka £50 miliyoni chaka chilichonse ku Malawi kudzera mu World Bank ndi Global Fund. Ndalamazi zimathandiza thanzi, minda, ndi nyengo kugwira ntchito.

Koma malonda pakati pa mayiko awiriwa adatsika mu 2025. Malonda onse anali £57 miliyoni pachaka. Izi zidatsika ndi 31.3 peresenti kuyambira 2024. Katundu waku UK wotumizidwa ku Malawi adatsika ndi 44.1 peresenti. Katundu wochokera ku Malawi kupita ku UK adakhalabe chimodzimodzi. Malawi ndi bwenzi lalikulu la 173 ku UK. Malondawa ndi ochepera 0.1 peresenti ya malonda onse aku UK.

Nduna ya Zakunja George Chaponda adati Malawi ikusangalala ndi thandizo la UK. "Timayamikira mgwirizano wathu wachikondi ndi United Kingdom," adatero. Anati mayiko awiriwa adzagwira ntchito limodzi pakukula ndi chitukuko. Kwa mabizinesi aku Malawi, uthenga wake ndi womveka bwino: ayenera kupeza malo atsopano ogulitsira katundu wawo ndikulimbitsa zinthu zawo.

Kutsika kwa malonda kukuwonetsa kuti Malawi iyenera kuyang'ana mayiko ndi madera ena. Magulu a m'madera monga SADC ndi AfCFTA angathandize Malawi kugulitsa katundu wambiri. Mafamu, tiyi, ndi migodi ku Malawi amatha kupeza ogula m'malo ambiri, osati ku UK kokha.

en.title: Malawi helps people back from South Africa attacks
en.body: The Government of Malawi says it will help Malawians who left South Africa after attacks on foreigners. Minister George Chaponda spoke at Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre on Sunday. He told returned Malawians they are safe and must use their skills to find jobs at home. By Sunday, 2,218 Malawians had come back. Another 514 were on the way.
en.body_more:
About 15,000 more Malawians still want to come home from South Africa. The Department of Disaster Management Affairs is helping them with food and places to stay.

Chaponda said the government will give loans to help people start businesses. The Malawi Enterprises Development Fund gives money to young people and women. He also said the government will work to stop Malawians going abroad without the right papers. South Africa has agreed to help bring Malawians home safely.

Amina Bakali, 27, from Mangochi District, came back to Malawi. She spent ten days in a camp with no good food, water, or beds. She had a baby at the camp. A health clinic only helped her one time. "They told me that foreigners were not wanted," she said. She will not go back to South Africa.

Chaponda told the returnees to stay in Malawi and use the work skills they learned. "We must help these people settle down so they do not go back," he said. "These people have different skills. Let us help them use these skills for our country." The government says it wants to keep Malawians safe and give them jobs at home.

ny.title: Dziko la Malawi lathandiza anthu omwe adaukiridwa ndi zigawenga ku South Africa
ny.body: Boma la Malawi lati lithandiza anthu aku Malawi omwe adachoka ku South Africa pambuyo pa ziwawa zomwe zidachitikira alendo. Nduna George Chaponda adalankhula Lamlungu ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre. Adauza anthu aku Malawi omwe adabwerera kuti ali otetezeka ndipo ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito luso lawo kuti apeze ntchito kunyumba kwawo. Pofika Lamlungu, anthu aku Malawi 2,218 adabweranso. Ena 514 anali paulendo.
ny.body_more:
Anthu ena pafupifupi 15,000 aku Malawi akufunabe kubwerera kwawo kuchokera ku South Africa. Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka ikuwathandiza ndi chakudya ndi malo okhala.

Chaponda adati boma lipereka ngongole zothandizira anthu kuyambitsa mabizinesi. Malawi Enterprises Development Fund imapereka ndalama kwa achinyamata ndi akazi. Anatinso boma lidzagwira ntchito yoletsa anthu aku Malawi kupita kunja popanda mapepala oyenera. South Africa yavomereza kuthandiza anthu aku Malawi kubwerera kwawo mosamala.

Amina Bakali, wazaka 27, wochokera ku Mangochi District, wabwerera ku Malawi. Anakhala masiku khumi mumsasa wopanda chakudya chabwino, madzi, kapena mabedi. Anabereka mwana pamsasa. Chipatala china chinamuthandiza kamodzi kokha. "Anandiuza kuti alendo sakufunidwa," adatero. Sadzabwerera ku South Africa.

Chaponda anauza obwererawo kuti akhale ku Malawi ndikugwiritsa ntchito luso la ntchito lomwe adaphunzira. "Tiyenera kuthandiza anthu awa kukhala pansi kuti asabwererenso," adatero. "Anthu awa ali ndi luso losiyana. Tiyeni tiwathandize kugwiritsa ntchito luso limeneli mdziko lathu." Boma likunena kuti likufuna kuteteza anthu aku Malawi ndikuwapatsa ntchito kunyumba.

en.title: Mutharika sends Chihana to lead SADC summit today
en.body: President Peter Mutharika has asked Second Vice President Enoch Kanzingeni Chihana to lead an important meeting today, 22 June 2026. The Extraordinary SADC Organ Troika Summit will be held online and hosted by Malawi. Mutharika chairs the SADC Organ on Politics, Defence and Security Cooperation.
en.body_more:
The Concerned Citizens of Malawi–People Power Movement (CCM-PPM) welcomed the choice. The group said the move shows unity and trust within the government. It rejects claims that senior leaders are divided.

In a statement, CCM-PPM leaders Edward Kambanje, Oliver Nakoma and Prince Mattaka said Mutharika's decision proves he has confidence in Chihana. They said the President and his Second Vice President work well together. The move strengthens national unity, they added.

CCM-PPM said the appointment shows Mutharika trusts Chihana to speak for Malawi at this key regional meeting. The group praised Mutharika for delegating such an important task. They said this kind of leadership decision helps Malawi take part in regional affairs. The movement also sent good wishes to Chihana for the summit.

ny.title: Mutharika atumiza Chihana kuti atsogolere msonkhano wa SADC lero
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika wapempha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enoch Kanzingeni Chihana kuti atsogolere msonkhano wofunika lero, pa 22 June 2026. Msonkhano Wapadera wa SADC Organ Troika udzachitika pa intaneti ndipo Malawi idzachititsa msonkhano. Mutharika ndiye mtsogoleri wa SADC Organ on Politics, Defense and Security Cooperation.
ny.body_more:
Gulu la Concerned Citizens of Malawi–People Power Movement (CCM-PPM) lalandira chisankhochi. Gululi lati chisankhochi chikusonyeza mgwirizano ndi kudalirana mkati mwa boma. Likukana zonena kuti atsogoleri akuluakulu ndi ogawanika.

Mu chikalata chawo, atsogoleri a CCM-PPM, Edward Kambanje, Oliver Nakoma ndi Prince Mattaka, adati chisankho cha Mutharika chikutsimikizira kuti ali ndi chidaliro mwa Chihana. Iwo anati Purezidenti ndi Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wake amagwira ntchito limodzi bwino. Iwo adawonjezera kuti chisankhochi chikulimbitsa mgwirizano wa dziko.

CCM-PPM idati kusankhidwa kwa Mutharika kukuwonetsa kuti akukhulupirira Chihana kuti alankhule m'malo mwa Malawi pamsonkhano wofunikawu wachigawo. Gululi lidayamika Mutharika chifukwa chopereka ntchito yofunika kwambiri. Iwo adati chisankho cha utsogoleri choterechi chimathandiza Malawi kutenga nawo mbali pazochitika zachigawo. Gululi lidatumizanso mafuno abwino kwa Chihana pamsonkhanowu.

en.title: Photographer Ras Peter Kansengwa Laid to Rest in Lilongwe
en.body: Ras Peter Kansengwa, a well-known freelance photographer, was buried on Sunday at Area 18 Cemetery in Lilongwe City. He died on Friday at Kamuzu Central Hospital after a long illness. Kansengwa was 44 years old. For more than 20 years, he took pictures of politics, business, entertainment, and community life in Malawi.
en.body_more:
Kansengwa died after a prolonged fight with abdominal cancer and kidney complications. He was admitted to the hospital on Wednesday. His funeral service was led by the Rastafarian community before he was laid to rest.

Throughout his career, Kansengwa documented state functions, political rallies, and cultural gatherings. His work included pictures of Presidents Lazarus Chakwera and Bingu wa Mutharika, and coverage of the HRDC protests. In 2021, his photographs of South African musician Makhadzi at the Sand Music Festival sparked debate across the region. He was named Best Photographer of the Year in 2016 by the Photographers Association of Malawi (Photama) and won K80,000 and a trophy. Beyond major events, Kansengwa also attended community gatherings, including the Great Angels Choir's Christmas celebrations at BICC. He trained young photographers to help them learn his skills.

Kansengwa faced challenges during his work. During a concert in Blantyre, security personnel accused him of taking pictures without permission. He was assaulted and forced to flee to Ndirande on foot. Health problems began in 2024 when he had trouble with his eye and leg. He went to India for medical care, but his condition did not improve. His weekly medicine cost around K60,000. In 2022, friends held a reggae aerobics fundraiser at Bingu National Stadium to help pay for his treatment.

His son Sylvate said he would continue his father's photography work and keep using his phone number so clients could stay in touch. Family member Yakobe Fabiano asked the public to support Sylvate's business. Photographers Association Vice Chairperson Jacob Phiri said Kansengwa's skills could not be replaced. Kansengwa was born on 10 April 1982 and is survived by three children.

ny.title: Wojambula zithunzi Ras Peter Kansengwa Waikidwa M'manda ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Ras Peter Kansengwa, wojambula zithunzi wodziwika bwino, adaikidwa m'manda Lamlungu ku manda a Area 18 mumzinda wa Lilongwe. Adamwalira Lachisanu ku Kamuzu Central Hospital atadwala kwa nthawi yayitali. Kansengwa anali ndi zaka 44. Kwa zaka zoposa 20, adajambula zithunzi za ndale, bizinesi, zosangalatsa, ndi moyo wa anthu ammudzi ku Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Kansengwa anamwalira atadwala khansa ya m'mimba komanso mavuto a impso kwa nthawi yayitali. Analowetsedwa kuchipatala Lachitatu. Mwambo wa maliro ake unatsogozedwa ndi anthu a Rastafari asanaikidwe m'manda.

Mu ntchito yake yonse, Kansengwa analemba zochitika za boma, misonkhano yandale, ndi misonkhano yachikhalidwe. Ntchito yake inali ndi zithunzi za a Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera ndi Bingu wa Mutharika, komanso nkhani za ziwonetsero za HRDC. Mu 2021, zithunzi zake za woimba waku South Africa Makhadzi pa Chikondwerero cha Nyimbo cha Mchenga zinayambitsa mkangano m'chigawo chonse. Anasankhidwa kukhala Wojambula Wabwino Kwambiri wa Chaka mu 2016 ndi Photographers Association of Malawi (Photama) ndipo adapambana K80,000 ndi chikho. Kupatula zochitika zazikulu, Kansengwa adapezekanso pamisonkhano ya anthu ammudzi, kuphatikizapo zikondwerero za Khirisimasi za Great Angels Choir ku BICC. Anaphunzitsa ojambula achinyamata kuti awathandize kuphunzira luso lake.

Kansengwa adakumana ndi zovuta pantchito yake. Pa konsati ku Blantyre, ogwira ntchito zachitetezo adamuimba mlandu wojambula zithunzi popanda chilolezo. Anamenyedwa ndikukakamizidwa kuthawira ku Ndirande wapansi. Mavuto azaumoyo adayamba mu 2024 pamene anali ndi vuto la maso ndi mwendo. Anapita ku India kukalandira chithandizo chamankhwala, koma vuto lake silinasinthe. Mankhwala ake a sabata iliyonse anali okwera mtengo pafupifupi K60,000. Mu 2022, anzake adachita chikondwerero chosonkhanitsa ndalama za reggae aerobics ku Bingu National Stadium kuti amuthandize kulipira chithandizo chake.

Mwana wake wamwamuna Sylvate anati apitiliza ntchito ya abambo ake yojambula zithunzi ndikupitiliza kugwiritsa ntchito nambala yake ya foni kuti makasitomala athe kulankhulana. Wachiwiri kwa banja lake Yakobe Fabiano adapempha anthu kuti athandizire bizinesi ya Sylvate. Wachiwiri kwa Wapampando wa Gulu la Ojambula Zithunzi Jacob Phiri adati luso la Kansengwa silingasinthidwe. Kansengwa anabadwa pa 10 Epulo 1982 ndipo wasiya ana atatu.

en.title: Solar Project Faces Delay Over Government Approval
en.body: Press Corporation plc wants to build a 50-megawatt solar power station in Nkhoma, Lilongwe. The project costs $55.1 million (about K100 billion). The company signed a power purchase agreement with Escom in December 2024. Now the project is waiting for a government implementation agreement before it can move forward.
en.body_more:
Ronald Mangani, the chief executive officer of PCL, said on Thursday that the company has made progress with the project's design and financing. Local and international partners want to invest money and provide loans. But they need the government to issue the implementation agreement first. This agreement shows that the government backs the project, and investors need this before they give their final approval.

Mangani said the project was planned to start work in October this year, but this will only happen if the government issues the agreement quickly. Once the agreement is signed, the company can finalise the money for the project and set firm dates for building and finishing the work. The Ministry of Energy spokesperson Joana Thaundi said on Friday that the agreement is in its final stages. She said the ministry plans to approve all similar agreements within six months.

Grain Malunga from the Chamber of Mines and Energy said delays are a problem. He said investors will not risk their money in projects that are uncertain. The implementation agreement is like a project certificate, and if it takes too long, the project may stop completely. Kandi Padambo, a former chief executive officer of Escom, said Malawi has a power shortage. The country should speed up clean energy projects like this one to help solve the problem.

Malawi has total electricity generation capacity of about 554 megawatts. Only 100 megawatts comes from solar power. PCL's new project will nearly double the solar capacity. The company will run the solar station through a new subsidiary called Press Energy Limited. This will be the first time a local company has built a power station for Malawi since Escom was broken into parts.

ny.title: Ntchito Yogwiritsa Ntchito Mphamvu ya Dzuwa Yachedwa Chifukwa Chovomerezedwa ndi Boma
ny.body: Press Corporation plc ikufuna kumanga siteshoni yamagetsi yamphamvu ya 50 megawatt ku Nkhoma, Lilongwe. Ntchitoyi yawononga $55.1 miliyoni (pafupifupi K100 biliyoni). Kampaniyo idasaina pangano logula magetsi ndi Escom mu Disembala 2024. Tsopano pulojekitiyi ikuyembekezera pangano lokhazikitsa boma lisanapite patsogolo.
ny.body_more:
Ronald Mangani, mkulu wa PCL, adati Lachinayi kuti kampaniyo yapita patsogolo pakupanga ndi ndalama za polojekitiyi. Ogwirizana nawo am'deralo ndi apadziko lonse akufuna kuyika ndalama ndikupereka ngongole. Koma akufunika kuti boma lipereke kaye mgwirizano wokhazikitsa. Panganoli likuwonetsa kuti boma likuchirikiza polojekitiyi, ndipo osunga ndalama amafunikira izi asanapereke chilolezo chawo chomaliza.

Mangani adati polojekitiyi idakonzedwa kuti iyambe kugwira ntchito mu Okutobala chaka chino, koma izi zidzachitika pokhapokha ngati boma litapereka mgwirizanowu mwachangu. Panganoli likasainidwa, kampaniyo ikhoza kumaliza ndalama za polojekitiyi ndikukhazikitsa masiku olimba omangira ndikumaliza ntchitoyo. Mneneri wa Unduna wa Zamagetsi Joana Thaundi adati Lachisanu kuti mgwirizanowu uli kumapeto. Iye adati undunawu ukukonzekera kuvomereza mapangano onse ofanana mkati mwa miyezi isanu ndi umodzi.

Grain Malunga wochokera ku Chamber of Mines and Energy adati kuchedwa ndi vuto. Iye adati osunga ndalama sadzayika ndalama zawo pachiwopsezo m'mapulojekiti omwe sakudziwika. Pangano lokhazikitsa liri ngati satifiketi ya polojekitiyi, ndipo ngati zitenga nthawi yayitali, polojekitiyi ikhoza kuyimitsidwa kwathunthu. Kandi Padambo, yemwe kale anali mkulu wa bungwe la Escom, anati dziko la Malawi lili ndi vuto la kusowa kwa magetsi. Dzikoli liyenera kufulumizitsa mapulojekiti a mphamvu zoyera ngati awa kuti athandize kuthetsa vutoli.

Malawi ali ndi mphamvu zonse zopangira magetsi zokwana ma megawatts 554. Ma megawatts 100 okha ndi omwe amachokera ku mphamvu ya dzuwa. Pulojekiti yatsopano ya PCL idzawirikiza kawiri mphamvu ya dzuwa. Kampaniyo idzayendetsa siteshoni ya dzuwa kudzera mu kampani yatsopano yotchedwa Press Energy Limited. Iyi idzakhala nthawi yoyamba kuti kampani yakomweko imange siteshoni yamagetsi ku Malawi kuyambira pomwe Escom idasweka m'zigawo.

en.title: Record 4000 runners join Be More Race in Lilongwe
en.body: Over 4000 runners will race on Saturday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe for the Standard Bank Be More Race. This is the highest number of people ever to join the race. Tamanda Ng'ombe, the bank's head of brand and marketing, said the race has grown since it started in 2017. The race is now in its eighth year.
en.body_more:
The race has four sections. The 21 kilometre section has 520 runners. The 10 kilometre section has 1268 runners. The 5 kilometre section has 1850 runners. The children's section has 440 runners. This year's theme is 'Keep growing'.

Ng'ombe said the race day will have music from popular Malawian artists, family fun, food stalls and a market for small shops. The race will use new tracking technology to time the runners. This helps to make the race fair for all runners, from young people to professionals. The timing is the same as in big international races.

Athletics Malawi president Kondwani Chamwala said the race shows how running brings people together. He said it helps people live healthy lives and build community spirit. He wished all runners well. The Blantyre Joggers Club has 20 people racing. Their vice-president Limbani Nsapato said they trained hard for this race, including a recent fun run at Kamuzu Stadium.

The race will give out K15.9 million in prize money. Each winner in the 21 kilometre race will get K2.5 million. The bank will match all the money from entry fees. This money will help build a sports place at a school so young people can run and be healthy.

ny.title: Anthu 4000 othamanga alowa nawo mpikisano wa Be More ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Othamanga opitilira 4000 adzathamanga Loweruka ku Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe pa mpikisano wa Standard Bank Be More Race. Ichi ndi chiwerengero chachikulu cha anthu omwe adalowa nawo mpikisanowu. Tamanda Ng'ombe, mkulu wa kampani yotsatsa malonda ku bankiyi, adati mpikisanowu wakula kuyambira pomwe unayamba mu 2017. Mpikisanowu tsopano uli m'chaka chachisanu ndi chitatu.
ny.body_more:
Mpikisanowu uli ndi magawo anayi. Gawo la makilomita 21 lili ndi othamanga 520. Gawo la makilomita 10 lili ndi othamanga 1268. Gawo la makilomita 5 lili ndi othamanga 1850. Gawo la ana lili ndi othamanga 440. Mutu wa chaka chino ndi 'Pitirizani kukula'.

Ng'ombe adati tsiku la mpikisano lidzakhala ndi nyimbo kuchokera kwa ojambula otchuka aku Malawi, zosangalatsa za mabanja, malo ogulitsira chakudya komanso msika wa masitolo ang'onoang'ono. Mpikisanowu udzagwiritsa ntchito ukadaulo watsopano wotsatira nthawi yothamanga. Izi zimathandiza kuti mpikisano ukhale wolungama kwa othamanga onse, kuyambira achinyamata mpaka akatswiri. Nthawi yake ndi yofanana ndi m'mipikisano yayikulu yapadziko lonse lapansi.

Purezidenti wa Athletics Malawi Kondwani Chamwala adati mpikisanowu ukuwonetsa momwe kuthamanga kumabweretsera anthu pamodzi. Anati kumathandiza anthu kukhala ndi moyo wathanzi ndikumanga mzimu wa anthu ammudzi. Anafunira othamanga onse zabwino. Blantyre Joggers Club ili ndi anthu 20 omwe akuthamanga. Wachiwiri kwa purezidenti wawo Limbani Nsapato adati adaphunzira mwakhama mpikisanowu, kuphatikizapo kuthamanga kosangalatsa komwe kunachitika posachedwapa ku Kamuzu Stadium.

Mpikisanowu upereka ndalama zokwana K15.9 miliyoni. Wopambana aliyense pa mpikisano wa makilomita 21 adzalandira K2.5 miliyoni. Banki idzagwiritsa ntchito ndalama zonse zomwe zimaperekedwa kuchokera ku ndalama zolowera. Ndalamazi zithandiza kumanga malo ochitira masewera kusukulu kuti achinyamata athe kuthamanga ndikukhala ndi thanzi labwino.

en.title: Malawian in US becomes ambassador for Lingadzi police
en.body: Chawezi Banda, known as Chacha, has signed a three-year agreement with Lingadzi police station in Lilongwe. She will be the station's community ambassador. Chacha lives in the United States. The agreement was signed on Friday. Chacha will help the police and local people work together better.
en.body_more:
At the signing ceremony, Lingadzi Police Officer-in-Charge Charles James Mpezeni said the partnership would bring improvements to the station. He said Lingadzi police lacks some facilities and equipment. He believes Chacha will help get resources to improve the station.

Charcha told people at the event that she was happy to be the ambassador. She said she had seen the challenges at the station and knew where help was needed. "I am committed to my role in helping the station become a better place," she said. The partnership will focus on strengthening trust between police officers and the communities around them. It will also support projects to improve service delivery.

Charcha has already helped the station. Earlier this year, she paid for the construction of a shelter at the station. At the signing ceremony, she gave a television set to the station for the waiting area where families visit people held there.
