Mpezeni said Chacha will also help with community outreach work, public awareness campaigns and youth engagement. He said she will help with publicity and community work.

Charcha is the daughter of Aleke Banda, who once served as Finance Minister. She lives in the United States but helps poor families in Malawi. She gives money to families for school fees, food and emergency help. Many Malawians abroad like her are helping public services in Malawi.

ny.title: Mzika ya Malawi yomwe ili ku US yakhala kazembe wa apolisi a ku Lingadzi
ny.body: Chawezi Banda, wodziwika kuti Chacha, wasayina pangano la zaka zitatu ndi siteshoni ya apolisi ya Lingadzi ku Lilongwe. Iye adzakhala kazembe wa siteshoniyo. Chacha amakhala ku United States. Panganoli linasainidwa Lachisanu. Chacha athandiza apolisi ndi anthu am'deralo kugwira ntchito limodzi bwino.
ny.body_more:
Pa mwambo wosainira, Wapolisi wa ku Lingadzi, Charles James Mpezeni, anati mgwirizanowu ubweretsa kusintha kwa siteshoniyi. Iye anati apolisi a ku Lingadzi alibe zipangizo ndi zida zina. Akukhulupirira kuti Chacha athandiza kupeza zinthu zoti akonze siteshoniyi.

Charcha anauza anthu omwe anali pamsonkhanowu kuti anali wokondwa kukhala kazembe. Iye anati adawona zovuta zomwe zikuchitika pa siteshoniyi ndipo ankadziwa komwe thandizo likufunika. "Ndikudzipereka pantchito yanga pothandiza siteshoniyi kukhala malo abwino," adatero. Mgwirizanowu udzayang'ana kwambiri pakulimbitsa chikhulupiriro pakati pa apolisi ndi madera ozungulira. Udzathandizanso mapulojekiti opititsa patsogolo ntchito zoperekera chithandizo.

Charcha wathandiza kale siteshoniyi. Kumayambiriro kwa chaka chino, adalipira ndalama zomangira malo obisalamo pa siteshoniyi. Pa mwambo wosainira, adapereka wailesi yakanema ku siteshoniyi kuti azitha kuyembekezera anthu omwe ali kumeneko. Mpezeni adati Chacha athandizanso pa ntchito yodziwitsa anthu za anthu, ma campaign odziwitsa anthu za anthu komanso kutenga nawo mbali kwa achinyamata. Anati athandiza pa ntchito zolengeza ndi ntchito za anthu ammudzi.

Charcha ndi mwana wamkazi wa Aleke Banda, yemwe kale anali Nduna ya Zachuma. Iye amakhala ku United States koma amathandiza mabanja osauka ku Malawi. Amapereka ndalama ku mabanja zolipirira sukulu, chakudya ndi thandizo ladzidzidzi. Anthu ambiri aku Malawi omwe ali kunja kwa dzikolo ngati iye akuthandiza ntchito za boma ku Malawi.

en.title: Area 26 residents ask President to stop eviction without pay
en.body: Residents of Area 26 in Lilongwe are asking President Arthur Peter Mutharika to stop a developer from evicting them without giving them money first. GM Properties Limited wants to remove the families, but a High Court judge ordered the company to pay compensation before any eviction. No money has been paid in 18 months.
en.body_more:
Senior Group Village Head Kwindanguwo spoke for the community on Friday. He said GM Properties Limited has not paid families for their homes, crops, graves and other things since Judge Howard Pemba made the order in October 2023. Instead, the company went to a court in Blantyre to get a new eviction order, while the first court order from Lilongwe remains unfulfilled.

"The court said GM Properties must pay compensation first before owning our land," Kwindanguwo said. "But up to now we have not been paid, yet they want to chase us away. How can a company own land before paying the people who live on it? We cannot leave our ancestral land and become homeless." He asked President Mutharika, the Ministry of Lands and the Attorney General to make GM Properties Limited obey the court order.

Resident Alton Chikaoneka said families are very worried. They have no other land to go to and no money to rebuild homes elsewhere. Children's schooling and jobs are at risk. The parallel court case in Blantyre has made people fear the Lilongwe High Court judgment is being ignored.

Legal expert Benedicto Kondowe said a High Court order stays in force until a higher court changes it. Not obeying it can be contempt of court. Malawi's Constitution, Section 28(2), also says the government and private people cannot take away property without paying compensation first. Until Judge Pemba's order is obeyed, hundreds of Area 26 families remain uncertain about their future.

ny.title: Anthu okhala m'dera la 26 akupempha Purezidenti kuti asiye kuthamangitsa anthu popanda malipiro
ny.body: Anthu okhala ku Area 26 ku Lilongwe akupempha Purezidenti Arthur Peter Mutharika kuti aletse wopanga mapulani kuti asawachotse popanda kuwapatsa ndalama kaye. GM Properties Limited ikufuna kuchotsa mabanjawo, koma woweruza wa Khoti Lalikulu adalamula kampaniyo kuti ilipire chipukuta misozi isanachotsedwe. Palibe ndalama zomwe zaperekedwa m'miyezi 18 yapitayi.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri wa Senior Group Village Kwindanguwo analankhula m'malo mwa anthu ammudzi Lachisanu. Anati GM Properties Limited sinalipire mabanja awo nyumba zawo, mbewu, manda ndi zina kuyambira pamene Woweruza Howard Pemba adapereka lamuloli mu Okutobala 2023. M'malo mwake, kampaniyo idapita kukhothi ku Blantyre kuti ikalandire lamulo latsopano loti anthu atuluke m'nyumba zawo, pomwe lamulo loyamba la khothi kuchokera ku Lilongwe silinakwaniritsidwe.

"Khothi linati GM Properties iyenera kulipira kaye chipukuta misozi isanayambe kukhala ndi malo athu," adatero Kwindanguwo. "Koma mpaka pano sitinalipidwe, komabe akufuna kutithamangitsa. Kodi kampani ingakhale bwanji ndi malo asanalipire anthu okhala mmenemo? Sitingachoke m'malo mwa makolo athu n'kukhala opanda pokhala." Anapempha Purezidenti Mutharika, Unduna wa Zamalo ndi Attorney General kuti apangitse GM Properties Limited kutsatira lamulo la khothi.

Wokhalamo Alton Chikaoneka adati mabanja ali ndi nkhawa kwambiri. Alibe malo ena oti apiteko ndipo alibe ndalama zomangiranso nyumba kwina. Maphunziro a ana ndi ntchito zili pachiwopsezo. Mlandu wofanana ndi wa khothi ku Blantyre wapangitsa anthu kuopa kuti chigamulo cha Khothi Lalikulu la Lilongwe chikunyalanyazidwa.

Katswiri wa zamalamulo Benedicto Kondowe anati lamulo la Khoti Lalikulu likugwirabe ntchito mpaka khoti lalikulu litasintha. Kusatsatira lamuloli kungakhale kunyoza khoti. Malamulo a dziko la Malawi, Gawo 28(2), amanenanso kuti boma ndi anthu wamba sangalande katundu popanda kulipira kaye chipukuta misozi. Mpaka lamulo la Woweruza Pemba litatsatiridwa, mabanja mazana ambiri a ku Area 26 sakudziwa za tsogolo lawo.

en.title: UK and Malawi have strong partnership, says foreign minister
en.body: Minister of Foreign Affairs George Chaponda said the United Kingdom gives good help to Malawi. He spoke on Thursday at a party for King Charles III's birthday in Lilongwe. Chaponda said the UK helps Malawi in schools, health, farms and elections. He spoke for President Peter Mutharika and all Malawians.
en.body_more:
The party was at the British High Commissioner's home. Chaponda told King Charles III well done. He said the UK gave money to help Malawi's elections in September 2025. This money made the vote fair and safe.

Chaponda also said King Charles III helps Malawi with fish farms, fertiliser and a dam on the Bua River. These things help poor farm families get more food and better lives. King Charles cares about clean air and young people. He helps many people around the world.

British High Commissioner Leigh Stubblefield said the UK is changing how it helps Malawi. The UK does not just give money now. It wants to put money into Malawi to make jobs and help businesses grow. She said the UK will help Malawi's government be fair and strong. She said the UK will help Malawi when bad weather or sickness comes. Stubblefield said the Mpatamanga Dam shows what kind of work the UK likes.

Three young Malawians got a big prize. They were named as young leaders for 2026. Sarah Msambira got the prize for farm work and clean power. Msambira works with Small Farm Cities Africa. This group teaches poor farmers and young people. Over 100 young people got help from the group's work. Msambira said young people must be hard workers and have a good team.

ny.title: Nduna ya Zakunja yati UK ndi Malawi ali ndi mgwirizano wolimba
ny.body: Nduna ya Zachilendo George Chaponda adati dziko la United Kingdom lithandiza Malawi bwino. Adalankhula Lachinayi pa phwando la kubadwa kwa Mfumu Charles III ku Lilongwe. Chaponda adati dziko la UK limathandiza dziko la Malawi pankhani za masukulu, zaumoyo, minda ndi zisankho. Adalankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti Peter Mutharika ndi anthu onse aku Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Phwandoli linali kunyumba kwa British High Commissioner. Chaponda adauza Mfumu Charles III kuti zinthu ziyende bwino. Anati UK idapereka ndalama zothandizira zisankho za Malawi mu Seputembala 2025. Ndalamazi zidapangitsa kuti voti ikhale yolungama komanso yotetezeka.

Chaponda adatinso Mfumu Charles III ikuthandiza Malawi ndi minda ya nsomba, feteleza ndi damu pamtsinje wa Bua. Zinthuzi zimathandiza mabanja osauka a m'minda kupeza chakudya chochuluka ndi miyoyo yabwino. Mfumu Charles amasamala za mpweya wabwino ndi achinyamata. Amathandiza anthu ambiri padziko lonse lapansi.

British High Commissioner Leigh Stubblefield adati UK ikusintha momwe imathandizira Malawi. UK sikuti imangopereka ndalama tsopano. Ikufuna kuyika ndalama ku Malawi kuti ipange ntchito ndikuthandizira mabizinesi kukula. Anati UK ithandiza boma la Malawi kukhala lolungama komanso lamphamvu. Anati UK ithandiza Malawi nyengo ikafika yoipa kapena matenda. Stubblefield adati Damu la Mpatamanga likuwonetsa mtundu wa ntchito yomwe UK imakonda.

Achinyamata atatu aku Malawi adalandira mphoto yayikulu. Adasankhidwa kukhala atsogoleri achinyamata a 2026. Sarah Msambira adalandira mphotho ya ntchito yaulimi ndi mphamvu zoyera. Msambira imagwira ntchito ndi Small Farm Cities Africa. Gululi limaphunzitsa alimi osauka ndi achinyamata. Achinyamata opitilira 100 adalandira thandizo kuchokera ku ntchito ya gululi. Msambira adati achinyamata ayenera kukhala ogwira ntchito molimbika komanso kukhala ndi gulu labwino.

en.title: Silver Strikers face Civil Service in Lilongwe derby
en.body: The FDH Bank Premiership returns this weekend after a three-week break. Silver Strikers will play Civil Service United on Saturday, June 20, at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe. This is the first Capital City derby of the season. Silver are second on the table with 13 points. Civil are 11th with seven points.
en.body_more:
Silver come into the match in good form. They have won four games, drawn once and lost once. Their forwards have scored 10 goals and let in only two. Civil have lost three games in a row and hope to turn things around. Both teams want to win the bragging rights of the capital city.

This will be the 29th league meeting between Silver and Civil. In their last 28 matches, Silver have won 13 times, Civil have won eight times, and seven games ended equal. Silver have scored 36 goals against Civil's 10 goals. The rivalry between the two teams is strong and often divides families in Lilongwe.

Silver coach Peter Mgangira will depend on forwards Charles Chipala and Festus Duwe. Duwe moved from Civil to Silver at the start of the season. Another player who crossed from Civil to Silver is Yankho Billiati. Both men will draw attention when they return to Area 9. Civil captain Righteous Banda has played in all games this season and will lead his team. Coach Abbas Makawa will hope strikers Madalitso Maso and Frank Chizuze can score goals.

Makawa said his team have trained hard and believe they can win. Silver's First Assistant Coach Mapopa Kent Msukwa said the Bankers are ready and want the three points. He said derby matches are always hard but Silver have worked on everything. The match starts on Saturday at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe.

ny.title: Silver Strikers ikukumana ndi Civil Service ku Lilongwe Derby
ny.body: Mpikisano wa FDH Bank Premiership ubwerera kumapeto kwa sabata ino pambuyo pa tchuthi cha milungu itatu. Silver Strikers idzasewera ndi Civil Service United Loweruka, pa 20 June, ku Civo Stadium ku Lilongwe. Iyi ndi derby yoyamba ya Capital City nyengo ino. Silver ili pa nambala 2 patebulo ndi mapointi 13. Civil ili pa nambala 11 ndi mapointi asanu ndi awiri.
ny.body_more:
Silver yalowa mumasewerawa ili bwino. Yapambana masewera anayi, yagoletsa kamodzi ndipo yataya kamodzi. Osewera awo akutsogolo apeza zigoli 10 ndipo alola zigoli ziwiri zokha. Civil yataya masewera atatu motsatizana ndipo ikuyembekeza kusintha zinthu. Magulu onsewa akufuna kupambana ufulu wodzitamandira wa likulu la dzikolo.

Uwu udzakhala mpikisano wa 29 wa ligi pakati pa Silver ndi Civil. M'masewera awo 28 apitawa, Silver yapambana nthawi 13, Civil yapambana nthawi zisanu ndi zitatu, ndipo masewera asanu ndi awiri atha ofanana. Silver yapeza zigoli 36 motsutsana ndi zigoli 10 za Civil. Mpikisano pakati pa magulu awiriwa ndi wamphamvu ndipo nthawi zambiri umagawa mabanja ku Lilongwe.

Mphunzitsi wa Silver Peter Mgangira adzadalira osewera akutsogolo Charles Chipala ndi Festus Duwe. Duwe adasamuka kuchoka ku Civil kupita ku Silver kumayambiriro kwa nyengo. Wosewera wina yemwe adachoka ku Civil kupita ku Silver ndi Yankho Billiati. Amuna onsewa adzakopa chidwi akabwerera ku Area 9. Kaputeni wa Civil Righteous Banda wasewera masewera onse nyengo ino ndipo adzatsogolera timu yake. Mphunzitsi Abbas Makawa akuyembekeza kuti osewera a Madalitso Maso ndi Frank Chizuze azitha kupeza zigoli.

Makawa adati timu yake yachita masewera olimbitsa thupi mwakhama ndipo ikukhulupirira kuti ikhoza kupambana. Mphunzitsi Woyamba Wothandizira wa Silver, Mapopa, Kent Msukwa, adati a Bankers ali okonzeka ndipo akufuna mfundo zitatuzi. Iye adati masewera a derby nthawi zonse amakhala ovuta koma Silver yagwira ntchito pa chilichonse. Masewerawa ayamba Loweruka ku Civo Stadium ku Lilongwe.

en.title: Mzuzu youth ask government to control South Africa travel
en.body: Young people in Mzuzu have written to the government asking for strict controls on travel to South Africa. They say 10,000 Malawians are stranded there after attacks on foreign people. About 400 more Malawians are crossing the border every day, they say. This is making it hard to bring people home.
en.body_more:
The letter was sent in June 2026 to Homeland Security Minister Peter Mukhito. Gomezgani Nkhoma, the leader of Mzuzu Youth Caucus, wrote that rescue work cannot continue if people keep leaving. "Thousands are now left stranded, urgently seeking the government's intervention. And since both government and well-wishers will continue coughing hard-earned resources to repatriate our countrymen, we are pleading for urgent control measures," Nkhoma wrote.

The young people want the government to ask people for a K2 million bond before they travel to South Africa. They also want fingerprint and face checks at the border to stop people who left before from leaving again. They asked the government to watch bus drivers and ticket sellers who may help people travel without papers. Nkhoma said Malawi should work with SADC countries on border safety and human trafficking. "By tightening border regulations, your ministry will not only protect vital national resources but also save the lives and preserve the dignity of Malawi's youth," the letter says.

Foreign Affairs Minister George Chaponda told the press that many Malawians want to come home. He said it may not be possible to bring everyone back by June 30. The government has sent 55 buses to South Africa to bring people home. President Peter Mutharika has talked to South African President Cyril Ramaphosa about the safety of Malawians there. The Human Rights Defenders Coalition asked the government to help people who come home with money for travel, new homes, and work help.

ny.title: Achinyamata aku Mzuzu apempha boma kuti liziyang'anira maulendo aku South Africa
ny.body: Achinyamata ku Mzuzu alembera kalata ku boma kupempha kuti pakhale malamulo okhwima okhudza maulendo opita ku South Africa. Akuti anthu aku Malawi okwana 10,000 asowa pokhala kumeneko pambuyo pa ziwawa zomwe anthu akunja achita. Anthu ena pafupifupi 400 akuwoloka malire tsiku lililonse, akutero. Izi zikupangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta kubweretsa anthu kunyumba.
ny.body_more:
Kalatayo inatumizidwa mu June 2026 kwa Nduna ya Zachitetezo cha Dziko Peter Mukhito. Gomezgani Nkhoma, mtsogoleri wa Mzuzu Youth Caucus, analemba kuti ntchito yopulumutsa anthu singapitirire ngati anthu akupitirizabe kuchoka. "Anthu zikwizikwi tsopano atsala okha, akufuna thandizo la boma mwachangu. Ndipo popeza boma ndi anthu abwino apitiliza kutsokomola zinthu zomwe zapezedwa movutikira kuti abwezeretse anthu athu, tikupempha kuti pakhale njira zowongolera mwachangu," Nkhoma analemba.

Achinyamatawa akufuna kuti boma lipemphe anthu chikole cha K2 miliyoni asanapite ku South Africa. Akufunanso kuti apeze zala ndi kuyang'aniridwa ndi nkhope kumalire kuti aletse anthu omwe adachoka kale kuti asachokenso. Anapempha boma kuti liziyang'anira oyendetsa mabasi ndi ogulitsa matikiti omwe angathandize anthu kuyenda opanda mapepala. Nkhoma anati Malawi iyenera kugwira ntchito ndi mayiko a SADC pankhani ya chitetezo cha malire ndi kugulitsa anthu. "Mwa kulimbitsa malamulo a malire, unduna wanu sudzangoteteza chuma chofunikira cha dziko komanso kupulumutsa miyoyo ndikusunga ulemu wa achinyamata aku Malawi," kalatayo ikutero.

Nduna ya Zachilendo George Chaponda idauza atolankhani kuti anthu ambiri aku Malawi akufuna kubwerera kwawo. Iye anati sizingatheke kubweretsa aliyense pofika pa 30 June. Boma latumiza mabasi 55 ku South Africa kuti akatenge anthu kunyumba. Purezidenti Peter Mutharika walankhula ndi Purezidenti wa South Africa Cyril Ramaphosa za chitetezo cha anthu aku Malawi kumeneko. Bungwe la Human Rights Defenders Coalition lapempha boma kuti lithandize anthu omwe amabwera kunyumba ndi ndalama zoyendera, nyumba zatsopano, ndi thandizo la ntchito.

en.title: Fuel prices fall but cost of living stays high
en.body: The Malawi Energy Regulatory Authority cut fuel prices on Thursday. Petrol drops 9.5 percent to K5,619 per litre. Diesel falls 5.7 percent to K6,306 per litre. Kerosene falls 16.43 percent to K4,771 per litre. The cuts follow lower global oil prices.
en.body_more:
MERA Board Chairperson Lucas Kondowe said the price changes came because Free on Board prices fell since the last review on 8 May 2026. All three fuels qualified for cuts under the Automatic Pricing Mechanism. The new prices take effect on 19 June 2026.

But many say the cuts are too small. Namiwa, Executive Director of the Centre for Democracy and Economic Development Initiatives, called the reduction "a mockery". He said fuel prices should be between K4,500 and K5,000 per litre. Namiwa argued that the government increased prices by 75 percent in three months, so cuts of 9 percent are not enough. He asked the government to remove the K350 under-recovery charge and reverse January levies on roads and rural power to help lower prices more.

Meanwhile, new inflation numbers show some relief. The National Statistical Office said inflation fell to 23.4 percent in May from 24.3 percent in April. Food prices dropped most, falling from 19.1 percent to 17.6 percent yearly. Non-food inflation eased slightly from 33.2 percent to 33.0 percent. But prices in rural areas fell 0.5 percent per month while urban prices rose 0.3 percent per month.

Economists say the fuel cuts and lower food prices may help households soon. But inflation stays very high, and many families still struggle with the cost of living. MERA warned that conflict in the Middle East continues to affect global fuel prices and how much fuels cost to bring into Malawi.

ny.title: Mitengo ya mafuta ikutsika koma mtengo wa moyo ukukwerabe
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Energy Regulatory Authority lachepetsa mitengo ya mafuta Lachinayi. Mafuta atsika ndi 9.5 peresenti kufika pa K5,619 pa lita. Dizilo yatsika ndi 5.7 peresenti kufika pa K6,306 pa lita. Kerosene yatsika ndi 16.43 peresenti kufika pa K4,771 pa lita. Kutsika kumeneku kukutsatira kutsika kwa mitengo ya mafuta padziko lonse lapansi.
ny.body_more:
Wapampando wa Bungwe la MERA, Lucas Kondowe, anati kusintha kwa mitengo kwabwera chifukwa mitengo ya Free on Board yatsika kuyambira pomwe idawunikidwa komaliza pa 8 Meyi 2026. Mafuta onse atatuwa adayenerera kuchepetsedwa pansi pa Automatic Pricing Mechanism. Mitengo yatsopanoyi iyamba kugwira ntchito pa 19 Juni 2026.

Koma ambiri amati kuchepetsako ndi kochepa kwambiri. Namiwa, Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa Centre for Democracy and Economic Development Initiatives, adati kuchepetsako ndi "kuseka". Iye anati mitengo ya mafuta iyenera kukhala pakati pa K4,500 ndi K5,000 pa lita imodzi. Namiwa adati boma lakweza mitengo ndi 75 peresenti m'miyezi itatu, kotero kuchepetsa kwa 9 peresenti sikukwanira. Iye adapempha boma kuti lichotse ndalama zobweza K350 zomwe sizikubwezedwa bwino ndikubweza misonkho ya Januwale pamisewu ndi magetsi akumidzi kuti athandize kuchepetsa mitengo.

Pakadali pano, ziwerengero zatsopano za kukwera kwa mitengo zikuwonetsa mpumulo. Ofesi ya Ziwerengero Zadziko Lonse idati kukwera kwa mitengo kwatsika kufika pa 23.4 peresenti mu Meyi kuchokera pa 24.3 peresenti mu Epulo. Mitengo ya chakudya yatsika kwambiri, kutsika kuchoka pa 19.1 peresenti kufika pa 17.6 peresenti pachaka. Kukwera kwa mitengo kwa zinthu zomwe sizili chakudya kwatsika pang'ono kuchoka pa 33.2 peresenti kufika pa 33.0 peresenti. Koma mitengo m'madera akumidzi yatsika ndi 0.5 peresenti pamwezi pomwe mitengo ya m'mizinda yakwera ndi 0.3 peresenti pamwezi.

Akatswiri azachuma akuti kuchepetsa mafuta ndi kutsika kwa mitengo ya chakudya kungathandize mabanja posachedwa. Koma kukwera kwa mitengo ya mafuta kukupitirirabe, ndipo mabanja ambiri akuvutikabe ndi mtengo wa zinthu. MERA yachenjeza kuti mikangano ku Middle East ikupitilizabe kukhudza mitengo ya mafuta padziko lonse lapansi komanso mtengo wa mafuta omwe amawononga ku Malawi.

en.title: Rapper Gwamba becomes Big Bullets brand ambassador
en.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets has named rapper Gwamba as the club's new brand ambassador. The deal was signed in Blantyre on June 19, 2026. Gwamba, whose real name is Duncan Zgambo, will help the club reach new fans and build its brand through music and football.
en.body_more:
Big Bullets Chief Executive Officer Albert Chigoga said the club chose Gwamba because of his wide influence and his long love for the team. Chigoga said the partnership will help both the rapper and the club grow and reach new people. "This partnership is about mutual growth. We believe in collaboration," Chigoga said.

Gwamba said the deal is important to him because he has supported Big Bullets since he was a child. "I have been a Bullets supporter since childhood. It is unique and it makes my work much easier because my passion for this club is genuine and comes naturally," he said. The award-winning rapper called the agreement a personal and professional milestone.

As brand ambassador, Gwamba will work on promotional campaigns, fan engagement activities, community outreach programmes, and other strategic projects for the club. Big Bullets said brand research showed the need to attract wider audiences beyond traditional football followers. The club has worked with musicians before, including artist Eli Njuchi, who signed as brand ambassador in August 2022. This new partnership is part of a growing strategy to merge entertainment and sport in building the club's brand.

ny.title: Rapper Gwamba wakhala kazembe wa Big Bullets
ny.body: FCB Nyasa Big Bullets yasankha rapper Gwamba kukhala kazembe watsopano wa kampaniyi. Mgwirizanowu unasainidwa ku Blantyre pa 19 June, 2026. Gwamba, yemwe dzina lake lenileni ndi Duncan Zgambo, athandiza kalabuyi kufikira mafani atsopano ndikumanga kampani yake kudzera mu nyimbo ndi mpira.
ny.body_more:
Mkulu wa Big Bullets, Albert Chigoga, anati kalabuyo yasankha Gwamba chifukwa cha mphamvu zake zambiri komanso chikondi chake cha nthawi yayitali pa timuyi. Chigoga adati mgwirizanowu uthandiza rapper ndi kalabuyo kukula ndikufikira anthu atsopano. "Mgwirizanowu ndi wokhudza kukula kwa mgwirizano. Timakhulupirira mgwirizano," adatero Chigoga.

Gwamba adati mgwirizanowu ndi wofunika kwa iye chifukwa wakhala akuthandiza Big Bullets kuyambira ali mwana. "Ndakhala wochirikiza Bullets kuyambira ali mwana. Ndi wapadera ndipo umapangitsa ntchito yanga kukhala yosavuta chifukwa chikondi changa pa kalabuyi ndi chenicheni ndipo chimabwera mwachibadwa," adatero. Rapper wopambana mphotoyo adati mgwirizanowu ndi gawo lofunika kwambiri paumwini komanso pantchito.

Pokhala kazembe wa kampaniyi, Gwamba adzagwira ntchito pa kampeni zotsatsa malonda, zochitika zokhudzana ndi mafani, mapulogalamu ofikira anthu ammudzi, ndi mapulojekiti ena anzeru a kalabuyo. Big Bullets idati kafukufuku wa kampaniyi wasonyeza kufunika kokopa omvera ambiri kuposa otsatira mpira wamba. Kalabuyi yakhala ikugwira ntchito ndi oimba nyimbo kale, kuphatikizapo wojambula Eli Njuchi, yemwe adasaina ngati kazembe wa kampani mu Ogasiti 2022. Mgwirizano watsopanowu ndi gawo la njira yomwe ikukula yophatikiza zosangalatsa ndi masewera pomanga kampaniyi.

en.title: Malawi must use digital tools for business growth
en.body: The Ministry of Industrialisation, Business, Trade and Tourism says Malawi must use new digital tools to grow its economy. Secretary Bright Molande told people that technology and partnerships with other African countries are now very important for business success.
en.body_more:
Molande said businesses can no longer do things the old way. They must use digital tools to work faster and reach more customers. This will help local business owners sell their goods to more people, both inside and outside Malawi.

The government also wants businesses to work together with other African countries. Malawi cannot grow alone. When local companies make strong links with businesses in other nations, they become stronger and can sell more goods around the world. This helps Malawi's products compete with goods from other countries.

The ministry is pushing businesses to change how they sell things. Instead of using old ways, companies should use modern marketing. This means using social media and online shops to show what they make. The goal is to make Malawi's goods easy to see and attractive to buyers everywhere.

The government says businesses should also add more value to what they make. Instead of just selling raw materials, companies should process goods before they are sold or shipped out. When goods are made into finished products here in Malawi, more jobs are created and more money stays in the country. This helps build a stronger economy. The ministry believes that businesses which use new technology and work with partners across Africa will lead the way in the next phase of growth for the nation.

ny.title: Malawi iyenera kugwiritsa ntchito zipangizo zamakono kuti bizinesi ikule
ny.body: Unduna wa Zamalonda, Mabizinesi, Malonda ndi Zokopa alendo wati dziko la Malawi liyenera kugwiritsa ntchito zida zatsopano za digito kuti likulitse chuma chake. Nduna Bright Molande adauza anthu kuti ukadaulo ndi mgwirizano ndi mayiko ena aku Africa tsopano ndizofunikira kwambiri kuti bizinesi ipambane.
ny.body_more:
Molande anati mabizinesi sangathenso kuchita zinthu mwanjira yakale. Ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito zida zama digito kuti agwire ntchito mwachangu ndikufikira makasitomala ambiri. Izi zithandiza eni mabizinesi am'deralo kugulitsa katundu wawo kwa anthu ambiri, mkati ndi kunja kwa Malawi.

Boma likufunanso kuti mabizinesi agwire ntchito limodzi ndi mayiko ena aku Africa. Malawi silingakulire yokha. Makampani am'deralo akamalumikizana mwamphamvu ndi mabizinesi m'maiko ena, amakhala olimba ndipo amatha kugulitsa katundu wambiri padziko lonse lapansi. Izi zimathandiza kuti zinthu za ku Malawi zipikisane ndi katundu wochokera kumayiko ena.

Undunawu ukukakamiza mabizinesi kuti asinthe momwe amagulitsira zinthu. M'malo mogwiritsa ntchito njira zakale, makampani ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito malonda amakono. Izi zikutanthauza kugwiritsa ntchito malo ochezera a pa Intaneti ndi masitolo apaintaneti kuti awonetse zomwe amapanga. Cholinga chake ndikupangitsa kuti katundu wa ku Malawi akhale wosavuta kuwona komanso wokopa ogula kulikonse.

Boma likunena kuti mabizinesi ayeneranso kuwonjezera phindu pa zomwe amapanga. M'malo mongogulitsa zinthu zopangira, makampani ayenera kukonza katundu asanagulitsidwe kapena kutumizidwa kunja. Zinthu zikapangidwa kukhala zinthu zomalizidwa kuno ku Malawi, ntchito zambiri zimapangidwa ndipo ndalama zambiri zimakhalabe mdzikolo. Izi zimathandiza kumanga chuma cholimba. Undunawu ukukhulupirira kuti mabizinesi omwe amagwiritsa ntchito ukadaulo watsopano komanso ogwira ntchito ndi anzawo ku Africa konse adzatsogolera gawo lotsatira la kukula kwa dzikolo.

en.title: Apostle Kawinga gives K13.5 million help to flood victims in Lilongwe
en.body: Apostle Clifford Kawinga gave food and other relief items to 878 families hit by hailstorms and heavy rains in Lilongwe on Thursday. The families live in the area of Group Village Headman Msambo, Traditional Authority Chimutu. Kawinga runs Salvation for All Ministries (SAMI) and the Hope Field Initiative (HFI). The total help was worth K13.5 million.
en.body_more:
The hailstorms and heavy rains fell on 6 June 2026 and destroyed many homes and crops. Families lost their basic things needed to live. The relief package included K8.7 million in cash given to 146 families, 318 bags of fertilizer, 318 packets of tomato and cabbage seeds, 100 iron sheets for rebuilding five homes, and plastic sheets for all 878 households.

Apostle Kawinga said the ministry wanted to help people recover and rebuild. He said the Help Field Initiative will also give long-term support, including help with farming and small business work. This will help families stop being hungry and poor. He told people to protect the environment by not making charcoal, which he said causes trees to fall and changes the weather.

Group Village Headman Msambo thanked Apostle Kawinga for the help. He said it gave hope back to the families. One woman, Manasse M'neno, said the help would let her rebuild her home and start a small business again. Another survivor, Manasseh Neno, lost her house, things, and business when the water came through the area.

Just one day before helping in Lilongwe, Apostle Kawinga gave the same kind of help to families in Dowa. This is part of his ministry's work to help weak communities all over Malawi.

ny.title: Apostle Kawinga apereka thandizo la K13.5 miliyoni kwa anthu omwe akhudzidwa ndi kusefukira kwa madzi ku Lilongwe
ny.body: Mtumwi Clifford Kawinga anapereka chakudya ndi zinthu zina zothandizira mabanja 878 omwe akhudzidwa ndi mvula yamkuntho ndi mvula yamphamvu ku Lilongwe Lachinayi. Mabanjawa amakhala mdera la Group Village Headman Msambo, Traditional Authority Chimutu. Kawinga amayendetsa Salvation for All Ministries (SAMI) ndi Hope Field Initiative (HFI). Thandizo lonse linali la K13.5 miliyoni.
ny.body_more:
Mvula yamkuntho ndi mvula yamphamvu inagwa pa 6 June 2026 ndipo inawononga nyumba zambiri ndi mbewu. Mabanja anataya zinthu zawo zofunika kuti akhale ndi moyo. Phukusi lothandizira linaphatikizapo ndalama zokwana K8.7 miliyoni zomwe zinaperekedwa kwa mabanja 146, matumba 318 a feteleza, mapaketi 318 a mbewu za phwetekere ndi kabichi, mapepala 100 achitsulo omangira nyumba zisanu, ndi mapepala apulasitiki a mabanja onse 878.

Apostle Kawinga anati undunawu ukufuna kuthandiza anthu kuti abwererenso ndikumanganso nyumba. Anati bungwe la Help Field Initiative lidzaperekanso chithandizo cha nthawi yayitali, kuphatikizapo thandizo la ulimi ndi ntchito zamabizinesi ang'onoang'ono. Izi zithandiza mabanja kusiya kukhala ndi njala ndi osauka. Anauza anthu kuti ateteze chilengedwe posapanga makala, zomwe anati zimayambitsa mitengo kugwa ndikusintha nyengo.

Mtsogoleri wa Gulu la Mudzi Msambo anayamikira Apostle Kawinga chifukwa cha thandizoli. Anati lapereka chiyembekezo kwa mabanjawo. Mayi wina, Manasse M'neno, anati thandizoli limulola kumanganso nyumba yake ndikuyambitsanso bizinesi yaying'ono. Munthu wina wopulumuka, Manasseh Neno, anataya nyumba yake, zinthu zake, ndi bizinesi yake pamene madzi anadutsa m'derali.

Tsiku limodzi lokha asanathandize ku Lilongwe, Mtumwi Kawinga anapereka thandizo lomweli kwa mabanja ku Dowa. Iyi ndi gawo la ntchito ya utumiki wake yothandiza madera ofooka ku Malawi konse.

en.title: Minister Tells Lilongwe: Stop Dirty Streets, Change Your Minds
en.body: Minister of Local Government Ben Phiri told Lilongwe people on Wednesday to stop blaming the government and clean their city themselves. He spoke at Phungu Primary School and said the capital is too dirty. He said Blantyre is clean because people there changed their habits. Lilongwe can be clean too, he said, but only if people stop throwing rubbish and start caring.
en.body_more:
Phiri said dirty streets hurt the city's image and scare away visitors and business. He warned that blocked drains cause floods and sickness. "Our mindset has to change," he said. "We cannot keep waiting for someone else to clean our streets. If Blantyre can do it, Lilongwe can do it better."

The Minister told people to start at home. He asked families to separate waste, use less plastic bags, and keep bins clean. He said people should turn old food into compost instead of throwing it away. But he warned that dirty things like diapers and syringes must be sealed and thrown away safely. He called for monthly clean-up days run by councils, and for shared compost places and drop-off points in neighbourhoods.

Lilongwe City Mayor Alex Banda said the council will enforce new rules and punish people who break them. Banda linked the work to Executive Order No. 2 of 2026 on Sanitation and Hygiene in Public Places, signed by the government. Member of Parliament Gift Nankhuni also gave his support. On the day of the launch, Lilongwe City Council, the police, the army, and others cleaned public spaces across the city to show they mean business.

Phiri said people must follow city bylaws about waste and tell the council when people dump rubbish illegally. He said Lilongwe people must attend council meetings and ask for more trucks, bins, and modern waste systems. "Cleanliness cannot be seasonal," he said. "It must be a culture." He ended by saying clean Lilongwe starts with its own people, not visitors or the government.

ny.title: Nduna Ikuuza Lilongwe Kuti: Siyani Misewu Yonyansa, Sinthani Maganizo Anu
ny.body: Nduna ya Boma la M'deralo Ben Phiri adauza anthu a ku Lilongwe Lachitatu kuti asiye kuimba mlandu boma ndikudziyeretsa okha mumzinda wawo. Adalankhula ku Phungu Primary School ndipo adati likulu la mzindawu ndi lodetsedwa kwambiri. Adati Blantyre ndi yoyera chifukwa anthu kumeneko adasintha zizolowezi zawo. Lilongwe nayonso ingakhale yoyera, adatero, koma pokhapokha ngati anthu asiya kutaya zinyalala ndikuyamba kusamala.
ny.body_more:
Phiri anati misewu yonyansa imavulaza chithunzi cha mzindawu ndikuopseza alendo ndi mabizinesi. Anachenjeza kuti ngalande zotsekedwa zimayambitsa kusefukira kwa madzi ndi matenda. "Maganizo athu ayenera kusintha," adatero. "Sitingathe kudikira kuti wina ayeretse misewu yathu. Ngati Blantyre angathe kuchita izi, Lilongwe akhoza kuchita bwino."

Nduna inauza anthu kuti ayambe kunyumba. Inapempha mabanja kuti alekanitse zinyalala, agwiritse ntchito matumba apulasitiki ochepa, ndikusunga zitini zoyera. Inati anthu ayenera kusandutsa chakudya chakale kukhala manyowa m'malo mochitaya. Koma inachenjeza kuti zinthu zonyansa monga matewera ndi ma syringe ziyenera kutsekedwa ndikutayidwa mosamala. Inapempha masiku oyeretsa mwezi uliwonse omwe amayendetsedwa ndi makhonsolo, komanso malo ogawana manyowa ndi malo osiyira anthu m'madera oyandikana nawo.

Meya wa Mzinda wa Lilongwe Alex Banda adati khonsolo idzakhazikitsa malamulo atsopano ndikulanga anthu omwe amawaswa. Banda adalumikiza ntchitoyi ndi Executive Order No. 2 ya 2026 yokhudza Ukhondo ndi Ukhondo m'Malo Opezeka Anthu Onse, yosainidwa ndi boma. Mlembi wa Nyumba Yamalamulo Gift Nankhuni nayenso adathandizira. Pa tsiku loyambitsa, Khonsolo ya Mzinda wa Lilongwe, apolisi, asilikali, ndi ena adayeretsa malo opezeka anthu ambiri mumzinda wonse kuti asonyeze kuti akuchita zinthu mopanda chilungamo.

Phiri adati anthu ayenera kutsatira malamulo a mzinda okhudza zinyalala ndikuuza khonsolo nthawi yomwe anthu amataya zinyalala mosaloledwa. Anati anthu a ku Lilongwe ayenera kupezeka pamisonkhano ya khonsolo ndikupempha magalimoto ambiri, zinyalala, ndi zinyalala zamakono. "Ukhondo sungakhale wa nyengo," adatero. "Uyenera kukhala chikhalidwe." Anamaliza ndi kunena kuti kuyeretsa ku Lilongwe kumayamba ndi anthu ake, osati alendo kapena boma.

en.title: Malawi Must Turn Recovery Plan into Real Growth
en.body: Malawi needs to focus on doing what the government plans, not just making plans. Standard Bank Chief Executive Phillip Madinga said this at a National Economic Recovery Plan workshop. He said the country has good ideas, but the big challenge is to make them work.
en.body_more:
Malinga told people at the workshop that Malawi is at a turning point. The choices the government and businesses make now about policy and teamwork will decide how fast and how well the country recovers. The recent NERP workshop brought many people together to talk about the country's money problems and to move closer to fixing them.

Malawi's economy has faced hard times. Prices have gone up fast. The government does not have enough money. The country owes too much money. And there is not enough foreign money to buy things from other countries. These problems come from outside shocks like COVID-19 and bad weather, but also from problems at home with policy and how the government runs things. The NERP has clear goals: bring prices down to single digits, lower the debt, sell more things to other countries, and return to real growth. But having big goals is not enough. The workshop showed that success needs careful choice of what to do first, steady policy, and hard work to carry out the plans.

The private sector must help drive the recovery. Government alone cannot fix the economy. Businesses need to invest money in farming, making goods, mining, tourism, and building things. For this to work, the government must give businesses confidence. There must be clear rules, steady policy, and fair access to foreign money and credit.

Standard Bank said it will help in three ways: send money into sectors that sell goods to other countries and create jobs, give more loans to small and large businesses, and expand digital banking to more people. The bank said it will keep lending to farms that grow tobacco and other export crops. It will also help local factories to make more goods and sell them abroad. Digital banking and agency banking will bring banking services to more communities and help more Malawians join the formal economy.

ny.title: Malawi Iyenera Kusintha Dongosolo Lobwezeretsa Zinthu Kukhala Kukula Kwenikweni
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi liyenera kuyang'ana kwambiri pa kuchita zomwe boma likukonzekera, osati kungopanga mapulani okha. Phillip Madinga, Mkulu wa Standard Bank, adanena izi pa msonkhano wa National Economic Recovery Plan. Iye anati dzikolo lili ndi malingaliro abwino, koma vuto lalikulu ndikuwapangitsa kuti agwire ntchito.
ny.body_more:
Malinga anauza anthu omwe anali pamsonkhanowo kuti Malawi ili pa nthawi yosintha zinthu. Zosankha zomwe boma ndi mabizinesi amapanga pakali pano pankhani ya mfundo ndi mgwirizano zidzasankha momwe dzikolo lidzachire mwachangu komanso momwe lidzakhalire bwino. Msonkhano waposachedwa wa NERP unasonkhanitsa anthu ambiri kuti akambirane za mavuto azachuma mdzikolo ndikuyandikira kuwakonza.

Chuma cha Malawi chakumana ndi mavuto. Mitengo yakwera mofulumira. Boma lilibe ndalama zokwanira. Dzikoli lili ndi ngongole zambiri. Ndipo palibe ndalama zokwanira zakunja zogulira zinthu kuchokera kumayiko ena. Mavutowa amachokera ku zoopsa zakunja monga COVID-19 ndi nyengo yoipa, komanso mavuto akunyumba ndi mfundo ndi momwe boma limayendetsera zinthu. NERP ili ndi zolinga zomveka bwino: kuchepetsa mitengo kufika pa manambala amodzi, kuchepetsa ngongole, kugulitsa zinthu zambiri kumayiko ena, ndikubwerera ku kukula kwenikweni. Koma kukhala ndi zolinga zazikulu sikokwanira. Msonkhanowo unawonetsa kuti kupambana kumafuna kusankha mosamala chochita choyamba, mfundo zokhazikika, ndi kugwira ntchito molimbika kuti akwaniritse mapulaniwo.

Mabungwe achinsinsi ayenera kuthandiza kuyendetsa bwino chuma. Boma lokha silingathe kukonza chuma. Mabizinesi ayenera kuyika ndalama mu ulimi, kupanga katundu, migodi, zokopa alendo, ndi kumanga zinthu. Kuti izi zitheke, boma liyenera kupatsa mabizinesi chidaliro. Payenera kukhala malamulo omveka bwino, mfundo zokhazikika, komanso mwayi wopeza ndalama zakunja ndi ngongole.

Standard Bank yati izi zithandiza m'njira zitatu: kutumiza ndalama m'magawo omwe amagulitsa katundu kumayiko ena ndikupanga ntchito, kupereka ngongole zambiri kumabizinesi ang'onoang'ono ndi akuluakulu, ndikukulitsa mabanki a digito kwa anthu ambiri. Bankiyo yati ipitiliza kubwereketsa ndalama kumafamu omwe amalima fodya ndi mbewu zina zotumizira kunja. Ithandizanso mafakitale am'deralo kupanga katundu wambiri ndikugulitsa kunja. Mabanki a digito ndi mabanki a mabungwe azibweretsa ntchito zamabanki kumadera ambiri ndikuthandiza anthu ambiri aku Malawi kulowa nawo mu chuma chovomerezeka.

en.title: NFRA wants to buy 108,000 tonnes of maize for grain stores
en.body: The National Food Reserve Agency (NFRA) plans to buy 108,000 metric tonnes of maize for Malawi's grain stores. Chief Bruce Munthali said NFRA will buy 86,500 tonnes through a tender process and 21,500 tonnes from traders. But NFRA has not yet received the money needed. Munthali said he hopes the government will give the funds this week.
en.body_more:
NFRA was given K60 billion in the 2025/26 budget. But at the farm gate price of K900 per kilogramme, buying 108,000 tonnes needs about K97.2 billion. This means there is a big money gap. Munthali said NFRA is also looking for money from other sources, including help from local and foreign partners. The maize will be used for emergency help as well as grain stores.

NFRA published a tender on 13 June 2026. The plan shows where the maize will go: 40,000 tonnes to Kanengo Depot in Lilongwe, 14,000 tonnes to Luchenza Depot in Thyolo, and 10,000 tonnes each to Mangochi and Mzuzu. Smaller amounts will go to Limbe Depot in Blantyre, Bangula in Nsanje, Kazomba in Mzimba, and Karonga depots. NFRA published the tender before getting the money because the buying process takes a long time.

Grain Traders Association of Malawi leader Grace Mijiga-Mhango said the tender is good news. But she warned that traders need at least K1,000 per kilogramme, not K900. She said traders have paid for moving the maize, fumigation and other costs. Agriculture expert Leonard Chimwaza said most farmers have already sold their maize. This means traders and buyers are now the main sellers. He said delivery costs will also be higher because sellers must move maize to NFRA depots themselves. "The danger of entering the market late is that NFRA ends up buying maize at a higher price," Chimwaza said.

Former NFRA chief George Macheka told Parliament in March that Malawi needs between 150,000 and 217,000 tonnes in grain stores to be safe from food shortages. NFRA can store up to 340,000 tonnes.

ny.title: NFRA ikufuna kugula chimanga chokwana matani 108,000 m'masitolo ogulitsa tirigu
ny.body: Bungwe la National Food Reserve Agency (NFRA) likukonzekera kugula matani 108,000 a chimanga kuti akagulitsidwe m'masitolo ogulitsa tirigu ku Malawi. Mkulu Bruce Munthali adati NFRA igula matani 86,500 kudzera mu njira yogulitsira ndi matani 21,500 kuchokera kwa amalonda. Koma NFRA sinalandirebe ndalama zomwe zikufunika. Munthali adati akuyembekeza kuti boma lipereka ndalamazo sabata ino.
ny.body_more:
NFRA idapatsidwa K60 biliyoni mu bajeti ya 2025/26. Koma pamtengo wa famu wa K900 pa kilogalamu, kugula matani 108,000 kumafuna pafupifupi K97.2 biliyoni. Izi zikutanthauza kuti pali kusiyana kwakukulu kwa ndalama. Munthali adati NFRA ikufunanso ndalama kuchokera kuzinthu zina, kuphatikizapo thandizo kuchokera kwa ogwirizana nawo am'deralo ndi akunja. Chimangachi chidzagwiritsidwa ntchito pothandiza mwadzidzidzi komanso m'masitolo ogulitsa tirigu.

NFRA idatulutsa tender pa 13 June 2026. Dongosololi likuwonetsa komwe chimanga chidzapita: matani 40,000 ku Kanengo Depot ku Lilongwe, matani 14,000 ku Luchenza Depot ku Thyolo, ndi matani 10,000 iliyonse ku Mangochi ndi Mzuzu. Ndalama zochepa zidzapita ku Limbe Depot ku Blantyre, Bangula ku Nsanje, Kazomba ku Mzimba, ndi malo osungiramo zinthu ku Karonga. NFRA idasindikiza tender isanalandire ndalamazo chifukwa njira yogulira imatenga nthawi yayitali.

Mtsogoleri wa Grain Traders Association of Malawi, Grace Mijiga-Mhango, anati panganoli ndi nkhani yabwino. Koma anachenjeza kuti amalonda amafunika K1,000 pa kilogalamu, osati K900. Iye anati amalonda alipira kale ndalama zosunthira chimanga, fumigation ndi zina. Katswiri wa zaulimi, Leonard Chimwaza, anati alimi ambiri agulitsa kale chimanga chawo. Izi zikutanthauza kuti amalonda ndi ogula tsopano ndi omwe akugulitsa kwambiri. Iye anati ndalama zotumizira zidzakhalanso zokwera chifukwa ogulitsa ayenera kusuntha chimanga ku malo osungiramo NFRA okha. "Ngozi yolowa msika mochedwa ndi yakuti NFRA imagula chimanga pamtengo wokwera," adatero Chimwaza.

Mkulu wakale wa NFRA, George Macheka, adauza Nyumba Yamalamulo mu Marichi kuti Malawi ikufunika matani pakati pa 150,000 ndi 217,000 m'masitolo ogulitsa chimanga kuti ikhale yotetezeka ku kusowa kwa chakudya. NFRA ikhoza kusunga matani okwana 340,000.

en.title: Mutharika Talks With South Africa President Over Malawians
en.body: President Peter Mutharika spoke by phone with South African President Cyril Ramaphosa. They talked about Malawians in South Africa who want to go home. The two leaders discussed how to bring them back safely. Foreign Affairs Minister George Chaponda said the talk happened.
en.body_more:
Chaponda told the news that both governments want Malawians to come home in a peaceful way. The two countries are now planning how to move the people. Malawi said many Malawians without papers live in South Africa and fear they are not safe.

Malawi said a group in South Africa gave a June 30 date for all undocumented people to leave. Lilongwe said this time is too short. Too many people cannot go home in such a small time. Malawi sent a task force to South Africa to help Malawians who want to go back home.

Ramaphosa said South Africa is not a bad place for foreigners. He said stories about this are not true. He told the world that South Africa has a hard problem with immigration. Ramaphosa said Mutharika told him: "We know you have a problem. We help you." South Africa will send people to African countries, with Malawi, to explain what the country does about immigration.

ny.title: A Mutharika Alankhula ndi Mtsogoleri wa dziko la South Africa pankhani ya Amalawi
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adalankhula pafoni ndi Purezidenti wa ku South Africa Cyril Ramaphosa. Adalankhula za anthu aku Malawi omwe ali ku South Africa omwe akufuna kubwerera kwawo. Atsogoleri awiriwa adakambirana momwe angawabwezeretsere bwino. Nduna ya Zakunja George Chaponda adati zokambiranazo zachitika.
ny.body_more:
Chaponda adauza nkhani kuti maboma onse awiri akufuna kuti anthu aku Malawi abwerere kwawo mwamtendere. Mayiko awiriwa tsopano akukonzekera momwe angasamutsire anthu. Malawi idati anthu ambiri aku Malawi opanda mapepala amakhala ku South Africa ndipo akuopa kuti sali otetezeka.

Malawi idati gulu ku South Africa lapereka tsiku la June 30 kuti anthu onse opanda zikalata achoke. Lilongwe idati nthawi ino ndi yochepa kwambiri. Anthu ambiri sangabwerere kwawo munthawi yochepa chonchi. Malawi idatumiza gulu lankhondo ku South Africa kuti lithandize anthu aku Malawi omwe akufuna kubwerera kwawo.

Ramaphosa adati South Africa si malo oipa kwa alendo. Adati nkhani zokhudza izi sizowona. Adauza dziko lonse kuti South Africa ili ndi vuto lalikulu ndi anthu osamukira. Ramaphosa adati Mutharika adamuuza kuti: "Tikudziwa kuti muli ndi vuto. Tikukuthandizani." South Africa idzatumiza anthu kumayiko aku Africa, limodzi ndi Malawi, kuti akafotokoze zomwe dzikolo limachita pankhani yosamukira.

en.title: Justice Minister Says Plane Crash Report Has Big Problems
en.body: Justice Minister Charles Mhango told Parliament's committee that the plane crash report from the Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation (BFU) does not answer key questions. The crash killed Vice President Saulos Chilima and eight others on June 10, 2024, in Chikangawa Forest. Mhango said the BFU report was not good enough and did not bring closure to families.
en.body_more:
Mhango appeared before the Parliamentary Ad-hoc Committee on June 17, 2026. He said the BFU report had big gaps. The report did not say why the plane had no flight recorders. Mhango asked: did the plane never have these recorders when it was made, or were they taken out later? The report also did not say what caused the crash.

Mhango said he did not see two important reports—one from the Malawi Defence Force and one from the Civil Aviation authorities. He used three other reports to make his ideas: the BFU interim report, a report from Germany's Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation, and a report from the Presidential Commission of Inquiry set up by former President Lazarus Chakwera.

Mhango also raised new questions. He said the plane was not seen on radar during its flight from Lilongwe to Mzuzu. Some people said they heard gunshots near the crash site. Six unknown people were seen at the crash site before help came. Mhango also linked the plane crash to the death of former Attorney General Ralph Kasambara, who died about 72 hours before the crash. He said these two deaths should be looked at together.

On the day of the crash, Chilima was going to the funeral of Kasambara in Nkhata Bay. The plane was to land in Mzuzu at 10:02 a.m. But at 10:00 a.m., people at the funeral said the Vice President was late. The plane crashed at 10:16 a.m. Mhango said this was strange because people announced the Vice President was late before the plane even landed. The committee will hear more people speak on June 18, 2026.

ny.title: Nduna Yachilungamo Yati Lipoti la Ngozi ya Ndege Lili ndi Mavuto Aakulu
ny.body: Nduna ya Zachilungamo Charles Mhango adauza komiti ya Nyumba Yamalamulo kuti lipoti la ngozi ya ndege lochokera ku Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation (BFU) siliyankha mafunso ofunikira. Ngoziyi idapha Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima ndi ena asanu ndi atatu pa June 10, 2024, ku Chikangawa Forest. Mhango adati lipoti la BFU silinali lokwanira ndipo silinabweretse kutsekedwa kwa mabanja.
ny.body_more:
Mhango anaonekera pamaso pa Komiti Yoyang'anira Nyumba Yamalamulo pa June 17, 2026. Anati lipoti la BFU linali ndi mipata yayikulu. Lipotilo silinanene chifukwa chake ndegeyo inalibe zojambulira ndege. Mhango anafunsa kuti: kodi ndegeyo sinali ndi zojambulira izi pamene inapangidwa, kapena zinachotsedwa pambuyo pake? Lipotilo silinanenenso chomwe chinayambitsa ngoziyi.

Mhango anati sanaone malipoti awiri ofunikira—limodzi lochokera ku Malawi Defense Force ndi limodzi lochokera kwa akuluakulu a Civil Aviation. Anagwiritsa ntchito malipoti ena atatu kupanga malingaliro ake: lipoti la BFU la nthawi yochepa, lipoti lochokera ku Federal Bureau of Aircraft Accident Investigation ku Germany, ndi lipoti lochokera ku Presidential Commission of Inquiry lomwe linakhazikitsidwa ndi Purezidenti wakale Lazarus Chakwera.

Mhango adafunsanso mafunso atsopano. Anati ndegeyo sinawoneke pa radar panthawi yomwe inkayenda kuchokera ku Lilongwe kupita ku Mzuzu. Anthu ena adati adamva kulira kwa mfuti pafupi ndi malo omwe ngoziyo inachitikira. Anthu asanu ndi mmodzi osadziwika adawonedwa pamalo omwe ngoziyo inachitikira asanalandire thandizo. Mhango adagwirizanitsanso ngozi ya ndegeyi ndi imfa ya wakale Loya Wamkulu Ralph Kasambara, yemwe anamwalira maola 72 ngoziyi isanachitike. Iye adati imfa ziwirizi ziyenera kuganiziridwa pamodzi.

Pa tsiku la ngoziyi, Chilima anali kupita ku maliro a Kasambara ku Nkhata Bay. Ndegeyo inkayenera kufika ku Mzuzu nthawi ya 10:02 koloko m'mawa. Koma nthawi ya 10:00 koloko m'mawa, anthu omwe anali pamalirowo anati Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wachedwa. Ndegeyo inagwa nthawi ya 10:16 koloko m'mawa. Mhango adati izi zinali zachilendo chifukwa anthu adalengeza kuti Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wachedwa ndegeyo isanatera. Komitiyi idzamva anthu ambiri akulankhula pa June 18, 2026.

en.title: Shoe maker wins award, vows to grow local brands
en.body: Annie Chifulemba Msiska, founder of Joan Footwear, has won the Best Leather Shoes Manufacturing Company award at the National Product Magazine Awards. She says this proves Malawian-made shoes can meet world standards. She will keep helping local brands and the country's shoe industry grow.
en.body_more:
Chifulemba Msiska said the award shows that Malawian shoes are made with excellent quality, strength and good design. "Made in Malawi means excellence," she told reporters. The company helps young people find jobs and builds the shoe industry in the country.

Demand for Joan Footwear shoes is growing fast. Schools, businesses and buyers from other countries now want the shoes. More people trust Malawian leather shoes. The company sells shoes across Malawi and to international markets.

However, Chifulemba Msiska said the company needs help to keep growing. The business needs better access to high-quality leather made in Malawi. Small shoe makers need more money and support from the government. Malawian people must buy more locally made shoes instead of shoes from other countries. When local shoe makers get these things, the industry can grow and create more jobs for Malawians.

ny.title: Wopanga nsapato wapambana mphoto, walonjeza kukulitsa mitundu yamakampani akumaloko
ny.body: Annie Chifulemba Msiska, yemwe anayambitsa kampani ya Joan Footwear, wapambana mphoto ya Best Leather Shoes Manufacturing Company pa National Product Magazine Awards. Iye akuti izi zikutsimikizira kuti nsapato zopangidwa ku Malawi zitha kukwaniritsa miyezo yapadziko lonse lapansi. Apitiliza kuthandiza makampani am'deralo komanso makampani opanga nsapato mdziko muno kukula.
ny.body_more:
Chifulemba Msiska adati mphotoyi ikuwonetsa kuti nsapato za ku Malawi zimapangidwa ndi khalidwe labwino kwambiri, mphamvu komanso kapangidwe kabwino. "Zopangidwa ku Malawi zikutanthauza kuchita bwino kwambiri," adatero kwa atolankhani. Kampaniyo imathandiza achinyamata kupeza ntchito ndikumanga makampani opanga nsapato mdziko muno.

Kufunika kwa nsapato za Joan Footwear kukukulirakulira mwachangu. Masukulu, mabizinesi ndi ogula ochokera kumayiko ena tsopano akufuna nsapatozi. Anthu ambiri amadalira nsapato zachikopa za ku Malawi. Kampaniyo imagulitsa nsapato ku Malawi konse komanso kumisika yapadziko lonse.

Komabe, Chifulemba Msiska adati kampaniyo ikufunika thandizo kuti ipitirire kukula. Bizinesiyi ikufunika kupeza bwino chikopa chapamwamba chopangidwa ku Malawi. Opanga nsapato ang'onoang'ono amafunika ndalama zambiri ndi chithandizo kuchokera ku boma. Anthu aku Malawi ayenera kugula nsapato zambiri zopangidwa m'deralo m'malo mwa nsapato zochokera kumayiko ena. Opanga nsapato zakomweko akapeza zinthuzi, makampaniwa amatha kukula ndikupanga ntchito zambiri kwa aku Malawi.

en.title: Malawi Women Football Team To Play Nigeria In Wafcon
en.body: Malawi's women football team, the Scorchers, will play Nigeria on 28 July in their first match at the 2026 Women's Africa Cup of Nations. The tournament takes place in Morocco from 25 July to 16 August. Malawi is in Group C with Nigeria, Egypt and Zambia. This is Malawi's first time at the Wafcon finals.
en.body_more:
The Scorchers qualified after beating Angola 2-0 on aggregate. Their Group C matches are: Nigeria on 28 July at Al Madina Stadium in Rabat, Egypt on 1 August at Moulay Hassan Stadium in Rabat, and Zambia on 5 August at Al Madina Stadium in Rabat.

Only the top two teams from each group will move to the quarter-finals. The semi-finalists will go to next year's Fifa Women's World Cup in Brazil. Quarter-finalists will play for one more World Cup spot.

The Scorchers have had mixed results before the tournament. They lost 5-0 to Australia and 3-2 to India at the Fifa Women's Series in Nairobi, Kenya, in April. In early June, they lost 1-0 to Tanzania and drew 0-0 in friendly matches in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. The team's last win was at the 2025 Cosafa Women's Championship in January, when they beat Lesotho 8-1 and Angola 1-0 at Old Peter Mokaba Stadium in Polokwane, South Africa.

Coach Lovemore Fazili said the team is ready. "We have learnt a lot from the tournament in Tanzania and this will help us fine-tune for the Wafcon finals. Many players have given me the reason to believe that we are on track and ready for the tournament," he said. Sports analyst Parry Chinyama said the team needs to improve. "There is room for improvement. I believe the technical panel will address the shortcomings before the tournament," he said.

ny.title: Timu ya mpira wa azimayi ya Malawi isewera ndi Nigeria mu Wafcon
ny.body: Gulu la mpira wa azimayi la Malawi, Scorchers, lidzasewera ndi Nigeria pa 28 Julayi pamasewera awo oyamba pa mpikisano wa Women's Africa Cup of Nations wa 2026. Mpikisanowu udzachitikira ku Morocco kuyambira pa 25 Julayi mpaka 16 Ogasiti. Malawi ili mu Gulu C ndi Nigeria, Egypt ndi Zambia. Iyi ndi nthawi yoyamba kuti Malawi ifike kumapeto kwa Wafcon.
ny.body_more:
A Scorchers adapambana mpikisanowu atagonjetsa Angola 2-0 pamasewera onse. Masewera awo a Gulu C ndi awa: Nigeria pa 28 Julayi pa Al Madina Stadium ku Rabat, Egypt pa 1 Ogasiti pa Moulay Hassan Stadium ku Rabat, ndi Zambia pa 5 Ogasiti pa Al Madina Stadium ku Rabat.

Magulu awiri apamwamba okha kuchokera mu gulu lililonse ndi omwe adzapita ku kotala fainali. Osewera mu semi-final adzapita ku Fifa Women's World Cup chaka chamawa ku Brazil. Osewera mu kotala fainali adzasewera malo ena a World Cup.

A Scorchers akhala ndi zotsatira zosiyana mpikisano usanachitike. Adagonja 5-0 ndi Australia ndi 3-2 ndi India pa Fifa Women's Series ku Nairobi, Kenya, mu Epulo. Kumayambiriro kwa Juni, adagonja 1-0 ndi Tanzania ndipo adagonja 0-0 pamasewera ochezeka ku Dar es Salaam, Tanzania. Kupambana komaliza kwa timuyi kunali pa mpikisano wa 2025 Cosafa Women's Championship mu Januwale, pomwe idagonjetsa Lesotho 8-1 ndi Angola 1-0 ku Old Peter Mokaba Stadium ku Polokwane, South Africa.

Mphunzitsi Lovemore Fazili adati timuyi yakonzeka. "Taphunzira zambiri kuchokera ku mpikisanowu ku Tanzania ndipo izi zitithandiza kukonzekera bwino masewera omaliza a Wafcon. Osewera ambiri andipatsa chifukwa chokhulupirira kuti tili panjira yoyenera komanso okonzeka mpikisanowu," adatero. Katswiri wa zamasewera Parry Chinyama adati timuyi ikufunika kusintha. "Pali malo oti tisinthe. Ndikukhulupirira kuti gulu laukadaulo lidzathetsa zofookazo mpikisano usanachitike," adatero.

en.title: Church Gives K11.5 Million Help to Storm Victims in Dowa
en.body: A church group gave money and help to 155 families hit by bad storms in Dowa. On 17 June 2026, the church brought iron sheets and plastic covers to Kashangula Ground. The hailstorm came on 6 June 2026 and damaged homes and crops. Apostle Clifford Kawinga led the Salvation For All Ministries International group.
en.body_more:
The hailstorm on 6 June 2026 brought heavy rain and large pieces of ice. The storm tore iron sheets from roofs and broke homes. It also flattened maize fields and other crops. Many families had no safe place to sleep. They stayed under torn plastic sheets or in broken houses.

Apostle Kawinga said the church wanted to help families rebuild. "Eleven days ago, the hailstorms stole roofs and destroyed crops," he said. "Today, we have come to Kashangula not only to pray with them but also to help them rebuild." Each family got iron sheets and plastic covers to fix their homes.

The church checked which families needed the most help. The 155 families got the help at Kashangula Ground, in the area of Village Head Kashangula and Senior Chief Inkosi Mponela. The relief materials were worth K11.5 million. The church said the storm hit parts of both Dowa and Lilongwe.

Apostle Kawinga said the church will do more work. The Salvation For All Ministries International group will watch how families rebuild. The church also plans to build new houses for the affected families. The group said this will help families rebuild their lives and feel hope again.

ny.title: Tchalitchi Chapereka Thandizo la K11.5 Miliyoni kwa Omwe Anakhudzidwa ndi Mphepo Yamkuntho ku Dowa
ny.body: Gulu la mpingo linapereka ndalama ndi kuthandiza mabanja 155 omwe anakhudzidwa ndi mphepo yamkuntho ku Dowa. Pa 17 June 2026, tchalitchicho chinabweretsa mapepala achitsulo ndi zophimba zapulasitiki ku Kashangula Ground. Mvula yamkuntho inagwa pa 6 June 2026 ndipo inawononga nyumba ndi mbewu. Mtumwi Clifford Kawinga anatsogolera gulu la Salvation For All Ministries International.
ny.body_more:
Mvula yamkuntho ya pa 6 June 2026 inabweretsa mvula yamphamvu ndi chisanu chachikulu. Mphepo yamkuntho inang'amba mapepala achitsulo padenga ndikuswa nyumba. Inaphwanyanso minda ya chimanga ndi mbewu zina. Mabanja ambiri analibe malo abwino ogona. Anakhala pansi pa mapepala apulasitiki osweka kapena m'nyumba zosweka.

Apostle Kawinga anati tchalitchichi chikufuna kuthandiza mabanja kumanganso nyumba. "Masiku khumi ndi limodzi apitawo, mvula yamkuntho inaba madenga ndikuwononga mbewu," adatero. "Lero, tabwera ku Kashangula osati kungopemphera nawo komanso kuwathandiza kumanganso nyumba." Banja lililonse linalandira mapepala achitsulo ndi zophimba zapulasitiki kuti akonze nyumba zawo.

Tchalitchichi chinayang'ana mabanja omwe amafunikira thandizo kwambiri. Mabanja 155 adalandira thandizo ku Kashangula Ground, mdera la Village Head Kashangula ndi Senior Chief Inkosi Mponela. Zipangizo zothandizira zinali zamtengo wapatali wa K11.5 miliyoni. Tchalitchichi chinati mphepo yamkunthoyo inagunda mbali zina za Dowa ndi Lilongwe.

Apostle Kawinga anati tchalitchichi chichita ntchito yambiri. Gulu la Salvation For All Ministries International lidzayang'ana momwe mabanja amamangiranso nyumba. Tchalitchichi chikukonzekeranso kumanga nyumba zatsopano za mabanja omwe akhudzidwa. Gululo linati izi zithandiza mabanja kumanganso miyoyo yawo ndikukhalanso ndi chiyembekezo.

en.title: Girl from Mangochi Wins Big Scholarship to Study in India
en.body: Duwama Muhammad, a student from Mangochi, has won a full scholarship to study at Sharda University in India. She is already studying at Maranatha Academy with help from a scholarship. Ernest Kaonga, the director of Maranatha Academy, helped her get this new chance. The university will pay for all her school costs in India.
en.body_more:
Muhammad sat her Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education exams at Mangale Primary School in Mangochi. She was the first girl to take these tests at that school since 2017. After she finishes her secondary school work, she will be able to go to Sharda University in India to study.

Sharda University said in a statement that it was very happy with what Maranatha Academy is doing. The university said it was impressed that Maranatha Academy paid for Muhammad's whole secondary school education. Because of this, the university chose to give her a place to study in India after she finishes secondary school.

Ernest Kaonga, the director of Maranatha Academy, worked hard to help Muhammad get this chance. Sharda University said thank you to Kaonga for opening doors to good education for young people. The university said it wants to help young people grow, find their best talents, and become leaders in the world.

Muhammad is now waiting for her Standard 8 exam results. The scholarship will help her go to university in India and study there. This chance is very big for her family and for Malawi. It shows how hard work and help from good people can change a young person's life and give them chances to learn in other countries.

ny.title: Mtsikana wochokera ku Mangochi Wapambana Maphunziro Aakulu Ophunzirira ku India
ny.body: Duwama Muhammad, wophunzira wochokera ku Mangochi, wapambana maphunziro onse ophunzirira ku Sharda University ku India. Akuphunzira kale ku Maranatha Academy mothandizidwa ndi maphunziro. Ernest Kaonga, mkulu wa Maranatha Academy, adamuthandiza kupeza mwayi watsopanowu. Yunivesiteyo imulipirira ndalama zonse zolipirira sukulu yake ku India.
ny.body_more:
Muhammad adachita mayeso ake a Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education ku Mangale Primary School ku Mangochi. Iye anali mtsikana woyamba kulemba mayeso amenewa kusukulu imeneyo kuyambira 2017. Akamaliza ntchito yake ya sekondale, adzatha kupita ku Sharda University ku India kukaphunzira.

Sharda University inanena m'mawu ake kuti ikusangalala kwambiri ndi zomwe Maranatha Academy ikuchita. Yunivesiteyo inati yasangalala kwambiri kuti Maranatha Academy yalipira maphunziro onse a Muhammad ku sekondale. Chifukwa cha izi, yunivesiteyo inasankha kumupatsa malo ophunzirira ku India akamaliza sukulu ya sekondale.

Ernest Kaonga, mkulu wa Maranatha Academy, adagwira ntchito mwakhama kuti athandize Muhammad kupeza mwayi umenewu. Sharda University yati zikomo kwa Kaonga chifukwa chotsegula zitseko za maphunziro abwino kwa achinyamata. Yunivesiteyo yati ikufuna kuthandiza achinyamata kukula, kupeza maluso awo abwino, ndikukhala atsogoleri padziko lonse lapansi.

Muhammad tsopano akuyembekezera zotsatira zake za mayeso a Standard 8. Maphunzirowa amuthandiza kupita ku yunivesite ku India ndikuphunzira kumeneko. Mwayi uwu ndi waukulu kwambiri kwa banja lake komanso ku Malawi. Zimasonyeza momwe kugwira ntchito mwakhama ndi thandizo lochokera kwa anthu abwino kungasinthire moyo wa wachinyamata ndikumupatsa mwayi wophunzira m'maiko ena.

en.title: Muluzi: Democracy Won, But Malawi Still Fights Poverty
en.body: Former president Bakili Muluzi says democracy alone is not enough for Malawi. On June 14, 1993, Malawians voted to end one-party rule. Muluzi led the fight against dictator Kamuzu Banda and won the 1994 election. Now, 33 years later, he says people need food and jobs, not just freedom to speak.
en.body_more:
Muluzi told the press that Malawi won the battle for political freedoms, but millions of people still live in poverty. "Democracy alone is not enough," he said. "People have to eat. We have to liberalise our economy because poverty levels are very high."

During his own time as president, Muluzi said he tried to fight poverty. He started the Malawi Social Action Fund (Masaf) and gave free primary school to all children. But he said more work needed to be done. He admitted that even these steps were not enough to help people escape hunger and unemployment.

Muluzi also spoke about the dangers he faced when he worked inside the one-party state. He said he was arrested seven times in 1992. Police held him at Chichiri Prison. He remembered how supporters of Kamuzu Banda called democracy fighters "abongololo" — millipedes — to insult them. People were forced to buy party cards to see doctors in public hospitals. Even unborn babies needed cards. After three Cabinet ministers and one MP died in the 1983 Mwanza Accident, Muluzi and others decided the country needed change. He left the Malawi Congress Party in 1981 and helped start a secret group to push for democracy. "I can only thank God that I came out alive," he said. When he became president in 1994, Muluzi set up an inquiry into the Mwanza deaths. Evidence showed the people were killed, not lost in an accident. The inquiry gave answers to grieving families.

ny.title: Muluzi: Demokalase Yapambana, Koma Malawi Ikumenyabe Umphawi
ny.body: Purezidenti wakale Bakili Muluzi akuti demokalase yokha sikokwanira ku Malawi. Pa June 14, 1993, anthu aku Malawi adavota kuti athetse ulamuliro wa chipani chimodzi. Muluzi adatsogolera nkhondo yolimbana ndi wolamulira wankhanza Kamuzu Banda ndipo adapambana chisankho cha 1994. Tsopano, patatha zaka 33, akunena kuti anthu amafunikira chakudya ndi ntchito, osati ufulu wolankhula wokha.
ny.body_more:
Muluzi adauza atolankhani kuti Malawi idapambana nkhondo yomenyera ufulu wandale, koma anthu mamiliyoni ambiri akukhalabe mu umphawi. "Demokalase yokha sikokwanira," adatero. "Anthu ayenera kudya. Tiyenera kumasula chuma chathu chifukwa umphawi ndi wokwera kwambiri."

Panthawi yake monga purezidenti, Muluzi adati adayesetsa kulimbana ndi umphawi. Adayambitsa Malawi Social Action Fund (Masaf) ndipo adapereka sukulu ya pulayimale yaulere kwa ana onse. Koma adati ntchito yambiri iyenera kuchitika. Adavomereza kuti ngakhale njira izi sizinali zokwanira kuthandiza anthu kuthawa njala ndi kusowa ntchito.

Muluzi adalankhulanso za zoopsa zomwe adakumana nazo pamene ankagwira ntchito m'boma la chipani chimodzi. Adati adamangidwa kasanu ndi kawiri mu 1992. Apolisi adamugwira ku Chichiri Prison. Adakumbukira momwe othandizira Kamuzu Banda adatcha omenyera ufulu wa demokalase "abongololo" - ma millipedes - kuti awanyoze. Anthu adakakamizika kugula makadi achipani kuti akawone madokotala m'zipatala za boma. Ngakhale makanda osabadwa amafunikira makadi. Nduna zitatu za Cabinet ndi MP m'modzi atamwalira mu ngozi ya Mwanza mu 1983, Muluzi ndi ena adaganiza kuti dzikolo likufunika kusintha. Iye anasiya chipani cha Malawi Congress mu 1981 ndipo anathandiza kuyambitsa gulu lachinsinsi lolimbikitsa demokalase. "Ndingothokoza Mulungu kuti ndinatuluka ndili moyo," adatero. Pamene anakhala purezidenti mu 1994, Muluzi anayambitsa kafukufuku wokhudza imfa za anthu a ku Mwanza. Umboni unasonyeza kuti anthuwo anaphedwa, osati atatayika pangozi. Kafukufukuyo anapereka mayankho kwa mabanja omwe anali ndi chisoni.

en.title: Vice President Ansah Says Malawians Judge for Themselves
en.body: Vice President Jane Ansah has spoken about her role in government. She said people see what happens and make their own choices about it. Ansah gave an interview to Luntha Television on Tuesday. She said her work with the President is good and professional.
en.body_more:
Ansah talked about how she speaks with the President. She said she sends memos to him about her work, and he always sends back answers. "When ambassadors from different countries came, I would write a memo to the President and a response would come," she said. "So my relationship is good."

The Vice President explained that the Constitution does not give the Vice President a fixed job. Instead, the President decides what work she will do. During her eight months in office, Ansah said she did only the tasks the President gave her. One of her main jobs was work with the Department of Disaster Management Affairs, or DoDMA. She visited places like Nsanje and checked storage buildings to see if the country was ready for disasters. She said she found problems and told officials to fix them before the rainy season came.

Ansah also answered questions about a trip to the United Kingdom in November. She said she asked the President for permission to go and collect her things after she moved to Malawi for campaign work. She said even while she was away, she could still work with DoDMA because preparations were already in place.

When asked about reports that some of her duties were taken away, Ansah said the President has the power to give and take work. She said she had no comment on this because it is the President's choice under the Constitution. She also said that people will talk and she cannot stop them. "Malawians here see things as they happen and they have their own expectations," she said. "People will judge for themselves."

ny.title: Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Ansah Ati Amalawi Adziweruza Okha
ny.body: Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Jane Ansah walankhula za udindo wake m'boma. Iye anati anthu amaona zomwe zimachitika ndipo amasankha okha. Ansah adayankhulana ndi Luntha Television Lachiwiri. Iye adati ntchito yake ndi Purezidenti ndi yabwino komanso yaukadaulo.
ny.body_more:
Ansah adalankhula za momwe amalankhulira ndi Purezidenti. Anati amamutumizira makalata okhudza ntchito yake, ndipo nthawi zonse amatumiza mayankho. "Akazembe ochokera kumayiko osiyanasiyana akabwera, ndimalemba kalata kwa Purezidenti ndipo yankho limabwera," adatero. "Chifukwa chake ubale wanga ndi wabwino."

Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti adafotokoza kuti Malamulo sapatsa Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti ntchito yokhazikika. M'malo mwake, Purezidenti amasankha ntchito yomwe angachite. Pa miyezi isanu ndi itatu yomwe adakhala muulamuliro, Ansah adati amangogwira ntchito zomwe Purezidenti adamupatsa. Imodzi mwa ntchito zake zazikulu inali kugwira ntchito ndi Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka, kapena DoDMA. Anapita kumadera ngati Nsanje ndikuyang'ana nyumba zosungiramo zinthu kuti awone ngati dzikolo lakonzeka masoka. Anati adapeza mavuto ndipo adauza akuluakulu kuti awakonze nyengo yamvula isanafike.

Ansah adayankhanso mafunso okhudza ulendo wopita ku United Kingdom mu Novembala. Anati adapempha chilolezo kwa Purezidenti kuti apite kukatenga zinthu zake atasamukira ku Malawi kukagwira ntchito yokopa anthu. Anati ngakhale ali kutali, akhoza kugwirabe ntchito ndi DoDMA chifukwa kukonzekera kunali kale.

Atafunsidwa za malipoti akuti ntchito zina zake zinachotsedwa, Ansah adati Purezidenti ali ndi mphamvu zopereka ndi kutenga ntchito. Iye anati alibe ndemanga pa izi chifukwa ndi chisankho cha Purezidenti malinga ndi Malamulo Oyendetsera Dziko. Ananenanso kuti anthu adzalankhula ndipo sangawaletse. "Anthu aku Malawi kuno amaona zinthu momwe zimachitikira ndipo ali ndi ziyembekezo zawo," adatero. "Anthu adzadziweruza okha."

en.title: Airtel and MACRA work together for digital growth
en.body: Airtel Malawi and the Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority (MACRA) say they will work together to grow digital services in Malawi. The new MACRA Board visited Airtel's head office in Lilongwe on Tuesday, June 16, 2026. Airtel Managing Director Aashish Dutt said the company wants to expand network coverage and support Malawi 2063.
en.body_more:
During the visit, the MACRA Board toured Airtel's operations and talked about key issues. These included network expansion, digital innovation, customer experience, and how regulators and operators work together. The Board praised Airtel for investing in network infrastructure and digital services. They said this kind of teamwork is important for building a safe and strong communications sector.

Airtel Malawi said it is investing in 5G networks, rural network coverage, and digital services. These investments help Malawi reach its Malawi 2063 goals. The company wants to bring digital services to all Malawians and help the economy grow. Aashish Dutt told MACRA: "We value the strong and collaborative relationship we share with MACRA. As partners in Malawi's digital journey, we remain committed to investing in innovative technologies, expanding connectivity, and supporting the Malawi 2063 vision of a digitally inclusive and empowered nation."

The visit was part of MACRA's plan to meet with all licensed telecommunications operators. Airtel said it wants to keep working closely with MACRA to help Malawi's digital and connectivity landscape grow. The Board said sustained collaboration between regulators and operators is essential for building a safe, competitive and future-ready communications sector.

ny.title: Airtel ndi MACRA zimagwira ntchito limodzi kuti digito ikule bwino
ny.body: Airtel Malawi ndi Malawi Communications Regulatory Authority (MACRA) ati agwira ntchito limodzi kuti akweze ntchito za digito ku Malawi. Bungwe latsopano la MACRA linapita ku ofesi yayikulu ya Airtel ku Lilongwe Lachiwiri, pa 16 Juni, 2026. Mtsogoleri wamkulu wa Airtel Aashish Dutt anati kampaniyo ikufuna kukulitsa njira zolumikizirana ndi maukonde ndikuthandizira Malawi 2063.
ny.body_more:
Paulendowu, Bungwe la MACRA linayendera ntchito za Airtel ndipo linakambirana za nkhani zazikulu. Izi zikuphatikizapo kukulitsa maukonde, luso la digito, zomwe makasitomala amakumana nazo, komanso momwe oyang'anira ndi ogwira ntchito amagwirira ntchito limodzi. Bungweli linayamika Airtel chifukwa choyika ndalama mu zomangamanga za maukonde ndi ntchito za digito. Iwo anati mgwirizano woterewu ndi wofunikira popanga gawo lotetezeka komanso lolimba la kulumikizana.

Airtel Malawi inati ikuyika ndalama mu maukonde a 5G, kufalikira kwa maukonde akumidzi, ndi ntchito za digito. Ndalama izi zimathandiza Malawi kukwaniritsa zolinga zake za Malawi 2063. Kampaniyo ikufuna kubweretsa ntchito za digito kwa anthu onse aku Malawi ndikuthandizira kukula kwachuma. Aashish Dutt adauza MACRA kuti: "Timayamikira ubale wolimba komanso wogwirizana womwe timagawana ndi MACRA. Monga ogwirizana nawo paulendo wa digito wa Malawi, tikupitirizabe kuyika ndalama mu ukadaulo watsopano, kukulitsa kulumikizana, ndikuthandizira masomphenya a Malawi 2063 a dziko lokhala ndi anthu ambiri komanso lopatsidwa mphamvu pa digito."

Ulendowu unali gawo la dongosolo la MACRA lokumana ndi ogwira ntchito onse olankhulana omwe ali ndi zilolezo. Airtel inati ikufuna kupitiriza kugwira ntchito limodzi ndi MACRA kuti ithandize dziko la Malawi kukhala ndi maukonde a digito komanso kulumikizana. Bungweli linati mgwirizano wokhazikika pakati pa oyang'anira ndi ogwira ntchito ndi wofunikira kwambiri popanga gawo lotetezeka, lopikisana komanso lokonzeka mtsogolo.

en.title: Opposition Leader Calls for Swift Action on Economy and Citizens Abroad
en.body: Opposition Leader Simplex Chithyola Banda has called for urgent government action on Malawi's economic hardship and the repatriation of Malawians stranded in South Africa. In a statement on Tuesday, Chithyola said many citizens are worried about rising living costs, unemployment and the direction of the country. He told the government that it must act decisively to help Malawians in distress.
en.body_more:
Chithyola said thousands of Malawians are trapped in South Africa following violence against foreign nationals. He argued that the government has a duty to protect its own people during moments of extreme difficulty. "These are not strangers. These are our brothers and sisters. They are Malawians," he said. He warned that slow action and endless meetings are not enough when citizens are in danger.

Chithyola also criticised remarks attributed to Foreign Affairs Minister George Chaponda about Malawians of Ngoni origin. He said such comments risk dividing the country when it should be united. "No Malawian should ever be made to feel like a visitor in their own homeland," Chithyola declared. He argued that diversity is the country's strength, not its weakness. With thousands of Malawians seeking help in South Africa, he said the government should focus on protecting citizens rather than becoming involved in divisive controversies.

The Opposition Leader also raised concern about the Office of the Vice President being weakened. He said this office is established by the Constitution and is an institution of the Republic. "Strong democracies respect institutions. They do not diminish them," he said. Chithyola warned that public institutions including Parliament, law enforcement and oversight bodies must remain impartial. When citizens believe institutions are being used for political purposes, public confidence falls and democracy suffers.
