Chithyola further criticised appointments to senior public positions that he believes did not follow transparent and competitive processes. He said positions should be earned through competence, experience and integrity, not political connections or family relationships. He warned that Malawi cannot build a prosperous future on patronage. Rising living costs and unemployment continue to affect many Malawians despite the large national budget, he added.

ny.title: Mtsogoleri wa Otsutsa Akupempha Kuti Pakhale Chisankho Chachangu pa Zachuma ndi Anthu Akunja
ny.body: Mtsogoleri wa chipani chotsutsa cha Democratic Party (DPP) Simplex Chithyola Banda wapempha boma kuti lichitepo kanthu mwachangu pa mavuto azachuma omwe ali ku Malawi komanso kubweza anthu aku Malawi omwe atsala ku South Africa. M'mawu ake Lachiwiri, Chithyola adati nzika zambiri zikuda nkhawa ndi kukwera kwa mitengo ya zinthu, kusowa ntchito komanso komwe dzikolo likupita. Iye adauza boma kuti liyenera kuchitapo kanthu mwachangu kuti lithandize anthu aku Malawi omwe ali pamavuto.
ny.body_more:
Chithyola anati anthu masauzande ambiri aku Malawi ali mumsampha ku South Africa chifukwa cha chiwawa chochitidwa ndi anthu akunja. Anati boma lili ndi udindo woteteza anthu ake panthawi yovuta kwambiri. "Awa si alendo. Awa ndi abale ndi alongo athu. Ndi anthu aku Malawi," adatero. Anachenjeza kuti kuchitapo kanthu pang'onopang'ono komanso misonkhano yosatha sikokwanira pamene nzika zili pachiwopsezo.

Chithyola adatsutsanso mawu omwe Nduna ya Zachilendo George Chaponda adanena okhudza anthu aku Malawi ochokera ku Ngoni. Anati ndemanga zotere zitha kugawa dzikolo ngati liyenera kukhala logwirizana. "Palibe munthu waku Malawi amene ayenera kuonedwa ngati mlendo m'dziko lawo," adatero Chithyola. Anati kusiyanasiyana ndiye mphamvu ya dzikolo, osati kufooka kwake. Popeza anthu masauzande ambiri aku Malawi akufuna thandizo ku South Africa, adati boma liyenera kuyang'ana kwambiri kuteteza nzika m'malo mochita nawo mikangano yogawanitsa anthu.

Mtsogoleri Wotsutsa adawonetsanso nkhawa kuti Ofesi ya Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti ikufooka. Anati ofesiyi idakhazikitsidwa ndi Constitution ndipo ndi bungwe la Republic. "Ma demokalase olimba amalemekeza mabungwe. Sawachepetsa," adatero. Chithyola anachenjeza kuti mabungwe aboma kuphatikizapo Nyumba Yamalamulo, mabungwe oteteza malamulo ndi oyang'anira ayenera kukhala opanda tsankho. Nzika zikakhulupirira kuti mabungwewa akugwiritsidwa ntchito pazandale, chidaliro cha anthu chimachepa ndipo demokalase imawonongeka.

Chithyola anatsutsanso kusankhidwa kwa maudindo akuluakulu aboma omwe akukhulupirira kuti satsatira njira zowonekera bwino komanso zopikisana. Anati maudindo ayenera kupezeka kudzera mu luso, chidziwitso ndi umphumphu, osati ubale wandale kapena ubale wa m'banja. Anachenjeza kuti Malawi singamange tsogolo lopambana pogwiritsa ntchito ndalama zothandizira anthu. Kukwera kwa mitengo ya moyo ndi kusowa ntchito kukupitirirabe kukhudza anthu ambiri aku Malawi ngakhale kuti bajeti ya dziko lonse yakwera, anawonjezera.

en.title: Malawi plants 59 million trees, beats 41 million target
en.body: Malawi planted 59 million trees in the 2025/2026 season, beating its goal of 41 million trees. Minister of Natural Resources Patricia Wiskes said this at the official close of the National Forestry Season in Lilongwe. She thanked non-governmental organisations, public institutions, and private people who took part. The season ran from January to April under the theme "Trees and Forests for Community Resilience".
en.body_more:
Second Vice President Enock Kamzingeni Chihana opened the season on 16 January 2026 at Mpata Primary School in Phalombe District. The planting exercise also managed 8,142.94 hectares of natural regeneration across the country.

Despite beating the planting target, Wiskes said the ministry is worried about tree survival. The current survival rate is between 50 and 60 percent. She said tree planting has often become ceremonial, where seedlings are planted and then left without proper care or follow-up. Some communities also uprooted seedlings during the season.

To improve survival, the ministry will take several steps. It will set aside money for aftercare activities such as weeding and making fire breaks. The ministry will limit planting to public land with clear ownership to stop disputes. Wiskes also said the ministry will engage communities more closely to give them ownership of the trees and provide seedlings where there is need. The ministry plans to work with non-governmental organisations and development partners to support investments in improving tree survival rates, as no funds were put in the national budget for this work this year.

ny.title: Malawi yadzala mitengo 59 miliyoni, yapambana cholinga cha 41 miliyoni
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi linabzala mitengo 59 miliyoni mu nyengo ya 2025/2026, zomwe zinapangitsa kuti mitengo 41 miliyoni ifike. Nduna ya Zachilengedwe Patricia Wiskes adanena izi potseka nyengo ya dziko lonse ya nkhalango ku Lilongwe. Iye anayamikira mabungwe omwe si aboma, mabungwe aboma, ndi anthu achinsinsi omwe adachita nawo. Nyengoyi inayamba kuyambira Januwale mpaka Epulo pansi pa mutu wakuti "Mitengo ndi Nkhalango Zothandiza Anthu Kupirira".
ny.body_more:
Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enock Kamzingeni Chihana adatsegula nyengoyi pa 16 Januwale 2026 ku Sukulu ya Pulayimale ya Mpata ku Phalombe District. Ntchito yobzala mitengoyi idathandizanso mahekitala 8,142.94 a kubwezeretsanso kwachilengedwe mdziko lonselo.

Ngakhale kuti adapambana cholinga chobzala, Wiskes adati undunawu ukuda nkhawa ndi kupulumuka kwa mitengo. Chiwerengero cha kupulumuka chomwe chilipo pano chili pakati pa 50 ndi 60 peresenti. Iye adati kubzala mitengo nthawi zambiri kumakhala mwambo, pomwe mbande zimabzalidwa kenako nkusiya popanda chisamaliro choyenera kapena kutsatira. Madera ena adachotsanso mbande nthawi ya nyengo.

Kuti undunawu upititse patsogolo kupulumuka, undunawu utenga njira zingapo. Udzapatula ndalama zogwirira ntchito zosamalira pambuyo pake monga kupalira udzu ndi kupanga zotchingira moto. Undunawu udzachepetsa kubzala m'malo aboma okhala ndi umwini womveka bwino kuti athetse mikangano. Wiskes adatinso undunawu udzalumikizana ndi madera kuti uwapatse umwini wa mitengoyo ndikupereka mbande pomwe pakufunika kutero. Undunawu ukukonzekera kugwira ntchito ndi mabungwe omwe si aboma ndi othandizana nawo pakukula kwachuma kuti athandizire ndalama pakukweza kuchuluka kwa kupulumuka kwa mitengo, chifukwa palibe ndalama zomwe zidayikidwa mu bajeti ya dziko lonse pantchitoyi chaka chino.

en.title: Auditor Finds K158 Billion Missing in Government
en.body: The Auditor General found K158 billion in financial irregularities across Malawi's government ministries, departments and agencies in the year ended March 31, 2024. Auditor General Thomas Makiwa said some institutions failed to give documents for audit checks. The findings raise serious concerns about how taxpayers' money was managed.
en.body_more:
The largest group of irregularities was miscellaneous spending worth K75.3 billion. Project-related irregularities totalled K28.8 billion, financial statement irregularities were K19.5 billion, and expenditure irregularities were K11.9 billion.

Education Infrastructure Management Unit had projects valued at K28.7 billion that remained unfinished after contractual deadlines expired. The National Registration Bureau failed to use K6.4 billion allocated for biometric registration kits, vehicles, servers and other equipment needed for national registration services. The Judiciary failed to remit K57.4 million in collected revenue. The Directorate of Road Traffic and Safety Services in the Eastern Region failed to keep cash books covering K1.19 billion in revenue collections. The Ministry of Agriculture overstated its revenue by K5.3 billion.

Auditor General Makiwa warned that the repeated failure by some public institutions to provide supporting documentation may not be accidental. The report states that the pattern is widespread enough to raise suspicions that records could be intentionally withheld. This matter has been referred to relevant authorities for further investigation.

The audit identified extensive irregularities and control failures but does not establish that funds were stolen or that criminal offences were committed. Those determinations would require further investigation by the relevant authorities. The findings are likely to increase calls for accountability as Malawi faces economic challenges and pressure on public finances.

ny.title: Auditor Apeza K158 Biliyoni Ikusowa M'boma
ny.body: Woyang'anira Mabuku adapeza ndalama zokwana K158 biliyoni m'maunduna, madipatimenti ndi mabungwe aboma la Malawi chaka chomwe chinatha pa 31 Marichi, 2024. Woyang'anira Mabuku Thomas Makiwa adati mabungwe ena alephera kupereka zikalata zowunikira. Zomwe zapezekazi zikubweretsa nkhawa yayikulu yokhudza momwe ndalama za okhometsa msonkho zimagwiritsidwira ntchito.
ny.body_more:
Gulu lalikulu la zinthu zosalongosoka linali ndalama zosiyanasiyana zokwana K75.3 biliyoni. Zosalongosoka zokhudzana ndi mapulojekiti zinali K28.8 biliyoni, zosalongosoka pa malipoti azachuma zinali K19.5 biliyoni, ndipo zosalongosoka pa ndalama zinali K11.9 biliyoni.

Chipinda Choyang'anira Zomangamanga Zamaphunziro chinali ndi mapulojekiti okwana K28.7 biliyoni omwe sanamalizidwe pambuyo poti nthawi yomaliza yogwirira ntchito yatha. Bungwe Lolembetsa Dziko Lonse linalephera kugwiritsa ntchito K6.4 biliyoni yomwe inaperekedwa pa zida zolembetsera za biometric, magalimoto, ma seva ndi zida zina zofunika pa ntchito zolembetsa dziko lonse. Oweruza alephera kutumiza K57.4 miliyoni mu ndalama zomwe zinasonkhanitsidwa. Directorate of Road Traffic and Safety Services ku Eastern Region inalephera kusunga mabuku andalama omwe anali ndi K1.19 biliyoni mu ndalama zomwe zinasonkhanitsidwa. Unduna wa Zaulimi unanena mopitirira muyeso kuti ndalama zake zinali K5.3 biliyoni.

Wowerengera Mabuku Wamkulu Makiwa anachenjeza kuti kulephera mobwerezabwereza kwa mabungwe ena aboma kupereka zikalata zothandizira sikungakhale mwangozi. Lipotilo likunena kuti njira imeneyi ndi yofala mokwanira kuti ipangitse kukayikira kuti zolemba zitha kubisidwa mwadala. Nkhaniyi yatumizidwa kwa akuluakulu oyenerera kuti akafufuze zambiri.


Kafukufukuyu adapeza kusayenda bwino kwakukulu ndi kulephera kwa kuwongolera koma sanatsimikizire kuti ndalama zinabedwa kapena kuti milandu yaupandu inachitidwa. Zomwe zapezekazi zingafunike kufufuza kwina ndi akuluakulu oyenerera. Zomwe zapezekazi zikuwonjezera kufunikira kwa udindo pamene dziko la Malawi likukumana ndi mavuto azachuma komanso kukakamizidwa pazachuma cha boma.

en.title: EU gives Malawi K34 billion for climate displacement
en.body: The European Union will give Malawi 17 million euros (about K34 billion) to help people who lost their homes due to climate disasters. The money is part of a four-year EU programme for six African countries. Malawi leads the programme, which also helps Mozambique, Madagascar, Mauritius, Comoros and Seychelles. About 45,000 families in Malawi are still displaced.
en.body_more:
EU Ambassador Daniel Aristi-Gaztelumendi said the money will help Malawi prepare for climate shocks and help displaced people return home. The total budget for all six countries is nearly 50 million euros. "We will work with the government, the United Nations, local communities and other partners to address climate displacement," he said at the programme launch on Monday in Lilongwe.

Fatma Said, chief of mission at the International Organisation of Migration (IOM) in Malawi, said 45,000 families remain displaced as of May this year. Many live in camps or with relatives after floods and storms destroyed their homes. Said said many displaced people want to move to higher ground and safer areas where they can rebuild their lives. "Based on the pattern of disasters in recent years, Malawi has a major disaster roughly every four years," she added.

Natural Resources Minister Patricia Wiskes said the government is worried many displaced people have not yet found new places to live. "We would love to see these people get back on their feet and rebuild their lives," she said. The project will work in Blantyre, Chikwawa, Nsanje, Mangochi, Phalombe, Mulanje and Dowa districts. One displaced person, Modester Stonkeni from Chantulo Village in Mangochi, said her house was destroyed in March when heavy rains and strong winds came for two days. "We do not want to go back because our houses are not habitable," she said.

Malawi has faced five major climate disasters in a row, including Cyclone Idai, Cyclone Gombe, Tropical Storm Ana, Cyclone Freddy and El Niño. These disasters have cost the country about K2.5 trillion. The programme aims to help communities prepare for climate shocks before they happen, not only respond after damage is done.

ny.title: EU yapatsa Malawi K34 biliyoni chifukwa cha kusamuka kwa anthu chifukwa cha kusintha kwa nyengo
ny.body: Bungwe la European Union lipereka ndalama zokwana mayuro 17 miliyoni (pafupifupi K34 biliyoni) ku Malawi kuti zithandize anthu omwe adataya nyumba zawo chifukwa cha masoka achilengedwe. Ndalamazi ndi gawo la pulogalamu ya zaka zinayi ya EU kumayiko asanu ndi limodzi aku Africa. Malawi ikutsogolera pulogalamuyo, yomwe imathandizanso Mozambique, Madagascar, Mauritius, Comoros ndi Seychelles. Mabanja pafupifupi 45,000 ku Malawi akadali othawa kwawo.
ny.body_more:
Kazembe wa EU, Daniel Aristi-Gaztelumendi, adati ndalamazo zithandiza Malawi kukonzekera mavuto a nyengo ndi kuthandiza anthu othawa kwawo kubwerera kwawo. Bajeti yonse ya mayiko onse asanu ndi limodzi ndi pafupifupi ma euro 50 miliyoni. "Tigwira ntchito ndi boma, bungwe la United Nations, madera am'deralo ndi mabungwe ena othandizana nawo kuti tithetse mavuto a kusamuka kwa nyengo," adatero poyambitsa pulogalamuyi Lolemba ku Lilongwe.

Fatma Said, mkulu wa bungwe la International Organisation of Migration (IOM) ku Malawi, adati mabanja 45,000 akupitilizabe kusamuka kuyambira mu Meyi chaka chino. Ambiri amakhala m'misasa kapena ndi achibale pambuyo poti kusefukira kwa madzi ndi mphepo yamkuntho zawononga nyumba zawo. Said adati anthu ambiri othawa kwawo akufuna kusamukira kumadera okwera komanso otetezeka komwe angakonzenso miyoyo yawo. "Kutengera ndi momwe masoka akuchitika m'zaka zaposachedwa, Malawi ili ndi tsoka lalikulu pafupifupi zaka zinayi zilizonse," adatero.

Nduna ya Zachilengedwe, Patricia Wiskes, adati boma likuda nkhawa kuti anthu ambiri othawa kwawo sanapeze malo atsopano okhala. "Tikufuna kuona anthuwa akubwerera m'mbuyo ndikumanganso miyoyo yawo," adatero. Ntchitoyi idzagwira ntchito m'maboma a Blantyre, Chikwawa, Nsanje, Mangochi, Phalombe, Mulanje ndi Dowa. Munthu wina wothawa kwawo, Modester Stonkeni wochokera ku Chantulo Village ku Mangochi, anati nyumba yake inawonongeka mu Marichi pamene mvula yamphamvu ndi mphepo yamphamvu zinabwera kwa masiku awiri. "Sitikufuna kubwerera chifukwa nyumba zathu sizingakhalemo," adatero.

Malawi akumana ndi masoka asanu akuluakulu a nyengo motsatizana, kuphatikizapo Cyclone Idai, Cyclone Gombe, Tropical Storm Ana, Cyclone Freddy ndi El Niño. Masoka awa awononga dzikolo pafupifupi K2.5 trillion. Cholinga cha pulogalamuyi ndi kuthandiza anthu kukonzekera masoka a nyengo asanachitike, osati kungoyankha pambuyo poti kuwonongeka kwachitika.

en.title: Men's conference to talk about suicide and mental health
en.body: Many men in Malawi are dying by suicide. Police say 88 out of every 100 suicides are men. From January to March this year, police recorded 95 suicide cases — 85 of them men. An event company in Blantyre is holding a conference to help men talk about their problems.
en.body_more:
Prestige Events Malawi director Chrispin Bondwe said the conference, called "Man in the Mirror", will be on 3 July at Apollo Auditorium in Blantyre. Men will be asked to talk openly about their struggles. Bondwe said these are not small problems. "They sit at the heart of work productivity, family stability, and national economic resilience," he said. Men face many pressures: work stress, failing marriages, money problems, and health issues. Yet most men do not talk about these things because of what society expects from them.

Global research shows men are up to three times more likely to die by suicide than women. In Malawi, only one in 10 men who need mental health help actually get it. "These are not private sadness. They are losses for organisations. They are family collapses," Bondwe said.

The conference will have speakers on different topics. Clinical psychologist Chiwoza Bandawe will speak on men's mental health and work stress. High Court Judge Allan Hans Muhome will discuss wills and legacy planning. Moses Chipemba Soko, a radiology technologist at Zomba Central Hospital, will talk about prostate cancer and men's health. Dr Duncan Goche, head of urology at the same hospital, will discuss erectile dysfunction, infertility, and enlarged prostate.

The event will also have music, poetry, and drinks to help men relax. Bondwe said they want to hold similar conferences in other regions of Malawi — in the central, east, and northern areas.

ny.title: Msonkhano wa amuna wokambirana za kudzipha ndi thanzi la maganizo
ny.body: Amuna ambiri ku Malawi akudzipha. Apolisi akuti 88 mwa anthu 100 aliwonse odzipha ndi amuna. Kuyambira Januwale mpaka Marichi chaka chino, apolisi adalemba milandu 95 yodzipha - 85 mwa iwo ndi amuna. Kampani yochitira zochitika ku Blantyre ikuchita msonkhano wothandiza amuna kukambirana za mavuto awo.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri wa Prestige Events Malawi, Chrispin Bondwe, anati msonkhanowu, wotchedwa "Man in the Mirror", udzakhala pa 3 Julayi ku Apollo Auditorium ku Blantyre. Amuna adzafunsidwa kuti alankhule momasuka za mavuto awo. Bondwe anati izi si mavuto ang'onoang'ono. "Ndizofunika kwambiri pa ntchito, kukhazikika kwa mabanja, komanso kulimba mtima pa chuma cha dziko," adatero. Amuna amakumana ndi mavuto ambiri: kupsinjika pantchito, kulephera kwa mabanja, mavuto azachuma, ndi mavuto azaumoyo. Komabe amuna ambiri salankhula za izi chifukwa cha zomwe anthu amayembekezera kuchokera kwa iwo.

Kafukufuku wapadziko lonse lapansi akuwonetsa kuti amuna ali ndi mwayi wodzipha katatu kuposa akazi. Ku Malawi, mwamuna m'modzi mwa amuna 10 omwe amafunikira thandizo la thanzi la maganizo ndi amene amapeza thandizoli. "Izi si chisoni chachinsinsi. Ndi zotayika kwa mabungwe. Ndi kusweka kwa mabanja," adatero Bondwe.

Msonkhanowu udzakhala ndi okamba nkhani pamitu yosiyanasiyana. Katswiri wa zamaganizo Chiwoza Bandawe adzalankhula za thanzi la maganizo la amuna ndi kupsinjika kwa ntchito. Woweruza wa Khoti Lalikulu Allan Hans Muhome adzakambirana za chifuniro ndi kukonzekera cholowa. Moses Chipemba Soko, katswiri wa zaukadaulo wa radiology ku Chipatala cha Zomba Central, adzalankhula za khansa ya prostate ndi thanzi la amuna. Dr Duncan Goche, mkulu wa urology kuchipatala chomwecho, adzakambirana za vuto la kusowa kwa erectile, kusabereka, ndi kukula kwa prostate.

Chochitikachi chidzakhalanso ndi nyimbo, ndakatulo, ndi zakumwa zothandizira amuna kumasuka. Bondwe adati akufuna kuchita misonkhano yofanana m'madera ena a Malawi - m'madera apakati, kum'mawa, ndi kumpoto.

en.title: Malawi fights to bring home people fleeing South Africa attacks
en.body: The Malawi Government is struggling to bring back thousands of its people from South Africa. They fled attacks against foreign workers. The Department of Disaster Management Affairs said the number needing help has grown from 3,000 to 7,000 people. The government hired 55 buses but now says it needs more help and more buses.
en.body_more:
Wilson Moleni, the head of the Department of Disaster Management Affairs, told reporters that the numbers keep rising. The government first planned to bring back 600 people. Now about 7,000 Malawians are at Sherwood Hall in Durban, South Africa. On Thursday there were 2,500 people there. By Sunday there were 7,000. Moleni said: "We hired about 55 buses when the figure was about 3,000, but now we are heading to 7,000. It means we need more buses and more other resources."

The government will help the most at-risk people first. These are older people, people with disabilities, sick people, women and children. Others are asked to find their own way home if they can. The buses came from three companies: Enkosi, Business Logistics and Ulemu. Some buses have already started bringing people back to Malawi.

Meanwhile, about 1,000 Malawians are still travelling to South Africa every week, even with the attacks happening. The Department of Immigration and Citizenship Services said this number went down from 4,000 before the attacks started. Fraser Nkhoma, the Malawi Consul General in Johannesburg, said many Malawians stopped going to work. They fear attacks on the streets. Without work, they have no money to buy food or pay for homes. Some Malawians have left wives and children behind in South Africa and are going home alone.

On the home side, 645 Malawians left Sherwood Hall and are expected to arrive at Mwanza Border Post soon. They will go to Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre for health checks before going to their home areas. The first group of 150 people came back last week through the same border.

ny.title: Dziko la Malawi likulimbana kuti anthu omwe athawa zigawenga ku South Africa abweze kwawo
ny.body: Boma la Malawi likuvutika kubweza anthu ake zikwizikwi ochokera ku South Africa. Anathawa ziwawa zomwe zinachitikira antchito akunja. Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka inati chiwerengero cha anthu omwe akufuna thandizo chakwera kuchoka pa 3,000 kufika pa 7,000. Boma lalemba ntchito mabasi 55 koma tsopano likunena kuti likufunika thandizo lochulukirapo komanso mabasi ambiri.
ny.body_more:
Wilson Moleni, mkulu wa Dipatimenti Yoona za Masoka, anauza atolankhani kuti chiwerengerochi chikupitirira kukwera. Boma linayamba kukonzekera kubweretsa anthu 600. Tsopano anthu aku Malawi okwana 7,000 ali ku Sherwood Hall ku Durban, South Africa. Lachinayi panali anthu 2,500 kumeneko. Pofika Lamlungu panali anthu 7,000. Moleni anati: "Tinalemba ntchito mabasi okwana 55 pamene chiwerengerocho chinali pafupifupi 3,000, koma tsopano tikupita ku 7,000. Izi zikutanthauza kuti tikufunika mabasi ambiri ndi zinthu zina zambiri."

Boma lidzathandiza anthu omwe ali pachiwopsezo choyamba. Awa ndi okalamba, olumala, odwala, akazi ndi ana. Ena akupemphedwa kuti apeze njira yawoyawo yobwerera kwawo ngati angathe. Mabasiwo amachokera ku makampani atatu: Enkosi, Business Logistics ndi Ulemu. Mabasi ena ayamba kale kubweretsa anthu ku Malawi.

Pakadali pano, anthu aku Malawi okwana 1,000 akupitabe ku South Africa sabata iliyonse, ngakhale ziwopsezo zikuchitika. Dipatimenti Yoona za Anthu Osamukira ku Dziko Lina ndi Ufulu wa Anthu inati chiwerengerochi chatsika kuchoka pa 4,000 ziwawa zisanayambe. Fraser Nkhoma, Kazembe Wamkulu wa Malawi ku Johannesburg, anati anthu ambiri aku Malawi anasiya kupita kuntchito. Amaopa kuukiridwa m'misewu. Popanda ntchito, alibe ndalama zogulira chakudya kapena kulipira nyumba. Anthu ena aku Malawi asiya akazi ndi ana ku South Africa ndipo akupita okha kunyumba.

Kumbali ya kwawo, anthu 645 aku Malawi anachoka ku Sherwood Hall ndipo akuyembekezeka kufika ku Mwanza Border Post posachedwa. Apita ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre kuti akafufuzidwe thanzi lawo asanapite kwawo. Gulu loyamba la anthu 150 linabwerera sabata yatha kudzera m'malire omwewo.

en.title: WHO Says Malawi Is Ready For Ebola
en.body: The World Health Organisation (WHO) has said Malawi is ready to stop Ebola. WHO country representative Charles Kuria Njuguna checked the airport in Lilongwe with Minister of Health Madalitso Baloyi. He said Malawi is moving fast to be ready if Ebola comes to the country.
en.body_more:
Njuguna said the airport is very important because Malawi does not touch countries where Ebola is. Health workers have learned to see Ebola signs and to ask where people came from. Big hospitals now have rooms to keep sick people alone.

Malawi can now test for Ebola here. Before, the tests had to go to South Africa. WHO gave Malawi tools and masks worth K127 million (US$73,000) to help fight Ebola and cholera. The government sent people to borders to check all who come in.

Baloyi said no Ebola is in Malawi now and no one is sick with it. She said the country works with its neighbours to check people at border posts. The airport chief Zione Themba said they clean planes and move waste safely. But some people take things from the waste. The airport changed how it moves waste and told people it is not safe to touch.

Njuguna said Malawi is not waiting for Ebola to come before it makes ready. The country is strong in illness watch, is teaching health staff, and is telling people about the risk. Baloyi said the gift from WHO helps Malawi to be safe from sick people coming from other lands.

ny.title: WHO Yati Malawi Yakonzeka Kulimbana ndi Ebola
ny.body: Bungwe la World Health Organization (WHO) lati Malawi ili okonzeka kuthetsa Ebola. Woyimira dziko la WHO Charles Kuria Njuguna adayendera bwalo la ndege ku Lilongwe ndi Nduna ya Zaumoyo Madalitso Baloyi. Iye anati Malawi ikukonzekera mwachangu ngati Ebola ibwera mdzikolo.
ny.body_more:
Njuguna anati bwalo la ndege ndi lofunika kwambiri chifukwa Malawi silikhudza mayiko omwe Ebola ili. Ogwira ntchito zachipatala aphunzira kuona zizindikiro za Ebola ndikufunsa komwe anthu akuchokera. Zipatala zazikulu tsopano zili ndi zipinda zosungira odwala okha.

Malawi tsopano akhoza kuyeza Ebola kuno. Kale, mayesowo anayenera kupita ku South Africa. WHO inapatsa Malawi zida ndi zophimba nkhope zamtengo wapatali wa K127 miliyoni (US$73,000) kuti zithandize kulimbana ndi Ebola ndi kolera. Boma linatumiza anthu kumalire kuti akaone onse omwe akubwera.

Baloyi anati palibe Ebola ku Malawi tsopano ndipo palibe amene akudwala nayo. Iye anati dzikolo limagwira ntchito ndi anansi ake kuti akaone anthu omwe ali m'malire. Mtsogoleri wa bwalo la ndege Zione Themba anati amayeretsa ndege ndikusuntha zinyalala mosamala. Koma anthu ena amatenga zinthu kuchokera ku zinyalala. Bwalo la ndege linasintha momwe limasunthira zinyalala ndipo linauza anthu kuti sikotetezeka kukhudza.

Njuguna anati Malawi sikuyembekezera kuti Ebola ibwere isanakonzekere. Dzikoli lili ndi mphamvu zoyang'anira matenda, likuphunzitsa ogwira ntchito zachipatala, ndipo likuuza anthu za chiopsezo. Baloyi anati mphatso yochokera ku WHO imathandiza Malawi kukhala yotetezeka ku anthu odwala ochokera kumayiko ena.

en.title: Nocma and Afreximbank agree on $120 million fuel loan
en.body: Nocma, the state oil company of Malawi, is making a deal with Afreximbank. The deal is for $120 million. The money will help Malawi buy fuel and keep fuel supplies safe. Nocma Chief Executive Officer Emmanuel Matapa said the money will cut fuel shortages and keep supply steady when foreign exchange is hard to find.
en.body_more:
The $120 million is a revolving trade finance facility. This means Nocma can use it again and again to buy fuel from other countries. Instead of using only Malawi's own foreign exchange, Nocma can get money from Afreximbank to buy fuel. This helps keep fuel prices steady and stops shortages.

Fuel is very important for Malawi. Transport companies need fuel. Farms need fuel for machines. Shops need fuel for trucks. When fuel is hard to find, all these businesses have problems. If fuel is not there, transport stops, farming stops, and shops close. The new money from Afreximbank will help make fuel more easy to find.

The deal also helps Malawi's economy. When fuel is steady, businesses can plan better. Transport drivers can work more days. Farmers can harvest and sell more food. Small shops can sell more things. If the facility works well, it will help many people in Malawi.

Nocma will need to use the money carefully. The company must buy fuel, store it, and sell it fairly. Businesses should watch for news about when the deal starts. Until then, companies should use fuel wisely and plan for what they need.

ny.title: Nocma ndi Afreximbank agwirizana pa ngongole ya mafuta ya $120 miliyoni
ny.body: Nocma, kampani ya mafuta ya boma ku Malawi, ikupanga mgwirizano ndi Afreximbank. Mgwirizanowu ndi wa $120 miliyoni. Ndalamazi zithandiza Malawi kugula mafuta ndikusunga mafuta otetezeka. Mkulu wa Nocma, Emmanuel Matapa, adati ndalamazi zichepetsa kusowa kwa mafuta ndikusunga mafutawo nthawi zonse pamene ndalama zakunja zikuvuta kupeza.
ny.body_more:
Ndalama zokwana $120 miliyoni ndi njira yogulitsira malonda yozungulira. Izi zikutanthauza kuti Nocma ingagwiritse ntchito mobwerezabwereza kugula mafuta kuchokera kumayiko ena. M'malo mongogwiritsa ntchito ndalama zakunja za ku Malawi zokha, Nocma ingapeze ndalama kuchokera ku Afreximbank kuti igule mafuta. Izi zimathandiza kuti mitengo yamafuta ikhale yokhazikika komanso kuletsa kusowa kwa mafuta.

Mafuta ndi ofunikira kwambiri ku Malawi. Makampani oyendetsa magalimoto amafunika mafuta. Mafamu amafunika mafuta a makina. Masitolo amafunika mafuta a magalimoto akuluakulu. Mafuta akavuta kupeza, mabizinesi onsewa amakhala ndi mavuto. Ngati mafuta palibe, magalimoto amasiya, ulimi umasiya, ndipo masitolo amatseka. Ndalama zatsopano kuchokera ku Afreximbank zithandiza kuti mafuta apezeke mosavuta.

Mgwirizanowu umathandizanso chuma cha Malawi. Mafuta akakhazikika, mabizinesi amatha kukonzekera bwino. Oyendetsa magalimoto amatha kugwira ntchito masiku ambiri. Alimi amatha kukolola ndikugulitsa chakudya chochuluka. Masitolo ang'onoang'ono amatha kugulitsa zinthu zambiri. Ngati malowa akugwira ntchito bwino, athandiza anthu ambiri ku Malawi.

Nocma iyenera kugwiritsa ntchito ndalamazo mosamala. Kampaniyo iyenera kugula mafuta, kusunga, ndikugulitsa mwachilungamo. Mabizinesi ayenera kuyang'anira nkhani zokhudza nthawi yomwe mgwirizanowu udzayamba. Mpaka nthawi imeneyo, makampani ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito mafuta mwanzeru ndikukonzekera zomwe akufunikira.

en.title: 645 Malawians Leave South Africa for Home
en.body: Eight buses carrying 645 Malawians left South Africa on Monday, June 15, 2026. The people were fleeing violence and heading home under a government rescue plan. They had taken shelter at Sherwood Town Hall in Durban after attacks on foreign workers. The buses will reach Malawi through Mwanza Border Post on Wednesday, June 17.
en.body_more:
When the group arrives in Blantyre, they will go to Kamuzu Stadium for health checks and paperwork. After this, they will travel to their home districts. This is the second group to come home through the government repatriation programme. Last week, 171 Malawians came back. Over the same weekend, South Africa also sent back more than 900 Malawians from Lindela Repatriation Centre.

Atupele Muluzi, president of the United Democratic Front, gave K5 million to help with the return. He said the money will help families who lost homes and jobs because of the attacks. Muluzi called for all Malawians to work together on this crisis. He said the government, private business, churches, and aid groups must all take action. "This is not the time for division," Muluzi said. He told people that one bus can cost around K100 million to hire, so the work is very expensive.

The Ministry of Foreign Affairs said more than 7,000 Malawians are still waiting in shelters in South Africa. They need help to get home. The government said it will keep helping Malawians abroad and will give them legal support. Officials said the repatriation work shows Malawi's care for its citizens. Muluzi said the situation needs formal talks between the Malawi and South Africa governments. He asked aid groups, business people, and churches to help if they can. The focus now is on getting all those still stranded back home safely and helping them start again.

ny.title: Anthu 645 a ku Malawi Achoka ku South Africa Kubwerera Kunyumba
ny.body: Mabasi asanu ndi atatu omwe anali ndi anthu 645 aku Malawi adachoka ku South Africa Lolemba, pa 15 Juni, 2026. Anthuwa anali kuthawa ziwawa ndipo anali kubwerera kwawo motsatira dongosolo la boma lopulumutsa anthu. Anabisala ku Sherwood Town Hall ku Durban pambuyo pa ziwawa zomwe zinachitikira ogwira ntchito ochokera kumayiko ena. Mabasiwa adzafika ku Malawi kudzera ku Mwanza Border Post Lachitatu, pa 17 Juni.
ny.body_more:
Gululi likafika ku Blantyre, lidzapita ku Kamuzu Stadium kukayezetsa thanzi ndi mapepala. Pambuyo pake, lidzapita kumadera awo. Ili ndi gulu lachiwiri kubwerera kunyumba kudzera mu pulogalamu ya boma yobwezeretsa anthu kudziko lawo. Sabata yatha, anthu 171 aku Malawi adabwerera. Kumapeto kwa sabata yomweyi, South Africa idatumizanso anthu oposa 900 aku Malawi ochokera ku Lindela Repatriation Centre.

Atupele Muluzi, purezidenti wa United Democratic Front, adapereka K5 miliyoni kuti athandize kubweza anthuwo. Anati ndalamazo zithandiza mabanja omwe adataya nyumba zawo ndi ntchito zawo chifukwa cha ziwawazo. Muluzi adapempha anthu onse aku Malawi kuti agwire ntchito limodzi pa vutoli. Anati boma, mabizinesi achinsinsi, matchalitchi, ndi magulu othandizira onse ayenera kuchitapo kanthu. "Ino si nthawi yogawa," adatero Muluzi. Adauza anthu kuti basi imodzi ingawononge ndalama zokwana K100 miliyoni kuti abwereke, kotero ntchitoyi ndi yokwera mtengo kwambiri.

Unduna wa Zachilendo unati anthu aku Malawi oposa 7,000 akadali kuyembekezera m'malo osungira anthu ku South Africa. Akufunika thandizo kuti abwerere kwawo. Boma lati lipitiliza kuthandiza anthu aku Malawi omwe ali kunja kwa dzikolo ndipo lidzawapatsa thandizo la zamalamulo. Akuluakulu aboma ati ntchito yobwezeretsa anthu kudziko lawo ikusonyeza kuti dziko la Malawi limasamalira nzika zake. Muluzi adati vutoli likufunika kukambirana mwalamulo pakati pa maboma a Malawi ndi South Africa. Iye wapempha magulu othandizira, amalonda, ndi mipingo kuti athandize ngati angathe. Cholinga chake tsopano ndi kuwathandiza onse omwe atsala m'nyumba zawo kuti abwerere bwino ndikuwathandiza kuyambanso.

en.title: Minister Chaponda Says His Words Were Cut and Changed
en.body: George Chaponda is the Foreign Affairs Minister. He gave an interview to South Africa's eNCA Television. Vitumbiko Mumba, a Member of Parliament, shared parts of that interview online. Chaponda says Mumba cut his words and changed what he said. Chaponda says his full words were not divisive.
en.body_more:
On Monday, Chaponda told Nyasa Times that people took his words out of context. He asked Malawians to watch the full interview themselves. When the full interview was checked against what Mumba shared online, big parts were missing. Chaponda's meaning changed because of these cuts.

In the interview, Chaponda spoke about why some Malawians stay in South Africa and face problems there. He was asked about Malawians who break South African laws. Chaponda said that all countries have people who move without papers. He said some Malawians have lived in South Africa a long time. He also spoke about Ngoni and Zulu people who have family ties between Malawi and South Africa from long ago. When asked what he would tell people breaking immigration laws, his answer was not finished in what people saw online.

Mumba is from Mzimba District. On Sunday, he called for Chaponda to leave his job. He said President Arthur Peter Mutharika should fire him if he will not go. Mumba said Chaponda's words showed a bad mind that does not fit in Malawi. Mumba said leaders must treat all Malawians the same and not favor one group. He said Malawi was built by many tribes that came together. Mumba warned that failure to act against Chaponda would show the government agrees with him.

Sources say Mumba took small parts of Chaponda's words and left out the rest. The parts left out explained what Chaponda really meant. Experts say this matters because Malawians in South Africa face danger and attacks. A wrong message from the Foreign Affairs Minister could harm Malawi and South Africa's friendship. The full eNCA interview record shows what Chaponda actually said.

ny.title: Nduna Chaponda Akuti Mawu Ake Anadulidwa Ndipo Anasinthidwa
ny.body: George Chaponda ndi Nduna Yoona za Zakunja. Iye adayankhulana ndi wailesi yakanema ya eNCA ku South Africa. Vitumbiko Mumba, phungu wa Nyumba Yamalamulo, adagawana mbali zina za kuyankhulana kumeneko pa intaneti. Chaponda akuti Mumba adadula mawu ake ndikusintha zomwe adanena. Chaponda akuti mawu ake onse sanali ogawanitsa.
ny.body_more:
Lolemba, Chaponda adauza Nyasa Times kuti anthu adachotsa mawu ake pamalingaliro. Adapempha anthu aku Malawi kuti aonere okha kuyankhulana konse. Pamene kuyankhulana konse kudawunikidwa motsutsana ndi zomwe Mumba adagawana pa intaneti, mbali zazikulu zidasowa. Tanthauzo la Chaponda linasintha chifukwa cha kudulidwa kumeneku.

Mu kuyankhulana, Chaponda adalankhula za chifukwa chake anthu ena aku Malawi amakhala ku South Africa ndipo amakumana ndi mavuto kumeneko. Adafunsidwa za anthu aku Malawi omwe amaswa malamulo aku South Africa. Chaponda adati mayiko onse ali ndi anthu omwe amasamuka opanda mapepala. Adati anthu ena aku Malawi akhala ku South Africa nthawi yayitali. Adalankhulanso za anthu a Ngoni ndi Zulu omwe ali ndi ubale wa mabanja pakati pa Malawi ndi South Africa kuyambira kalekale. Atafunsidwa zomwe angauze anthu omwe amaswa malamulo osamukira, yankho lake silinamalizidwe pazomwe anthu amawona pa intaneti.

Mumba ndi wochokera ku Chigawo cha Mzimba. Lamlungu, adapempha Chaponda kuti asiye ntchito yake. Adati Purezidenti Arthur Peter Mutharika ayenera kumuchotsa ntchito ngati sakufuna kupita. Mumba adati mawu a Chaponda akuwonetsa malingaliro oipa omwe sakugwirizana ndi Malawi. Mumba adati atsogoleri ayenera kuchitira anthu onse aku Malawi chimodzimodzi osati kukonda gulu limodzi. Iye anati Malawi idamangidwa ndi mafuko ambiri omwe adasonkhana pamodzi. Mumba adachenjeza kuti kulephera kuchitapo kanthu motsutsana ndi Chaponda kudzawonetsa kuti boma likugwirizana naye.

Magwero akuti Mumba adatenga magawo ang'onoang'ono a mawu a Chaponda ndikusiya ena. Magawo omwe adasiyidwa adafotokoza zomwe Chaponda amatanthauza kwenikweni. Akatswiri amati izi ndizofunikira chifukwa anthu aku Malawi ku South Africa akukumana ndi zoopsa komanso ziwopsezo. Uthenga wolakwika wochokera kwa Nduna Yoona Zakunja ukhoza kuwononga ubwenzi wa Malawi ndi South Africa. Zolemba zonse za kuyankhulana kwa eNCA zikuwonetsa zomwe Chaponda adanenadi.

en.title: MANEPO Calls for Action on Elder Abuse in Malawi
en.body: Malawi marked World Elder Abuse Awareness Day on June 15. The Malawi Network of Older Persons Organisation (MANEPO) called for strong action to stop abuse. MANEPO says many older people face violence, abandonment, and property theft. The group warned that words alone are not enough.
en.body_more:
MANEPO Executive Director Andrew Kavala told the media that older persons in Malawi face many problems. He said they suffer violence, property grabbing, discrimination, and harmful beliefs like witchcraft accusations. Kavala said these are not small social problems. They are serious breaks of human rights given by the Constitution, the Older Persons Act of 2024, and international laws.

Kavala asked the government to do more. He said the government must make the Older Persons Act work properly and give it money. He called for stronger social protection systems. He also asked that all cases of abuse be investigated and the people who do harm be taken to court. Kavala said laws that are not used are just words on paper. They do not help people.

Beyond the government, Kavala called on traditional leaders, faith leaders, families, and communities to help. He asked them to fight against false beliefs that hurt older people. He said older persons should not live in fear after they have worked hard for their families and towns. Kavala stressed that protecting older people must be a main goal for the whole country, not just something remembered one day a year.

MANEPO says the way a nation treats its old people shows what kind of nation it is. The group will not accept that older people are harmed. It wants real change, not just talk. True progress will come when old people can live safely and with respect in their towns.

ny.title: MANEPO Ikupempha Kuti Pakhale Chisankho Pankhani Yokhudza Nkhanza za Okalamba ku Malawi
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi linakondwerera Tsiku Lodziwitsa Anthu Okalamba za Nkhanza Padziko Lonse pa June 15. Bungwe la Malawi Network of Older Persons Organisation (MANEPO) linapempha kuti pakhale njira zolimbirana kuti athetse nkhanza. MANEPO inati okalamba ambiri akukumana ndi nkhanza, kusiyidwa, komanso kuba katundu. Gululo linachenjeza kuti mawu okha si okwanira.
ny.body_more:
Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa MANEPO, Andrew Kavala, adauza atolankhani kuti okalamba ku Malawi akukumana ndi mavuto ambiri. Anati amakumana ndi mavuto ambiri monga nkhanza, kulandidwa katundu, kusalidwa, ndi zikhulupiriro zoopsa monga milandu ya ufiti. Kavala adati izi si mavuto ang'onoang'ono a anthu. Ndi kuphwanya kwakukulu ufulu wa anthu komwe kumaperekedwa ndi Constitution, Older Persons Act ya 2024, ndi malamulo apadziko lonse lapansi.

Kavala adapempha boma kuti lichite zambiri. Anati boma liyenera kupangitsa kuti Older Persons Act igwire ntchito bwino ndikuipereka ndalama. Adapempha kuti pakhale njira zolimba zotetezera anthu. Adapemphanso kuti milandu yonse ya nkhanza ifufuzidwe ndipo anthu omwe amachita zoipa atengedwe kukhothi. Kavala adati malamulo omwe sagwiritsidwa ntchito ndi mawu okha. Sathandiza anthu.

Kupatula boma, Kavala adapempha atsogoleri achikhalidwe, atsogoleri achipembedzo, mabanja, ndi madera kuti athandize. Adawapempha kuti amenyane ndi zikhulupiriro zabodza zomwe zimapweteka okalamba. Anati okalamba sayenera kukhala ndi mantha atatha kugwira ntchito mwakhama m'mabanja awo ndi matauni awo. Kavala adagogomezera kuti kuteteza okalamba kuyenera kukhala cholinga chachikulu cha dziko lonse, osati kungokumbukira tsiku limodzi pachaka.

MANEPO imati momwe dziko limachitira ndi okalamba ake zimasonyeza mtundu wa dzikolo. Gululo silidzavomereza kuti okalamba avulazidwa. Likufuna kusintha kwenikweni, osati kungolankhula. Kupita patsogolo kwenikweni kudzabwera pamene okalamba adzatha kukhala otetezeka komanso mwaulemu m'matauni awo.

en.title: Malawi approves 34 candidates for June 30 by-elections
en.body: Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) approved 34 candidates for by-elections on June 30. The votes will choose members in three parliamentary areas and two local government wards. Five political parties fielded candidates. The Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) is the only party competing in all five areas.
en.body_more:
MEC will hold parliamentary by-elections in Lilongwe City Bwaila, Machinga Mikoko, and Balaka Mulunguzi constituencies. Local government by-elections will happen in Uliwa Ward in Karonga South Constituency and Kanjuli Ward in Machinga Central East Constituency. MEC director Sangwani Mwafulirwa said on Friday that five political parties are taking part.

Beyond DPP, UTM Party is competing in all three parliamentary races and one ward election. The Malawi Congress Party (MCP) fielded candidates in two constituencies and one ward. People's Party is contesting in Machinga Mikoko Constituency and in the two wards. United Democratic Front is competing in two constituencies and one ward. Independent candidates make up about 47 percent of all nominations, with 16 out of the 34 candidates.

Among the 22 parliamentary candidates, seven are women, representing about 32 percent. At local government level, two women are contesting out of 12 candidates. Political analyst George Chaima said limited party participation is not surprising. He noted that most of Malawi's 24 registered political parties are inactive between general elections. Some parties only become active during general elections and rarely join by-elections. Chaima said parties must participate in by-elections to strengthen democracy.

The vacancies came from deaths of sitting members or court orders nullifying results from the September 16, 2025 general election. In Balaka Mulunguzi, a court ordered a fresh election after finding irregularities. MEC conducted earlier by-elections on March 17, 2026, where DPP, MCP, UTM Party, and one independent each won a parliamentary seat.

ny.title: Malawi yavomereza anthu 34 omwe adzapikisane pa chisankho cha pa 30 June
ny.body: Bungwe Loona za Chisankho la Malawi (MEC) lavomereza anthu 34 omwe adzapikisane pa zisankho zapang'onopang'ono pa June 30. Mavotiwa adzasankha mamembala m'madera atatu a nyumba yamalamulo ndi m'magawo awiri aboma am'deralo. Zipani zisanu zandale zidapereka opikisana nawo. Chipani cha Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) ndicho chokhacho chomwe chikupikisana m'madera onse asanu.
ny.body_more:
MEC ichititsa zisankho za aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo m'madera a Lilongwe City Bwaila, Machinga Mikoko, ndi Balaka Mulunguzi. Zisankho za boma la m'deralo zidzachitika ku Uliwa Ward ku Karonga South Constituency ndi Kanjuli Ward ku Machinga Central East Constituency. Mtsogoleri wa MEC, Sangwani Mwafulirwa, adati Lachisanu kuti zipani zisanu zandale zikutenga nawo mbali.

Kupatula DPP, UTM Party ikupikisana m'mipikisano yonse itatu ya aphungu ndi chisankho chimodzi cha ward. Chipani cha Malawi Congress Party (MCP) chapereka oimira m'madera awiri ndi ward imodzi. Chipani cha People's Party chikupikisana ku Machinga Mikoko Constituency komanso m'ma ward awiri. United Democratic Front ikupikisana m'ma madera awiri ndi ward imodzi. Oimira odziyimira pawokha ndi pafupifupi 47 peresenti ya osankhidwa onse, ndi 16 mwa oimira 34.

Pakati pa oimira 22 a nyumba yamalamulo, asanu ndi awiri ndi akazi, omwe akuyimira pafupifupi 32 peresenti. Pamlingo wa boma la m'deralo, akazi awiri akupikisana pa oimira 12. Katswiri wa ndale George Chaima adati kutenga nawo mbali kochepa kwa zipani sikudabwitsa. Iye adati zipani zambiri 24 zolembetsedwa ku Malawi sizigwira ntchito pakati pa zisankho zazikulu. Zipani zina zimagwira ntchito nthawi ya zisankho zazikulu ndipo sizilowa nawo kawirikawiri pazisankho zazikulu. Chaima adati zipani ziyenera kutenga nawo mbali pazisankho zazing'ono kuti zilimbikitse demokalase.

Mipata ya anthu sinalipo chifukwa cha imfa ya mamembala omwe adakhalapo kapena malamulo a khothi oletsa zotsatira za chisankho chachikulu cha pa Seputembala 16, 2025. Ku Balaka Mulunguzi, khothi linalamula kuti chisankho chatsopano chichitike pambuyo poti chapeza zinthu zosalongosoka. MEC idachita zisankho zoyambirira pa Marichi 17, 2026, pomwe DPP, MCP, UTM Party, ndi chipani chimodzi chodziyimira pawokha chinapambana mpando wa nyumba yamalamulo.

en.title: Silver and Wanderers win quarter-final first legs
en.body: Silver Strikers beat Karonga United 2-0 at Silver Stadium in Lilongwe. Mighty Wanderers won 2-0 against Creck Sporting Club at Mpira Stadium in Blantyre. Both teams now have an advantage going into their second leg matches of the Airtel Top 8 quarter-finals 2026.
en.body_more:
At Silver Stadium, striker Charles Chipala scored twice for Silver. He opened the scoring in the 11th minute after Festus Duwe set him up. Chipala then added the second goal in the 34th minute from a MacDonald Lameck cross. Chipala was later denied a third goal when his penalty was saved by Karonga goalkeeper Eddie Jamu. The penalty was given after Lovemore Ngolombe fouled Chipala in the box. Ngolombe was sent off after receiving a second yellow card.

Silver coach Peter 'Shoes' Mgangira said he was happy with the win but disappointed his team did not score more goals. "It is a very disappointing result looking at the chances we missed. We were supposed to score a minimum of five goals," he said. Karonga coach Audilow Makonyola blamed poor focus for the loss but said his team could still recover in the second leg.

At Mpira Stadium in Blantyre, Mighty Wanderers came strong in the second half. Blessings Mwalilino scored first in the 47th minute. Two minutes later, new player Lameck Mithi added the second goal after Isaac 'Tcheya' Kaliyati set him up. Creck's interim coach Chisomo Nkhoma said her team's defence was not well organised in the second half. Wanderers coach Bob Mpinganjira said it was a tough match but his team got the goals needed.

In other matches on Saturday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe, FCB Nyasa Bullets drew 1-1 with Ekhaya FC. Blue Eagles and Civil Service United also played to a goalless draw.

ny.title: Silver ndi Wanderers apambana ma quarter finals
ny.body: Silver Strikers yagonjetsa Karonga United 2-0 pa Silver Stadium ku Lilongwe. Mighty Wanderers yapambana 2-0 motsutsana ndi Creck Sporting Club pa Mpira Stadium ku Blantyre. Magulu onse awiri tsopano ali ndi mwayi wopita kumasewera awo achiwiri a Airtel Top 8 quarter-finals 2026.
ny.body_more:
Ku Silver Stadium, wosewera mpira Charles Chipala adagoletsa zigoli ziwiri kwa Silver. Iye ndiye adatsegula zigoli mu mphindi ya 11 Festus Duwe atamuyika pampando. Kenako Chipala adawonjezera chigoli chachiwiri mu mphindi ya 34 kuchokera ku cross yomwe MacDonald Lameck adagoletsa. Pambuyo pake Chipala adakanidwa chigoli chachitatu pomwe penalty yake idapulumutsidwa ndi golikipa wa Karonga Eddie Jamu. Chilangocho chidaperekedwa Lovemore Ngolombe atachita cholakwika ndi Chipala mu bokosi. Ngolombe adatulutsidwa atalandira khadi lachiwiri lachikasu.

Mphunzitsi wa Silver Peter 'Shoes' Mgangira adati adakondwera ndi kupambana koma adakhumudwa kuti timu yake sinagole zigoli zambiri. "Ndi zotsatira zokhumudwitsa kwambiri poganizira mwayi womwe tidaphonya. Tinkayenera kupeza zigoli zosachepera zisanu," adatero. Mphunzitsi wa Karonga Audilow Makonyola adati kugoletsa kosakhazikika ndi komwe kudapangitsa kuti timu yake igole koma adati timu yake ikadali bwino mu gawo lachiwiri.

Ku Mpira Stadium ku Blantyre, Mighty Wanderers idabwera mwamphamvu mu theka lachiwiri. Blessings Mwalilino adagoletsa chigoli choyamba mu mphindi ya 47. Patatha mphindi ziwiri, wosewera watsopano Lameck Mithi adawonjezera chigoli chachiwiri Isaac 'Tcheya' Kaliyati atamuyika pampando. Mphunzitsi wa Creck, Chisomo Nkhoma, adati chitetezo cha timu yake sichinakonzedwe bwino mu theka lachiwiri. Mphunzitsi wa Wanderers, Bob Mpinganjira, adati masewerawa anali ovuta koma timu yake idapeza zigoli zofunikira.

M'masewera ena omwe adachitika Loweruka ku Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe, FCB Nyasa Bullets idasewera 1-1 ndi Ekhaya FC. Blue Eagles ndi Civil Service United nawonso adasewera 1-0.

en.title: Rights Campaigner Warns Over ESCOM Procurement Concerns
en.body: Governance and human rights campaigner Undule Mwakasungula has warned that political interference in ESCOM's procurement process could damage public trust in government. In a statement on Saturday in Lilongwe, he said reports of outside involvement in ESCOM's purchase of transformers and wooden poles raise concerns about transparency and fairness.
en.body_more:
Mwakasungula said the law gives ESCOM and the Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority (PPDA) the power to make procurement decisions. He warned that the 2025 Public Procurement Act does not allow the President to approve or clear procurement awards. "Political involvement creates perceptions of favoritism and weakens confidence in merit-based decision-making," he said.

Documents show that officials from the Office of the President and Cabinet asked for details about the ownership of a company bidding to supply transformers. This sparked debate about whether politics influenced the choice of contractor. Mwakasungula said such actions could harm public trust in procurement systems and raise questions about transparency and accountability. He suggested that some government advisors may be giving bad advice that could hurt the independence of state-owned enterprises.

Mwakasungula called on the Office of the President and Cabinet, including the Chief Secretary, to stop actions that could be seen as influencing procurement outcomes. He stressed that ESCOM's technical experts should choose the most qualified contractor based on skill and merit alone. He also urged the Anti-Corruption Bureau to investigate the matter thoroughly to establish the facts and protect public confidence in procurement procedures.

Mwakasungula said that each time a technical institution is overruled by political authority, it sends a message to citizens that the law is optional. "That is how corruption takes root. That is how public trust collapses," he said. He called for ESCOM and PPDA to execute their mandates without undue influence, and for the government to demonstrate commitment to transparency, institutional independence and the rule of law.

ny.title: Wolimbikitsa Ufulu Wa Anthu Achenjeza za Nkhawa Zokhudza Kugula Malonda kwa ESCOM
ny.body: Wolimbikitsa ulamuliro ndi ufulu wa anthu, Undule Mwakasungula, wachenjeza kuti kulowererapo kwa ndale pa njira yogulira zinthu ya ESCOM kungawononge chidaliro cha anthu ku boma. Mu lipoti lake Loweruka ku Lilongwe, adati malipoti okhudza kutenga nawo mbali kwa anthu akunja pakugula ma transformer ndi mitengo yamatabwa a ESCOM akubweretsa nkhawa yokhudza kuwonekera poyera komanso chilungamo.
ny.body_more:
Mwakasungula anati lamuloli limapatsa ESCOM ndi Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority (PPDA) mphamvu zopangira zisankho zogula. Anachenjeza kuti lamulo la 2025 Public Procurement Act sililola Purezidenti kuvomereza kapena kuvomereza mphoto zogula. "Kulowerera ndale kumapangitsa kuti anthu aziona kuti pali tsankho ndipo kumafooketsa chidaliro pakupanga zisankho zozikidwa pa mfundo," adatero.

Zikalata zikusonyeza kuti akuluakulu a Ofesi ya Purezidenti ndi Kabinati adafunsa tsatanetsatane wa umwini wa kampani yomwe ikupereka ma transformer. Izi zidayambitsa mkangano wokhudza ngati ndale zidakhudza kusankha kontrakitala. Mwakasungula adati zochita zotere zitha kuwononga chidaliro cha anthu m'machitidwe ogula ndikubweretsa mafunso okhudza kuwonekera poyera ndi kuyankha mlandu. Adanenanso kuti alangizi ena aboma akhoza kukhala akupereka upangiri woipa womwe ungawononge ufulu wa mabizinesi aboma.

Mwakasungula adapempha Ofesi ya Purezidenti ndi Kabinati, kuphatikiza Mlembi Wamkulu, kuti asiye zochita zomwe zingawoneke ngati zikukhudza zotsatira zogula. Adagogomezera kuti akatswiri aukadaulo a ESCOM ayenera kusankha kontrakitala woyenerera kwambiri kutengera luso ndi luso lokha. Iye analimbikitsanso bungwe lolimbana ndi ziphuphu kuti lifufuze nkhaniyi mokwanira kuti litsimikizire zoona zake ndikuteteza chidaliro cha anthu pa njira zogulira zinthu.

Mwakasungula anati nthawi iliyonse bungwe laukadaulo likagonjetsedwa ndi akuluakulu andale, limatumiza uthenga kwa nzika kuti lamuloli ndi losankha. "Umu ndi momwe ziphuphu zimakhalira. Umu ndi momwe chidaliro cha anthu chimasokonekera," adatero. Anapempha ESCOM ndi PPDA kuti achite zomwe akuyenera kuchita popanda kukakamizidwa mosayenera, komanso kuti boma liwonetse kudzipereka kwawo poyera, kudziyimira pawokha kwa mabungwe komanso ulamuliro wa malamulo.

en.title: Exploits University Gives Degrees to Over 1,200 Students
en.body: Exploits University held its graduation ceremony in Lilongwe on Saturday. More than 1,200 students got degrees and diplomas. University President Professor Desmond Bikoko said the students worked hard. He said the university has passed new checks from the government. New study programmes are now allowed.
en.body_more:
Prof. Bikoko told the graduates to use their education to help Malawi grow. He said they must be brave and learn new things fast. "Create chances where none exist. Lead with good values. Help build Malawi Vision 2063," he said. He also thanked the teachers for their hard work since he came to lead the university in 2018.

Dr. Fritz Kadyoma, Director of Higher Education in the Ministry of Education, Science and Technology, came to the ceremony. He said private universities like Exploits help more people get good learning. He praised the university for teaching science, maths, and technology well. He said universities must do more research and help students get skills for real jobs.

Gift Nankhuni, Chairperson of the University Council, told graduates that their degree gives them the right to think and to act. The ceremony had the theme "Moving Forward with Purpose: Building Malawi Vision 2063." Exploits University started in May 2010. It has two places to study—one in Area 4, Lilongwe, and one in Blantyre. The government first knew it was a real university in 2012. By now, it has given degrees to more than 14,500 people. It teaches eight degree courses, three master courses, and three diploma courses.

ny.title: Yunivesite ya Exploits Yapereka Madigiri kwa Ophunzira Opitilira 1,200
ny.body: Yunivesite ya Exploits inachita mwambo womaliza maphunziro ku Lilongwe Loweruka. Ophunzira oposa 1,200 adalandira madigiri ndi ma diploma. Purezidenti wa yunivesite Pulofesa Desmond Bikoko anati ophunzirawo adagwira ntchito molimbika. Iye anati yunivesite yapambana macheke atsopano ochokera ku boma. Mapulogalamu atsopano ophunzirira tsopano aloledwa.
ny.body_more:
Pulofesa Bikoko adauza omaliza maphunzirowa kuti agwiritse ntchito maphunziro awo kuthandiza Malawi kukula. Anati ayenera kukhala olimba mtima ndikuphunzira zinthu zatsopano mwachangu. "Pangani mwayi pomwe palibe. Tsatirani ndi makhalidwe abwino. Thandizani kumanga Malawi Vision 2063," adatero. Anayamikiranso aphunzitsi chifukwa cha khama lawo kuyambira pomwe adabwera kudzatsogolera yunivesite mu 2018.

Dr. Fritz Kadyoma, Mtsogoleri wa Maphunziro Apamwamba mu Unduna wa Maphunziro, Sayansi ndi Ukadaulo, adabwera ku mwambowu. Anati mayunivesite achinsinsi monga Exploits amathandiza anthu ambiri kuphunzira bwino. Anayamika yunivesiteyi chifukwa chophunzitsa sayansi, masamu, ndi ukadaulo bwino. Anati mayunivesite ayenera kuchita kafukufuku wambiri ndikuthandizira ophunzira kupeza luso la ntchito zenizeni.

Gift Nankhuni, Wapampando wa Bungwe la Yunivesite, adauza omaliza maphunzirowo kuti digiri yawo imawapatsa ufulu woganiza ndi kuchitapo kanthu. Mwambowu unali ndi mutu wakuti "Kupita Patsogolo ndi Cholinga: Kumanga Malawi Vision 2063." Exploits University idayamba mu Meyi 2010. Ili ndi malo awiri ophunzirira - limodzi ku Area 4, Lilongwe, ndi limodzi ku Blantyre. Boma linadziwa koyamba kuti ndi yunivesite yeniyeni mu 2012. Pakadali pano, lapereka madigiri kwa anthu oposa 14,500. Limaphunzitsa maphunziro asanu ndi atatu a digiri, atatu a masters, ndi atatu a diploma.

en.title: Bullets draw with Ekhaya in Top 8 start
en.body: The 2026 Airtel Top 8 began yesterday at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe with two quarter-final first-leg matches. Defending champions FCB Nyasa Bullets drew 1-1 with Ekhaya FC in a late game. Earlier, Blue Eagles and Civil Service United played a goalless draw. Both matches were tight and both teams had chances to win.
en.body_more:
Bullets scored first when George Chaomba tapped in a cross just seconds into the second half. The striker had missed many chances in the first half. Ekhaya came back and equalised in the 70th minute when Allen Chihana scored after Bullets failed to clear a cross.

Bullets assistant coach Heston Munthali said the team wasted too many chances. "We could have scored more goals but we missed a lot of chances, especially in the first half. In the second half we scored early but we were not comfortable on the ball," he said. Ekhaya coach Enos Chatama agreed his team also had chances. "We had several chances and could have wrapped up the game in the second half but we failed to convert," Chatama said. This was the first game for Bullets under new Zambian coach Wedson Nyirenda.

In the early match, Blue Eagles and Civil Service United also could not find a winner. Eagles striker Tony Viyuyi had a clear chance but Civil defender John Dambuleni cleared the ball off the line. Civil Service player Righteous Banda also missed a big chance. Eagles coach Deklerk Msakakuona said the draw was fair. Civil Service coach Abbas Makawa disagreed, saying his team played better and controlled the game.

The second leg matches will happen next month. Ekhaya will host Bullets at Mpira Stadium while Civil Service will welcome Eagles at Civo Stadium in Lilongwe.

ny.title: Bullets yapanga chigoli ndi Ekhaya pa mpikisano wa Top 8
ny.body: Mpikisano wa 2026 Airtel Top 8 unayamba dzulo pa Bingu National Stadium ku Lilongwe ndi masewera awiri a gawo loyamba la gawo la kotala fainali. FCB Nyasa Bullets, omwe ndi akatswiri oteteza, adasewera 1-1 ndi Ekhaya FC kumapeto kwa masewera. Poyamba, Blue Eagles ndi Civil Service United adasewera 1-0. Masewero onsewa anali ochepa ndipo magulu onse awiri anali ndi mwayi wopambana.
ny.body_more:
Bullets inayamba kugoletsa pomwe George Chaomba anagoletsa cross masekondi ochepa chabe theka lachiwiri. Wosewerayo anali ataphonya mipata yambiri mu theka loyamba. Ekhaya adabweranso ndikupeza chigoli mu mphindi ya 70 pomwe Allen Chihana adagoletsa pambuyo poti Bullets yalephera kuchotsa cross.

Wothandizira mphunzitsi wa Bullets Heston Munthali adati timuyi idataya mipata yambiri. "Tikanatha kupeza zigoli zambiri koma tinaphonya mipata yambiri, makamaka mu theka loyamba. Mu theka lachiwiri tinagoletsa molawirira koma sitinali omasuka pa mpira," adatero. Mphunzitsi wa Ekhaya Enos Chatama adavomereza kuti timu yake inalinso ndi mipata. "Tinali ndi mipata ingapo ndipo tikadatha kumaliza masewerawa mu theka lachiwiri koma sitinathe kusintha," adatero Chatama. Uwu unali masewera oyamba a Bullets pansi pa mphunzitsi watsopano wa ku Zambia Wedson Nyirenda.

Mu masewera oyambirira, Blue Eagles ndi Civil Service United sanathe kupeza wopambana. Wosewera wa Eagles Tony Viyuyi anali ndi mwayi womveka koma woteteza Civil John Dambuleni adachotsa mpirawo pamzere. Wosewera wa Civil Service Righteous Banda nayenso anaphonya mwayi waukulu. Mphunzitsi wa Eagles, Deklerk Msakakuona, anati masewerowa anali abwino. Mphunzitsi wa Civil Service, Abbas Makawa, sanagwirizane ndi zimenezi, ponena kuti timu yake idasewera bwino komanso idalamulira masewerawa.

Masewero achiwiri achitika mwezi wamawa. Ekhaya idzalandira Bullets ku Mpira Stadium pomwe Civil Service idzalandira Eagles ku Civo Stadium ku Lilongwe.

en.title: Chilima mausoleum unveiled in Ntcheu
en.body: Family and friends gathered in Nsipe, Ntcheu District, to unveil a mausoleum for former Vice-President Saulos Chilima. He died in a military plane crash on 10 June 2024. His widow Mary and others called for the truth about his death. Speakers praised Chilima as a hardworking public servant who served Malawi faithfully.
en.body_more:
Mary Chilima said she remains deeply sad and wants to know the truth about what happened. She thanked President Peter Mutharika for ordering a fresh inquiry into the crash. She said the family hopes the new inquiry will help them find peace. Mary recalled that her husband was arrested and treated badly during his political work, but he never gave up. She described him as a humble Christian who loved prayer and his family.

Mary called for the country to build a museum in honour of Chilima and create a special day each year to remember him. She said Chilima woke up at 3:00 a.m. every day to pray and fast. She asked Malawians to help others and do volunteer work to keep his values alive. Chilima's son Sean said the past two years were very hard. He said his father was a humble servant who helped many people. Chilima's sister Tionge said he was kind and humble, and would even cook in the kitchen when he was home.

Minister of Education Bright Msaka spoke on behalf of President Mutharika. He said Chilima served the country well under two presidents and left a mark on the nation. UTM Party president Dalitso Kabambe, whose party Chilima founded, said the truth about the crash must be found. He promised the family that UTM would stand with them. Nine people died in the plane crash, including former First Lady Shanil Dzimbiri and eight others. They were flying to Mzuzu to attend the burial of former Minister Ralph Kasambara. A new inquiry is now looking into what caused the crash.

ny.title: Mausoleum a Chilima avumbulutsidwa ku Ntcheu
ny.body: Achibale ndi abwenzi adasonkhana ku Nsipe, Chigawo cha Ntcheu, kuti avumbulutse manda a Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima. Iye anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndege yankhondo pa 10 June 2024. Mkazi wake wamasiye Mary ndi ena adapempha kuti pakhale chowonadi chokhudza imfa yake. Olankhula adayamikira Chilima ngati mtumiki wa boma wolimbikira ntchito amene adatumikira Malawi mokhulupirika.
ny.body_more:
Mary Chilima anati adakali wachisoni kwambiri ndipo akufuna kudziwa zoona zake pa zomwe zinachitika. Anathokoza Purezidenti Peter Mutharika chifukwa cholamula kuti pakhale kafukufuku watsopano wokhudza ngoziyi. Anati banjali likuyembekeza kuti kafukufuku watsopanowu uwathandiza kupeza mtendere. Mary anakumbukira kuti mwamuna wake anamangidwa ndipo anachitiridwa nkhanza panthawi ya ntchito yake yandale, koma sanasiye. Anamufotokoza ngati Mkristu wodzichepetsa amene ankakonda kupemphera komanso banja lake.

Mary anapempha dzikolo kuti limange nyumba yosungiramo zinthu zakale polemekeza Chilima ndikupanga tsiku lapadera chaka chilichonse kuti limukumbukire. Anati Chilima ankadzuka 3:00 koloko m'mawa tsiku lililonse kuti apemphere komanso kusala kudya. Anapempha anthu aku Malawi kuti athandize ena ndikuchita ntchito zodzipereka kuti asunge makhalidwe ake. Mwana wa Chilima, Sean, anati zaka ziwiri zapitazi zinali zovuta kwambiri. Anati abambo ake anali mtumiki wodzichepetsa amene anathandiza anthu ambiri. Mlongo wake wa Chilima, Tionge, anati anali wachifundo komanso wodzichepetsa, ndipo ankaphika ngakhale kukhitchini akafika kunyumba.

Nduna ya Maphunziro, Bright Msaka, analankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti Mutharika. Anati Chilima anatumikira bwino dzikolo pansi pa apurezidenti awiri ndipo anasiya chizindikiro pa dzikolo. Purezidenti wa chipani cha UTM, Dalitso Kabambe, yemwe chipani chake Chilima chinayambitsa, anati zoona zake za ngoziyi ziyenera kupezeka. Analonjeza banjali kuti UTM idzakhala nawo limodzi. Anthu asanu ndi anayi anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndegeyi, kuphatikizapo mkazi woyamba Shanil Dzimbiri ndi ena asanu ndi atatu. Iwo anali paulendo wopita ku Mzuzu kukaika maliro a Nduna yakale Ralph Kasambara. Kafukufuku watsopano tsopano akuyang'ana chomwe chinayambitsa ngoziyi.

en.title: African Development Bank Warns of Malawi Economic Problems
en.body: The African Development Bank says Malawi faces big money problems. Real GDP growth was below two percent from 2020 to 2024. The bank says growth will reach two percent in 2025. Low growth, high prices, and money shortages are the main problems.
en.body_more:
The bank says two things are making the economy weak. On one side, people have less money. Prices are high. The government controls money tightly. People cannot buy as much. On the other side, bad weather has hurt farms. Farms make less food. Food prices go up.

Foreign exchange money is very low. It covers less than 0.7 months of imports. In 2024, Malawi bought more than three times more goods than it sold to other countries. The money exchange rate has become more stable, but the country still has very little foreign cash.

The World Bank also warned that Malawi is poor at managing money. This makes the fight against extreme poverty harder. The International Monetary Fund said reforms have stopped. Public debt is growing. Malawi wants to become a lower middle-income country by 2030 and an upper middle-income country by 2063. But the current growth of 1.8 percent is too slow. The population grows at 2.7 percent each year. This is faster than the economy grows.

However, the African Development Bank sees hope ahead. It projects that real GDP growth will reach 3.8 percent in 2026. This growth will come from farms recovering, tourism growing again, and new mining projects. Christopher Mbukwa, an economics teacher at Mzuzu University, says rising poverty shows past programmes have failed. The National Planning Commission agrees. It says the gap between economic growth and population growth is making the 2063 goal harder to reach.

ny.title: Banki Yachitukuko ku Africa Yachenjeza Mavuto Azachuma ku Malawi
ny.body: Banki Yachitukuko ku Africa ikunena kuti dziko la Malawi likukumana ndi mavuto azachuma ambiri. Kukula kwenikweni kwa GDP kunali pansi pa magawo awiri pa zana kuyambira 2020 mpaka 2024. Bankiyo ikunena kuti kukula kudzafika pa magawo awiri pa zana mu 2025. Kukula kochepa, mitengo yokwera, ndi kusowa kwa ndalama ndiye mavuto akulu.
ny.body_more:
Bankiyi ikunena kuti zinthu ziwiri zikufooketsa chuma. Kumbali imodzi, anthu ali ndi ndalama zochepa. Mitengo ndi yokwera. Boma limayang'anira ndalama mwamphamvu. Anthu sangagule zambiri. Kumbali ina, nyengo yoipa yawononga minda. Minda imapanga chakudya chochepa. Mitengo ya chakudya ikukwera.

Ndalama zosinthira ndalama zakunja ndi zotsika kwambiri. Zimaphimba zinthu zosakwana miyezi 0.7 kuchokera kumayiko ena. Mu 2024, Malawi idagula zinthu zochulukirapo katatu kuposa zomwe idagulitsa kumayiko ena. Mtengo wosintha ndalama wakhala wokhazikika, koma dzikolo likadali ndi ndalama zochepa zakunja.

Banki Yapadziko Lonse idachenjezanso kuti Malawi ndi yosauka pakuwongolera ndalama. Izi zimapangitsa kuti nkhondo yolimbana ndi umphawi wadzaoneni ikhale yovuta. Bungwe la International Monetary Fund lati kusintha kwatha. Ngongole za anthu onse zikukulirakulira. Malawi ikufuna kukhala dziko lotsika lapakati pofika chaka cha 2030 ndi dziko lokwera lapakati pofika chaka cha 2063. Koma kukula kwa 1.8 peresenti komwe kukuchitika panopa ndi kochedwa kwambiri. Chiwerengero cha anthu chikukwera ndi 2.7 peresenti chaka chilichonse. Izi ndi zachangu kuposa momwe chuma chikukulira.

Komabe, Banki Yachitukuko ya ku Africa ikuwona chiyembekezo patsogolo. Likulingalira kuti kukula kwenikweni kwa GDP kudzafika pa 3.8 peresenti mu 2026. Kukula kumeneku kudzachokera ku minda yomwe ikuchira, zokopa alendo zikukulirakuliranso, ndi mapulojekiti atsopano a migodi. Christopher Mbukwa, mphunzitsi wa zachuma ku Mzuzu University, akuti kukwera kwa umphawi kukuwonetsa kuti mapulogalamu akale alephera. National Planning Commission ikuvomereza. Likuti kusiyana pakati pa kukula kwachuma ndi kukula kwa anthu kukupangitsa kuti cholinga cha 2063 chikhale chovuta kuchikwaniritsa.

en.title: President Mutharika Leaves for South Africa on Private Visit
en.body: President Peter Mutharika left Malawi on Friday for South Africa on a private visit. He departed from Kamuzu International Airport in Lilongwe at 2:00 pm with the First Lady. This is his third private trip to South Africa in the past six months. The President said he would return in a few days.
en.body_more:
Before he left, Mutharika told people he was going on a short trip and that everything was under control in Malawi. He was in good spirits and spoke warmly with staff members at State House. The First Lady went with him, along with Press Secretary Maulidi.

State House gave no details about why the President was going or what he would do in South Africa. This lack of information has caused questions from people and politicians. Some ask what a "private visit" means when a sitting President travels, because his trips use State money and need security from government.

Both Vice Presidents did not go with the President. First Vice President Jane Ansah was in Blantyre. Second Vice President Enock Chihana was in Ntcheu for events about the late Saulos Chilima's tombstone. In the past, one Vice President usually went on such trips.

UDF President Atupele Muluzi said Mutharika should use this visit to talk to South Africa's leaders about attacks on foreign people there. Many Malawians live and work in South Africa and send money home. Muluzi said the President should help protect Malawians abroad. People are watching to see if State House gives more facts about this trip.

ny.title: Purezidenti Mutharika Apita ku South Africa Pa Ulendo Wapadera
ny.body: Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adachoka ku Malawi Lachisanu kupita ku South Africa paulendo wake wachinsinsi. Adachoka pa Kamuzu International Airport ku Lilongwe nthawi ya 2:00 pm ndi Mkazi Wachiwiri. Uwu ndi ulendo wake wachitatu wachinsinsi ku South Africa m'miyezi isanu ndi umodzi yapitayi. Purezidenti adati abwerera m'masiku ochepa.
ny.body_more:
Asananyamuke, Mutharika anauza anthu kuti akupita paulendo waufupi ndipo zonse zili bwino ku Malawi. Anali ndi mzimu wabwino ndipo analankhula mwachikondi ndi ogwira ntchito ku State House. Mkazi Woyamba anapita naye, pamodzi ndi Mlembi wa Nkhani Maulidi.

State House sinapereke tsatanetsatane wa chifukwa chake Purezidenti akupita kapena zomwe angachite ku South Africa. Kusowa kwa chidziwitso kumeneku kwapangitsa anthu ndi andale kufunsa mafunso. Ena amafunsa kuti "ulendo wachinsinsi" umatanthauza chiyani Purezidenti akamayenda, chifukwa maulendo ake amagwiritsa ntchito ndalama za Boma ndipo amafunika chitetezo kuchokera ku boma.

A Vice President onse awiri sanapite ndi Purezidenti. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Jane Ansah anali ku Blantyre. Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enock Chihana anali ku Ntcheu pazochitika zokhudza mwala wa manda wa malemu Saulos Chilima. Kale, Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti nthawi zambiri ankapita paulendo wotere.

Purezidenti wa UDF Atupele Muluzi anati Mutharika ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito ulendowu kulankhula ndi atsogoleri a South Africa za kuukira anthu akunja kumeneko. Anthu ambiri aku Malawi amakhala ndi kugwira ntchito ku South Africa ndikutumiza ndalama kunyumba. Muluzi anati Purezidenti ayenera kuthandiza kuteteza anthu aku Malawi akunja. Anthu akuyang'ana kuti aone ngati Nyumba Yamalamulo ipereka zambiri zokhudza ulendowu.

en.title: ESCOM and Police Fight Power Line Theft with Chiefs
en.body: ESCOM and the Malawi Police Service held an anti-vandalism meeting on Thursday at Mkukula Primary School in Lumbadzi, Dowa. Chiefs and community members came to learn how to protect electricity transformers and lines. The area has seen many theft and damage cases recently.
en.body_more:
ESCOM Senior Control Engineer Peter Murotho told people that protecting power lines is everyone's job. He said that when people damage ESCOM equipment, the power stops and the country loses money. "The network is left open and puts people at risk. We have to spend big money to fix damaged things instead of building new lines," Murotho said.

Lumbadzi Police Officer-in-Charge Mable Msefula was the guest of honour. She said vandalism stops people from getting power they need for their homes, shops, and offices. "We must protect these things. Please tell the police if you see people doing wrong things," Msefula told the community.

ESCOM's Chief Public Relations and Communications Officer Pilirani Phiri said that from November 2025 to March 2026, ESCOM found 138 cases of theft and damage to power lines. "We want people to think differently about public things. We all own this equipment. If we work as one, we can stop theft and give everyone good power," Phiri said.

Traditional Authority Mkukula thanked ESCOM and said the chiefs would help guard the power lines in their areas. The meeting had a question time where people asked how to tell the police about theft, how to stay safe, and ways to guard local power equipment.

ny.title: ESCOM ndi Apolisi Alimbana ndi Kuba kwa Zingwe za Magetsi ndi Akuluakulu
ny.body: ESCOM ndi a Malawi Police Service adachita msonkhano wotsutsana ndi kuwononga nyumba Lachinayi ku Mkukula Primary School ku Lumbadzi, Dowa. Mafumu ndi anthu ammudzi anabwera kudzaphunzira momwe angatetezere ma transformer amagetsi ndi zingwe. Derali lakhala ndi milandu yambiri ya kuba ndi kuwonongeka posachedwapa.
ny.body_more:
Peter Murotho, Senior Control Engineer wa ESCOM, anauza anthu kuti kuteteza mawaya amagetsi ndi ntchito ya aliyense. Iye anati anthu akawononga zida za ESCOM, magetsi amasiya ndipo dziko limataya ndalama. "Netiweki imasiyidwa yotseguka ndipo imaika anthu pachiwopsezo. Tiyenera kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama zambiri kukonza zinthu zowonongeka m'malo momanga mawaya atsopano," adatero Murotho.

Apolisi a Lumbadzi, Mable Msefula, anali mlendo wolemekezeka. Iye anati kuwononga zinthu kumalepheretsa anthu kupeza magetsi omwe amafunikira m'nyumba zawo, m'masitolo, ndi m'maofesi. "Tiyenera kuteteza zinthuzi. Chonde uzani apolisi ngati muwona anthu akuchita zinthu zolakwika," adatero Msefula kwa anthu ammudzi.

Mkulu wa Maubwenzi ndi Kulankhulana kwa ESCOM, Pilirani Phiri, adati kuyambira Novembala 2025 mpaka Marichi 2026, ESCOM yapeza milandu 138 ya kuba ndi kuwonongeka kwa mawaya amagetsi. "Tikufuna kuti anthu aziganiza mosiyana za zinthu zapagulu. Tonsefe tili ndi zidazi. Ngati tigwira ntchito limodzi, titha kusiya kuba ndikupatsa aliyense mphamvu zabwino," adatero Phiri.

Akuluakulu a boma Mkukula adathokoza ESCOM ndipo adati mafumu athandiza kuteteza mawaya amagetsi m'madera awo. Msonkhanowu unali ndi nthawi yofunsa mafunso pomwe anthu adafunsa momwe angauzire apolisi za kuba, momwe angakhalire otetezeka, komanso njira zotetezera zida zamagetsi zakomweko.

en.title: K400 Million Push to Fund Surgeries for 256 Children
en.body: Sparc Systems Limited and Beit-CURE Children's Hospital of Malawi have started a new fundraising campaign. They want to raise K400 million. This money will pay for surgeries to help 256 children on the waiting list. The plan was shown at a press meeting on Thursday at the Sparc Complex in Lilongwe.
en.body_more:
The campaign is called "Renewing Hope: One Child, One Surgery, One Future." The two groups say every K2.5 million raised will pay for one child's surgery and care after surgery. This will help children walk, run, go to school, and live better lives.

Sparc Systems Executive Director Dr. Wisely Phiri said the company wants to help Malawi's future. "We believe in investing in Malawi's brightest future," he said. "Our partnership with Beit-CURE is a testament to our commitment to corporate social responsibility and our desire to drive sustainable change by eliminating the surgical waiting list for these 256 children." Beit-CURE Children's Hospital Executive Director Dr. Rhoda Jura Kriek said many children are waiting for surgeries that could change their lives. "These children are waiting for surgeries that will change their lives forever. With Sparc Systems as our official partner, we are reaching out to the corporate community and the public to help us turn this hope into reality," she said.

Two big events will help raise the money. The first is an Annual Charity Golf Tournament on 29 August 2026 in Blantyre. It aims to raise K200 million. The second is an Annual Fundraising Gala Dinner in Lilongwe in September 2026. This event also aims to raise K200 million. Together, the two events will raise the full K400 million needed.

The campaign comes at a critical time. Many children with correctable orthopedic conditions are waiting for surgery. These conditions stop them from moving well, going to school, and living normal lives. The partnership shows how private companies can help hospitals give better care to children.

ny.title: K400 Miliyoni Yalimbikitsa Kupereka Ndalama Zothandizira Maopaleshoni a Ana 256
ny.body: Sparc Systems Limited ndi Beit-CURE Children's Hospital of Malawi ayambitsa kampeni yatsopano yopezera ndalama. Akufuna kusonkhanitsa K400 miliyoni. Ndalamazi zilipira opaleshoni yothandiza ana 256 omwe ali pamndandanda woyembekezera. Dongosololi linawonetsedwa pamsonkhano wa atolankhani Lachinayi ku Sparc Complex ku Lilongwe.
ny.body_more:
Kampeniyi imatchedwa "Kubwezeretsa Chiyembekezo: Mwana Mmodzi, Opaleshoni Imodzi, Tsogolo Limodzi."
