Magulu awiriwa akuti K2.5 miliyoni iliyonse yomwe yasonkhanitsidwa idzalipira opaleshoni ya mwana mmodzi ndi chisamaliro pambuyo pa opaleshoni. Izi zithandiza ana kuyenda, kuthamanga, kupita kusukulu, ndikukhala ndi moyo wabwino.

Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa Sparc Systems Dr. Wisely Phiri adati kampaniyo ikufuna kuthandiza tsogolo la Malawi. "Timakhulupirira kuti tiyika ndalama mu tsogolo labwino kwambiri la Malawi," adatero. "Mgwirizano wathu ndi Beit-CURE ndi umboni wa kudzipereka kwathu ku udindo wa anthu komanso chikhumbo chathu choyendetsa kusintha kosatha mwa kuchotsa mndandanda woyembekezera opaleshoni wa ana 256 awa." Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa Chipatala cha Ana cha Beit-CURE Dr. Rhoda Jura Kriek adati ana ambiri akuyembekezera opaleshoni yomwe ingasinthe miyoyo yawo. "Ana awa akuyembekezera opaleshoni yomwe idzasintha miyoyo yawo kwamuyaya. Ndi Sparc Systems ngati mnzathu wovomerezeka, tikulumikizana ndi anthu am'makampani ndi anthu kuti atithandize kusintha chiyembekezochi kukhala chenicheni," adatero.

Zochitika ziwiri zazikulu zithandiza kupeza ndalama. Choyamba ndi mpikisano wa pachaka wa gofu wachifundo womwe umachitika pa 29 Ogasiti 2026 ku Blantyre. Cholinga chake ndi kusonkhanitsa K200 miliyoni. Chachiwiri ndi chakudya chamadzulo cha pachaka cha kusonkhanitsa ndalama ku Lilongwe mu Seputembala 2026. Cholinga cha chochitikachi ndi kusonkhanitsa K200 miliyoni. Pamodzi, zochitika ziwirizi zisonkhanitsa K400 miliyoni yonse yomwe ikufunika.

Kampeniyi ikubwera panthawi yofunika kwambiri. Ana ambiri omwe ali ndi matenda a mafupa omwe amakonzedwa akuyembekezera opaleshoni. Matendawa amawalepheretsa kuyenda bwino, kupita kusukulu, komanso kukhala moyo wabwinobwino. Mgwirizanowu ukuwonetsa momwe makampani achinsinsi angathandizire zipatala kupereka chisamaliro chabwino kwa ana.

en.title: Business group calls for single exchange rate in Malawi
en.body: The Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry (MCCCI) has asked the government to use one exchange rate for all money deals. In a paper released on Wednesday, MCCCI says the country's multiple exchange rates are making it hard to do business. The official rate is around K1,750 to one US dollar. But the parallel market rate is between K3,500 and K4,500 to one dollar.
en.body_more:
MCCCI says this gap has grown from less than 25 percent in 2021 to over 100 percent by 2025. The gap is making it hard for people to buy foreign money and invest in Malawi. The chamber wants a single rate that the market decides, not the government.

MCCCI also wants to remove export surrender rules. These are rules that make business people sell foreign money to the government. The chamber wants to remove rules that stop people from getting foreign money. It also wants the government to spend money carefully.

But economists warn that one exchange rate would mean the Kwacha loses value. Marvin Banda is an economist. He said the idea is good in theory. But he said in 2023, Malawi tried to make the official rate match the parallel rate. It did not work. The gap got bigger again.

Bertha Bangara Chikadza is the head of the Economics Association of Malawi. She said a weaker Kwacha would make imports cost more. This would hurt people who buy foreign goods. She said Malawi must fix the deeper problems first. These are macroeconomic problems. When these are fixed, the exchange rate can be unified. The Reserve Bank of Malawi and the government money office have not said what they think about the MCCCI plan.

ny.title: Gulu la amalonda likupempha kuti pakhale kusinthana kwa ndalama kamodzi ku Malawi
ny.body: Bungwe la Malawi Confederation of Chambers of Commerce and Industry (MCCCI) lapempha boma kuti ligwiritse ntchito ndalama imodzi yosinthira ndalama pa mgwirizano uliwonse wa ndalama. Mu pepala lomwe linatulutsidwa Lachitatu, MCCCI ikunena kuti mitengo yosiyanasiyana yosinthira ndalama mdziko muno ikupangitsa kuti zikhale zovuta kuchita bizinesi. Mtengo wovomerezeka ndi pafupifupi K1,750 mpaka dola imodzi yaku US. Koma mtengo womwewo pamsika uli pakati pa K3,500 ndi K4,500 mpaka dola imodzi.
ny.body_more:
MCCCI ikunena kuti kusiyana kumeneku kwakula kuchoka pa 25 peresenti mu 2021 kufika pa 100 peresenti pofika chaka cha 2025. Kusiyana kumeneku kukupangitsa kuti anthu azivutika kugula ndalama zakunja ndikuyika ndalama ku Malawi. Nyumba yamalamulo ikufuna mtengo umodzi womwe msika umasankha, osati boma.

MCCCI ikufunanso kuchotsa malamulo opereka katundu kunja. Awa ndi malamulo omwe amapangitsa amalonda kugulitsa ndalama zakunja ku boma. Nyumba yamalamulo ikufuna kuchotsa malamulo omwe amaletsa anthu kupeza ndalama zakunja. Ikufunanso boma kugwiritsa ntchito ndalama mosamala.

Koma akatswiri azachuma akuchenjeza kuti mtengo umodzi wosinthira ndalama ungatanthauze kuti Kwacha itaya mtengo. Marvin Banda ndi katswiri wazachuma. Anati lingaliroli ndi labwino m'malingaliro. Koma adati mu 2023, Malawi idayesa kupanga mtengo wovomerezeka kuti ugwirizane ndi mtengo womwewo. Sizinagwire ntchito. Kusiyanaku kunakulanso.

Bertha Bangara Chikadza ndiye mtsogoleri wa Economics Association of Malawi. Anati Kwacha yofooka ingapangitse kuti zinthu zotumizidwa kunja zikhale zodula kwambiri. Izi zingapweteke anthu omwe amagula katundu wakunja. Anati Malawi iyenera kukonza mavuto akuya kaye. Izi ndi mavuto azachuma. Izi zikakhazikika, chiwongola dzanja cha ndalama chikhoza kugwirizanitsidwa. Banki Yaikulu ya Malawi ndi ofesi ya ndalama ya boma sananene zomwe akuganiza pa dongosolo la MCCCI.

en.title: Malawi students get full STEM scholarships from Airtel
en.body: Malawi students are among young Africans who won full university scholarships for STEM studies. The Airtel Africa Foundation gave 257 scholarships to students in Malawi, Nigeria, Tanzania, the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. This is part of the Foundation's US$6.2 million investment in education and digital skills.
en.body_more:
The scholarships come through the Airtel Africa Tech Fellowship. Education got the largest share of the Foundation's money. The Foundation's first annual report shows many other achievements across Africa.

The Foundation connected 1,028 new schools to the internet with help from UNICEF. This brought the total to 3,296 schools across 13 African countries. More than 2 million learners and nearly 39,000 teachers now use these schools. The Foundation also gave free educational content to over 11 million learners through 64 zero-rated digital platforms. Seven public schools were fully repaired, and 43 more are being fixed under the School Adoption Programme.

Beyond schools, the Foundation trained 30,530 youth and women in digital skills. These programmes work with governments and other organisations. Segun Ogunsanya, Chair of the Airtel Africa Foundation, said the goal is to remove barriers to education and digital opportunity. He said talent and ambition are common in Africa, but access to education, digital tools and jobs is not fair. The Foundation wants to help communities that have the least opportunity.

The Foundation plans to do more next year. It will give scholarships to more than 600 students. It will connect 2,000 more schools to free internet. It will also expand digital skills training in underserved communities. The Foundation works with governments to help Africa's economy grow and change for the better.

ny.title: Ophunzira aku Malawi alandira maphunziro a STEM kuchokera ku Airtel
ny.body: Ophunzira aku Malawi ndi m'gulu la achinyamata aku Africa omwe adapambana maphunziro a ku yunivesite a STEM. Airtel Africa Foundation idapereka maphunziro 257 kwa ophunzira aku Malawi, Nigeria, Tanzania, Democratic Republic of Congo ndi Uganda. Izi ndi gawo la ndalama zomwe bungweli lidayika $6.2 miliyoni mu maphunziro ndi luso la digito.
ny.body_more:
Ma scholarship amabwera kudzera mu Airtel Africa Tech Fellowship. Maphunziro adapeza gawo lalikulu la ndalama za Foundation. Lipoti loyamba la pachaka la Foundation likuwonetsa zinthu zina zambiri zomwe zachitika ku Africa konse.

Foundation idalumikiza masukulu atsopano 1,028 pa intaneti ndi thandizo la UNICEF. Izi zidabweretsa masukulu onse 3,296 m'maiko 13 aku Africa. Ophunzira opitilira 2 miliyoni ndi aphunzitsi pafupifupi 39,000 tsopano akugwiritsa ntchito masukulu awa. Foundation idaperekanso maphunziro aulere kwa ophunzira opitilira 11 miliyoni kudzera pa nsanja 64 za digito zomwe zilibe zilolezo. Masukulu asanu ndi awiri aboma adakonzedwa kwathunthu, ndipo ena 43 akukonzedwa pansi pa Pulogalamu Yothandizira Sukulu.

Kupatula masukulu, Foundation idaphunzitsa achinyamata ndi akazi 30,530 maluso a digito. Mapulogalamuwa amagwira ntchito ndi maboma ndi mabungwe ena. Segun Ogunsanya, Wapampando wa Airtel Africa Foundation, adati cholinga chake ndikuchotsa zopinga pamaphunziro ndi mwayi wa digito. Anati luso ndi chikhumbo ndizofala ku Africa, koma mwayi wopeza maphunziro, zida za digito ndi ntchito siwolungama. Foundation ikufuna kuthandiza madera omwe ali ndi mwayi wochepa.


Bungweli likukonzekera kuchita zambiri chaka chamawa. Lipereka maphunziro kwa ophunzira oposa 600. Lidzalumikiza masukulu ena 2,000 ku intaneti yaulere. Lidzakulitsanso maphunziro aukadaulo m'madera omwe alibe chithandizo chokwanira. Bungweli limagwira ntchito ndi maboma kuti lithandize chuma cha Africa kukula ndikusintha kukhala chabwino.

en.title: World Cup starts with Mexico win over South Africa
en.body: The 2026 World Cup has started in the United States, Mexico and Canada. Mexico beat South Africa 2-0 in the opening match on Thursday night at Estadio Azteca. Julian Quinones scored in the ninth minute and Raul Jimenez scored in the 67th minute. South Africa had ten players sent off the field during the match.
en.body_more:
South Africa's first player to be sent off was Sphephelo Sithole in the 49th minute. His teammate Themba Zwane received a red card in the 79th minute. Mexico also lost a player when Cesar Montes was sent off in the 90th minute. The match had three red cards and only two goals, making it one of the most talked about games as the World Cup began.

In the same group, South Korea won their opening match. They beat Czech Republic 2-1 on Friday morning in Guadalajara. Czech Republic scored first through Krejci in the 59th minute. South Korea came back with I. Beom Hwang scoring in the 67th minute to make it equal. Hyeon-gyn scored the winning goal in the 80th minute.

After the first matches, Mexico and South Korea have three points each at the top of Group A. South Africa and Czech Republic both lost their opening games and will want to play better in their next matches. Ten teams from Africa qualified for this World Cup, which is being held across three countries for the first time.

ny.title: World Cup yayamba ndi Mexico kupambana South Africa
ny.body: Mpikisano wa World Cup wa 2026 wayamba ku United States, Mexico ndi Canada. Mexico idagonjetsa South Africa 2-0 pamasewera otsegulira Lachinayi usiku ku Estadio Azteca. Julian Quinones adagoletsa chigoli mu mphindi yachisanu ndi chinayi ndipo Raul Jimenez adagoletsa mu mphindi ya 67. South Africa idalandira osewera khumi atatulutsidwa pabwalo pamasewerawa.
ny.body_more:
Wosewera woyamba wa South Africa kutulutsidwa anali Sphephelo Sithole mu mphindi ya 49. Mnzake wa timu yake Themba Zwane adalandira khadi lofiira mu mphindi ya 79. Mexico idatayanso wosewera pomwe Cesar Montes adatulutsidwa mu mphindi ya 90. Masewerawa anali ndi makadi ofiira atatu ndi zigoli ziwiri zokha, zomwe zimapangitsa kuti akhale amodzi mwa masewera omwe amakambidwa kwambiri pamene World Cup imayamba.

Mu gulu lomweli, South Korea idapambana masewera awo otsegulira. Adagonjetsa Czech Republic 2-1 Lachisanu m'mawa ku Guadalajara. Czech Republic idapeza chigoli choyamba kudzera mwa Krejci mu mphindi ya 59. South Korea idabweranso ndi I. Beom Hwang akuponya chigoli mu mphindi ya 67 kuti agwirizane. Hyeon-gyn adapeza chigoli chopambana mu mphindi ya 80.

Pambuyo pa masewera oyamba, Mexico ndi South Korea zili ndi mfundo zitatu pamwamba pa Gulu A. South Africa ndi Czech Republic zonse zidataya masewera awo otsegulira ndipo zidzafuna kusewera bwino pamasewera awo otsatira. Magulu khumi ochokera ku Africa adayenerera mpikisano wa World Cup, womwe ukuchitikira m'maiko atatu koyamba.

en.title: Mausoleum for Late Vice President Chilima Opens Saturday
en.body: A mausoleum for the late Vice President Dr Saulos Klaus Chilima will be unveiled on Saturday in Nsipe, Ntcheu District. The event marks two years since his death in a plane crash on 10 June 2024. A memorial mass will start at 9am at Nsipe Catholic Grounds, followed by the mausoleum unveiling. Family organiser Dr Efrem Chilima said all preparations are ready.
en.body_more:
Bishop Alfred Mateyu Chaima of Zomba Diocese will lead the memorial mass. After the service, family members, clergy, traditional leaders and close friends will speak about Dr Chilima's life and work. Education Minister Bright Msaka will attend as Guest of Honour and speak for President Peter Mutharika.

Many senior leaders have said they will come to the event. These include First Vice President Jane Mayemu Ansah, Second Vice President Enock Chihana, former President Joyce Banda, and former Vice Presidents Cassim Chilumpha, Khumbo Kachali and Michael Usi. Chief Justice Rezine Mzikamanda, Speaker of Parliament Sameer Suleman, other ministers, MPs and diplomats will also attend. Families of the eight people who died with Dr Chilima in the crash have been invited.

Dr Efrem Chilima asked guests to wear black, white and purple to show respect. He said the family wants to honour Dr Chilima in a dignified way. He noted that the UTM Party will hold a separate remembrance event at Ntcheu Stadium later the same day. "We know that Saulos touched so many lives," said Dr Chilima. "Let us honour his memory and legacy with dignity and respect."

Dr Chilima died on 10 June 2024 when a military plane crashed at Thungwa in Chikangawa while flying to Nkhata Bay for lawyer Ralph Kasambara's funeral. Eight other people died in the crash. The family said Dr Chilima was a respected leader who changed many lives across Malawi.

ny.title: Mausoleum a Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Chilima Atsegulidwa Loweruka
ny.body: Manda a Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Dr Saulos Klaus Chilima adzatsegulidwa Loweruka ku Nsipe, Chigawo cha Ntcheu. Chochitikachi chikukumbukira zaka ziwiri kuchokera pamene anamwalira pa ngozi ya ndege pa 10 June 2024. Misa yokumbukira idzayamba nthawi ya 9 koloko m'mawa ku Nsipe Catholic Grounds, kenako manda a manda adzatsegulidwa. Wokonza mabanja Dr Efrem Chilima adati kukonzekera konse kwakonzeka.
ny.body_more:
Bishopu Alfred Mateyu Chaima wa ku Zomba Dayosizi adzatsogolera mwambo wokumbukira anthu. Pambuyo pa mwambowu, achibale, atsogoleri achipembedzo, atsogoleri achikhalidwe ndi mabwenzi apamtima adzalankhula za moyo wa Dr Chilima ndi ntchito yake. Nduna ya zamaphunziro Bright Msaka adzapezekapo ngati Mlendo Wolemekezeka ndipo adzalankhula m'malo mwa Purezidenti Peter Mutharika.

Atsogoleri ambiri akuluakulu anena kuti adzabwera ku mwambowu. Awa ndi Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Jane Mayemu Ansah, Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Enock Chihana, Purezidenti wakale Joyce Banda, ndi Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wakale Cassim Chilumpha, Khumbo Kachali ndi Michael Usi. Woweruza Wamkulu Rezine Mzikamanda, Sipikala wa Nyumba Yamalamulo Sameer Suleman, nduna zina, aphungu a nyumba yamalamulo ndi akazembe nawonso adzapezekapo. Mabanja a anthu asanu ndi atatu omwe anamwalira ndi Dr Chilima pangoziyi ayitanidwa.

Dr Efrem Chilima adapempha alendo kuti avale zakuda, zoyera ndi zofiirira kuti asonyeze ulemu. Anati banjali likufuna kulemekeza Dr Chilima mwaulemu. Ananenanso kuti chipani cha UTM chidzachita mwambo wokumbukira anthu ena ku Ntcheu Stadium tsiku lomwelo. "Tikudziwa kuti Saulos adakhudza miyoyo yambiri," adatero Dr Chilima. "Tiyeni tilemekeze kukumbukira kwake ndi cholowa chake mwaulemu ndi ulemu."

Dr Chilima anamwalira pa 10 June 2024 pamene ndege yankhondo inagwa ku Thungwa ku Chikangawa pamene inkapita ku Nkhata Bay kukachita maliro a loya Ralph Kasambara. Anthu ena asanu ndi atatu anamwalira pangoziyi. Banjali linati Dr Chilima anali mtsogoleri wolemekezeka yemwe anasintha miyoyo yambiri ku Malawi konse.

en.title: Malawi maize harvest to be average but still falls short
en.body: Malawi will harvest an average amount of maize this year, but experts warn the crop will still not meet the country's food needs. The Famine Early Warning Systems Network (FEWS NET) said in a report released on 9 June that the 2025/26 harvest looks average due to good rainfall. However, the country will still face a maize shortage.
en.body_more:
The Reserve Bank of Malawi projected in March that the country would harvest about 3.3 million metric tonnes of maize in the 2025/26 season. This is an increase of about 400,000 metric tonnes from the 2.9 million metric tonnes harvested in the previous season. However, Malawi needs about 3.5 million metric tonnes of maize each year. This means the country will still be short by about 200,000 metric tonnes. The government may need to use grain reserves, buy maize from other countries, use irrigation harvests and private stocks to fill the gap.

Harvesting started in April in the Southern Region and has moved to the central and northern districts. Rain-fed farming produces about 80 percent of Malawi's maize. The other 20 percent comes from irrigation and residual-moisture farming. However, some areas faced problems. Below-average rainfall and long dry spells hurt crops in the Southern Lakeshore, Middle Shire and Lower Shire zones during the maize flowering stage. Heavy rains between 15 and 20 March caused flash floods in the central and southern regions, damaging crops in several districts. These households will harvest less than normal and may face food shortages.

The improved harvest is already helping food prices fall. Between March and April, maize prices dropped by about 15 percent, falling from K1,025 per kilogramme to K892 per kilogramme. The Southern Region saw the sharpest fall of about 25 percent as new maize entered the market. But prices remain very high — about 85 percent above the average from 2022 to 2024. Poor families who bought food at the market before harvest still struggle to afford maize.

Agriculture expert Leonard Chimwaza said the Ministry of Agriculture was slow in releasing third-round crop estimates. He said updated figures are needed for good planning. The Ministry of Agriculture has not yet given an official comment on the harvest outlook.

ny.title: Kukolola chimanga ku Malawi kudzakhala koyenera koma sikunakwanire
ny.body: Malawi idzakolola chimanga chaka chino, koma akatswiri akuchenjeza kuti mbewuyi sidzakwaniritsa zosowa za chakudya mdzikolo. Bungwe la Famine Early Warning Systems Network (FEWS NET) linanena mu lipoti lomwe linatulutsidwa pa 9 June kuti zokolola za 2025/26 zikuwoneka ngati zapakati chifukwa cha mvula yabwino. Komabe, dzikolo lidzakumanabe ndi kusowa kwa chimanga.
ny.body_more:
Banki Yaikulu ya Malawi inaneneratu mu Marichi kuti dzikolo lidzakolola matani pafupifupi 3.3 miliyoni a chimanga mu nyengo ya 2025/26. Izi ndi kuwonjezeka kwa matani pafupifupi 400,000 kuchokera ku matani 2.9 miliyoni omwe adasonkhanitsidwa nyengo yapitayi. Komabe, Malawi ikufunika matani pafupifupi 3.5 miliyoni a chimanga chaka chilichonse. Izi zikutanthauza kuti dzikolo lidzasowa matani pafupifupi 200,000. Boma lingafunike kugwiritsa ntchito tirigu wosungidwa, kugula chimanga kuchokera kumayiko ena, kugwiritsa ntchito zokolola zothirira ndi zokolola zachinsinsi kuti lidzaze malo osowa.

Kukolola kunayamba mu Epulo ku Southern Region ndipo kwasamukira ku madera apakati ndi kumpoto. Ulimi wodya mvula umabala pafupifupi 80 peresenti ya chimanga cha ku Malawi. 20 peresenti ina imachokera ku ulimi wothirira ndi chinyezi chotsalira. Komabe, madera ena adakumana ndi mavuto. Mvula yocheperako komanso nthawi yayitali youma zimavulaza mbewu m'madera a Southern Lakeshore, Middle Shire ndi Lower Shire panthawi ya maluwa a chimanga. Mvula yamphamvu pakati pa 15 ndi 20 Marichi inachititsa kuti madzi asefukire m'madera apakati ndi kum'mwera, zomwe zinawononga mbewu m'maboma angapo. Mabanja awa adzakolola zochepa kuposa masiku onse ndipo akhoza kusowa chakudya.

Kukolola kwabwinoku kukuthandiza kale mitengo ya chakudya kutsika. Pakati pa Marichi ndi Epulo, mitengo ya chimanga yatsika ndi pafupifupi 15 peresenti, kuchoka pa K1,025 pa kilogalamu kufika pa K892 pa kilogalamu. Chigawo cha Kumwera chinagwa kwambiri ndi pafupifupi 25 peresenti pamene chimanga chatsopano chinalowa pamsika. Koma mitengo ikadali yokwera kwambiri - pafupifupi 85 peresenti kuposa avareji kuyambira 2022 mpaka 2024. Mabanja osauka omwe adagula chakudya pamsika asanakololebe akuvutikabe kugula chimanga.

Katswiri wa zaulimi Leonard Chimwaza adati Unduna wa Zaulimi ukuchedwa kutulutsa ziwerengero za mbewu zachitatu. Iye anati ziwerengero zatsopano zikufunika kuti pakhale kukonzekera bwino. Unduna wa Zaulimi sunapereke ndemanga yovomerezeka pa momwe mbewu zidzakololeredwere.

en.title: Government Stops Case Against Chinese Prisoner Lin Yunhua
en.body: The State is trying to stop a case against Lin Yunhua, a Chinese man in prison. The Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) asked the High Court to drop the case on June 9, 2026. Lin Yunhua was charged with giving money to prison workers. The case was already at trial. Two witnesses had already spoken in court.
en.body_more:
Lin Yunhua was in prison for money laundering and wildlife crimes. While he was there, people said he gave money to prison workers. This gave him special treatment that other prisoners did not get. The ACB heard these complaints and started a case against him. The ACB was ready to call 15 to 18 witnesses.

The trial began on May 13, 2026. Two witnesses came to court and gave evidence. Then the case stopped. The Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) sent a letter on May 19, 2026. The letter told the ACB to drop the case. The DPP said the prison workers' bad actions should be dealt with through other government processes, not through the courts. Peter Sambani, the ACB's Chief Legal and Prosecution Officer, put this in a sworn statement to the court.

Many people in Malawi are angry about this choice. They say it is not fair. They ask: if the case was strong enough to start, why stop it now? Lawyers and rights groups say this looks bad. They worry it shows that rich and powerful people get treated differently. They say ordinary people go to prison for small crimes, but important people can escape. Some people say the government should explain why it stopped the case.

The High Court has not yet said yes to the request to drop the case. The court must still decide. Many Malawians are waiting to see what the judge will do. The case is called Criminal Case No. 5 of 2025 in the Financial Crimes Division of the High Court in Lilongwe.

ny.title: Boma Layimitsa Mlandu Wokhudza Mkaidi Wachi China Lin Yunhua
ny.body: Boma likuyesera kuletsa mlandu wotsutsana ndi Lin Yunhua, mwamuna waku China yemwe ali m'ndende. Bungwe Lolimbana ndi Ziphuphu (ACB) linapempha Khothi Lalikulu kuti lichotse mlanduwu pa June 9, 2026. Lin Yunhua anaimbidwa mlandu wopereka ndalama kwa ogwira ntchito m'ndende. Mlanduwu unali kale kukhoti. Mboni ziwiri zinali zitalankhula kale kukhoti.
ny.body_more:
Lin Yunhua anali m'ndende chifukwa cha kuswa ndalama ndi milandu ya nyama zakuthengo. Ali kumeneko, anthu anati anapereka ndalama kwa ogwira ntchito m'ndende. Izi zinamupatsa chithandizo chapadera chomwe akaidi ena sanalandire. ACB anamva madandaulo awa ndipo anayamba mlandu wotsutsana naye. ACB anali okonzeka kuyitana mboni 15 mpaka 18.

Mlanduwu unayamba pa Meyi 13, 2026. Mboni ziwiri zinabwera kukhothi ndikupereka umboni. Kenako mlanduwo unayima. Mtsogoleri wa Madandaulo a Anthu (DPP) anatumiza kalata pa Meyi 19, 2026. Kalatayo inauza ACB kuti ichotse mlanduwo. DPP inati zochita zoipa za ogwira ntchito m'ndende ziyenera kuthetsedwa kudzera m'njira zina za boma, osati kudzera m'makhothi. Peter Sambani, Chief Legal and Prosecution Officer wa ACB, anaika izi mu chikalata cholumbira kukhothi.

Anthu ambiri ku Malawi akwiya ndi chisankhochi. Amati sichilungamo. Amafunsa kuti: ngati mlanduwo unali wolimba mokwanira kuti uyambe, bwanji usiye tsopano? Maloya ndi magulu omenyera ufulu akuti izi zikuwoneka zoyipa. Akuda nkhawa kuti zikusonyeza kuti anthu olemera komanso amphamvu amachitiridwa mosiyana. Amati anthu wamba amapita kundende chifukwa cha milandu ing'onoing'ono, koma anthu ofunikira amathawa. Anthu ena amati boma liyenera kufotokoza chifukwa chake linayimitsa mlanduwo.

Khoti Lalikulu silinavomereze pempho loti mlanduwo uchotsedwe. Khotilo liyenerabe kusankha. Anthu ambiri aku Malawi akuyembekezera kuona zomwe woweruza milandu achite. Mlanduwu umatchedwa Mlandu Wachifwamba Nambala 5 wa 2025 mu Dipatimenti Yoona za Milandu Yachuma ku Khoti Lalikulu ku Lilongwe.

en.title: Experts say wait for facts on leaked ESCOM letter
en.body: A leaked government letter about ESCOM buying transformers and wooden poles has started a debate. But experts tell Malawians not to say it is corruption before the facts are clear. Legal scholar Myles Banda says the real problem is how a secret letter came out to the public.
en.body_more:
The letter appears to give ESCOM permission to buy the items. But experts say this one letter does not prove any crime. They say only proper checks by the government can decide if anything was wrong.

Myles Banda, a legal scholar, says people should ask why a secret letter leaked out. He says good government needs trust and private talks inside offices. When someone shares only part of a letter, it can confuse people and make them guess. A private lawyer also said the same thing — people should not say it is bad when they do not have all the facts.

Malawi has places that check these things. The Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority, the Anti-Corruption Bureau, and others can look at what happened. Experts say if there is a real problem, these groups should look at it, not people talking on the street.

Some people are saying President Peter Mutharika did wrong. But experts say this is not fair without proof. The President has always said he does not like corruption. Professor Fedex Houverghton, who knows about how government works, says Malawi must be fair. He says people must use facts, not guesses. Before anyone says someone did wrong, the right groups must check all the papers and all the rules. Only then can anyone know the truth.

ny.title: Akatswiri akuti dikirani mfundo zenizeni pa kalata ya ESCOM yomwe yatuluka
ny.body: Kalata ya boma yowuluka yokhudza kugula ma transformer ndi mitengo yamatabwa yayambitsa mkangano. Koma akatswiri akuuza anthu aku Malawi kuti asanene kuti ndi ziphuphu asanadziwe zoona zake. Katswiri wazamalamulo Myles Banda akuti vuto lenileni ndi momwe kalata yachinsinsi inafikira anthu onse.
ny.body_more:
Kalatayo ikuoneka kuti ikupatsa ESCOM chilolezo chogula zinthuzo. Koma akatswiri akuti kalata imodzi iyi siitsimikizira mlandu uliwonse. Amati kufufuza koyenera kokha ndi boma ndiko kungawone ngati pali cholakwika.

Myles Banda, katswiri wa zamalamulo, akuti anthu ayenera kufunsa chifukwa chake kalata yachinsinsi yatuluka. Iye akuti boma labwino limafuna kudalirika ndi zokambirana zachinsinsi mkati mwa maofesi. Munthu akagawana gawo lokha la kalata, zimatha kusokoneza anthu ndikupangitsa kuti aganize. Loya wachinsinsi nayenso ananena zomwezo - anthu sayenera kunena kuti ndi zoipa pamene alibe mfundo zonse.

Malawi ali ndi malo omwe amafufuza zinthuzi. Boma la Public Procurement and Disposal of Assets Authority, Anti-Corruption Bureau, ndi ena akhoza kuyang'ana zomwe zinachitika. Akatswiri amati ngati pali vuto lenileni, maguluwa ayenera kuliyang'ana, osati anthu omwe amalankhula mumsewu.

Anthu ena akunena kuti Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adachita cholakwika. Koma akatswiri amati izi sizolungama popanda umboni. Purezidenti nthawi zonse amanena kuti sakonda ziphuphu. Pulofesa Fedex Houverghton, yemwe amadziwa momwe boma limagwirira ntchito, akuti Malawi iyenera kukhala yolungama. Iye akuti anthu ayenera kugwiritsa ntchito mfundo zenizeni, osati kungoganizira chabe. Aliyense asananene kuti wina wachita cholakwika, magulu oyenera ayenera kuyang'ana mapepala onse ndi malamulo onse. Pamenepo munthu aliyense angadziwe zoona zake.

en.title: CDEDI Says Fuel Prices Must Fall, Blames Cartels
en.body: The Centre for Democracy and Economic Development Initiatives (CDEDI) says fuel prices in Malawi should be around K4,500 per litre. CDEDI Executive Director Sylvester Namiwa spoke on Thursday in Blantyre. He says cartels in fuel supply, foreign exchange, and public buying are making prices too high and weakening the Kwacha.
en.body_more:
Namiwa accused the National Oil Company of Malawi (NOCMA) of not doing its real job. NOCMA was made to keep 60 million litres of fuel safe in Mzuzu, Lilongwe and Blantyre. Instead, Namiwa said, NOCMA now buys and sells fuel like a normal fuel seller and forgets about public good.

NOCMA gives most fuel transport work to foreign companies paid in US dollars, Namiwa said. This wastes the country's scarce foreign money. He said local transporters have over 900,000 tankers that sit empty with no work. NOCMA also buys fuel through middlemen from Tanzania, which makes import costs high. These high costs go to consumers as high pump prices. NOCMA also uses foreign insurance firms instead of local ones, Namiwa added.

CDEDI wants the government to remove the K350 per litre levy added under the Automatic Pricing Mechanism. This levy was made to pay back K1.2 trillion in money owed since May 2022, but Namiwa said it unfairly burdens ordinary people. He asked why consumers should pay this money when both NOCMA and Petroleum Importers Limited also owe money to the energy watchdog, the Malawi Energy Regulatory Authority (MERA).

Namiwa called for long-term changes to how fuel comes to Malawi. He urged government to use rail and pipelines instead of only road transport. CDEDI also wants Malawi to buy a share in Angola's Lobito Oil Refinery project, like Zambia did. A local refinery could also help Malawi make its own fuel and cut import costs. Without these changes, Namiwa warned, cost of living will stay high and Malawians face "another wasted five years."

ny.title: CDEDI Ikuti Mitengo ya Mafuta Iyenera Kutsika, Ikuimba Mlandu Magulu Ogulitsa Mafuta
ny.body: Bungwe la Centre for Democracy and Economic Development Initiatives (CDEDI) lati mitengo ya mafuta ku Malawi iyenera kukhala pafupifupi K4,500 pa lita imodzi. Mkulu wa bungwe la CDEDI, Sylvester Namiwa, adalankhula Lachinayi ku Blantyre. Iye akuti magulu a anthu ogulitsa mafuta, kusinthana ndalama zakunja, ndi kugula zinthu m'maboma akupangitsa mitengo kukhala yokwera kwambiri ndipo akufooketsa Kwacha.
ny.body_more:
Namiwa adadzudzula National Oil Company of Malawi (NOCMA) kuti sagwira ntchito yake yeniyeni. NOCMA idapangidwa kuti isunge malita 60 miliyoni a mafuta otetezeka ku Mzuzu, Lilongwe ndi Blantyre. M'malo mwake, Namiwa adati, NOCMA tsopano imagula ndikugulitsa mafuta ngati wogulitsa mafuta wamba ndipo imaiwala zabwino za anthu onse.

NOCMA imapereka ntchito zambiri zoyendera mafuta kumakampani akunja omwe amalipidwa ndi madola aku US, adatero Namiwa. Izi zikuwononga ndalama zochepa zakunja mdzikolo. Anati onyamula mafuta am'deralo ali ndi matanki opitilira 900,000 omwe amakhala opanda kanthu popanda ntchito. NOCMA imagulanso mafuta kudzera mwa amalonda ochokera ku Tanzania, zomwe zimapangitsa kuti mitengo yolowera kunja ikhale yokwera. Mitengo yokwerayi imapita kwa ogula ngati mitengo yokwera ya pampu. NOCMA imagwiritsanso ntchito makampani a inshuwaransi akunja m'malo mwa am'deralo, adatero Namiwa.

CDEDI ikufuna kuti boma lichotse msonkho wa K350 pa lita womwe wawonjezeredwa pansi pa Automatic Pricing Mechanism. Ndalama iyi idapangidwa kuti ibweze ndalama za K1.2 trillion zomwe zidabwezedwa kuyambira Meyi 2022, koma Namiwa adati imalemetsa anthu wamba mopanda chilungamo. Iye anafunsa chifukwa chake ogula ayenera kulipira ndalamazi pomwe NOCMA ndi Petroleum Importers Limited nawonso ali ndi ngongole ku bungwe loona za mphamvu, Malawi Energy Regulatory Authority (MERA).

Namiwa anapempha kusintha kwa nthawi yayitali momwe mafuta amafikira ku Malawi. Anapempha boma kuti ligwiritse ntchito njanji ndi mapaipi m'malo mongogwiritsa ntchito mayendedwe a pamsewu. CDEDI ikufunanso kuti Malawi igule gawo mu projekiti ya Lobito Oil Refinery ku Angola, monga momwe Zambia inachitira. Kampani yoyeretsa mafuta m'deralo ingathandizenso Malawi kupanga mafuta ake ndikuchepetsa ndalama zogulira kunja. Popanda kusinthaku, Namiwa anachenjeza, mtengo wa moyo udzakhalabe wokwera ndipo aku Malawi akukumana ndi "zaka zina zisanu zotayika."

en.title: Attorney General fights to stop MEC relocation court case
en.body: Attorney General Frank Mbeta has asked the High Court in Lilongwe to throw out a case brought by the Malawi Congress Party (MCP) and three lawmakers. The case stopped the move of the Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) headquarters from Lilongwe to Blantyre. Mbeta says the MCP and lawmakers have no right to be in court.
en.body_more:
Mbeta argues that the three MCP Members of Parliament — Emmanuel Chambulanyina Jere, Abraham Mwakhwawa and Daniel Chitonya Mwanyongo — are not workers or leaders of MEC. He says they have not shown how the move harms them. "None of the claimants is a member or employee of the Malawi Electoral Commission," Mbeta states in court papers.

The Attorney General says MEC itself has already gone to court about the same matter. He says there is no need for the three lawmakers to bring another case. He points to an earlier court case involving activist Limbani Phiri. In that case, the court said the applicants lacked standing because they did not prove they suffered harm different from ordinary people. Mbeta says the MCP lawmakers are in the same position.

Mbeta also says the case should not be treated as a constitutional matter. He argues the lawmakers are challenging how the order was carried out, not the order itself. The case came from Executive Order Number One of 2025, which President Peter Mutharika issued to move MEC's headquarters. Secretary to the President Justin Saidi gave a directive on February 27, 2026, to carry this out. The Ministry of Lands, Housing and Urban Development decided not to renew MEC's lease in Lilongwe.

On June 5, 2026, Justice Kenyatta Nyirenda gave the MCP and the three lawmakers permission to challenge the relocation in court. He also stopped the government from moving MEC until the court makes a decision. Now Mbeta wants the court to overturn that permission and cancel the stop order.

ny.title: Loya Wamkulu akulimbana kuti aletse mlandu wa khothi la MEC wosamukira kudziko lina
ny.body: Loya Wamkulu Frank Mbeta wapempha Khothi Lalikulu ku Lilongwe kuti lichotse mlandu womwe chipani cha Malawi Congress Party (MCP) ndi opanga malamulo atatu adapereka. Mlanduwu unaletsa kusamutsa likulu la Malawi Electoral Commission (MEC) kuchoka ku Lilongwe kupita ku Blantyre. Mbeta akuti MCP ndi opanga malamulo alibe ufulu wokhala kukhothi.
ny.body_more:
Mbeta akunena kuti aphungu atatu a MCP a Nyumba Yamalamulo — Emmanuel Chambulanyina Jere, Abraham Mwakhwawa ndi Daniel Chitonya Mwanyongo — si antchito kapena atsogoleri a MEC. Iye akuti sanawonetse momwe kusunthaku kuwapwetekera. "Palibe aliyense mwa omwe akupempha kuti achite izi ndi membala kapena wantchito wa Malawi Electoral Commission," Mbeta akunena m'mapepala a khothi.

Woyimira milandu wamkulu akuti MEC yokha yapita kale kukhothi pankhani yomweyi. Iye akuti palibe chifukwa choti aphungu atatuwa abweretse mlandu wina. Akulozera ku mlandu wakale wa khothi wokhudza wolimbikitsa ufulu wa anthu Limbani Phiri. Pankhaniyi, khothi linati opemphawo analibe udindo chifukwa sanatsimikizire kuti adakumana ndi mavuto mosiyana ndi anthu wamba. Mbeta akunena kuti aphungu a MCP ali pamalo omwewo.

Mbeta akunenanso kuti nkhaniyi siyenera kuonedwa ngati nkhani yokhudza malamulo. Iye akunena kuti aphungu akutsutsa momwe lamuloli linachitikira, osati lamulolo lokha. Mlanduwu unachokera ku Executive Order Number One ya 2025, yomwe Purezidenti Peter Mutharika adapereka kuti likulu la MEC lisamukire. Mlembi wa Purezidenti Justin Saidi adapereka lamulo pa February 27, 2026, kuti achite izi. Unduna wa Zamalo, Nyumba ndi Chitukuko cha Mizinda unaganiza zosakonzanso lendi ya MEC ku Lilongwe.

Pa June 5, 2026, Woweruza milandu Kenyatta Nyirenda adapatsa MCP ndi opanga malamulo atatu chilolezo chotsutsa kusamutsidwa kukhothi. Analetsanso boma kusamutsa MEC mpaka khothi litapanga chisankho. Tsopano Mbeta akufuna khothi lisinthe chilolezocho ndikuletsa lamulo loletsa.

en.title: School gives full scholarship to lone girl exam candidate
en.body: Maranatha Academy has given a full secondary school scholarship to Duwama Muhammad, a 16-year-old from Mangale Primary School in Mangochi. She was the only girl who took the 2026 Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education examinations at the school. Managing Director Dr Ernest Kaonga also gave her K500,000 per term for living costs.
en.body_more:
Duwama's achievement stands out in her community. Between 2017 and 2021, no girls sat for the examinations at Mangale Primary School. Her decision to take the exams shows courage in a place where many girls leave school early due to marriage and pregnancy.

Mangale Primary School Head Teacher Boniface Gomiwa said Duwama is a top student. He noted that the school faces serious problems. By 2024, it had only four classrooms and no houses for teachers. Some teachers walk up to 10 kilometres to reach the school. The school was built in 1975.

Dr Kaonga said the scholarship is an investment in Malawi's future. He stated: "We believe every talented girl deserves a fair chance to reach her full potential, regardless of her background." The K500,000 per term will cover all her school needs so she can focus on her studies.

Civil Society Education Coalition Executive Director Benedicto Kondowe praised the scholarship. He called on other people, schools and companies to help more poor girls stay in school. He said chiefs and community leaders must protect girls from early marriage and child labour. He also named successful women in Malawi — Vice President Dr Jane Ansah, Electoral Commission Chairperson Annabel Mtalimanja, and National Examinations Board Executive Director Dr Dorothy Nampota — as role models for girls to follow.

ny.title: Sukulu imapereka maphunziro onse kwa wophunzira yemwe ali ndi mayeso a mtsikana yekha
ny.body: Maranatha Academy yapereka ndalama zonse za sukulu ya sekondale kwa Duwama Muhammad, mtsikana wazaka 16 wochokera ku Sukulu ya Pulayimale ya Mangale ku Mangochi. Iye ndiye mtsikana yekhayo amene adalemba mayeso a 2026 Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education kusukuluyi. Mtsogoleri wamkulu Dr Ernest Kaonga adamupatsanso K500,000 pa semesita iliyonse yolipirira ndalama zogulira zinthu.
ny.body_more:
Kupambana kwa Duwama kumawonekera kwambiri mdera lake. Pakati pa 2017 ndi 2021, palibe atsikana omwe adachita mayeso ku Sukulu ya Pulayimale ya Mangale. Kusankha kwake kulemba mayeso kukuwonetsa kulimba mtima pamalo omwe atsikana ambiri amasiya sukulu msanga chifukwa cha ukwati ndi mimba.

Mphunzitsi Wamkulu wa Sukulu ya Pulayimale ya Mangale, Boniface Gomiwa, adati Duwama ndi wophunzira wabwino kwambiri. Ananena kuti sukuluyi ikukumana ndi mavuto akulu. Pofika chaka cha 2024, inali ndi makalasi anayi okha ndipo inalibe nyumba za aphunzitsi. Aphunzitsi ena amayenda makilomita 10 kuti akafike kusukuluyi. Sukuluyi idamangidwa mu 1975.

Dr Kaonga adati maphunzirowa ndi ndalama zomwe zingathandize tsogolo la Malawi. Anati: "Timakhulupirira kuti mtsikana aliyense waluso ayenera kukhala ndi mwayi wokwanira kuti akwaniritse luso lake lonse, mosasamala kanthu za mbiri yake." K500,000 pa semesita iliyonse idzakwaniritsa zosowa zake zonse kusukulu kuti athe kuyang'ana kwambiri maphunziro ake.

Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa Civil Society Education Coalition, Benedicto Kondowe, adayamika maphunzirowa. Adapempha anthu ena, masukulu ndi makampani kuti athandize atsikana osauka ambiri kuti apitirize sukulu. Iye anati mafumu ndi atsogoleri ammudzi ayenera kuteteza atsikana ku ukwati wachinyamata komanso kugwiritsa ntchito ana mwankhanza. Anatchulanso akazi opambana ku Malawi — Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Dr. Jane Ansah, Wapampando wa Electoral Commission Annabel Mtalimanja, ndi Mtsogoleri Wamkulu wa National Examinations Board Dr. Dorothy Nampota — ngati zitsanzo zabwino kwa atsikana.

en.title: Poet Chiwamba and singer Sangie join music and poetry together
en.body: Poet Robert Chiwamba and musician Sangie have made a new song and poem together. The work is called Chikondano. It is based on a Christian hymn. Chiwamba and Sangie take turns to sing and speak the words. Chikondano came out yesterday and got over 50,000 views on YouTube in one day.
en.body_more:
Chiwamba said music and poetry are like two sides of the same coin. "Music is just the song form of spoken words," he said. He told a person yesterday that when artists work together from different types of art, they can reach each other's fans. "We both get new ideas and make better art," he said.

Chikondano is the first song from Chiwamba's new album. The album is called Evangelist Shadreck Wame and will come out on August 3, 2026. It has eight songs and two poems. Sangie sings on all eight songs. This album marks Chiwamba's 15 years as a poet. He is the head of the Poetry Association of Malawi.

Chiwamba and Sangie have worked together two times before. Their first work was Ngwazi Zazikazi in 2018. This song won a big award from the European Union. It was the best communications work from a European Union project in the world. Their second work is Mtanda. It got 750,000 views on YouTube.

Sangie said she was happy to work with Chiwamba. She said the work brought her to new fans who like poetry. "These people do not just listen to music. They think about the words," she said. Sangie is known for her song Mayi Wangwiro. Chiwamba told her to pick the hymns for the album. Some hymns Sangie knew. Others she had to learn when she first heard them. The song was made at Don Foxxy Productions. The video shows singers from St Peter's and Paul Balaka Anglican Parish.

ny.title: Wolemba ndakatulo Chiwamba ndi woimba Sangie akugwirizana nyimbo ndi ndakatulo
ny.body: Wolemba ndakatulo Robert Chiwamba ndi woimba nyimbo Sangie apanga nyimbo yatsopano ndi ndakatulo pamodzi. Ntchitoyi imatchedwa Chikondano. Imachokera pa nyimbo yachikhristu. Chiwamba ndi Sangie amasinthasintha kuyimba ndikulankhula mawu. Chikondano adatuluka dzulo ndipo adapeza ma view opitilira 50,000 pa YouTube tsiku limodzi.
ny.body_more:
Chiwamba anati nyimbo ndi ndakatulo zili ngati mbali ziwiri za ndalama imodzi. "Nyimbo ndi nyimbo chabe ya mawu olankhulidwa," adatero. Dzulo adauza munthu wina kuti ojambula akamagwira ntchito limodzi kuchokera ku mitundu yosiyanasiyana ya zaluso, amatha kufikira mafani a wina ndi mnzake. "Tonsefe timapeza malingaliro atsopano ndikupanga zaluso zabwino," adatero.

Chikondano ndi nyimbo yoyamba kuchokera mu album yatsopano ya Chiwamba. Album iyi imatchedwa Evangelist Shadreck Wame ndipo idzatulutsidwa pa Ogasiti 3, 2026. Ili ndi nyimbo zisanu ndi zitatu ndi ndakatulo ziwiri. Sangie amaimba pa nyimbo zonse zisanu ndi zitatu. Album iyi ikukumbukira zaka 15 za Chiwamba monga wolemba ndakatulo. Iye ndi mtsogoleri wa Poetry Association of Malawi.

Chiwamba ndi Sangie agwira ntchito limodzi kawiri kale. Ntchito yawo yoyamba inali Ngwazi Zazikazi mu 2018. Nyimboyi idapambana mphoto yayikulu kuchokera ku European Union. Unali ntchito yabwino kwambiri yolumikizirana kuchokera ku projekiti ya European Union padziko lonse lapansi. Ntchito yawo yachiwiri ndi Mtanda. Idawonedwa ndi anthu 750,000 pa YouTube.

Sangie anati anali wokondwa kugwira ntchito ndi Chiwamba. Anati ntchitoyi inamubweretsa kwa mafani atsopano omwe amakonda ndakatulo. "Anthu awa samangomvera nyimbo zokha. Amaganizira za mawu," adatero. Sangie amadziwika ndi nyimbo yake ya Mayi Wangwiro. Chiwamba anamuuza kuti asankhe nyimbo za mu album. Nyimbo zina zomwe Sangie ankadziwa. Zina anayenera kuphunzira atazimva koyamba. Nyimboyi inapangidwa ku Don Foxxy Productions. Kanemayo akuwonetsa oimba ochokera ku St Peter's ndi Paul Balaka Anglican Parish.

en.title: FDH Bank gives K100m to Team Malawi for Commonwealth Games
en.body: FDH Bank Plc gave K100 million to help Team Malawi get ready for the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland. The games will happen from 23 July to 2 August. FDH Bank's Head of Personal Business Banking Daniel Pinto Khamula gave the money on Wednesday at a ceremony. Sports Ministry PS Jameson Ndalama and Malawi Olympic Committee Vice President Francis Kakhuta-Banda were there.
en.body_more:
Khamula said the bank sees this as a duty to help Malawi grow. "We look at it as a national duty. It is a patriotic thing to do," he said. He told people that the private sector must help the country grow, not wait for others to do the work. "We don't believe that this is something that other people need to do for us. We believe that as Malawians, we need to participate in our own growth," Khamula said. He added that the bank wants the money to push athletes to train hard and do well.

Malawi National Council of Sports Acting Chief Executive Officer Ivy Chinangwa said the gift comes at the right time. She said companies and sports must work together so athletes have enough money to race well in other countries. Francis Kakhuta-Banda also asked other big companies to give money to help Team Malawi. "Participation in international competitions requires substantial financial resources, and increased corporate support would help athletes focus on training and performance," Banda said.

This gift comes as FDH Bank grows bigger. On Thursday, at the bank's 19th Annual General Meeting, Managing Director Noel Mkulichi said the bank bought Ecobank Mozambique. Mkulichi said this move helps FDH Bank serve more people across Southern Africa and do trade with other countries. The bank's profit after tax was K147.8 billion for the year ending 31 December 2025. This is up from K74.1 billion the year before. Frank Harawa from the Minority Shareholders Association said the move into Mozambique is good for long-term growth.

ny.title: Banki ya FDH yapereka K100m ku Team Malawi pa Masewera a Commonwealth
ny.body: FDH Bank Plc yapereka K100 miliyoni kuti ithandize Team Malawi kukonzekera Masewera a Commonwealth a 2026 ku Glasgow, Scotland. Masewerawa adzachitika kuyambira pa 23 Julayi mpaka 2 Ogasiti. Mtsogoleri wa Banki ya Zamalonda ya FDH Bank, Daniel Pinto Khamula, adapereka ndalamazo Lachitatu pamwambo. Wapampando wa Unduna wa Zamasewera, Jameson Ndalama, ndi Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti wa Komiti ya Olimpiki ya Malawi, Francis Kakhuta-Banda, analipo.
ny.body_more:
Khamula anati banki ikuona izi ngati udindo wothandiza Malawi kukula. "Timaona izi ngati udindo wa dziko. Ndi chinthu chokonda dziko," adatero. Anauza anthu kuti mabungwe achinsinsi ayenera kuthandiza dziko kukula, osati kudikira kuti ena agwire ntchitoyo. "Sitikhulupirira kuti ichi ndi chinthu chomwe anthu ena ayenera kutichitira. Tikukhulupirira kuti monga a Malawi, tiyenera kutenga nawo mbali pakukula kwathu," adatero Khamula. Anawonjezera kuti banki ikufuna ndalamazo kuti ikakamize othamanga kuti azichita masewera olimbitsa thupi mwakhama komanso bwino.

Woyang'anira wamkulu wa bungwe la Malawi National Council of Sports, Ivy Chinangwa, anati mphatsoyi ikubwera panthawi yoyenera. Anati makampani ndi masewera ayenera kugwirira ntchito limodzi kuti othamanga akhale ndi ndalama zokwanira kuti azitha kuthamanga bwino m'maiko ena. Francis Kakhuta-Banda adapemphanso makampani ena akuluakulu kuti apereke ndalama zothandizira Team Malawi. "Kutenga nawo mbali pa mpikisano wapadziko lonse kumafuna ndalama zambiri, ndipo thandizo lowonjezera la makampani lingathandize othamanga kuyang'ana kwambiri pa maphunziro ndi magwiridwe antchito," adatero Banda.

Mphatso iyi ikubwera pamene FDH Bank ikukula. Lachinayi, pa Msonkhano Wapachaka wa Banki wa 19, Mtsogoleri Wamkulu Noel Mkulichi adati bankiyo idagula Ecobank Mozambique. Mkulichi adati izi zimathandiza FDH Bank kutumikira anthu ambiri ku South Africa konse komanso kuchita malonda ndi mayiko ena. Phindu la bankiyo pambuyo pa msonkho linali K147.8 biliyoni chaka chomwe chimatha pa 31 Disembala 2025. Izi zakwera kuchokera ku K74.1 biliyoni chaka chatha. Frank Harawa wochokera ku Minority Shareholders Association adati kusamukira ku Mozambique ndikwabwino pakukula kwanthawi yayitali.

en.title: Court Frees East Bridge Tobacco After ACB Loss
en.body: A high court judge in Lilongwe has ruled that the Anti-Corruption Bureau (ACB) acted unlawfully when it stopped East Bridge from accessing tobacco held at Limbe Leaf Tobacco warehouses. Judge Redson Kapindu made the decision on Wednesday. The court said the ACB abused its power and must pay costs to East Bridge.
en.body_more:
East Bridge's lawyer, Chimwemwe Kalua, explained what happened. The ACB first issued a restriction notice in February this year. When that notice expired, the ACB issued a new one on May 19, 2026 instead of asking the court to renew the old one. Kalua argued this was wrong. The court agreed. "The court has agreed with us that what the director did was an improper exercise of power," Kalua said. "The process was irregular and illegal."

The ACB had stopped East Bridge from moving or selling its tobacco as part of a criminal investigation. The investigation started after the Ministry of Agriculture made a complaint. The ACB restricted tobacco held at several places: warehouses in Njewa and Kanengo in Lilongwe, and Luwinga in Mzuzu. The restricted tobacco belonged to East Bridge Tobacco Buying Limited, East Bridge Commodities and Logistics, and East Bridge Estate SRL. According to the ACB's acting chief Gabriel Chembezi, the tobacco was bought using K45.5 billion of public funds.

Three years ago, the Malawi Government hired East Bridge to supply 600,000 metric tonnes of fertiliser. In return, East Bridge was to give the government agricultural produce such as tobacco and soya beans. The court's decision now means East Bridge can remove its tobacco from the warehouses and do business with it again. The ACB did not make any comment after the ruling.

ny.title: Khoti Lamasula Fodya wa East Bridge Pambuyo Pa Kutayika kwa ACB
ny.body: Woweruza milandu wa khoti lalikulu ku Lilongwe wagamula kuti bungwe lolimbana ndi ziphuphu (ACB) lachita zinthu mopanda lamulo pamene linaletsa East Bridge kugwiritsa ntchito fodya womwe uli m'nyumba zosungiramo zinthu za Limbe Leaf Tobacco. Woweruza milandu Redson Kapindu adapanga chisankho Lachitatu. Khotilo linati bungwe la ACB lidagwiritsa ntchito mphamvu zake molakwika ndipo liyenera kulipira ndalama ku East Bridge.
ny.body_more:
Loya wa East Bridge, Chimwemwe Kalua, anafotokoza zomwe zinachitika. ACB idapereka chidziwitso choyamba choletsa malonda mu February chaka chino. Chidziwitsocho chitatha ntchito, ACB idapereka chatsopano pa Meyi 19, 2026 m'malo mopempha khothi kuti lisinthe chakale. Kalua adati izi zinali zolakwika. Khothi linavomereza. "Khothi lavomerezana nafe kuti zomwe director adachita zinali kugwiritsa ntchito mphamvu molakwika," adatero Kalua. "Ndondomekoyi inali yosaloledwa komanso yosaloledwa."

ACB idaletsa East Bridge kusuntha kapena kugulitsa fodya wake ngati gawo la kafukufuku waupandu. Kafukufukuyu adayamba Unduna wa Zaulimi utapereka madandaulo. ACB idaletsa fodya yomwe idachitikira m'malo angapo: m'nyumba zosungiramo katundu ku Njewa ndi Kanengo ku Lilongwe, ndi Luwinga ku Mzuzu. Fodya woletsedwayo anali wa East Bridge Tobacco Buying Limited, East Bridge Commodities and Logistics, ndi East Bridge Estate SRL. Malinga ndi mkulu wa ACB, Gabriel Chembezi, fodyayo idagulidwa pogwiritsa ntchito ndalama za boma za K45.5 biliyoni.

Zaka zitatu zapitazo, Boma la Malawi linalemba ntchito East Bridge kuti ipereke matani 600,000 a feteleza. Pobwezera, East Bridge inali kupatsa boma zokolola zaulimi monga fodya ndi soya. Chigamulo cha khoti tsopano chikutanthauza kuti East Bridge ikhoza kuchotsa fodya wake m'nyumba zosungiramo katundu ndikuchita bizinesi nayo kachiwiri. ACB sananene chilichonse pambuyo pa chigamulocho.

en.title: Malawi Queens get new home in Blantyre
en.body: Malawi's national netball team, the Queens, now has a permanent home. One NICO leased them a house in Namiwawa, Blantyre on Monday. The 10-room house will save the Netball Association of Malawi K75 million every year. Before, the Queens moved from place to place for training camps.
en.body_more:
The house sits on 5.6 hectares of land. The lease is for three years, and can be made longer. The building has rooms for sleeping, cooking, and washing. It also has a physiotherapy room, an ice bath, and a recovery area for athletes.

NAM President Vitumbiko Gubuduza said the Queens spent about K25 million on each training camp. The team does at least three camps every year. Now they save money on moving and hotels. Gubuduza said the house will help the Queens, the Under-23 team, and the Kings men's team. She said other sports teams can use the house too when the netball teams are not there. This will make money for NAM.

Eris Properties Chief Executive Officer Washington Chimuzu said One NICO wanted to help. The company saw that moving teams for camps cost too much money and time. He said the house helps athletes rest and plan for big games.

Ivy Chinangwa from the Malawi National Council of Sports said the project shows how business and government can work together for sport. She said the council will try to build a netball court at the house. Africa Netball Secretary General Khungekile Madise Matiya said this is good news for netball in Malawi. The Queens are ranked ninth in the world.

ny.title: A Malawi Queens apeza nyumba yatsopano ku Blantyre
ny.body: Timu ya mpira wa netiboli ya dziko la Malawi, a Queens, tsopano ili ndi nyumba yokhazikika. NICO imodzi inawabwereketsa nyumba ku Namiwawa, Blantyre Lolemba. Nyumba ya zipinda 10 iyi idzapulumutsa bungwe la Netball Association of Malawi K75 miliyoni chaka chilichonse. Kale, a Queens ankasamukasamuka kupita kumalo ena kukachita masewera olimbitsa thupi.
ny.body_more:
Nyumbayi ili pa mahekitala 5.6 a malo. Lendi ndi ya zaka zitatu, ndipo ikhoza kupangidwa nthawi yayitali. Nyumbayi ili ndi zipinda zogona, kuphika, ndi kusamba. Ilinso ndi chipinda chochiritsira thupi, bafa la ayezi, ndi malo opumulirako a osewera.

Purezidenti wa NAM Vitumbiko Gubuduza adati a Queens adagwiritsa ntchito ndalama zokwana K25 miliyoni pamsasa uliwonse wophunzitsira. Gululi limachita misasa itatu pachaka. Tsopano akusunga ndalama zosamukira ndi mahotela. Gubuduza adati nyumbayi ithandiza a Queens, gulu la Under-23, ndi gulu la amuna la Kings. Iye adati magulu ena amasewera angagwiritsenso ntchito nyumbayi pamene magulu a netball salipo. Izi zipangitsa NAM kupeza ndalama.

Mkulu wa Eris Properties, Washington Chimuzu, adati One NICO ikufuna kuthandiza. Kampaniyo idawona kuti kusuntha magulu kupita kumisasa kumawononga ndalama zambiri komanso nthawi. Iye adati nyumbayo imathandiza othamanga kupuma ndikukonzekera masewera akuluakulu.

Ivy Chinangwa wochokera ku Malawi National Council of Sports adati pulojekitiyi ikuwonetsa momwe bizinesi ndi boma zingagwirire ntchito limodzi pamasewera. Iye anati bungweli lidzayesa kumanga bwalo la netiboli kunyumbayo. Mlembi Wamkulu wa Africa Netball Khungekile Madise Matiya adati iyi ndi nkhani yabwino kwa netiboli ku Malawi. A Queens ali pa nambala 9 padziko lonse lapansi.

en.title: Bank Gives Money to Help 57 Poor Students at University
en.body: National Bank of Malawi (NBM) plc has given scholarships to 57 poor students at public universities across the country. Four of these students go to Mzuzu University. Each student gets K1.7 million. The money helps pay for classes, a place to sleep, a laptop, health care, and books. The bank also gives K1 million extra to the five best students at Mzuzu University.
en.body_more:
NBM plc started this new part of the Mo Excellence Programme at Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources (LUANAR) before coming to Mzuzu University last week. Bessie Nyirenda is a board director at NBM plc. She said the bank knows that education helps build workers that the bank needs. "We used to help only the best students," Nyirenda said. "But we found many students have no money. So we grew the plan to help more students. We want to help make future workers for Malawi."

Nyirenda told the students to work hard and do well in their classes. She said the scholarship is a big task that asks for good work. Simeon Gwayi is Deputy Vice Chancellor at Mzuzu University. He said thank you to NBM plc for helping good students. "When we reward top students, it makes the school better," Gwayi said. "We want to make students who have good skills and are ready for work."

For 40 years, the scholarship plan only helped the five best students at seven public universities: the University of Malawi, Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences, Malawi University of Science and Technology, Kamuzu University of Health Sciences, Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources, Catholic University of Malawi, and Mzuzu University. Now the plan is much bigger. It went from helping 35 students to helping 100 students. The money pays for school fees, beds, computers, health care, and help from older workers. The top five students at Mzuzu University also get K1 million more.

ny.title: Banki Yapereka Ndalama Zothandizira Ophunzira Osauka 57 ku Yunivesite
ny.body: Banki ya National Bank of Malawi (NBM) plc yapereka maphunziro aukadaulo kwa ophunzira osauka 57 m'mayunivesite aboma mdziko lonselo. Anayi mwa ophunzirawa amapita ku yunivesite ya Mzuzu. Wophunzira aliyense amalandira K1.7 miliyoni. Ndalamazi zimathandiza kulipira makalasi, malo ogona, laputopu, chisamaliro chaumoyo, ndi mabuku. Bankiyi imaperekanso K1 miliyoni yowonjezera kwa ophunzira asanu abwino kwambiri ku yunivesite ya Mzuzu.
ny.body_more:
NBM plc inayamba gawo latsopanoli la Mo Excellence Programme ku Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources (LUANAR) isanabwere ku Mzuzu University sabata yatha. Bessie Nyirenda ndi director wa bungwe ku NBM plc. Iye anati bankiyo ikudziwa kuti maphunziro amathandiza kumanga antchito omwe bankiyo imafunikira. "Tinkathandiza ophunzira abwino kwambiri okha," adatero Nyirenda. "Koma tinapeza kuti ophunzira ambiri alibe ndalama. Choncho tinakulitsa dongosolo loti tithandize ophunzira ambiri. Tikufuna kuthandiza kupanga antchito amtsogolo ku Malawi."

Nyirenda anauza ophunzirawo kuti azigwira ntchito molimbika ndikuchita bwino m'makalasi awo. Iye anati maphunzirowa ndi ntchito yaikulu yomwe imafuna ntchito yabwino. Simeon Gwayi ndi Wachiwiri kwa Wachiwiri kwa Chancellor ku Mzuzu University. Anati zikomo kwa NBM plc pothandiza ophunzira abwino. "Tikamapereka mphoto kwa ophunzira abwino, zimapangitsa sukulu kukhala yabwino," adatero Gwayi. "Tikufuna kupanga ophunzira omwe ali ndi luso labwino ndipo ali okonzeka kugwira ntchito."

Kwa zaka 40, dongosolo la maphunziro linathandiza ophunzira asanu okha abwino kwambiri m'mayunivesite asanu ndi awiri aboma: University of Malawi, Malawi University of Business and Applied Sciences, Malawi University of Science and Technology, Kamuzu University of Health Sciences, Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources, Catholic University of Malawi, ndi Mzuzu University. Tsopano dongosololi ndi lalikulu kwambiri. Linayamba kuthandiza ophunzira 35 mpaka kuthandiza ophunzira 100. Ndalamazo zimalipira ndalama za sukulu, mabedi, makompyuta, chisamaliro chaumoyo, ndi thandizo kuchokera kwa ogwira ntchito okalamba. Ophunzira asanu apamwamba ku Mzuzu University amalandiranso K1 miliyoni yowonjezera.

en.title: Malawi Rejects Businessman's Claim to Diplomatic Status
en.body: Malawi's Ministry of Foreign Affairs says businessman Mahmood Azhar Chaudry never held diplomatic status. In a 2022 letter, then Secretary Joel Immanuel Matonga told lawyers that the ministry only gave Chaudry an identity card to help set up an honorary consulate. The card gave him no diplomatic or consular rights under Malawi law.
en.body_more:
Despite this, Chaudry put a sign at his Sunnyside home in Blantyre that said "Pakistan Consulate." He also drove cars with CD registration plates—a sign used only for real diplomats. After the ministry made its position clear, Chaudry took down the sign and changed his car registration back to normal.

Chaudry came from Pakistan but now has Malawian citizenship. He did not answer questions about what he did. The ministry's letter came because Chaudry claimed diplomatic status in court and wanted diplomatic rights. His lawyers then asked the ministry to confirm his status. The ministry said no.

A political scientist at the University of Malawi said Chaudry's conduct was "a spat in the face of Malawi" and warned that such lies must be punished. Governance analyst Thomas Cham'dimba said falsely claiming diplomatic status could be fraud and impersonation under Malawi's Penal Code. International security expert Haswell Sikusa called for the Ministry of Homeland Security to investigate. He said authorities should also check how Chaudry got his Malawian citizenship and passport.

Chaudry has faced other legal troubles. He has been investigated for tax evasion, money laundering, and land disputes including the Kanengo Northgate land case. He is now in a court case with Zoa Tea Estates Limited. The company says he made false claims and hid facts. No criminal charges have yet been brought against Chaudry for falsely claiming to be a diplomat. Experts warn that doing nothing will weaken Malawi's law and damage its standing abroad.

ny.title: Malawi Yakana Chigamulo cha Amalonda Chokhudza Ukadaulo
ny.body: Unduna wa Zachilendo ku Malawi wanena kuti wamalonda Mahmood Azhar Chaudry sanakhalepo ndi udindo wa unduna wa zandale. Mu kalata ya 2022, mlembi wa nthawi imeneyo Joel Immanuel Matonga adauza maloya kuti undunawu udangopatsa Chaudry chiphaso chomuthandiza kukhazikitsa kazembe wolemekezeka. Khadilo silinamupatse ufulu wa unduna wa zandale kapena waunduna motsatira malamulo a Malawi.
ny.body_more:
Ngakhale izi zili choncho, Chaudry anaika chikwangwani kunyumba kwake ku Sunnyside ku Blantyre chomwe chinali ndi mawu akuti "Pakistan Consulate." Analinso kuyendetsa magalimoto okhala ndi ma CD-plate olembetsera—chikwangwani chomwe chimagwiritsidwa ntchito kwa a diplomat enieni okha. Unduna utafotokoza momveka bwino zomwe ukunena, Chaudry anachotsa chikwangwanicho ndikusintha chikwangwani chake cha galimoto kukhala chachizolowezi.

Chaudry anachokera ku Pakistan koma tsopano ali ndi nzika ya ku Malawi. Sanayankhe mafunso okhudza zomwe anachita. Kalata ya unduna inabwera chifukwa Chaudry adanena kuti ali ndi udindo wa unduna kukhothi ndipo amafuna ufulu wa unduna. Maloya ake kenako anapempha unduna kuti utsimikizire kuti ali ndi udindo. Unduna unati ayi.

Katswiri wa zandale ku University of Malawi anati khalidwe la Chaudry linali "mkangano waukulu kwa Malawi" ndipo anachenjeza kuti mabodza otere ayenera kulangidwa. Katswiri wa zaulamuliro Thomas Cham'dimba anati kunena zabodza kuti ali ndi udindo wa unduna kungakhale chinyengo komanso kunamizira motsatira malamulo a chilango cha Malawi. Katswiri wa zachitetezo padziko lonse lapansi Haswell Sikusa anapempha Unduna wa Zachitetezo cha Dziko kuti ufufuze. Anati akuluakulu a boma ayeneranso kuwona momwe Chaudry adapezera unzika wake wa ku Malawi ndi pasipoti.

Chaudry wakumana ndi mavuto ena azamalamulo. Iye wafufuzidwa chifukwa chozemba misonkho, kuswa ndalama, ndi mikangano ya malo kuphatikizapo mlandu wa malo ku Kanengo Northgate. Tsopano ali kukhothi ndi Zoa Tea Estates Limited. Kampaniyo ikunena kuti adapereka mabodza ndipo adabisa mfundo. Palibe mlandu uliwonse womwe waperekedwa motsutsana ndi Chaudry chifukwa chonamizira kuti ndi kazembe. Akatswiri akuchenjeza kuti kusachita chilichonse kudzafooketsa malamulo a Malawi ndikuwononga mbiri yake kunja.

en.title: Vice President Saulos Chilima dies in plane crash
en.body: Vice President Saulos Chilima died on 10 June 2024 when his plane crashed in northern Malawi. The plane, owned by the Malawi Defence Force, was flying from Lilongwe to Mzuzu. Chilima was going to Nkhata Bay for a burial. The plane did not land at Mzuzu Airport.
en.body_more:
Airport officials at Mzuzu said the plane did not arrive. The aircraft left Lilongwe at about 10 am. By 3:45 pm, there was no word from the plane. His party, the United Transformation Movement, and his office staff said they did not know where he was.

Chilima was a leader in Malawi politics. He worked in business before he became Vice President. In 2020, he joined with President Lazarus Chakwera to form the Tonse Alliance. This group won the vote and removed the Democratic Progressive Party from power. Chilima spoke about ending bad government and stopping theft of state money. He wanted young people to have more say in government.

His death shocked the country. Many people felt sad. Questions came up about how safe planes are for state leaders. The government began a study into what happened to the plane. People said the state did not speak well to each other when the crash took place.

Chilima's loss changed Malawi's government. The Tonse Alliance lost a key leader. Young voters and city people who liked him had to find a new political home. Other parties said they would take up his ideas about stopping theft and making government work better. Civil groups use his memory to push for stronger laws against corruption and better state workers.

ny.title: Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima wamwalira pangozi ya ndege
ny.body: Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti Saulos Chilima anamwalira pa 10 June 2024 pamene ndege yake inagwa kumpoto kwa Malawi. Ndegeyo, yomwe inali ya Malawi Defense Force, inali kuuluka kuchokera ku Lilongwe kupita ku Mzuzu. Chilima anali kupita ku Nkhata Bay kukaika maliro. Ndegeyo sinafike pa Mzuzu Airport.
ny.body_more:
Akuluakulu a bwalo la ndege ku Mzuzu anati ndegeyo sinafike. Ndegeyo inachoka ku Lilongwe pafupifupi 10 koloko m'mawa. Pofika 3:45 pm, palibe chomwe chinanenedwa kuchokera ku ndegeyo. Chipani chake, United Transformation Movement, ndi ogwira ntchito ku ofesi yake anati sakudziwa komwe anali.

Chilima anali mtsogoleri wa ndale ku Malawi. Anagwira ntchito mu bizinesi asanakhale Wachiwiri kwa Purezidenti. Mu 2020, adagwirizana ndi Purezidenti Lazarus Chakwera kupanga Tonse Alliance. Gululi linapambana voti ndipo linachotsa chipani cha Democratic Progressive Party pampando. Chilima adalankhula za kuthetsa boma loipa ndikuletsa kuba ndalama za boma. Amafuna kuti achinyamata akhale ndi mawu ambiri m'boma.

Imfa yake idadabwitsa dzikolo. Anthu ambiri adamva chisoni. Mafunso adabuka okhudza momwe ndege zilili zotetezeka kwa atsogoleri a boma. Boma linayamba kufufuza zomwe zidachitikira ndegeyo. Anthu adati boma silinalankhulane bwino pamene ngoziyo idachitika.

Kutayika kwa Chilima kunasintha boma la Malawi. Tonse Alliance idataya mtsogoleri wofunikira. Achinyamata ovota ndi anthu am'mizinda omwe adamukonda adayenera kupeza nyumba yatsopano yandale. Magulu ena adati adzatenga malingaliro ake okhudza kuletsa kuba ndi kupangitsa boma kugwira ntchito bwino. Magulu a anthu wamba amagwiritsa ntchito kukumbukira kwake kulimbikitsa malamulo olimba oletsa ziphuphu ndi antchito abwino aboma.

en.title: Police arrest former State House Chief of Staff over hotel deal
en.body: Police arrested Prince Kapondamgaga on Monday. He was chief of staff at State House under former president Lazarus Chakwera. The arrest relates to the Amaryllis Hotel purchase. The Public Service Pension Trust Fund bought the hotel for K128 billion. Kapondamgaga faces abuse of office and fraud charges.
en.body_more:
Kapondamgaga was taken to Lilongwe Police Station in Area 3. His lawyer Donvan Silungwe said police claim he misused public office, committed fraud, and was involved in money laundering. Police did not give full details of the charges. National Police spokesperson Lael Chimtembo confirmed the arrest but said more information would come later.

A court in Mkukula, Dowa had issued a stay order that morning at 10.45am to stop the arrest. The order said Kapondamgaga should not be arrested until the court heard his case. Police said the order was irregular and did not follow it. Lawyer Benedicto Kondowe said this was wrong. He said police must obey court orders even if the orders have problems. Kapondamgaga's team planned to fix the order and present it again.

Parliament's Public Accounts Committee investigated the hotel deal. In March 2024, a meeting took place in Mzuzu about the purchase. Kapondamgaga attended this meeting. He said he was an observer sent by the Office of the President and Cabinet. The committee found his presence was irregular and interfered with the fund's independence. The hotel was bought from Yusuf Investments Limited. Independent valuations said the hotel was worth about K48 billion, much less than the K128 billion paid.

Kapondamgaga is one of several senior officials from Chakwera's government who have faced arrest or investigation. These include former Finance Ministers Sosten Gwengwe and Simplex Chithyola Banda, former Secretary to the President Colleen Zamba, and others. In April, a court had stopped police from arresting Colleen Zamba over abuse of office charges linked to the hotel deal.

ny.title: Apolisi amanga yemwe kale anali mkulu wa asilikali a State House chifukwa chogwirizana ndi hotelo
ny.body: Apolisi adamanga Prince Kapondamgaga Lolemba. Iye anali mkulu wa ogwira ntchito ku State House motsogozedwa ndi purezidenti wakale Lazarus Chakwera. Kumangidwa kumeneku kukukhudzana ndi kugula hotelo ya Amaryllis. Bungwe la Public Service Pension Trust Fund linagula hoteloyi pa K128 biliyoni. Kapondamgaga akukumana ndi milandu yogwiritsa ntchito molakwika udindo wake komanso chinyengo.
ny.body_more:
Kapondamgaga adatengedwa kupita ku siteshoni ya apolisi ya Lilongwe ku Area 3. Loya wake Donvan Silungwe adati apolisi akuti adagwiritsa ntchito molakwika udindo wa boma, adachita zachinyengo, komanso adagwira nawo ntchito yochotsa ndalama. Apolisi sanapereke tsatanetsatane wa milanduyi. Wolankhulira apolisi a dziko lino Lael Chimtembo adatsimikiza za kumangidwa kwa munthuyo koma adati zambiri zidzabwera mtsogolo.

Khothi ku Mkukula, Dowa lidapereka lamulo loletsa kumangidwa kwa munthuyo m'mawa womwewo nthawi ya 10:45am kuti aletse kumangidwa kwa munthuyo. Lamuloli lidati Kapondamgaga sayenera kumangidwa mpaka khothi litamva mlandu wake. Apolisi adati lamuloli silinali lolondola ndipo silinatsatire. Loya Benedicto Kondowe adati izi ndi zolakwika. Adati apolisi ayenera kumvera malamulo a khothi ngakhale malamulowo ali ndi mavuto. Gulu la Kapondamgaga likukonzekera kukonza lamuloli ndikuliperekanso.

Komiti Yoona za Maakaunti a Anthu ku Nyumba Yamalamulo idafufuza za mgwirizano wa hoteloyi. Mu Marichi 2024, msonkhano unachitika ku Mzuzu wokhudza kugula kwa munthuyo. Kapondamgaga adapezeka pamsonkhanowu. Adati anali wowonera wotumizidwa ndi Ofesi ya Purezidenti ndi Kabinet. Komitiyi inapeza kuti kupezeka kwake kunali kosakhazikika ndipo kunasokoneza ufulu wa thumba. Hoteloyi inagulidwa ku Yusuf Investments Limited. Mayeso odziyimira pawokha anati hoteloyi inali yamtengo wa pafupifupi K48 biliyoni, yocheperako poyerekeza ndi K128 biliyoni yomwe inaperekedwa.

Kapondamgaga ndi m'modzi mwa akuluakulu angapo a boma la Chakwera omwe akumana ndi kumangidwa kapena kufufuzidwa. Awa akuphatikizapo Nduna zakale za Zachuma Sosten Gwengwe ndi Simplex Chithyola Banda, yemwe kale anali Mlembi wa Purezidenti Colleen Zamba, ndi ena. Mu Epulo, khothi linaletsa apolisi kumanga Colleen Zamba chifukwa chogwiritsa ntchito molakwika udindo wake molakwika chifukwa cha mgwirizano wa hoteloyi.

en.title: Over 254,000 Students Begin PSLCE Exams Across Malawi
en.body: A total of 254,486 Standard Eight learners began sitting for the Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education (PSLCE) examinations today. The Malawi National Examinations Board (Maneb) is running the exams. The first paper is English at 8am. The exams will finish on Wednesday. This is more students than last year.
en.body_more:
Last year, 247,958 candidates sat the PSLCE exams. Of those students, 216,972 passed. This was 87.5 percent of all candidates. Male students had a pass rate of 90.61 percent. Female students had a pass rate of 84.56 percent. This was an increase of 1.34 percentage points from the year before.

Maneb Executive Director Dorothy Nampota said strong security measures are in place. The Malawi Defence Force (MDF) will guard the places where exam papers are kept. The Malawi Police Service (MPS) will provide security at all exam centres across the country. These security arrangements are part of three-year agreements signed in 2024. They will last until 2026.

Many students have said they are ready for the exams. In Zomba, a 12-year-old named Miracle Jambo said he has prepared well and wants to become a doctor. Another student, Princess Mountain, also age 12, said she wants to become a surgeon. In Zomba Malosa Constituency, Member of Parliament Grace Kwelepeta held a meeting with 1,334 exam candidates on Saturday. She told them to work hard and stay focused during the three days of exams. The results will help decide if students can go to secondary school and move towards their future jobs.

ny.title: Ophunzira Opitilira 254,000 Ayamba Mayeso a PSLCE Ku Malawi
ny.body: Ophunzira onse a Standard Eight okwana 254,486 ayamba kulemba mayeso a Primary School Leaving Certificate of Education (PSLCE) lero. Bungwe la Malawi National Examinations Board (Maneb) likuchita mayeso. Pepala loyamba ndi la Chingerezi nthawi ya 8 koloko m'mawa. Mayeso adzatha Lachitatu. Apa pali ophunzira ambiri kuposa chaka chatha.
ny.body_more:
Chaka chatha, ophunzira 247,958 adalemba mayeso a PSLCE. Mwa ophunzirawo, 216,972 adapambana. Izi zinali 87.5 peresenti ya ophunzira onse. Ophunzira aamuna anali ndi chiŵerengero chopambana cha 90.61 peresenti. Ophunzira achikazi anali ndi chiŵerengero chopambana cha 84.56 peresenti. Izi zinali kuwonjezeka kwa 1.34 peresenti poyerekeza ndi chaka chatha.

Mkulu wa Maneb, Dorothy Nampota, anati pali njira zolimba zotetezera. Asilikali a Malawi Defence Force (MDF) adzateteza malo omwe mapepala a mayeso amasungidwa. Apolisi a Malawi (MPS) adzapereka chitetezo m'malo onse oyeserera mdziko lonselo. Makonzedwe achitetezo awa ndi gawo la mapangano azaka zitatu omwe adasainidwa mu 2024. Adzakhalapo mpaka 2026.

Ophunzira ambiri anena kuti ali okonzeka mayeso. Ku Zomba, mtsikana wazaka 12 wotchedwa Miracle Jambo adati wakonzekera bwino ndipo akufuna kukhala dokotala. Wophunzira wina, Princess Mountain, yemwenso ali ndi zaka 12, adati akufuna kukhala dokotala wa opaleshoni. Mu boma la Zomba Malosa, phungu wa Nyumba Yamalamulo Grace Kwelepeta adachita msonkhano ndi ophunzira 1,334 omwe adalemba mayeso Loweruka. Iye adawauza kuti agwire ntchito molimbika ndikukhalabe maso pa masiku atatu a mayeso. Zotsatira zake zithandiza kusankha ngati ophunzira angapite kusukulu ya sekondale ndikupita kuntchito zawo zamtsogolo.

en.title: Malawi brings home 150 people from South Africa
en.body: Malawi has started to bring back its people from South Africa. One hundred and fifty Malawians left Western Cape Province on Saturday on two buses. They were living in camps in Mossel Bay. Now they are going home by road through Zimbabwe and Mozambique. They will reach Mwanza Border Post on Monday.
en.body_more:
The Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation said this is a voluntary repatriation. A task team from Malawi worked with the Malawi High Commission to help them. When the people arrive at Mwanza Border Post, they will go to Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre. There they will be checked before going to their home areas.

Secretary for Foreign Affairs Mwayiwawo Polepole said the government wants to keep all Malawians safe. The ministry said it will give them food, travel help, and other support. The government will help them get to their home districts.

Many foreign people left their homes in South Africa because of xenophobia. They had to go to camps in Mossel Bay. The Malawi High Commission had been getting calls from people who wanted to come home. This repatriation shows the government wants to help Malawians who are in danger. The ministry said more people could be brought back if they need help. The 150 people are part of a bigger group of foreign nationals who were in the camps.

Lawyer Benedicto Kondowe said it is good that the government helped these people. But he said people should not ignore travel warnings. He said people should not go to places that are not safe just for work.

ny.title: Dziko la Malawi labweretsa anthu 150 ochokera ku South Africa
ny.body: Dziko la Malawi layamba kubweretsa anthu ake ochokera ku South Africa. Anthu okwana 150 aku Malawi adachoka ku Western Cape Province Loweruka pa mabasi awiri. Ankakhala m'misasa ku Mossel Bay. Tsopano akupita kwawo kudzera mumsewu kudzera ku Zimbabwe ndi Mozambique. Afika ku Mwanza Border Post Lolemba.
ny.body_more:
Unduna wa Zachilendo ndi Mgwirizano Wapadziko Lonse unati uku ndi kubwerera kwawo mwaufulu. Gulu logwira ntchito kuchokera ku Malawi linagwira ntchito ndi bungwe la Malawi High Commission kuti liwathandize. Anthuwa akafika ku Mwanza Border Post, adzapita ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre. Kumeneko adzayang'aniridwa asanapite kwawo.

Nduna ya Zachilendo Mwayiwawo Polepole anati boma likufuna kuteteza anthu onse aku Malawi. Undunawu unati udzawapatsa chakudya, thandizo la maulendo, ndi thandizo lina. Boma lidzawathandiza kufika m'madera awo.

Anthu ambiri ochokera kumayiko ena adachoka m'nyumba zawo ku South Africa chifukwa cha tsankho la alendo. Anayenera kupita kumisasa ku Mossel Bay. Bungwe la Malawi High Commission lakhala likulandira mafoni kuchokera kwa anthu omwe akufuna kubwerera kwawo. Kubwerera kwawo kukusonyeza kuti boma likufuna kuthandiza anthu aku Malawi omwe ali pachiwopsezo. Undunawu unati anthu ambiri akhoza kubwezeretsedwa ngati akufuna thandizo. Anthu 150 ndi m'gulu lalikulu la anthu ochokera kumayiko ena omwe anali m'misasa.

Loya Benedicto Kondowe anati ndibwino kuti boma lathandiza anthuwa. Koma anati anthu sayenera kunyalanyaza machenjezo oyendera. Iye anati anthu sayenera kupita kumalo omwe si otetezeka chifukwa chongofuna ntchito.

en.title: New law makes construction rules tough
en.body: Malawi's new Construction Industry Act came into law in September 2025. The Construction Regulatory Authority (Cira) now has more power to check quality and punish people who break the rules. The law gives Cira authority over all builders, not just some. CEO Gerald Khonje said the group will be firm and strong.
en.body_more:
The act, called Construction Industry Act Number 28 of 2025, was made to tighten rules, build local skill, and make projects better. Cira, which used to be called National Construction Industry Council, must now check who builds, register builders, watch compliance, and enforce standards across the country.

Khonje said Cira now has power to punish clients who do not follow the rules. Before, the group only had power over people who were already in the industry.
